verses.hebrew_full: אִ֛ישׁ הָיָ֥ה בְאֶֽרֶץ־ע֖וּץ אִיּ֣וֹב שְׁמ֑וֹ וְהָיָ֣ה הָאִ֣ישׁ הַה֗וּא תָּ֧ם וְיָשָׁ֛ר וִירֵ֥א אֱלֹהִ֖ים וְסָ֥ר מֵרָֽע
verses.translation: There was a man in the land of Uz named Job. That man was blameless and upright; he feared God and shunned evil.
verses.rashi: There was a man in the land of Uz This means in the land of Aram, as it is written (Gen. 22: 21): “Uz, his firstborn,” of the sons of Nahor. and that man was Although that is a future expression, that is the style of the Hebrew language, and the proper expression for it is “Now that man was greater than all the children of the land of the East”; the children of the land of the East, for Aram is in the east of the world, as it is stated (Num. 23:7): “From Aram has Balak, king of Moab, brought me, from the mountains of the East.” Another explanation: Uz is Job. Buz is Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite. This is what Job said (31:34): “and the most contemptible of families shatters me.” This one (the Buzite) who shatters my words is of my family. This is what people say: “From the very forest, the ax handle is formed.” Another explanation: in the land of Uz In the land whence they take evil counsels against the Holy One, blessed be He. As it is stated (Gen. 10:11): “Out of that land Asshur went forth.” Now what counsel did they take? “He has no right to take the upper worlds and to give us the lower world.” Instead, come to the land of Shinar, where there are many gods, concerning which it is stated: “for it is a land of graven images” (Jer. 50:38): “And let us make a tower whose top shall reach to the heavens, and let us make a name for ourselves” (Gen. 11:4). The word “name” stated here refers only to idolatry, as it is stated (Exod. 23:13): “You shall not make mention of the name of other gods.” What did the Holy One, blessed be He, do? He sat in judgment over them and stood up witnesses against them, as it is stated (Gen. 11:5): “And the Lord came down to see the city and the tower.” Now does the Omnipresent have to come down to see? Is it not written: “The eyes of the Lord are roving to and fro throughout the entire earth”? Rather, it is to teach a ruling to future generations, that judges may not pass judgment except with eyewitnesses, as it is stated (Lev.5:1): “Having seen or known.” was Heb. והיה. According to its simple meaning, it is like ויהי, and its meaning is both past and future. sincere and upright But [I could think] that as regards matters between him and his Maker, he was not righteous. Therefore, it is stated: God- fearing and shunning evil.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּוָּ֥לְדוּ ל֛וֹ שִׁבְעָ֥ה בָנִ֖ים וְשָׁל֥וֹשׁ בָּנֽוֹת
verses.translation: Seven sons and three daughters were born to him;

verses.hebrew_full: וַיְהִ֣י מִ֠קְנֵ֠הוּ שִֽׁבְעַ֨ת אַלְפֵי־צֹ֜אן וּשְׁלֹ֧שֶׁת אַלְפֵ֣י גְמַלִּ֗ים וַחֲמֵ֨שׁ מֵא֤וֹת צֶֽמֶד־בָּקָר֙ וַחֲמֵ֣שׁ מֵא֣וֹת אֲתוֹנ֔וֹת וַעֲבֻדָּ֖ה רַבָּ֣ה מְאֹ֑ד וַֽיְהִי֙ הָאִ֣ישׁ הַה֔וּא גָּד֖וֹל מִכׇּל־בְּנֵי־קֶֽדֶם
verses.translation: his possessions were seven thousand sheep, three thousand camels, five hundred yoke of oxen and five hundred jennies, and a very large household. That man was wealthier than anyone in the East.
verses.rashi: seven thousand sheep He counted the largest number first. greater than all the children of the East Than the generation of the division, as it is written (Gen. 11:2): “And it came to pass as they migrated from the east.” But concerning Abraham, it is not so, for concerning Abraham, it is written הגדול, the greatest, as it is stated (Josh. 14:15): “And the name of Hebron before was Kirjath-Arba; he was the greatest man among the “Anakim.” It should have said אדם גדול, a great man. Why did he write האדם הגדול, the greatest man? By this, Scripture hinted that he was a great man who passed ten tests. Therefore, his title is written with two “heys”, the numerical value of which is ten. Job, however, described by the word גדול, greater, passed only one [test], as it is stated: sincere and upright, etc., that out of that entire generation, he was not apprehended for that iniquity.

verses.hebrew_full: וְהָלְכ֤וּ בָנָיו֙ וְעָשׂ֣וּ מִשְׁתֶּ֔ה בֵּ֖ית אִ֣ישׁ יוֹמ֑וֹ וְשָׁלְח֗וּ וְקָֽרְאוּ֙ לִשְׁלֹ֣שֶׁת אַחְיֹתֵיהֶ֔ם לֶאֱכֹ֥ל וְלִשְׁתּ֖וֹת עִמָּהֶֽם
verses.translation: It was the custom of his sons to hold feasts, each on his set day in his own home. They would invite their three sisters to eat and drink with them.
verses.rashi: Now his sons would go and make a feast in each one’s house on his day In the house of each one on his day. and they would send and invite Heb. ושלחו וקראו, like ושולחים וקוראים, that they would do so constantly.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיְהִ֡י כִּ֣י הִקִּ֩יפוּ֩ יְמֵ֨י הַמִּשְׁתֶּ֜ה וַיִּשְׁלַ֧ח אִיּ֣וֹב וַֽיְקַדְּשֵׁ֗ם וְהִשְׁכִּ֣ים בַּבֹּ֘קֶר֮ וְהֶעֱלָ֣ה עֹלוֹת֮ מִסְפַּ֣ר כֻּלָּם֒ כִּ֚י אָמַ֣ר אִיּ֔וֹב אוּלַי֙ חָטְא֣וּ בָנַ֔י וּבֵרְכ֥וּ אֱלֹהִ֖ים בִּלְבָבָ֑ם כָּ֛כָה יַעֲשֶׂ֥ה אִיּ֖וֹב כׇּל־הַיָּמִֽים
verses.translation: When a round of feast days was over, Job would send word to them to sanctify themselves, and, rising early in the morning, he would make burnt offerings, one for each of them; for Job thought, “Perhaps my children have sinned and blasphemed God in their thoughts.” This is what Job always used to do.
verses.rashi: Now it would come about when the cycle of the feasting days would be over When the days of the feasting would be over, Job would act in this manner; upon every termination of the seven days on which the days of feasting rotated, he would send and summon them (ויקדשם) ; i.e., he would prepare them and summon them, as in (Num. 11:18). “Prepare yourselves for tommorow.” and offer up... early in the morning The meaning is that he would offer up burnt-offerings early. Therefore, Scripture wrote in the future tense, that he was wont to do so always at the end of every seven days. the number of all of them The sons and the daughters. and blasphemed Heb. וברכו, lit. will do. A euphemism [used in] referring to the Most High. So would Job do Heb. יעשה, lit. will do. He would always do in this manner. When Scripture writes about a constant occurrence, sometimes it writes in the past tense, and sometimes in the future tense.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיְהִ֣י הַיּ֔וֹם וַיָּבֹ֙אוּ֙ בְּנֵ֣י הָאֱלֹהִ֔ים לְהִתְיַצֵּ֖ב עַל־יְהֹוָ֑ה וַיָּב֥וֹא גַֽם־הַשָּׂטָ֖ן בְּתוֹכָֽם
verses.translation: One day the divine beings presented themselves before GOD, and the Adversary. came along with them.
verses.rashi: Now the day came about That day which was Rosh Hashanah, (known as a day of sounding the shofar, and the Holy One, blessed be He, commanded the Adversary to bring the merit and the guilt of all creatures. This is the meaning of “from going to and fro on the earth.”) and the angels of God came to stand beside the Lord to contend with Him, because the expression of standing refers only to judgment, as it is stated (Isa. 3:13): “The Lord stands to plead.” and the Adversary, too, came among them to accuse the people.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֧אמֶר יְהֹוָ֛ה אֶל־הַשָּׂטָ֖ן מֵאַ֣יִן תָּבֹ֑א וַיַּ֨עַן הַשָּׂטָ֤ן אֶת־יְהֹוָה֙ וַיֹּאמַ֔ר מִשּׁ֣וּט בָּאָ֔רֶץ וּמֵֽהִתְהַלֵּ֖ךְ בָּֽהּ
verses.translation: GOD said to the Adversary, “Where have you been?” The Adversary answered GOD, “I have been roaming all over the earth.”
verses.rashi: “Where are you coming from?” Scripture speaks of etiquette, that the greater one speaks first and permits the smaller one to reply. “From going to and fro on the earth” As is explained above, and it is in the homiletic section of the chapter [that begins], “The partners.” “From going to and fro on the earth and from walking in it.” So is my wont to go to and fro, to see the evil ones and the good ones. Now I went to and fro throughout the entire earth and I did not find anyone like Abraham, about whom it is said (Gen. 13:17): “Rise, go to and fro in the land.” The Sages of blessed memory (Baba Bathra 16a) said that the Adversary meant this for the sake of Heaven, in order that Abraham’s merit not be forgotten by our God.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Commonly transcribed YHWH

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר יְהֹוָה֙ אֶל־הַשָּׂטָ֔ן הֲשַׂ֥מְתָּ לִבְּךָ֖ עַל־עַבְדִּ֣י אִיּ֑וֹב כִּ֣י אֵ֤ין כָּמֹ֙הוּ֙ בָּאָ֔רֶץ אִ֣ישׁ תָּ֧ם וְיָשָׁ֛ר יְרֵ֥א אֱלֹהִ֖ים וְסָ֥ר מֵרָֽע
verses.translation: GOD said to the Adversary, “Have you noticed My servant Job? There is no one like him on earth, a blameless and upright man who fears God and shuns evil!”
verses.rashi: Have you paid attention to My servant Job that you should desire to denounce him? “Have you paid, etc.” An expression of a wonder. sincere and upright Perfect in his deeds.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֧עַן הַשָּׂטָ֛ן אֶת־יְהֹוָ֖ה וַיֹּאמַ֑ר הַֽחִנָּ֔ם יָרֵ֥א אִיּ֖וֹב אֱלֹהִֽים
verses.translation: The Adversary answered GOD, “Does Job not have good reason to fear God?
verses.rashi: “Does Job fear God for nothing?” This is a question.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Commonly transcribed YHWH

verses.hebrew_full: הֲלֹֽא־אַ֠תָּ֠ה (את) שַׂ֣כְתָּ בַעֲד֧וֹ וּבְעַד־בֵּית֛וֹ וּבְעַ֥ד כׇּל־אֲשֶׁר־ל֖וֹ מִסָּבִ֑יב מַעֲשֵׂ֤ה יָדָיו֙ בֵּרַ֔כְתָּ וּמִקְנֵ֖הוּ פָּרַ֥ץ בָּאָֽרֶץ
verses.translation: Why, it is You who have fenced him round, him and his household and all that he has. You have blessed his efforts so that his possessions spread out in the land.
verses.rashi: Haven’t You made a hedge around him Because he knows that You visit him frequently to perform miracles for him, and he is fruitful and multiplies; therefore he performs all these righteous deeds—but You have not tested him. made a hedge around him You protected him like a wall, as in (Isa. 5:5) “I will remove its hedge (משוכתו),” its wall. Every expression of בעד stated in the Hebrew language is only an expression of “opposite and before him,” as in (Lev.16:6), “and he shall effect atonement for himself (וכפר בעדו),” he shall wipe away the sin from opposite you and before you (sic), so that the atonement shall be a shield about you. Similarly (Ps. 3:4), “[But You,] O Lord, are a shield about me (בעדי) .” spread out in the land Heb. פרץ, gained strength, as in (Exod. 1:12), “So would they gain strength (יפרץ), which the Targum renders: And so would they become strong. This is its simple meaning, but our Sages of blessed memory stated in the Aggadah (Baba Bathra 15b, 16a) that they broke the fence of the world, for the goats would slay the wolves.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object
to hedge or fence up or about

verses.hebrew_full: וְאוּלָם֙ שְֽׁלַֽח־נָ֣א יָֽדְךָ֔ וְגַ֖ע בְּכׇל־אֲשֶׁר־ל֑וֹ אִם־לֹ֥א עַל־פָּנֶ֖יךָ יְבָרְכֶֽךָּ
verses.translation: But lay Your hand upon all that he has and he will surely blaspheme You to Your face.”
verses.rashi: But were you to touch all that he has, would he not blaspheme You to Your face immediately? This is a question. And then, the Lord said to the Adversary, “Behold, all that he has is in your hands.”

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יְהֹוָ֜ה אֶל־הַשָּׂטָ֗ן הִנֵּ֤ה כׇל־אֲשֶׁר־לוֹ֙ בְּיָדֶ֔ךָ רַ֣ק אֵלָ֔יו אַל־תִּשְׁלַ֖ח יָדֶ֑ךָ וַיֵּצֵא֙ הַשָּׂטָ֔ן מֵעִ֖ם פְּנֵ֥י יְהֹוָֽה
verses.translation: GOD replied to the Adversary, “See, all that he has is in your power; only do not lay a hand on him.” The Adversary departed from GOD’s presence.
verses.rashi: “Behold, all that he has etc. Another explanation: Now the day came about That day when they recommenced the cycle [and were] to eat at the house of their eldest brother. the angels of God The heavenly hosts, which are near the Divine Presence, to be the members of His household, and were therefore called His children. to stand beside the Lord Heb. Around Him, as (Gen. 45:1) “all who stood around him (עליו).”

verses.hebrew_full: וַיְהִ֖י הַיּ֑וֹם וּבָנָ֨יו וּבְנֹתָ֤יו אֹֽכְלִים֙ וְשֹׁתִ֣ים יַ֔יִן בְּבֵ֖ית אֲחִיהֶ֥ם הַבְּכֽוֹר
verses.translation: One day, as his sons and daughters were eating and drinking wine in the house of their eldest brother,

verses.hebrew_full: וּמַלְאָ֛ךְ בָּ֥א אֶל־אִיּ֖וֹב וַיֹּאמַ֑ר הַבָּקָר֙ הָי֣וּ חֹֽרְשׁ֔וֹת וְהָאֲתֹנ֖וֹת רֹע֥וֹת עַל־יְדֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: a messenger came to Job and said, “The oxen were plowing and the jennies were grazing alongside them
verses.rashi: beside them Heb. על ידיהם lit. on their hands. This is an expression of a place only because it is prepared and available at his hand. Our Sages of blessed memory said in the Aggadah (Baba Bathra 15b, 16a), that the Holy One, blessed be He, gave them a taste of the world to come; for after the plowing by the she-donkeys, they would sow with the plowing, and the she-donkeys would immediately graze on the young greens from the furrow.

verses.hebrew_full: וַתִּפֹּ֤ל שְׁבָא֙ וַתִּקָּחֵ֔ם וְאֶת־הַנְּעָרִ֖ים הִכּ֣וּ לְפִי־חָ֑רֶב וָאִמָּ֨לְטָ֧ה רַק־אֲנִ֛י לְבַדִּ֖י לְהַגִּ֥יד לָֽךְ
verses.translation: when Sabeans attacked them and carried them off, and put the attendants to the sword; I alone have escaped to tell you.”
verses.rashi: And Sheba The kingdom of Sheba fell upon them and encamped beside them, as in (Gen. 25: 18), “before all his brethren he settled.” the youths Your servants who were guarding them. and I... escaped Heb. ואמלטה. The word מליטה is only an expression of withdrawal, that one withdrew from trouble.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·a boy
adj·I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: ע֣וֹד זֶ֣ה מְדַבֵּ֗ר וְזֶה֮ בָּ֣א וַיֹּאמַר֒ אֵ֣שׁ אֱלֹהִ֗ים נָֽפְלָה֙ מִן־הַשָּׁמַ֔יִם וַתִּבְעַ֥ר בַּצֹּ֛אן וּבַנְּעָרִ֖ים וַתֹּֽאכְלֵ֑ם וָאִמָּ֨לְטָ֧ה רַק־אֲנִ֛י לְבַדִּ֖י לְהַגִּ֥יד לָֽךְ
verses.translation: This one was still speaking when another came and said, “God’s fire fell from heaven, took hold of the sheep and the attendants, and burned them up; I alone have escaped to tell you.”
verses.rashi: and the youths Scripture speaks of the guards of the flocks.

words.eng: adj·I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: ע֣וֹד זֶ֣ה מְדַבֵּ֗ר וְזֶה֮ בָּ֣א וַיֹּאמַר֒ כַּשְׂדִּ֞ים שָׂ֣מוּ שְׁלֹשָׁ֣ה רָאשִׁ֗ים וַֽיִּפְשְׁט֤וּ עַל־הַגְּמַלִּים֙ וַיִּקָּח֔וּם וְאֶת־הַנְּעָרִ֖ים הִכּ֣וּ לְפִי־חָ֑רֶב וָאִמָּ֨לְטָ֧ה רַק־אֲנִ֛י לְבַדִּ֖י לְהַגִּ֥יד לָֽךְ
verses.translation: This one was still speaking when another came and said, “A Chaldean formation of three columns made a raid on the camels and carried them off and put the attendants to the sword; I alone have escaped to tell you.”
verses.rashi: spread out on the camels This is an expression of plundering plunder according to their custom that as long as they are walking, they stick together and walk stealthily lest people recognize them and drive the cattle away. When they reach the place of the cattle, they spread out in the width of the herd, one on each side, in order to gather the cattle. Now these messengers were messengers of the Adversary, and in order to provoke him [Job] and cause him to sin, they first informed him of a small loss and later of a larger one. So is the sequence of all of them.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·a boy
adj·I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: עַ֚ד זֶ֣ה מְדַבֵּ֔ר וְזֶ֖ה בָּ֣א וַיֹּאמַ֑ר בָּנֶ֨יךָ וּבְנוֹתֶ֤יךָ אֹֽכְלִים֙ וְשֹׁתִ֣ים יַ֔יִן בְּבֵ֖ית אֲחִיהֶ֥ם הַבְּכֽוֹר
verses.translation: This one was still speaking when another came and said, “Your sons and daughters were eating and drinking wine in the house of their eldest brother

verses.hebrew_full: וְהִנֵּה֩ ר֨וּחַ גְּדוֹלָ֜ה בָּ֣אָה מֵעֵ֣בֶר הַמִּדְבָּ֗ר וַיִּגַּע֙ בְּאַרְבַּע֙ פִּנּ֣וֹת הַבַּ֔יִת וַיִּפֹּ֥ל עַל־הַנְּעָרִ֖ים וַיָּמ֑וּתוּ וָאִמָּ֨לְטָ֧ה רַק־אֲנִ֛י לְבַדִּ֖י לְהַגִּ֥יד לָֽךְ
verses.translation: when suddenly a mighty wind came from the wilderness. It struck the four corners of the house so that it collapsed upon the young people and they died; I alone have escaped to tell you.”
verses.rashi: from the other side of the desert From the other side of the desert, for the house was on this side. This is the meaning of עֵבֶר, as it is found in Scriptures. upon the youths The sons, but it is unnecessary to mention the daughters. (to tell you He was permitted only to tell, and at that moment, he died. Not found in certain editions.)

words.eng: adj·I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: וַיָּ֤קׇם אִיּוֹב֙ וַיִּקְרַ֣ע אֶת־מְעִל֔וֹ וַיָּ֖גׇז אֶת־רֹאשׁ֑וֹ וַיִּפֹּ֥ל אַ֖רְצָה וַיִּשְׁתָּֽחוּ
verses.translation: Then Job arose, tore his robe, cut off his hair, and threw himself on the ground and worshiped.
verses.rashi: tore Heb. ויגז, tore out, as in (Jer. 7:29), “Tear out (גזי) your hair for your great men,” which Jonathan renders: תלישה tear out, and like (Ps. 71:6) “From my mother’s womb You pull me (גוזי),” pull me.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·to act unfaithfully
sign of the definite direct object·head

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֩אמֶר֩ עָרֹ֨ם יָצָ֜תִי מִבֶּ֣טֶן אִמִּ֗י וְעָרֹם֙ אָשׁ֣וּב שָׁ֔מָּה יְהֹוָ֣ה נָתַ֔ן וַֽיהֹוָ֖ה לָקָ֑ח יְהִ֛י שֵׁ֥ם יְהֹוָ֖ה מְבֹרָֽךְ
verses.translation: He said, “Naked came I out of my mother’s womb, and naked shall I return there; GOD has given, and GOD has taken away; blessed be GOD’s name.”
verses.rashi: From my mother’s womb the earth, “whence I was taken,” or actually, “my mother.” and I will return there naked He is not referring to the womb. What then is the meaning of “there”? To the place of his return, for which he is destined, and he will not change the law, to return anywhere but to the earth. Therefore, it was not necessary to mention it.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּכׇל־זֹ֖את לֹא־חָטָ֣א אִיּ֑וֹב וְלֹא־נָתַ֥ן תִּפְלָ֖ה לֵאלֹהִֽים
verses.translation: For all that, Job did not sin nor did he cast reproach on God.
verses.rashi: unseemliness Heb. תפלה. He did not ascribe any blemish or unseemliness to the Holy One, Blessed be He. Similarly (Jer. 23:13), “And in the prophets of Samaria I saw unseemliness (תפלה).” neither did he ascribe Heb. נתן lit. gave, to ascribe unseemliness and injustice to the Holy One, blessed be He, like (I Sam. 18:8) “They ascribed (נתנו) to David ten thousands, and to me they ascribed (נתנו) the thousands.”

verses.hebrew_full: וַיְהִ֣י הַיּ֔וֹם וַיָּבֹ֙אוּ֙ בְּנֵ֣י הָאֱלֹהִ֔ים לְהִתְיַצֵּ֖ב עַל־יְהֹוָ֑ה וַיָּב֤וֹא גַֽם־הַשָּׂטָן֙ בְּתֹכָ֔ם לְהִתְיַצֵּ֖ב עַל־יְהֹוָֽה
verses.translation: One day the divine beings presented themselves before GOD. The Adversary came along with them to present himself before GOD.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר יְהֹוָה֙ אֶל־הַשָּׂטָ֔ן אֵ֥י מִזֶּ֖ה תָּבֹ֑א וַיַּ֨עַן הַשָּׂטָ֤ן אֶת־יְהֹוָה֙ וַיֹּאמַ֔ר מִשֻּׁ֣ט בָּאָ֔רֶץ וּמֵֽהִתְהַלֵּ֖ךְ בָּֽהּ
verses.translation: GOD said to the Adversary, “Where have you been?” The Adversary answered GOD, “I have been roaming all over the earth.”
verses.rashi: “Where are you coming from?” Where is the place about which you may say, “From this place I come”?

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Commonly transcribed YHWH

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יְהֹוָ֜ה אֶל־הַשָּׂטָ֗ן הֲשַׂ֣מְתָּ לִבְּךָ֮ אֶל־עַבְדִּ֣י אִיּוֹב֒ כִּי֩ אֵ֨ין כָּמֹ֜הוּ בָּאָ֗רֶץ אִ֣ישׁ תָּ֧ם וְיָשָׁ֛ר יְרֵ֥א אֱלֹהִ֖ים וְסָ֣ר מֵרָ֑ע וְעֹדֶ֙נּוּ֙ מַחֲזִ֣יק בְּתֻמָּת֔וֹ וַתְּסִיתֵ֥נִי ב֖וֹ לְבַלְּע֥וֹ חִנָּֽם
verses.translation: GOD said to the Adversary, “Have you noticed My servant Job? There is no one like him on earth, a blameless and upright man who fears God and shuns evil. He still keeps his integrity; so you have incited Me against him to destroy him for no good reason.”
verses.rashi: maintains Heb. מחזיק, holds, as in (Exod. 4:4) “and he held (ויחזק) onto it”; (Prov. 26:17), “One who holds (מחזיק) onto a dog’s ears.” Yet you enticed Me against him You enticed Me to destroy him. Every expression of הֲסָתָה is only an expression of attracting a person to his counsel.

words.eng: inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֧עַן הַשָּׂטָ֛ן אֶת־יְהֹוָ֖ה וַיֹּאמַ֑ר ע֣וֹר בְּעַד־ע֗וֹר וְכֹל֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר לָאִ֔ישׁ יִתֵּ֖ן בְּעַ֥ד נַפְשֽׁוֹ
verses.translation: The Adversary answered GOD, “Skin for skin—all that the man has he will give up for his life.
verses.rashi: Skin for skin One limb for another limb. It is human nature that, when one sees a sword coming toward his head, he protects it with his arm in front of it. Surely a person will give all the money he has. for his life For his life to protect it. Because this person knows that he is liable to lose his life, he does not worry about his money.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Commonly transcribed YHWH

verses.hebrew_full: אוּלָם֙ שְֽׁלַֽח־נָ֣א יָֽדְךָ֔ וְגַ֥ע אֶל־עַצְמ֖וֹ וְאֶל־בְּשָׂר֑וֹ אִם־לֹ֥א אֶל־פָּנֶ֖יךָ יְבָרְכֶֽךָּ
verses.translation: But lay a hand on his bones and his flesh, and he will surely blaspheme You to Your face.”
verses.rashi: But Heb. אולם, but. will he not Will You not see immediately that he will blaspheme You to Your face?

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֧אמֶר יְהֹוָ֛ה אֶל־הַשָּׂטָ֖ן הִנּ֣וֹ בְיָדֶ֑ךָ אַ֖ךְ אֶת־נַפְשׁ֥וֹ שְׁמֹֽר
verses.translation: So GOD said to the Adversary, “See, he is in your power; only spare his life.”
verses.rashi: but preserve his life that it does not go out. From here the Sages said, “The Adversary’s pain was greater than Job’s. This is analogous to one saying to his friend, ‘Break this barrel but preserve its wine.’”

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·soul

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֵּצֵא֙ הַשָּׂטָ֔ן מֵאֵ֖ת פְּנֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֑ה וַיַּ֤ךְ אֶת־אִיּוֹב֙ בִּשְׁחִ֣ין רָ֔ע מִכַּ֥ף רַגְל֖וֹ (עד) וְעַ֥ד קׇדְקֳדֽוֹ
verses.translation: The Adversary departed from GOD’s presence and inflicted a severe inflammation on Job from the sole of his foot to the crown of his head.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Job = "hated"

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּֽקַּֽח־ל֣וֹ חֶ֔רֶשׂ לְהִתְגָּרֵ֖ד בּ֑וֹ וְה֖וּא יֹשֵׁ֥ב בְּתוֹךְ־הָאֵֽפֶר
verses.translation: He took a potsherd to scratch himself as he sat in ashes.
verses.rashi: to scratch himself with Heb. להתגרד. This word is spelled with a “dalet.” It is a Mishnaic expression (Rosh Hashanah 27b): “If he scraped it (גרדו) and reduced it to its normal size,” but גורר with a “reish” is only an expression of dragging, like (Shabbath 29b), “A person may drag a bed or a bench.”

words.eng: inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: וַתֹּ֤אמֶר לוֹ֙ אִשְׁתּ֔וֹ עֹדְךָ֖ מַחֲזִ֣יק בְּתֻמָּתֶ֑ךָ בָּרֵ֥ךְ אֱלֹהִ֖ים וָמֻֽת
verses.translation: His wife said to him, “You still keep your integrity! Blaspheme God and die!”
verses.rashi: maintain Heb. מחזיק, hold. and die Heb. ומת. This is an imperative expression, as (Deut. 32: 50), “And die on the mountain.”

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֵלֶ֗יהָ כְּדַבֵּ֞ר אַחַ֤ת הַנְּבָלוֹת֙ תְּדַבֵּ֔רִי גַּ֣ם אֶת־הַטּ֗וֹב נְקַבֵּל֙ מֵאֵ֣ת הָאֱלֹהִ֔ים וְאֶת־הָרָ֖ע לֹ֣א נְקַבֵּ֑ל בְּכׇל־זֹ֛את לֹא־חָטָ֥א אִיּ֖וֹב בִּשְׂפָתָֽיו
verses.translation: But he said to her, “You talk as any shameless woman might talk! Should we accept only good from God and not accept evil?” For all that, Job said nothing sinful.
verses.rashi: also Is it not enough that He created us? Should we also accept the good, but not accept the evil? This is a wonder. with his lips but in his heart he did sin.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·to be good
sign of the definite direct object·to be bad

verses.hebrew_full: וַֽיִּשְׁמְע֞וּ שְׁלֹ֣שֶׁת רֵעֵ֣י אִיּ֗וֹב אֵ֣ת כׇּל־הָרָעָ֣ה הַזֹּאת֮ הַבָּ֣אָה עָלָיו֒ וַיָּבֹ֙אוּ֙ אִ֣ישׁ מִמְּקֹמ֔וֹ אֱלִיפַ֤ז הַתֵּֽימָנִי֙ וּבִלְדַּ֣ד הַשּׁוּחִ֔י וְצוֹפַ֖ר הַנַּֽעֲמָתִ֑י וַיִּוָּעֲד֣וּ יַחְדָּ֔ו לָב֥וֹא לָנֽוּד־ל֖וֹ וּֽלְנַחֲמֽוֹ
verses.translation: When Job’s three friends heard about all these calamities that had befallen him, each came from his home—Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite. They met together to go and console and comfort him.
verses.rashi: Job’s three friends Heb. רעי, those who loved him. to bemoan Heb. לנוד, as in (Isa. 51:19): “who will lament (ינוד) for you?”; (Jer. 22:10), “neither bemoan (תנדו) him.”

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּשְׂא֨וּ אֶת־עֵינֵיהֶ֤ם מֵֽרָחוֹק֙ וְלֹ֣א הִכִּירֻ֔הוּ וַיִּשְׂא֥וּ קוֹלָ֖ם וַיִּבְכּ֑וּ וַֽיִּקְרְעוּ֙ אִ֣ישׁ מְעִל֔וֹ וַיִּזְרְק֥וּ עָפָ֛ר עַל־רָאשֵׁיהֶ֖ם הַשָּׁמָֽיְמָה
verses.translation: When they saw him from a distance, they could not recognize him, and they broke into loud weeping; each one tore his robe and threw dust into the air onto his head.
verses.rashi: but they did not recognize him because his face had changed as a result of the agonies. towards heaven i.e., high over their heads. That was their mourning custom.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·eye

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֵּשְׁב֤וּ אִתּוֹ֙ לָאָ֔רֶץ שִׁבְעַ֥ת יָמִ֖ים וְשִׁבְעַ֣ת לֵיל֑וֹת וְאֵין־דֹּבֵ֤ר אֵלָיו֙ דָּבָ֔ר כִּ֣י רָא֔וּ כִּי־גָדַ֥ל הַכְּאֵ֖ב מְאֹֽד
verses.translation: They sat with him on the ground seven days and seven nights. None spoke a word to him for they saw how very great was his suffering.
verses.rashi: on the ground On the ground, to share his pain.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: אַֽחֲרֵי־כֵ֗ן פָּתַ֤ח אִיּוֹב֙ אֶת־פִּ֔יהוּ וַיְקַלֵּ֖ל אֶת־יוֹמֽוֹ
verses.translation: Afterward, Job began to speak and cursed the day of his birth.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·peh
sign of the definite direct object·day

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֥עַן אִיּ֗וֹב וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Job spoke up and said:
verses.rashi: Job’s AddressNow Job raised his voice Heb. ויען, he cried out, for every expression of עניה in the Torah is only an expression of raising the voice, and the “father” of them all is (Deut. 27:14): “The Levites shall raise their voices (וענו) etc. with a loud voice.”

verses.hebrew_full: יֹ֣אבַד י֭וֹם אִוָּ֣לֶד בּ֑וֹ וְהַלַּ֥יְלָה אָ֝מַ֗ר הֹ֣רָה גָֽבֶר
verses.translation: Perish the day on which I was born,And the night it was announced,“A male has been conceived!”
verses.rashi: in which I was to be born This is a future expression, that he was saying, “Would that the day in which I was destined to be born be lost, and then I would not have been born.” and the night That too should have been lost. when one said that the reporter, upon whom it was incumbent [to report] would say. “A man has impregnated.” That my father impregnated my mother. Concerning a female, it is customary to say הרתה, she conceived, and concerning a male, it is customary to say, הורה, he impregnated the female. and the night when one said This is one of the places where Scripture abbreviates its words because it is unnecessary to explain who [the subject] is, and the expression means, “the sayer said.” [Similar to this is (Amos. 6:12), “Will horses run on the rock, or will one plow with cattle?” He should have said, “Will a man plow with cattle?”] However, our Sages said, “The angel appointed over conception is named Laylah.”

words.eng: inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: הַיּ֥וֹם הַה֗וּא יְֽהִ֫י־חֹ֥שֶׁךְ אַֽל־יִדְרְשֵׁ֣הוּ אֱל֣וֹהַּ מִמַּ֑עַל וְאַל־תּוֹפַ֖ע עָלָ֣יו נְהָרָֽה
verses.translation: May that day be darkness;May God above have no concern for it;May light not shine on it;
verses.rashi: May that day be dark constantly; every year, when that day arrives. let God not seek it from above for good. and let no light shine upon it Heb. נהרה, light, like צהר, which is translated נהור.

verses.hebrew_full: יִגְאָלֻ֡הוּ חֹ֣שֶׁךְ וְ֭צַלְמָוֶת תִּשְׁכׇּן־עָלָ֣יו עֲנָנָ֑ה יְ֝בַעֲתֻ֗הוּ כִּֽמְרִ֥ירֵי יֽוֹם
verses.translation: May darkness and deep gloom reclaim it;May a pall lie over it;May what blackens the day terrify it.
verses.rashi: defile it Heb. יגאלהו, like (Malachi 1:7), “defiled (מגאל) bread”; “How have we defiled You (גאלנוך)?” an expression of contamination. the shadow of death Heb. צלמות, the shadow of death, darkness that never lights up. like demons of the day Heb. כמרירי, demons who rule at noon, like (Deut. 32: 34), “Ketev Meriri,” who rules at noon; as it is stated (Ps. 91:6): “from Ketev, who rules at noontime.”

verses.hebrew_full: הַלַּ֥יְלָה הַהוּא֮ יִקָּחֵ֢ה֫וּ אֹ֥פֶל אַל־יִ֭חַדְּ בִּימֵ֣י שָׁנָ֑ה בְּמִסְפַּ֥ר יְ֝רָחִ֗ים אַל־יָבֹֽא
verses.translation: May obscurity carry off that night;May it not be counted among the days of the year;May it not appear in any of its months;
verses.rashi: it shall not rejoice among the days Heb. אל יחד, it shall not rejoice, as in (Exod. 18:9), “And Jethro rejoiced (וַיִחַד).” (Another explanation is: It shall not join. This does not appear in certain editions.)

words.eng: (= אַל־לי׳ = עַל־לי׳) according to the opinion

verses.hebrew_full: הִנֵּ֤ה הַלַּ֣יְלָה הַ֭הוּא יְהִ֣י גַלְמ֑וּד אַל־תָּב֖וֹא רְנָנָ֣ה בֽוֹ
verses.translation: May that night be desolate;May no sound of joy be heard in it;
verses.rashi: lonely Alone and lacking any man or creature.

words.eng: inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: יִקְּבֻ֥הוּ אֹֽרְרֵי־י֑וֹם הָ֝עֲתִידִ֗ים עֹרֵ֥ר לִוְיָתָֽן
verses.translation: May those who cast spells upon the day as equivalent of yam; compare the combination of sea with Leviathan in Ps. 74.13, 14 and with Dragon in Job 7.12; cf. also Isa. 27.1. damn it,Those prepared to disable Leviathan;
verses.rashi: curse it Heb. יקבהו, curse it, as in (Num. 22:17), “curse (קבה) for me.” those who curse the day Those who curse their days, since they intend to pronounce a curse because of their anguish. those destined to be childless in their union Heb. ערר לויתן, to be childless (ערירים) in their union; to join their mate in the union of man and wife, without children. And I saw in the Jerusalem Talmud (Moed Katan 1:5) that עֹרֵר means, to lament their mate when he dies, as in (Moed Katan 1:6): A person may not inspire lamentations (יערער) for his dead relative.

verses.hebrew_full: יֶחְשְׁכוּ֮ כּוֹכְבֵ֢י נִ֫שְׁפּ֥וֹ יְקַו־לְא֥וֹר וָאַ֑יִן וְאַל־יִ֝רְאֶ֗ה בְּעַפְעַפֵּי־שָֽׁחַר
verses.translation: May its twilight stars remain dark;May it hope for light and have none;May it not see the glimmerings of the dawn—
verses.rashi: its evening Heb. נשפו, the darkness of its night. the rays of Heb. עפעפי, rays of, like (Jer. 9:17), “and our eyelids (or pupils) (ועפעפינו) gush water.”

words.eng: to wait·to be or become light

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י לֹ֣א סָ֭גַר דַּלְתֵ֣י בִטְנִ֑י וַיַּסְתֵּ֥ר עָ֝מָ֗ל מֵעֵינָֽי
verses.translation: Because it did not block my mother’s womb,And hide trouble from my eyes.
verses.rashi: For He did not shut He Who was able to shut. This too is one of the verses that resemble “the night when one said” (verse 2). the doors of my womb from which I emerged, for had He done so, He would have hidden trouble from my eyes.

verses.hebrew_full: לָ֤מָּה לֹּ֣א מֵרֶ֣חֶם אָמ֑וּת מִבֶּ֖טֶן יָצָ֣אתִי וְאֶגְוָֽע
verses.translation: Why did I not die at birth,Expire as I came forth from the womb?
verses.rashi: Why did I not die from the womb? why was I not ready to die immediately upon my emergence from the womb? He speaks in the future tense, as though he were standing now on the day of his emergence from the womb and saying, “Why will I not die now, and why did I not... emerge from the belly and perish?” Why was my death not imminent?

verses.hebrew_full: מַ֭דּוּעַ קִדְּמ֣וּנִי בִרְכָּ֑יִם וּמַה־שָּׁ֝דַ֗יִם כִּ֣י אִינָֽק
verses.translation: Why were there knees to receive me,Or breasts for me to suck?
verses.rashi: Why did knees receive me Why did the knees of my mother receive me before my death? that I should suck [Why] was it decreed upon me to suck them? As he is speaking on the day of his birth, he mentions those subsequent events in the future tense.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־עַ֭תָּה שָׁכַ֣בְתִּי וְאֶשְׁק֑וֹט יָ֝שַׁ֗נְתִּי אָ֤ז יָנ֬וּחַֽ לִֽי
verses.translation: For now would I be lying in repose, asleep and at rest,
verses.rashi: I would be lying Heb. ושכבתי, I would be lying in the grave.

verses.hebrew_full: עִם־מְ֭לָכִים וְיֹ֣עֲצֵי אָ֑רֶץ הַבֹּנִ֖ים חֳרָב֣וֹת לָֽמוֹ
verses.translation: With the world’s kings and counselors who rebuild ruins for themselves,
verses.rashi: who build ruins for themselves It is customary for those who seek fame to rebuild ruined cities as a memorial for themselves, as in (Isa. 58: 12), “And those coming from you shall be called the repairer of the breaches.” That is to say, with princes and famous people. Or, would that I was unknown, and I would be like a stillborn. What Scripture states: I was not like a hidden stillborn, is because it continues with the language with which it started to speak, viz. in the interrogative, and it refers to “Why did knees receive me...or why was I not like a hidden stillborn child?”

verses.hebrew_full: א֣וֹ עִם־שָׂ֭רִים זָהָ֣ב לָהֶ֑ם הַֽמְמַלְאִ֖ים בָּתֵּיהֶ֣ם כָּֽסֶף
verses.translation: Or with nobles who possess gold and who fill their houses with silver.

verses.hebrew_full: א֤וֹ כְנֵ֣פֶל טָ֭מוּן לֹ֣א אֶהְיֶ֑ה כְּ֝עֹלְלִ֗ים לֹא־רָ֥אוּ אֽוֹר
verses.translation: Or why was I not like a buried stillbirth,Like babies who never saw the light?
verses.rashi: like infants Heb. כעוללים, infants. He calls them עוללים because they are all play and dirt, like (below 16:15), “And I sullied (ועללתי) with dust,” and like (Jud. 19:25), “and abused (ויתעללו) her the entire night.”

verses.hebrew_full: שָׁ֣ם רְ֭שָׁעִים חָ֣דְלוּ רֹ֑גֶז וְשָׁ֥ם יָ֝נ֗וּחוּ יְגִ֣יעֵי כֹֽחַ
verses.translation: There the wicked cease from troubling;There rest those whose strength is spent.
verses.rashi: cease from anger They restrain their anger, with which they were accustomed and wont to cause anger in the earth. and there...rest Heb. ינוחו, and there rested. Something that occurs regularly is written in the future tense.

verses.hebrew_full: יַ֭חַד אֲסִירִ֣ים שַׁאֲנָ֑נוּ לֹ֥א שָׁ֝מְע֗וּ ק֣וֹל נֹגֵֽשׂ
verses.translation: Prisoners are wholly at ease;They do not hear the taskmaster’s voice.

verses.hebrew_full: קָטֹ֣ן וְ֭גָדוֹל שָׁ֣ם ה֑וּא וְ֝עֶ֗בֶד חׇפְשִׁ֥י מֵאֲדֹנָֽיו
verses.translation: Small and great alike are there,And the slave is free of his master.
verses.rashi: Small and great are there There it is obvious who is esteemed and who is humble, and since that greatness is perpetual greatness, and that humility is perpetual humility...

verses.hebrew_full: לָ֤מָּה יִתֵּ֣ן לְעָמֵ֣ל א֑וֹר וְ֝חַיִּ֗ים לְמָ֣רֵי נָֽפֶשׁ
verses.translation: Why does [God] give light to the suffererAnd life to the bitter in spirit;
verses.rashi: Why does He give (the giver, i.e., the Holy One, blessed be He.) the toiler light He who grew up with toil and trouble—why did He give him perpetual light and not slay him at birth?

verses.hebrew_full: הַֽמְחַכִּ֣ים לַמָּ֣וֶת וְאֵינֶ֑נּוּ וַֽ֝יַּחְפְּרֻ֗הוּ מִמַּטְמוֹנִֽים
verses.translation: To those who wait for death but it does not come,Who search for it more than for treasure,
verses.rashi: Who await death to those of bitter spirit, who long for and desire death and complain, saying, “it is not here.” and they spy after it Heb. ויתפרהו, and they spy after it. more than after hidden treasures More than people spy after hidden treasures.

verses.hebrew_full: הַשְּׂמֵחִ֥ים אֱלֵי־גִ֑יל יָ֝שִׂ֗ישׂוּ כִּ֣י יִמְצְאוּ־קָֽבֶר
verses.translation: Who rejoice to exultation,And are glad to reach the grave;
verses.rashi: Those who are happy over joyous occurrences and rejoice if they find a grave. over joyous occurrences Because of the joy and the happiness of finding a grave.

verses.hebrew_full: לְ֭גֶבֶר אֲשֶׁר־דַּרְכּ֣וֹ נִסְתָּ֑רָה וַיָּ֖סֶךְ אֱל֣וֹהַּ בַּעֲדֽוֹ
verses.translation: To the man who has lost his way,Whom God has hedged about?
verses.rashi: for a man whose way is hidden All the good deeds that he performed were hidden from the Great Recompenser, and He did not look at them. and God hedged him in He made a wall in front of him to shut him up in a prison, as in (Hos. 2:8) “Behold I will close off your way.”

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־לִפְנֵ֣י לַ֭חְמִי אַנְחָתִ֣י תָבֹ֑א וַֽיִּתְּכ֥וּ כַ֝מַּ֗יִם שַׁאֲגֹתָֽי
verses.translation: My groaning serves as my bread;My roaring pours forth as water.
verses.rashi: comes Heb. תבא, lit. shall come. pour An expression of (Exod. 9:33) “pour (נתך) to the ground.” They run and descend like water. The word ויתכו may also be interpreted as an expression denoting abundance.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י פַ֣חַד פָּ֭חַדְתִּי וַיֶּאֱתָיֵ֑נִי וַאֲשֶׁ֥ר יָ֝גֹ֗רְתִּי יָ֣בֹא לִֽי
verses.translation: For what I feared has overtaken me;What I dreaded has come upon me.
verses.rashi: the thing I feared All my life, I stood in fear of this, as I said (1:5): “Now it would come about, when the cycle of the feasting would days be over, etc.” What I dreaded—Behold!

verses.hebrew_full: לֹ֤א שָׁלַ֨וְתִּי וְלֹ֖א שָׁקַ֥טְתִּי וְֽלֹא־נָ֗חְתִּי וַיָּ֥בֹא רֹֽגֶז
verses.translation: I had no repose, no quiet, no rest,And trouble came.
verses.rashi: I was not at ease from this worry, and behold, trouble has come.

verses.hebrew_full: וַ֭יַּעַן אֱלִיפַ֥ז הַֽתֵּימָנִ֗י וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Then Eliphaz the Temanite said in reply:
verses.rashi: Eliphaz’s AddressEliphaz He was Esau’s son, and because he was nurtured in Isaac’s bosom, he merited that the Shechinah rest upon him. the Temanite From the land of Tema, Esau’s country. (Not found in some editions.)

verses.hebrew_full: הֲנִסָּ֬ה דָבָ֣ר אֵלֶ֣יךָ תִּלְאֶ֑ה וַעְצֹ֥ר בְּ֝מִלִּ֗ין מִ֣י יוּכָֽל
verses.translation: If one ventures a word with you, will it be too much?But who can hold back his words?
verses.rashi: Because He tested This is an expression of wonder. Because He tested you with one thing, should you weary? With one rest that the Holy One, blessed be He, tested you, you have become weary. From now on, who will be able to withhold his words from answering you?

verses.hebrew_full: הִ֭נֵּה יִסַּ֣רְתָּ רַבִּ֑ים וְיָדַ֖יִם רָפ֣וֹת תְּחַזֵּֽק
verses.translation: See, you have encouraged many;You have strengthened failing hands.
verses.rashi: Behold, you have chastised many who multiplied words; you admonished them and chastised them. and...weak hands of those who feared the retribution that came upon them. You used to strengthen [them] and say, “Fear not, for that is the Divine Standard of Justice.”

verses.hebrew_full: כּ֭וֹשֵׁל יְקִימ֣וּן מִלֶּ֑יךָ וּבִרְכַּ֖יִם כֹּרְע֣וֹת תְּאַמֵּֽץ
verses.translation: Your words have kept him who stumbled from falling;You have braced knees that gave way.
verses.rashi: would pick up Your words were wont to pick up the stumbler.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י עַתָּ֨ה תָּב֣וֹא אֵלֶ֣יךָ וַתֵּ֑לֶא תִּגַּ֥ע עָ֝דֶ֗יךָ וַתִּבָּהֵֽל
verses.translation: But now that it overtakes you, it is too much;It reaches you, and you are unnerved.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲלֹ֣א יִ֭רְאָתְךָ כִּסְלָתֶ֑ךָ תִּ֝קְוָתְךָ֗ וְתֹ֣ם דְּרָכֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: Is not your piety your confidence,Your integrity your hope?
verses.rashi: Surely now, your end reveals your beginning, that your fear, that you feared heaven... your foolishness is due to foolishness and not to full understanding, and so are your hope and the sincerity of your ways all foolishness.

verses.hebrew_full: זְכׇר־נָ֗א מִ֤י ה֣וּא נָקִ֣י אָבָ֑ד וְ֝אֵיפֹ֗ה יְשָׁרִ֥ים נִכְחָֽדוּ
verses.translation: Think now, what innocent ever perished?Where have the upright been destroyed?
verses.rashi: destroyed Heb. נכחדו, disappeared and perished, like (Exod. 9:15), “and you would vanish (ותכחד) from the earth.”

verses.hebrew_full: כַּאֲשֶׁ֣ר רָ֭אִיתִי חֹ֣רְשֵׁי אָ֑וֶן וְזֹרְעֵ֖י עָמָ֣ל יִקְצְרֻֽהוּ
verses.translation: As I have seen, those who plow evilAnd sow mischief reap them.
verses.rashi: As I have seen that those who plow violence and prepare the iniquity in their thoughts, like a plower who prepares [the field] before sowing, afterwards they sow mischief with their deeds. And I saw that they will reap it—i.e., their evil that comes upon them, and I saw that.

verses.hebrew_full: מִנִּשְׁמַ֣ת אֱל֣וֹהַּ יֹאבֵ֑דוּ וּמֵר֖וּחַ אַפּ֣וֹ יִכְלֽוּ
verses.translation: They perish by a blast from the divine,Are gone at the breath of God’s nostrils.
verses.rashi: From the breath of God they will perish From the blowing of His stormwind and His anger. and from the wind of His nostrils they are always destroyed, and he spoke of the Generation of the Flood.

verses.hebrew_full: שַׁאֲגַ֣ת אַ֭רְיֵה וְק֣וֹל שָׁ֑חַל וְשִׁנֵּ֖י כְפִירִ֣ים נִתָּֽעוּ
verses.translation: The lion may roar, the cub may howl,But the teeth of the king of beasts are broken.
verses.rashi: The roar of an old lion, the voice of a young lion The אריה is the old one, the שַׁחַל the middle one, and the כפיר the cub; all of them were misled, i.e., the kings, the princes and the slaves; all these names are names of the lion.

verses.hebrew_full: לַ֭יִשׁ אֹבֵ֣ד מִבְּלִי־טָ֑רֶף וּבְנֵ֥י לָ֝בִ֗יא יִתְפָּרָֽדוּ
verses.translation: The lion perishes for lack of prey,And its whelps are scattered.
verses.rashi: A lion perishes without prey The princes perished like a lion who perishes without food; so did their strength to commit violence dissipate. The word אֹבֵד is not an expression of a man who loses something, but [of a man who has] himself become lost, like (Ps. 119.176), “I have gone astray like a lost (אבד) sheep.” and like (ibid. 31:13), “I was like a lost (אבד) vessel.” and the young of the lion will scatter He is speaking of the Generation of the Dispersion, who were scattered from there [Babel] over the face of the entire earth.

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֭אֵלַי דָּבָ֣ר יְגֻנָּ֑ב וַתִּקַּ֥ח אׇ֝זְנִ֗י שֵׁ֣מֶץ מֶֽנְהֽוּ
verses.translation: A word came to me in stealth;My ear caught a whisper of it.
verses.rashi: Now a word was conveyed secretly to me He returns to his rebuke with which he has been chastising him till now: Why are you crying? “Remember now who was innocent that perished...As I have seen, etc.” And concerning you a word of prophecy was secretly conveyed to me, [lit. was stolen to me], from the expression like a stolen thing, because the Holy Spirit does not reveal itself to the prophets of the heathens in public. This is analogous to a king who had a wife and a mistress. When he would go to his wife, he would go in public, but to his mistress, he would go clandestinely and stealthily. So it is with the Holy One, blessed be He. Regarding the prophets of the heathens [Scripture states:] (Gen. 20:3), “And God came to Abimelech in the dream of the night,” and similarly (ibid. 31: 24), “And God came to Laban the Aramean in the dream of the night”; to Balaam (Num. 24:4), “who falls, but his eyes are open” [in Gen. Rabbah 52:5 (Num. 22:20), “And God came to Balaam at night”]; to Eliphaz, “in the visions of the night.” But concerning the prophets of Israel it is written (ibid. 12:8): “With him I speak mouth to mouth manifestly and not with riddles.” In Midrash Tanchuma, I found it emended [See Tanchuma Balak 8, Tanchuma Buber Vayishlach 24.] Now [in] the expression, “And my ear grasped a bit of it,” שֶׁמֶץ, means a bit of the spirit of understanding.

verses.hebrew_full: בִּ֭שְׂעִפִּים מֵחֶזְיֹנ֣וֹת לָ֑יְלָה בִּנְפֹ֥ל תַּ֝רְדֵּמָ֗ה עַל־אֲנָשִֽׁים
verses.translation: In thought-filled visions of the night,When deep sleep falls on everyone,

verses.hebrew_full: פַּ֣חַד קְ֭רָאַנִי וּרְעָדָ֑ה וְרֹ֖ב עַצְמוֹתַ֣י הִפְחִֽיד
verses.translation: Fear and trembling came upon me,Causing all my bones to quake with fright.
verses.rashi: terror befell me from the spirit that came to me. and the spirit was an angel. as it is stated (Ps. 104:4): “He makes his angels spirits.”

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֭רוּחַ עַל־פָּנַ֣י יַחֲלֹ֑ף תְּ֝סַמֵּ֗ר שַׂעֲרַ֥ת בְּשָׂרִֽי
verses.translation: A wind passed by me,Making the hair of my flesh bristle.
verses.rashi: it made stand on end The spirit made the hair of my flesh (stand on end]. תסמר. That is an expression of a man whose hair stood on end.

verses.hebrew_full: יַעֲמֹ֤ד וְֽלֹא־אַכִּ֬יר מַרְאֵ֗הוּ תְּ֭מוּנָה לְנֶ֣גֶד עֵינָ֑י דְּמָמָ֖ה וָק֣וֹל אֶשְׁמָֽע
verses.translation: It halted; its appearance was strange to me;A form loomed before my eyes;I heard a murmur, a voice,
verses.rashi: It was standing It was standing before me, but I did not recognize its appearance. a faint voice I heard the sound of faint words. Another explanation: I heard silence and a voice. I heard the voice of the silence, but the silence I did not hear. “Silence” denotes angels reciting praise silently, as [Jonathan] renders (I Kings 19:12): “a still small voice” as: a voice of those praising quietly. Moses heard the silence [i.e., the voice of the angels praising]—from here we deduce that the early prophets heard [lit. used] the voice, whereas the later ones heard an echo, like a man who hits with a hammer, and the echo resounds far away. From here... far away. Here it is written: silence and a voice, whereas concerning Elijah it is written: a still small voice; because Eliphaz heard only the silencing of the voice, like a person who shouts into a barrel, that the voice of the barrel shouts toward him. That is an echo, not the voice itself, but concerning Elijah if is written: a still, small voice, for he heard the voice itself] And this is what it said.

verses.hebrew_full: הַ֭אֱנוֹשׁ מֵאֱל֣וֹהַּ יִצְדָּ֑ק אִ֥ם מֵ֝עֹשֵׂ֗הוּ יִטְהַר־גָּֽבֶר
verses.translation: “Can a mortal be acquitted by God?Can a man be cleared by his Maker?
verses.rashi: Can a mortal be more righteous than God? This is a wonder. or can a man be purer than his Maker? Or can a man be more pure than his Maker?

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֣ן בַּ֭עֲבָדָיו לֹ֣א יַאֲמִ֑ין וּ֝בְמַלְאָכָ֗יו יָשִׂ֥ים תׇּהֳלָֽה
verses.translation: If God’s own servants cannot be trusted,And angels receive reproach,
verses.rashi: Behold...His servants the righteous. He does not trust that they will not sin against Him, and He takes them out of the world before their time. reproach Heb. תהלה, an expression of folly.

verses.hebrew_full: אַ֤ף שֹׁכְנֵ֬י בָֽתֵּי־חֹ֗מֶר אֲשֶׁר־בֶּעָפָ֥ר יְסוֹדָ֑ם יְ֝דַכְּא֗וּם לִפְנֵי־עָֽשׁ
verses.translation: How much less those who dwell in houses of clay,Whose origin is dust,Who are crushed like the moth,
verses.rashi: Surely Surely. those who dwell in mud houses, whose foundation is in dust, meaning their graves. they crush them before the worms From heaven, their strength is sapped until they are humble and crushed before the worms that eat them in death.

verses.hebrew_full: מִבֹּ֣קֶר לָעֶ֣רֶב יֻכַּ֑תּוּ מִבְּלִ֥י מֵ֝שִׂ֗ים לָנֶ֥צַח יֹאבֵֽדוּ
verses.translation: Shattered between daybreak and evening,Perishing forever, unnoticed.
verses.rashi: From morning to evening they are crushed in one day, they are crushed and subdued. without giving [a thought] lit. without putting it upon their heart to return to their Maker. they perish to eternity This too is an elliptical verse.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲלֹא־נִסַּ֣ע יִתְרָ֣ם בָּ֑ם יָ֝מ֗וּתוּ וְלֹ֣א בְחׇכְמָֽה
verses.translation: Their cord is pulled upAnd they die, and not with wisdom.”
verses.rashi: Their haughtiness, which is absorbed within them does it not leave [them]? Heb. יתרם בם, like (Isa. 15:7), “Because of haughty things (יתרה) they did.” Their haughtiness and their wisdom, which is absorbed into their body—behold it will move away and be uprooted from them, like (ibid. 33: 20), “whose pegs shall never be moved (יסע).”

verses.hebrew_full: קְֽרָא־נָ֭א הֲיֵ֣שׁ עוֹנֶ֑ךָּ וְאֶל־מִ֖י מִקְּדֹשִׁ֣ים תִּפְנֶֽה
verses.translation: Call now! Will anyone answer you?To whom among the holy beings will you turn?
verses.rashi: Now call Up to here are words of prophecy. From here on, he returns to his rebuke: Cry out to your utmost, who will answer you? To which of the holy ones [i.e., of the angels] who decrees this upon you will you turn, to strive with him?

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־לֶ֭אֱוִיל יַהֲרׇג־כָּ֑עַשׂ וּ֝פֹתֶ֗ה תָּמִ֥ית קִנְאָֽה
verses.translation: Vexation kills the fool;Passion slays the simpleton.
verses.rashi: a fool like you, anger will kill him, for had you remained silent, perhaps the Divine standard of clemency would return upon you. wrath That is anger and fury, as in (Deut. 32:21), “They have provoked Me (קנאוני) to wrath.”

verses.hebrew_full: אֲֽנִי־רָ֭אִיתִי אֱוִ֣יל מַשְׁרִ֑ישׁ וָאֶקּ֖וֹב נָוֵ֣הוּ פִתְאֹֽם
verses.translation: I myself saw a fool who had struck roots;Impulsively, I cursed his home:

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)·to see

verses.hebrew_full: יִרְחֲק֣וּ בָנָ֣יו מִיֶּ֑שַׁע וְיִֽדַּכְּא֥וּ בַ֝שַּׁ֗עַר וְאֵ֣ין מַצִּֽיל
verses.translation: May his children be far from success;May they be oppressed in the gate with none to deliver them;
verses.rashi: May his sons distance themselves from salvation This is the curse with which I cursed him. and may they be crushed It is punctuated with a dagesh: וְיִדַּכְּאוּ, and may they be crushed, but if it was punctuated without a dagesh (וִידַכְּאוּ), it would mean that they should crush others.

verses.hebrew_full: אֲשֶׁ֤ר קְצִיר֨וֹ רָ֘עֵ֤ב יֹאכֵ֗ל וְאֶֽל־מִצִּנִּ֥ים יִקָּחֵ֑הוּ וְשָׁאַ֖ף צַמִּ֣ים חֵילָֽם
verses.translation: May the hungry devour his harvest,Carrying it off in baskets;May the thirsty swallow their wealth.
verses.rashi: his harvest of the wicked man. The hungry shall eat When the wicked man dies, the hungry whom he cut off during his lifetime shall come and take payment from his property. and he will take it for the one saved from his weapons Like (Exod. 27:20), “and they shall take to you”; for the benefit of the poor man who was saved from the weapons of this wicked man, the judge who is appointed for that purpose shall take it [the estate of the wicked man]. shall swallow One of the thirsty ones whom he robbed during his lifetime shall swallow. their riches Their money that this one stole during his lifetime.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י לֹא־יֵצֵ֣א מֵעָפָ֣ר אָ֑וֶן וּ֝מֵאֲדָמָ֗ה לֹא־יִצְמַ֥ח עָמָֽל
verses.translation: Evil does not grow out of the soil,Nor does mischief spring from the ground;
verses.rashi: For not from the dust does ruin emerge A plague that comes upon a person is not for nothing, nor does it spring from the ground. ruin Heb. און. It is an expression of ruin.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־אָ֭דָם לְעָמָ֣ל יוּלָּ֑ד וּבְנֵי־רֶ֝֗שֶׁף יַגְבִּ֥יהוּ עֽוּף
verses.translation: For people are born to [do] mischief,Just as sparks fly upward.
verses.rashi: Because man is born for trouble For it is impossible that he should not sin and receive trouble as punishment for sin. He is not like the flying creatures—the angels and spirits—who fly upward, and are not of the earthly creatures (other editions read: fly upward so as not to be among the earthly creatures), over whom the Adversary and temptation rule. flying Heb. רשף, as in (Ps. 76:4), “arrows of (רשפי) a bow.” When the arrow flies, it is called רשפי, an expression of flying. This is what I heard.

verses.hebrew_full: אוּלָ֗ם אֲ֭נִי אֶדְרֹ֣שׁ אֶל־אֵ֑ל וְאֶל־אֱ֝לֹהִ֗ים אָשִׂ֥ים דִּבְרָתִֽי
verses.translation: But I would resort to God;I would lay my case before God,
verses.rashi: But I would seek of God If these pains came over me, I would appeal to the Holy One, blessed be He, with prayer and supplication, and if I would commit my cause to a request.

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: עֹשֶׂ֣ה גְ֭דֹלוֹת וְאֵ֣ין חֵ֑קֶר נִ֝פְלָא֗וֹת עַד־אֵ֥ין מִסְפָּֽר
verses.translation: Who performs great deeds that cannot be fathomed,Wondrous things without number;

verses.hebrew_full: הַנֹּתֵ֣ן מָ֭טָר עַל־פְּנֵי־אָ֑רֶץ וְשֹׁ֥לֵֽחַ מַ֝֗יִם עַל־פְּנֵ֥י חוּצֽוֹת
verses.translation: Who gives rain to the earth,And sends water over the fields;
verses.rashi: Who gives rain upon the face of the land The land of Israel. and sends water upon the face of outside places through a messenger to other countries.

verses.hebrew_full: לָשׂ֣וּם שְׁפָלִ֣ים לְמָר֑וֹם וְ֝קֹדְרִ֗ים שָׂ֣גְבוּ יֶֽשַׁע
verses.translation: Who raises the lowly up high,So that the dejected are secure in victory;
verses.rashi: to place the humble on high He gives the rain, and He frustrates the plans of the crafty, who intend to raise the prices and buy the fields of the poor for little grain. and those blackened whose fares are shriveled from hunger.

verses.hebrew_full: מֵ֭פֵר מַחְשְׁב֣וֹת עֲרוּמִ֑ים וְֽלֹא־תַעֲשֶׂ֥נָה יְ֝דֵיהֶ֗ם תֻּֽשִׁיָּֽה
verses.translation: Who thwarts the designs of the crafty,So that their hands cannot gain success;
verses.rashi: so that their hands do not carry out their plans The counsel that they planned to perform.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹכֵ֣ד חֲכָמִ֣ים בְּעׇרְמָ֑ם וַעֲצַ֖ת נִפְתָּלִ֣ים נִמְהָֽרָה
verses.translation: Who traps the clever in their own wiles;The plans of the crafty go awry.
verses.rashi: is hasty Any counsel that is planned hastily is foolishness.

verses.hebrew_full: יוֹמָ֥ם יְפַגְּשׁוּ־חֹ֑שֶׁךְ וְ֝כַלַּ֗יְלָה יְֽמַשְׁשׁ֥וּ בַֽצׇּהֳרָֽיִם
verses.translation: By day they encounter darkness,At noon they grope as in the night.
verses.rashi: By day, they meet darkness For their counsel is not executed nor accomplished, and the light of their wisdom is converted to darkness.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֣שַׁע מֵ֭חֶרֶב מִפִּיהֶ֑ם וּמִיַּ֖ד חָזָ֣ק אֶבְיֽוֹן
verses.translation: But [God] saves the needy from the sword of their mouth,From the clutches of the strong.
verses.rashi: And He saved the stumblers. from the sword. From what sword? From their mouth, for they thought to swallow them and longed to destroy them. and... from the hand of the mighty He saves the needy.

verses.hebrew_full: וַתְּהִ֣י לַדַּ֣ל תִּקְוָ֑ה וְ֝עֹלָ֗תָה קָ֣פְצָה פִּֽיהָ
verses.translation: So there is hope for the wretched;The mouth of wrongdoing is stopped.
verses.rashi: and libel Heb. ועלתה. The libel (עלילה) and the wickedness, as (Ps. 64:7), “They search for false charges,” equivalent to עלילות, libels. shut Heb. קפצה, shut, as (Deut. 15:7), “nor shut (תקפץ).”

verses.hebrew_full: הִנֵּ֤ה אַשְׁרֵ֣י אֱ֭נוֹשׁ יוֹכִחֶ֣נּוּ אֱל֑וֹהַּ וּמוּסַ֥ר שַׁ֝דַּ֗י אַל־תִּמְאָֽס
verses.translation: See how happy is the person whom God reproves;Do not reject the discipline of the Almighty.
verses.rashi: Behold, fortunate is the man etc. with pains, and just as He heals the pain of these [people], so would I [myself] appeal to Him about these pains that would come upon me. So should you accept them and not despise them.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י ה֣וּא יַכְאִ֣יב וְיֶחְבָּ֑שׁ יִ֝מְחַ֗ץ וְיָדָ֥ו תִּרְפֶּֽינָה
verses.translation: [God] injures, but also binds up;God’s hands wound, but also heal.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּשֵׁ֣שׁ צָ֭רוֹת יַצִּילֶ֑ךָּ וּבְשֶׁ֓בַע לֹֽא־יִגַּ֖ע בְּךָ֣ רָֽע
verses.translation: You will be delivered from six troubles;In seven no harm will reach you:
verses.rashi: In six troubles When they come to the world, he will save you through the pains with which you wee tormented, and also with the seventh one, which when it comes will not touch you.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּֽ֭רָעָב פָּדְךָ֣ מִמָּ֑וֶת וּ֝בְמִלְחָמָ֗ה מִ֣ידֵי חָֽרֶב
verses.translation: In famine you will be redeemed from death,In war, from the sword.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּשׁ֣וֹט לָ֭שׁוֹן תֵּחָבֵ֑א וְֽלֹא־תִירָ֥א מִ֝שֹּׁ֗ד כִּ֣י יָבֽוֹא
verses.translation: You will be sheltered from the scourging tongue;You will have no fear when violence comes.
verses.rashi: from the scourging tongue of the Adversary you shall be hidden. These are the seven troubles: 1 famine, 2) the sword, 3) the tongue, 4) plunder etc., and 5) the beasts of the land, which are brigands.

verses.hebrew_full: לְשֹׁ֣ד וּלְכָפָ֣ן תִּשְׂחָ֑ק וּֽמֵחַיַּ֥ת הָ֝אָ֗רֶץ אַל־תִּירָֽא
verses.translation: You will laugh at violence and starvation,And have no fear of wild beasts.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י עִם־אַבְנֵ֣י הַשָּׂדֶ֣ה בְרִיתֶ֑ךָ וְחַיַּ֥ת הַ֝שָּׂדֶ֗ה הׇשְׁלְמָה־לָּֽךְ
verses.translation: For you will have a pact with the rocks in the field,And the beasts of the field will be your allies.
verses.rashi: the stones of the field They are: 6) a type of man. and the beasts of the field That is what is known as grouse(?) in Old French, and this is actually a beast of the field. In the language of the Mishnah in Torath Kohanim, they are called “adnei hasadeh.” made peace with you will have peace with you.

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace·unto thee

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽ֭יָדַעְתָּ כִּֽי־שָׁל֣וֹם אׇהֳלֶ֑ךָ וּֽפָקַדְתָּ֥ נָ֝וְךָ֗ וְלֹ֣א תֶחֱטָֽא
verses.translation: You will know that all is well in your tent;When you visit your wife you will never fail.
verses.rashi: Then you shall know that there is peace in your tent Then, wherever you are, you will be confident that all is well in your tent. and you shall visit your habitation and miss nothing Heb. תחטא, like (Jud. 20:16), “at a hairbreadth and not miss (יחטא).” This is the seventh [trouble] that we counted above.

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽ֭יָדַעְתָּ כִּֽי־רַ֣ב זַרְעֶ֑ךָ וְ֝צֶאֱצָאֶ֗יךָ כְּעֵ֣שֶׂב הָאָֽרֶץ
verses.translation: You will see that your offspring are many,Your descendants like the grass of the earth.

verses.hebrew_full: תָּב֣וֹא בְכֶ֣לַח אֱלֵי־קָ֑בֶר כַּעֲל֖וֹת גָּדִ֣ישׁ בְּעִתּֽוֹ
verses.translation: You will come to the grave in ripe old age, As shocks of grain are taken away in their season.
verses.rashi: You shall come to the grave at a ripe old age Heb. בְכֶלַח. [In other words,] you will be saved from pestilence. As for the word בְכֶלַח: there is no Biblical support to understand its meaning, but according to the context, it can be interpreted as an expression of the completion of the ripening of the grain, when it is completely ripe. Our Rabbis, however, interpret בְכֶלַח as “sixty years,” according to its numerical value. as the grain stock is taken away As the grain stack is taken off the ground in its time.

verses.hebrew_full: הִנֵּה־זֹ֖את חֲקַרְנ֥וּהָ כֶּֽן־הִ֑יא שְׁ֝מָעֶ֗נָּה וְאַתָּ֥ה דַֽע־לָֽךְ
verses.translation: See, we have inquired into this and it is so;Hear it and accept it.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֥עַן אִיּ֗וֹב וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Then Job said in reply:

verses.hebrew_full: ל֗וּ שָׁק֣וֹל יִשָּׁקֵ֣ל כַּעְשִׂ֑י (והיתי) וְ֝הַוָּתִ֗י בְּֽמֹאזְנַ֥יִם יִשְׂאוּ־יָֽחַד
verses.translation: If my anguish were weighed,My full calamity laid on the scales,
verses.rashi: and my calamity my ruin. placed together on a scale with a weight opposite it. even if [this weight] were the sand of the seas, it would outweigh it.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־עַתָּ֗ה מֵח֣וֹל יַמִּ֣ים יִכְבָּ֑ד עַל־כֵּ֝֗ן דְּבָרַ֥י לָֽעוּ
verses.translation: It would be heavier than the sand of the sea;That is why I spoke recklessly.
verses.rashi: stammer Heb. לעו. They are uncertain, like a person who has no strength to pronounce a word properly. Likewise (Obad. 16), “And they shall drink and stammer (ולעו),” because it is usual for a drunkard to stammer in his speech.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י חִצֵּ֪י שַׁדַּ֡י עִמָּדִ֗י אֲשֶׁ֣ר חֲ֭מָתָם שֹׁתָ֣ה רוּחִ֑י בִּעוּתֵ֖י אֱל֣וֹהַּ יַעַרְכֽוּנִי
verses.translation: For the arrows of the Almighty are in me;My spirit absorbs their poison;God’s terrors are arrayed against me.
verses.rashi: venom Heb. חמתם, their poison. It was the custom of the Persians to apply snake venom to their arrows.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲיִֽנְהַק־פֶּ֥רֶא עֲלֵי־דֶ֑שֶׁא אִ֥ם יִגְעֶה־שּׁ֝֗וֹר עַל־בְּלִילֽוֹ
verses.translation: Does a wild ass bray when it has grass?Does a bull bellow over its fodder?
verses.rashi: Does a wild donkey bray That is to say, do I cry out for nothing? Even a stupid animal does not bray when it has food, neither does an ox low unless it does not have grain fodder. With a wild donkey, the expression used is נַהַק, braying, and with an ox, the expression of געיה, lowing.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲיֵאָכֵ֣ל תָּ֭פֵל מִבְּלִי־מֶ֑לַח אִם־יֶשׁ־טַ֝֗עַם בְּרִ֣יר חַלָּמֽוּת
verses.translation: Can what is tasteless be eaten without salt?Does mallow juice have any flavor?
verses.rashi: Can bland food be eaten A thing that has no salt and must be salted, but was not salted, is called תָּפֵל in Mishnaic Hebrew (Shabbath 128a, Hullin 113a, Nedarim 51a). or is there taste in the saliva that comes from strong tasting foods, for the one who eats a strong-tasting food that increases the saliva, e.g. garlic. That is to say, Do you think their replies that have no substance are acceptable? רִיר [means saliva] as in (I Sam. 2 1:14), “and let his saliva (ריר) run down upon his beard.” חַלָמוּת is like (below 39:4), “Their sons wax strong (יחלמו),” an expression of strength. Some interpret it as an expression of a dream (חלום), but this does not appeal to me because of the dagesh in the “lammed.”

verses.hebrew_full: מֵאֲנָ֣ה לִנְגּ֣וֹעַ נַפְשִׁ֑י הֵ֝֗מָּה כִּדְוֵ֥י לַחְמִֽי
verses.translation: I refuse to touch them;They are like food when I am sick.
verses.rashi: What my body refused to touch That is to say, I have reason to cry increasingly, because things that my body refused [to touch] and was disgusted to touch... [לנגוֹע] is an expression of touching. as cloths for my food Now they are prepared for me to touch them like my tablecloths upon which my food is placed. כִּדְוֵי is an expression like (II Sam. 10:4), “and he cut off their garments (מדויהם).” I heard this from Rabbi Meshullam the physician. Another interpretation: Like cloths with which they strain the cooked foods and the food runs out of them. דְוֵי is an expression of דָוֶה, running. my food Heb. לחמי, lit. my bread. All food is called לחם, as in (Dan. 5:1), “made a great feast (לחם).” and as in (Jer. 11:19), “Let us destroy his food (בלחמו) with wood.” which Jonathan renders: poison into his food. Another explanation: They are as the sights of my food. That is to say: I am accustomed to looking at them, at the worms, as I look at my food. This is Mishnaic Hebrew: “and they walk when they look (דוי)” in Tractate Shabbath (53b).

verses.hebrew_full: מִֽי־יִ֭תֵּן תָּב֣וֹא שֶׁאֱלָתִ֑י וְ֝תִקְוָתִ֗י יִתֵּ֥ן אֱלֽוֹהַּ
verses.translation: Would that my request were granted,That God gave me what I wished for;

verses.hebrew_full: וְיֹאֵ֣ל אֱ֭לוֹהַּ וִידַכְּאֵ֑נִי יַתֵּ֥ר יָ֝ד֗וֹ וִיבַצְּעֵֽנִי
verses.translation: Would that God consented to crush me,Loosed a divine hand and cut me off.
verses.rashi: desire and may He want. and crush me [This is] an expression of death. enlarge his hand May He enlarge His hand with His plague like (Exod. 9:3), “Behold God’s hand is.” and finish me off Heb. ויבצעני, and finish me off, as in (Lam. 2:17), “He has carried out (בצע) His word,” and (Zech. 4:9), “and his hands shall finish it (תבצענה).”

verses.hebrew_full: וּ֥תְהִי־ע֨וֹד נֶ֘חָ֤מָתִ֗י וַאֲסַלְּדָ֣ה בְ֭חִילָה לֹ֣א יַחְמ֑וֹל כִּי־לֹ֥א כִ֝חַ֗דְתִּי אִמְרֵ֥י קָדֽוֹשׁ
verses.translation: Then this would be my consolation,As I writhed in unsparing pains:That I did not suppress my words against the Holy One.
verses.rashi: Then should I yet have comfort And this shall yet be to me for comfort. and I will beg, shuddering, that He should have no pity And I will beg of Him with shuddering (Shem Ephraim) that He not refrain from finishing me off: I cannot find any similar word in Scriptures, except that in Mishnaic Hebrew it is an expression of fear and concern, that the hand fears them lest it be scalded in boiling water. That is to say, I will shudder with concern and with shuddering request this. for I have not denied I did not fail to fulfill His words.

verses.hebrew_full: מַה־כֹּחִ֥י כִֽי־אֲיַחֵ֑ל וּמַה־קִּ֝צִּ֗י כִּֽי־אַאֲרִ֥יךְ נַפְשִֽׁי
verses.translation: What strength have I, that I should endure?How long have I to live, that I should be patient?
verses.rashi: What is my strength How strong is it to bear [my pains]? that I should wait That I should wait until the day of recovery or until the day of death? I should wait Heb. איחל, like (Gen. 8:10), “And he waited (ויחל) again,” concerning Noah. and what Of what importance is my end that I should be able to restrain my desire to bear? my desire Heb. נפשי. This [word denoting] a person’s temptation toward desire [is used] in many places, as (Gen. 23:8), “If it is truly your will (נפשכם).”

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־כֹּ֣חַ אֲבָנִ֣ים כֹּחִ֑י אִֽם־בְּשָׂרִ֥י נָחֽוּשׁ
verses.translation: Is my strength the strength of rock?Is my flesh bronze?

verses.hebrew_full: הַאִ֬ם אֵ֣ין עֶזְרָתִ֣י בִ֑י וְ֝תֻשִׁיָּ֗ה נִדְּחָ֥ה מִמֶּֽנִּי
verses.translation: Truly, I cannot help myself;I have been deprived of resourcefulness.
verses.rashi: Have I no help This is an expression of wonder. Will this too come upon me, that I have no help? Those friends that I had to help me are not helping me. or is counsel lost The counsel of counselors is lost from me, because you have risen against me to provoke and to reject.

verses.hebrew_full: לַמָּ֣ס מֵרֵעֵ֣הוּ חָ֑סֶד וְיִרְאַ֖ת שַׁדַּ֣י יַעֲזֽוֹב
verses.translation: A friend owes loyalty to one who fails,Though he forsakes the fear of the Almighty;
verses.rashi: By one who withholds kindness from his friend Heb. למס, by one who withholds kindness. The “lammed” is a prefix, as in (Num. 26:54), “to the numerous one (לָרַב)”; “to the one who returns (?) (לָשָב),” and (Isa. 28:10, 13) “for a precept (לצו).” מָס too is an expression of a verb, like בָּא, comes: שָב, returns; גָר, dwells; these also are an expression of doing [i.e., the present tense]: who withholds kindness, who destroys it, like (Exod. 16:21), “when the sun grew hot, it melted (ונמס).”

words.eng: gang or body of forced labourers

verses.hebrew_full: אַ֭חַי בָּגְד֣וּ כְמוֹ־נָ֑חַל כַּאֲפִ֖יק נְחָלִ֣ים יַעֲבֹֽרוּ
verses.translation: My comrades are fickle, like a wadi,Like a bed on which streams once ran.
verses.rashi: My brethren Those who defend me. betrayed me like the stream that betrays [many] betrayals, as is explained in this chapter. like the source of streams the source of streams, they pass from the proper trait of friendship. Now what is the betrayal of a stream?

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: הַקֹּדְרִ֥ים מִנִּי־קָ֑רַח עָ֝לֵ֗ימוֹ יִתְעַלֶּם־שָֽׁלֶג
verses.translation: They are dark with ice;Snow obscures them;
verses.rashi: which wrinkle from the ice if ice comes upon them, they wrinkle and become like boards (Shem Ephraim), and upon them the snow disappears—it is covered, for it falls with the ice. This is one betrayal, for it [the stream] is hidden from the eye, and a thirsty man does not find water to drink.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּעֵ֣ת יְזֹרְב֣וּ נִצְמָ֑תוּ בְּ֝חֻמּ֗וֹ נִדְעֲכ֥וּ מִמְּקוֹמָֽם
verses.translation: But when they thaw, they vanish;In the heat, they disappear where they are.
verses.rashi: At the time they become warm Heb. יזרבו, an expression of יְצֹרָבוּ, they become warm from the heat of the sun. they retreat Heb. נצמתו, retrait in Old French, shrink, contract, as “and the vinegar shrinks them (צומתן), (Pesachim 41a) in Talmudic Hebrew. [In the word יְזֹרָבוּ], the “zaddi” is converted to a “zayin,” like צעקה and זעקה, a cry. [Therefore,] יְזֹרָבוּ is an expression of (Ezekiel 21:3), “and all faces shall be scorched (ונצרבו),” and like (Prov. 16:27), “searing (צרבת) fire.” they jump they jump from their place.

verses.hebrew_full: יִ֭לָּ֣פְתוּ אׇרְח֣וֹת דַּרְכָּ֑ם יַעֲל֖וּ בַתֹּ֣הוּ וְיֹאבֵֽדוּ
verses.translation: Their course twists and turns;They run into the desert and perish.
verses.rashi: are held Heb. ילפתו. They hold the paths of their way, as in (Jud. 16:29), “And Samson grasped (וילפת),” and similarly (Ruth 3:8), “and the man quaked and was taken around (וילפת),” for the woman embraced him.

verses.hebrew_full: הִ֭בִּיטוּ אׇרְח֣וֹת תֵּמָ֑א הֲלִיכֹ֥ת שְׁ֝בָ֗א קִוּוּ־לָֽמוֹ
verses.translation: Caravans from Tema look to them;Processions from Sheba count on them.
verses.rashi: They look at the paths of Tema The land of Ishmael, which is low, and the water flows there. they make a line Heb. קוו, an expression of an extended line. Another explanation: it is an expression of (Gen. 1:9) “Let the waters...gather (יקוו).”

verses.hebrew_full: בֹּ֥שׁוּ כִֽי־בָטָ֑ח בָּ֥אוּ עָ֝דֶ֗יהָ וַיֶּחְפָּֽרוּ
verses.translation: They are disappointed in their hopes;When they reach the place, they stand aghast.
verses.rashi: They are ashamed because they had hoped; they came to it Those who drink it are ashamed because everyone had hoped to drink thereof.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־עַ֭תָּה הֱיִ֣יתֶם לֹ֑א תִּֽרְא֥וּ חֲ֝תַ֗ת וַתִּירָֽאוּ
verses.translation: So you are as nothing:, with Targum; meaning of Heb. uncertain. At the sight of misfortune, you take fright.
verses.rashi: For now you are like it You are compared to that stream you fear ruin The fear of the plague, and you are afraid to say the truth, thereby flattering my opponent.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲֽכִי־אָ֭מַרְתִּי הָ֣בוּ לִ֑י וּ֝מִכֹּחֲכֶ֗ם שִׁחֲד֥וּ בַעֲדִֽי
verses.translation: Did I say to you, “I need your gift;Pay a bribe for me out of your wealth;
verses.rashi: or “Offer a bribe for me from your wealth” [Heb. מכחכם, lit. from your strength.] And from your wealth.

verses.hebrew_full: וּמַלְּט֥וּנִי מִיַּד־צָ֑ר וּמִיַּ֖ד עָרִיצִ֣ים תִּפְדּֽוּנִי
verses.translation: Deliver me from the clutches of my enemy;Redeem me from the violent”?

verses.hebrew_full: ה֭וֹרוּנִי וַאֲנִ֣י אַחֲרִ֑ישׁ וּמַה־שָּׁ֝גִ֗יתִי הָבִ֥ינוּ לִֽי
verses.translation: Teach me; I shall be silent;Tell me where I am wrong.

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: מַה־נִּמְרְצ֥וּ אִמְרֵי־יֹ֑שֶׁר וּמַה־יּוֹכִ֖יחַ הוֹכֵ֣חַ מִכֶּֽם
verses.translation: How trenchant honest words are; But what sort of reproof comes from you?
verses.rashi: How clear Heb. נמרצו, like (I Kings 2:8), “a clear curse (נמרצת),” an expression of (Prov. 25:11) “a word spoken with proper basis.” and similarly (Ps. 119:103), “How clear (נמלצו) are Your words to my palate.” All of them are expressions of clarification (מליצה), and the “lammed” is converted to a “resh.” words of uprightness If you were saying words of uprightness, they would be accepted, but now, what does your proof prove? Every expression of תוֹכָחָה in Job means the clarification and verification of matters.

words.eng: interr pron·to be or make sick

verses.hebrew_full: הַלְהוֹכַ֣ח מִלִּ֣ים תַּחְשֹׁ֑בוּ וּ֝לְר֗וּחַ אִמְרֵ֥י נֹאָֽשׁ
verses.translation: Do you devise words of reproof,But count the words of someone hopeless as wind?
verses.rashi: Do you think to clarify words Heb. הלהוכח, Do you think to clarify words? They are like wind They are likened [to wind]. words of futility which have no substance, like (Jer. 2:25), “but you said. I despair (נואש).” I am not concerned with the words of the prophets.

verses.hebrew_full: אַף־עַל־יָת֥וֹם תַּפִּ֑ילוּ וְ֝תִכְר֗וּ עַֽל־רֵיעֲכֶֽם
verses.translation: You would even cast lots over an orphan,Or barter away your friend.
verses.rashi: You cast with your judgment, you cast wrath upon a poor man like me, with the line of judgment; and you dig a pit for your friend into which to cast him.

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֭עַתָּה הוֹאִ֣ילוּ פְנוּ־בִ֑י וְעַל־פְּ֝נֵיכֶ֗ם אִם־אֲכַזֵּֽב
verses.translation: Now be so good as to face me;I will not lie to your face.
verses.rashi: agree Be willing to turn to me and to hear my words. Then you will see whether I lie to your face.

verses.hebrew_full: שֽׁוּבוּ־נָ֭א אַל־תְּהִ֣י עַוְלָ֑ה (ושבי) וְשׁ֥וּבוּ ע֝֗וֹד צִדְקִי־בָֽהּ
verses.translation: Relent! Let there not be injustice;Relent! I am still in the right.
verses.rashi: Return now to test, and you will determine that there shall be no more injustice; return to investigate, and behold, my righteousness will be found in it.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲיֵשׁ־בִּלְשׁוֹנִ֥י עַוְלָ֑ה אִם־חִ֝כִּ֗י לֹא־יָבִ֥ין הַוּֽוֹת
verses.translation: Is injustice on my tongue?Can my palate not discern evil?
verses.rashi: or will my palate not understand wickedness? Will it not understand when I speak words of wickedness?

verses.hebrew_full: הֲלֹֽא־צָבָ֣א לֶֽאֱנ֣וֹשׁ (על) עֲלֵי־אָ֑רֶץ וְכִימֵ֖י שָׂכִ֣יר יָמָֽיו
verses.translation: Truly mortals have a term of service on earth;Their days are like those of a hireling—
verses.rashi: Is not man on earth for a limited time This is what I said to you. ‘Agree and turn to me.’ because how can I be silent from crying our about my misfortune? Do you not know that the time a man has to live is limited? and...like the days of a hireling and I—that time that is allotted to me...

verses.hebrew_full: כְּעֶ֥בֶד יִשְׁאַף־צֵ֑ל וּ֝כְשָׂכִ֗יר יְקַוֶּ֥ה פׇעֳלֽוֹ
verses.translation: Like a slave who longs for [evening’s] shadows,Like a hireling who waits to be paid.
verses.rashi: As a slave who longs for the shadow As a slave, who toils all day through and longs and yearns, “When will the shadow of evening come?” And as a hireling, who hopes for his wages at eventide, because the whole day was to him for toil, and he yearns for the sunset.

verses.hebrew_full: כֵּ֤ן הׇנְחַ֣לְתִּי לִ֭י יַרְחֵי־שָׁ֑וְא וְלֵיל֥וֹת עָ֝מָ֗ל מִנּוּ־לִֽי
verses.translation: So have I been allotted months of futility;Nights of misery have been apportioned to me.
verses.rashi: So was I made to possess from Heaven months of futility and torments, that limited time that is given to man upon earth. and they appointed wearisome nights for me from Heaven

words.eng: to count·inflected pers. pron. meaning ‘to me’.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־שָׁכַ֗בְתִּי וְאָמַ֗רְתִּי מָתַ֣י אָ֭קוּם וּמִדַּד־עָ֑רֶב וְשָׂבַ֖עְתִּי נְדֻדִ֣ים עֲדֵי־נָֽשֶׁף
verses.translation: When I lie down, I think,“When shall I rise?”Night drags on, And I am sated with tossings till morning twilight.
verses.rashi: If I would lie down at night, I would hope and say, etc. and the evening depart When will it become light and when will the time of arising come, and when will the evening depart? [The word מדד ] is an expression of (Gen. 31:40) “and sleep was banished (ותדד).” The “mem” is a defective radical, as in מאמר, statement; משמר, watch. And I was sated with restlessness I was sated with restlessness on my bed by day until twilight, because, due to the torments I could not sleep until the twilight of day, since my flesh was clothed with worms.

verses.hebrew_full: לָ֘בַ֤שׁ בְּשָׂרִ֣י רִ֭מָּה (וגיש) וְג֣וּשׁ עָפָ֑ר עוֹרִ֥י רָ֝גַ֗ע וַיִּמָּאֵֽס
verses.translation: My flesh is covered with maggots and clods of earth;My skin is broken and festering.
verses.rashi: and clods of earth Clods of earth (?) Now this is the development of rust, because it is the custom of clods of earth to moisten and cause rust. wrinkled Heb. רגע wrinkled, as in (Isa. 51:15), “Who wrinkles (רגע) the sea.” Another explanation: עוֹרִי רָגַע is an expression of resting a wave after a wave, like wrinkles (?).

verses.hebrew_full: יָמַ֣י קַ֭לּוּ מִנִּי־אָ֑רֶג וַ֝יִּכְל֗וּ בְּאֶ֣פֶס תִּקְוָֽה
verses.translation: My days fly faster than a weaver’s shuttle,And come to their end without hope.
verses.rashi: My days are swifter The days of my prosperity hastened to go away. than a weaver’s shuttle Than weaving that is done swiftly. So said Hezekiah (Isa. 38:12), “I severed, like a weaver, my life.” without hope I no longer hope for good.

verses.hebrew_full: זְ֭כֹר כִּי־ר֣וּחַ חַיָּ֑י לֹא־תָשׁ֥וּב עֵ֝ינִ֗י לִרְא֥וֹת טֽוֹב
verses.translation: Consider that my life is but wind;I shall never see happiness again.
verses.rashi: my eye will not return after death. Here Job denies the dogma of the Resurrection of the Dead.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־תְ֭שׁוּרֵנִי עֵ֣ין רֹ֑אִי עֵינֶ֖יךָ בִּ֣י וְאֵינֶֽנִּי
verses.translation: The eye that gazes on me will not see me;Your eye will seek me, but I shall be gone.
verses.rashi: shall see me no more The eye that wishes to see me shall see me no more after I die. set Your eyes upon me and I will be here no longer He is addressing the Holy One, blessed be He: “Why did You have to finish me off and crush me with pains? With one look that you set Your eyes on me, I am no longer in the world.”

verses.hebrew_full: כָּלָ֣ה עָ֭נָן וַיֵּלַ֑ךְ כֵּ֥ן יוֹרֵ֥ד שְׁ֝א֗וֹל לֹ֣א יַעֲלֶֽה
verses.translation: As a cloud fades away,So whoever goes down to Sheol does not come up;

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־יָשׁ֣וּב ע֣וֹד לְבֵית֑וֹ וְלֹא־יַכִּירֶ֖נּוּ ע֣וֹד מְקֹמֽוֹ
verses.translation: They return no more to their home;Their place does not know them.

verses.hebrew_full: גַּם־אֲנִי֮ לֹ֤א אֶחֱשׇׂ֫ךְ־פִּ֥י אֲֽ֭דַבְּרָה בְּצַ֣ר רוּחִ֑י אָ֝שִׂ֗יחָה בְּמַ֣ר נַפְשִֽׁי
verses.translation: On my part, I will not speak with restraint;I will give voice to the anguish of my spirit;I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.
verses.rashi: Neither will I restrain my mouth Since You do not desist from me, neither will I restrain my speech from complaining about Your ways.

words.eng: also·I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: הֲֽיָם־אָ֭נִי אִם־תַּנִּ֑ין כִּֽי־תָשִׂ֖ים עָלַ֣י מִשְׁמָֽר
verses.translation: Am I the sea or the Dragon, That You have set a watch over me?
verses.rashi: Am I a sea that You placed sand over me as a guard? or a sea monster A huge fish that You imprisoned in the depths of the sea, that You place this Adversary as a guard over me, to watch me lest my soul depart?

words.eng: sea·I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־אָ֭מַרְתִּי תְּנַחֲמֵ֣נִי עַרְשִׂ֑י יִשָּׂ֥א בְ֝שִׂיחִ֗י מִשְׁכָּבִֽי
verses.translation: When I think, “My bed will comfort me,My couch will share my sorrow,”
verses.rashi: that my couch shall bear my speech The couch upon which I sleep at night will bear my trouble somewhat, and I will be able to restrain my speech.

verses.hebrew_full: וְחִתַּתַּ֥נִי בַחֲלֹמ֑וֹת וּֽמֵחֶזְיֹנ֥וֹת תְּבַעֲתַֽנִּי
verses.translation: You frighten me with dreams,And terrify me with visions,

verses.hebrew_full: וַתִּבְחַ֣ר מַחֲנָ֣ק נַפְשִׁ֑י מָ֝֗וֶת מֵעַצְמוֹתָֽי
verses.translation: Till I prefer strangulation,Death, to my wasted frame.
verses.rashi: death rather than these my bones I choose death rather than these limbs in my body.

verses.hebrew_full: מָ֭אַסְתִּי לֹא־לְעֹלָ֣ם אֶחְיֶ֑ה חֲדַ֥ל מִ֝מֶּ֗נִּי כִּי־הֶ֥בֶל יָמָֽי
verses.translation: I am sick of it.I shall not live forever;Let me be, for my days are a breath.
verses.rashi: I despised my life, because ultimately I will not live forever. desist from me From harming me, because my days are futile and few.

verses.hebrew_full: מָֽה־אֱ֭נוֹשׁ כִּ֣י תְגַדְּלֶ֑נּוּ וְכִֽי־תָשִׁ֖ית אֵלָ֣יו לִבֶּֽךָ
verses.translation: What are mortals, that You make much of them,That You fix Your attention upon them?
verses.rashi: that You should give him importance to pay attention to him, to visit his deeds every morning and test him every moment?

verses.hebrew_full: וַתִּפְקְדֶ֥נּוּ לִבְקָרִ֑ים לִ֝רְגָעִ֗ים תִּבְחָנֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: You inspect them every morning,Examine them every minute.

verses.hebrew_full: כַּ֭מָּה לֹא־תִשְׁעֶ֣ה מִמֶּ֑נִּי לֹֽא־תַ֝רְפֵּ֗נִי עַד־בִּלְעִ֥י רֻקִּֽי
verses.translation: Will You not look away from me for a while,Let me be, till I swallow my spittle?
verses.rashi: How long For what length of time will You not desist from me, like (Exod. 5:9), “and let them not turn (ישעו) to false words.” Similarly (below 14:6), “Turn away (שעה) from him and desist.” until I swallow my spit long enough to swallow my spit.

verses.hebrew_full: חָטָ֡אתִי מָ֤ה אֶפְעַ֨ל לָךְ֮ נֹצֵ֢ר הָ֫אָדָ֥ם לָ֤מָה שַׂמְתַּ֣נִי לְמִפְגָּ֣ע לָ֑ךְ וָאֶהְיֶ֖ה עָלַ֣י לְמַשָּֽׂא
verses.translation: If I have sinned, what have I done to You,Watcher of humankind?Why make of me Your target,And a burden to myself?
verses.rashi: as a mark for You As an object that a man strikes when he is angry and vents upon it the entire act of his hatred (other editions: his sin). so that I have become a burden to myself [Etz Chayim ms: Like “and I have become a burden to You.”] This is one of the words in which Scripture euphemized, and they are known as the emendation of the scribes.

verses.hebrew_full: וּמֶ֤ה לֹא־תִשָּׂ֣א פִשְׁעִי֮ וְתַעֲבִ֢יר אֶת־עֲוֺ֫נִ֥י כִּֽי־עַ֭תָּה לֶעָפָ֣ר אֶשְׁכָּ֑ב וְשִׁ֖חַרְתַּ֣נִי וְאֵינֶֽנִּי
verses.translation: Why do You not pardon my transgressionAnd forgive my iniquity?For soon I shall lie down in the dust;When You seek me, I shall be gone.
verses.rashi: Now why do You not forgive my transgression What is this matter that You do not forgive my transgression? and You shall seek me You shall seek me and not find me.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through
sign of the definite direct object·perversity
