verses.hebrew_full: וַ֭יַּעַן בִּלְדַּ֥ד הַשּׁוּחִ֗י וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Bildad the Shuhite said in reply:

verses.hebrew_full: עַד־אָ֥ן תְּמַלֶּל־אֵ֑לֶּה וְר֥וּחַ כַּ֝בִּ֗יר אִמְרֵי־פִֽיךָ
verses.translation: How long will you speak such things?Your utterances are a mighty wind!
verses.rashi: Bildad’s Addressa mighty wind Many words. mighty Heb. כביר, an expression of multiplicity.

verses.hebrew_full: הַ֭אֵל יְעַוֵּ֣ת מִשְׁפָּ֑ט וְאִם־שַׁ֝דַּ֗י יְעַוֵּת־צֶֽדֶק
verses.translation: Will God pervert the right?Will the Almighty pervert justice?
verses.rashi: Does God pervert judgment Heb. הא-ל. The “hey” is vowelized with a “patach,” and it means an expression of wonder.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־בָּנֶ֥יךָ חָֽטְאוּ־ל֑וֹ וַֽ֝יְשַׁלְּחֵ֗ם בְּיַד־פִּשְׁעָֽם
verses.translation: If your children sinned against [God],They were dispatched for their transgression.
verses.rashi: He sent them away in the hands of their transgression Through their transgression. (The transgression) became the agent to carry them off.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־אַ֭תָּה תְּשַׁחֵ֣ר אֶל־אֵ֑ל וְאֶל־שַׁ֝דַּ֗י תִּתְחַנָּֽן
verses.translation: But if you seek GodAnd supplicate the Almighty,
verses.rashi: If you seek God etc., He will arouse your merit over you.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־זַ֥ךְ וְיָשָׁ֗ר אָ֥֫תָּה כִּֽי־עַ֭תָּה יָעִ֣יר עָלֶ֑יךָ וְ֝שִׁלַּ֗ם נְוַ֣ת צִדְקֶֽךָ
verses.translation: If you are blameless and upright,[God] will protect you,And grant well-being to your righteous home.
verses.rashi: and will complete your righteous home And He will make your righteous home complete and perfect.

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace

verses.hebrew_full: וְהָיָ֣ה רֵאשִׁיתְךָ֣ מִצְעָ֑ר וְ֝אַחֲרִֽיתְךָ֗ יִשְׂגֶּ֥ה מְאֹֽד
verses.translation: Though your beginning be small,In the end you will grow very great.
verses.rashi: And your beginning shall be small Your beginning, which has passed, shall be small as compared to your end, which shall increase exceedingly.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־שְׁאַל־נָ֭א לְדֹ֣ר רִישׁ֑וֹן וְ֝כוֹנֵ֗ן לְחֵ֣קֶר אֲבוֹתָֽם
verses.translation: Ask the generation past,Study what their forebears have searched out—
verses.rashi: about the past generation About the past generation, like (Deut. 4:32), “For inquire now about the earlier times.” and apply yourself Apply and prepare yourself to understand what their fathers have searched out.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־תְמ֣וֹל אֲ֭נַחְנוּ וְלֹ֣א נֵדָ֑ע כִּ֤י צֵ֖ל יָמֵ֣ינוּ עֲלֵי־אָֽרֶץ
verses.translation: For we are of yesterday and know nothing;Our days on earth are a shadow—

words.eng: we (first pers. pl. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: הֲלֹא־הֵ֣ם י֭וֹרוּךָ יֹ֣אמְרוּ לָ֑ךְ וּ֝מִלִּבָּ֗ם יוֹצִ֥אוּ מִלִּֽים
verses.translation: Surely they will teach you and tell you,Speaking out of their understanding.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲיִֽגְאֶה־גֹּ֭מֶא בְּלֹ֣א בִצָּ֑ה יִשְׂגֶּה־אָ֥חוּ בְלִי־מָֽיִם
verses.translation: Can papyrus thrive without marsh?Can rushes grow without water?
verses.rashi: Can papyrus shoot up They will say this: Papyrus cannot grow except when the marsh is wet with its water and its moisture. בִצָה is marese in Old French, a marsh.

verses.hebrew_full: עֹדֶ֣נּוּ בְ֭אִבּוֹ לֹ֣א יִקָּטֵ֑ף וְלִפְנֵ֖י כׇל־חָצִ֣יר יִיבָֽשׁ
verses.translation: While still tender, not yet plucked,They would wither before any other grass.
verses.rashi: in its greenness In its moisture and with its produce. will not be plucked Heb. יקטף, will not be broken or pulled off; like (Deut. 23:26), “you may pluck (וקטפת) ears.” but before any other grass it will dry up Before any other grass [does], it will dry up when the water evaporates and the marsh dries up.

verses.hebrew_full: כֵּ֗ן אׇ֭רְחוֹת כׇּל־שֹׁ֣כְחֵי אֵ֑ל וְתִקְוַ֖ת חָנֵ֣ף תֹּאבֵֽד
verses.translation: Such is the fate of all who forget God;The hope of the impious man comes to naught—
verses.rashi: So are the ways of all those who forget God to prosper when it goes well with him until his measure [of sin] is full. and the hope the end of the flatterer will be lost.

verses.hebrew_full: אֲשֶׁר־יָק֥וֹט כִּסְל֑וֹ וּבֵ֥ית עַ֝כָּבִ֗ישׁ מִבְטַחֽוֹ
verses.translation: Whose confidence is a thread of gossamer, Whose trust is a spider’s web.
verses.rashi: whose confidence shall be cut off His confidence and his trust Will be cut off confidence Heb. כסלו. That is the thought of his confidence. like (below 31:24), “If I put my confidence (כסלי) in gold. יקוט is an expression of cutting off; as (Ezek. 16:47): “for after a very little while (קט).” a spider’s web which does not last, is his trust.

verses.hebrew_full: יִשָּׁעֵ֣ן עַל־בֵּ֭יתוֹ וְלֹ֣א יַעֲמֹ֑ד יַחֲזִ֥יק בּ֝֗וֹ וְלֹ֣א יָקֽוּם
verses.translation: He leans on his house—it will not stand;He seizes hold of it, but it will not hold.

words.eng: inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: רָטֹ֣ב ה֭וּא לִפְנֵי־שָׁ֑מֶשׁ וְעַ֥ל גַּ֝נָּת֗וֹ יֹנַקְתּ֥וֹ תֵצֵֽא
verses.translation: He stays fresh even in the sun;His shoots spring up in his garden;
verses.rashi: It is fresh His fortune is moist and strong before the time of the visitation of the heat plague—which dries him out—comes to him. And because he [Bildad] compares him [the wicked] to the papyrus, he speaks according to that metaphor. and on its garden its tender branch emerges Heb. יונקתו, its root that draws sustenance, or a young branch that grows this year, like (Ezek. 17: 22), “I will pluck from the top of his young twigs (ינקותיו).”

verses.hebrew_full: עַל־גַּ֭ל שׇׁרָשָׁ֣יו יְסֻבָּ֑כוּ בֵּ֖ית אֲבָנִ֣ים יֶחֱזֶֽה
verses.translation: His roots are twined around a heap,They take hold of a house of stones.
verses.rashi: are entwined Will be entwined with the branch and with the thicket. a stone house He sees a strong place and builds it for himself as a stronghold. Menachem, however explains יֶחֶזֶה as an expression of a border: will border him; he will build it up to his border. [Perlow ed, reads: on his border.] Likewise (Ps. 107:30), “their desired haven.” Likewise (I Kings 7:4), “an edge to an edge (מחזה אל מחזה)” (Machbereth Menachem p. 86). All these are before his plague.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־יְבַלְּעֶ֥נּוּ מִמְּקֹמ֑וֹ וְכִ֥חֶשׁ בּ֝֗וֹ לֹ֣א רְאִיתִֽיךָ
verses.translation: When he is uprooted from his place,It denies him, [saying,]“I never saw you.”
verses.rashi: If one destroys him from his place the one who requites him, and this is an elliptical verse. then it shall deny him his place [shall deny him]. ‘I have not seen you,’ For neither root nor branch shall remain of him, and it will be as though he had never existed, for his fall shall not ascend like the downfall of the righteous—for the righteous will fall and rise, but this one will fall without rising.

words.eng: inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: הֶן־ה֭וּא מְשׂ֣וֹשׂ דַּרְכּ֑וֹ וּ֝מֵעָפָ֗ר אַחֵ֥ר יִצְמָֽחוּ
verses.translation: Such is his happy lot;And from the earth others will grow.
verses.rashi: Behold this is the joy of his way Of the wicked man, for all his joy and also his success will end up without hope. and from the earth, others will spring forth And people who were until now bent down to the ground will spring forth. others Another man will spring forth to receive the greatness of this one, as we find (Eccles. 2:26): “and to the sinner He has given the task...” Therefore, you should know about yourself, if you were wicked, you will not rise after your downfall. If you were innocent...

verses.hebrew_full: הֶן־אֵ֭ל לֹ֣א יִמְאַס־תָּ֑ם וְלֹֽא־יַ֝חֲזִ֗יק בְּיַד־מְרֵעִֽים
verses.translation: Surely God does not despise the blameless,And gives no support to evildoers.
verses.rashi: Behold God will not reject the innocent and He will not uphold your enemies who are harming you, until He returns to grant you clemency, and [then] He will fill your mouth with laughter.

verses.hebrew_full: עַד־יְמַלֵּ֣ה שְׂח֣וֹק פִּ֑יךָ וּשְׂפָתֶ֥יךָ תְרוּעָֽה
verses.translation: Your mouth will yet be filled with laughter,And your lips with shouts of joy.

words.eng: perpetuity·Imlah or Imla = "whom God will fill up"

verses.hebrew_full: שֹׂנְאֶ֥יךָ יִלְבְּשׁוּ־בֹ֑שֶׁת וְאֹ֖הֶל רְשָׁעִ֣ים אֵינֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: Your enemies will be clothed in disgrace;The tent of the wicked will vanish.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֥עַן אִיּ֗וֹב וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Job said in reply:

verses.hebrew_full: אׇ֭מְנָם יָדַ֣עְתִּי כִי־כֵ֑ן וּמַה־יִּצְדַּ֖ק אֱנ֣וֹשׁ עִם־אֵֽל
verses.translation: Indeed I know that it is so:A mortal cannot win a suit against God.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־יַ֭חְפֹּץ לָרִ֣יב עִמּ֑וֹ לֹֽא־יַ֝עֲנֶ֗נּוּ אַחַ֥ת מִנִּי־אָֽלֶף
verses.translation: If someone insisted on such a trial,Not one charge in a thousand would be answered.
verses.rashi: Job’s Replyto contend with Him To debate with Him.

verses.hebrew_full: חֲכַ֣ם לֵ֭בָב וְאַמִּ֣יץ כֹּ֑חַ מִֽי־הִקְשָׁ֥ה אֵ֝לָ֗יו וַיִּשְׁלָֽם
verses.translation: Wise of heart and mighty in power—Who ever challenged divine decree and came out whole?—
verses.rashi: He is wise in heart to contend. and mighty in strength to requite. who hardened his heart and went away whole? Pharaoh hardened [his heart] and was destroyed, and so it is with all who hardened [their hearts].

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace

verses.hebrew_full: הַמַּעְתִּ֣יק הָ֭רִים וְלֹ֣א יָדָ֑עוּ אֲשֶׁ֖ר הֲפָכָ֣ם בְּאַפּֽוֹ
verses.translation: The One who moves mountains without their knowing it,Who overturns them in anger;
verses.rashi: He Who moved mountains He moved them away when He overturned Sodom. and they knew not until He turned them over.

verses.hebrew_full: הַמַּרְגִּ֣יז אֶ֭רֶץ מִמְּקוֹמָ֑הּ וְ֝עַמּוּדֶ֗יהָ יִתְפַּלָּצֽוּן
verses.translation: Who shakes the earth from its place,Till its pillars quake;
verses.rashi: He Who causes the earth to quake when He looks at it, and it trembles.

verses.hebrew_full: הָאֹמֵ֣ר לַ֭חֶרֶס וְלֹ֣א יִזְרָ֑ח וּבְעַ֖ד כּוֹכָבִ֣ים יַחְתֹּֽם
verses.translation: Who commands the sun not to shine;Who seals up the stars;
verses.rashi: He Who spoke to the sun Heb. לחרס, to the sun, “Stand still.” and it kept the command of the King and did not shine. and He settled up the stars He closed off the stars with a partition and they did not shine.

verses.hebrew_full: נֹטֶ֣ה שָׁמַ֣יִם לְבַדּ֑וֹ וְ֝דוֹרֵ֗ךְ עַל־בָּ֥מֳתֵי יָֽם
verses.translation: Who alone spread out the heavens,And trod on the back of the sea;
verses.rashi: He Who clone stretched out the heavens and the angels were created on the second day, lest you say that Michael stretched it out in the north and Gabriel in the south. on the high places of the sea On the heights of the sea. This was stated regarding the Creation, that the lower waters raised themselves up on high, and He trod them.

verses.hebrew_full: עֹֽשֶׂה־עָ֭שׁ כְּסִ֥יל וְכִימָ֗ה וְחַדְרֵ֥י תֵמָֽן
verses.translation: Who made the Bear and Orion,Pleiades, and the chambers of the south wind;
verses.rashi: Orion, and the Pleiades they are constellations. and the chambers of the south into which to bring the tempest, as he states (below 37:9): “From the chamber shall come the tempest.”

verses.hebrew_full: עֹשֶׂ֣ה גְ֭דֹלוֹת עַד־אֵ֣ין חֵ֑קֶר וְ֝נִפְלָא֗וֹת עַד־אֵ֥ין מִסְפָּֽר
verses.translation: Who performs great deeds that cannot be fathomed,And wondrous things without number.

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֤ן יַעֲבֹ֣ר עָ֭לַי וְלֹ֣א אֶרְאֶ֑ה וְ֝יַחֲלֹ֗ף וְֽלֹא־אָבִ֥ין לֽוֹ
verses.translation: [God] passes me by—I am oblivious;[God] goes by me, but I do not notice.
verses.rashi: Behold, He goes by me I know that all He wishes, He does. He goes by me Before me, but I do not see Him.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֣ן יַ֭חְתֹּף מִ֣י יְשִׁיבֶ֑נּוּ מִי־יֹאמַ֥ר אֵ֝לָ֗יו מַֽה־תַּעֲשֶֽׂה
verses.translation: [God] snatches away—who can put a stop to it?Who can say, “What are You doing?”
verses.rashi: Behold, He strikes suddenly Heb. יחתף, He strikes suddenly, like (Prov. 23:28), “She, too, will suddenly (כחתף) lurk,” an expression of suddenness.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱ֭לוֹהַּ לֹא־יָשִׁ֣יב אַפּ֑וֹ תַּחְתָּ֥ו שָׁ֝חֲח֗וּ עֹ֣זְרֵי רָֽהַב
verses.translation: Rahab’s helpers sink down under GodWho does not refrain from anger.
verses.rashi: will not hold back His wrath because of fear (other editions: righteousness) of a man. [all the helpers of Rahab] stoop under Him The celestial host that came to aid the Egyptians. Egypt is called Rahab, as it is said (Isa. 30:7): “Therefore, I called this, ‘They are haughty (רהב) idlers,’” by dint of their haughtiness, for they said, “Who is the Lord? etc.” (Exod. 5:2).

verses.hebrew_full: אַ֭ף כִּֽי־אָנֹכִ֣י אֶעֱנֶ֑נּוּ אֶבְחֲרָ֖ה דְבָרַ֣י עִמּֽוֹ
verses.translation: How then can I answer,Or choose what arguments to make?
verses.rashi: Surely Heb. אַף.

verses.hebrew_full: אֲשֶׁ֣ר אִם־צָ֭דַקְתִּי לֹ֣א אֶעֱנֶ֑ה לִ֝מְשֹׁפְטִ֗י אֶתְחַנָּֽן
verses.translation: Though I were in the right, I could not speak out,But I would plead for mercy with my judge.
verses.rashi: I will not reply I will fear to raise my voice before Him. my Judge Heb. למשפטי, like לשופטי.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־קָרָ֥אתִי וַֽיַּעֲנֵ֑נִי לֹֽא־אַ֝אֲמִ֗ין כִּֽי־יַאֲזִ֥ין קוֹלִֽי
verses.translation: If I issued a summons and were answered,I do not believe my voice would be heard.
verses.rashi: I will not believe I will be unable to believe out of my fear for Him, for how do I not see Him?

verses.hebrew_full: אֲשֶׁר־בִּשְׂעָרָ֥ה יְשׁוּפֵ֑נִי וְהִרְבָּ֖ה פְצָעַ֣י חִנָּֽם
verses.translation: For [God] crushes me for a hair, And wounds me much for no cause—
verses.rashi: He Who would crush me with a tempest My form was changed (other editions: my status) before Him. would crush me Heb. ישופני, would crush me. “I crushed it and ground it” (Deut. 9:21) is translated into Aramaic as: ושפית יתֵה. my wounds Heb. פצעי, a wound that oozes blood and pus.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹֽא־יִ֭תְּנֵנִי הָשֵׁ֣ב רוּחִ֑י כִּ֥י יַ֝שְׂבִּעַ֗נִי מַמְּרֹרִֽים
verses.translation: Not letting me catch my breath,But sating me with bitterness.
verses.rashi: He Who would not let me He does not let me bring back my breath to myself.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־לְכֹ֣חַ אַמִּ֣יץ הִנֵּ֑ה וְאִם־לְ֝מִשְׁפָּ֗ט מִ֣י יוֹעִידֵֽנִי
verses.translation: If a trial of strength—[God] is the strong one;If a trial in court—who will make the arrangements for me?
verses.rashi: If it is a trial of strength How can I contend with Him? If He comes with strength, behold He is mighty. and if of judgment who will be able to summon me before Him and confirm my verdict? Is it not so that...

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־אֶ֭צְדָּק פִּ֣י יַרְשִׁיעֵ֑נִי תׇּֽם־אָ֝֗נִי וַֽיַּעְקְשֵֽׁנִי
verses.translation: Though I were innocent,My mouth would condemn me;Though I were blameless, [God] would prove me crooked.
verses.rashi: If I am innocent, my mouth will condemn me because my words will be silenced out of fear, and my mouth will make me crooked.

words.eng: perfect·I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: תׇּֽם־אָ֭נִי לֹֽא־אֵדַ֥ע נַפְשִׁ֗י אֶמְאַ֥ס חַיָּֽי
verses.translation: I am blameless—I am distraught;I am sick of life.
verses.rashi: I am blameless I know of myself that I am blameless; nonetheless, I do not know my rest, how I will find rest.

words.eng: perfect·I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: אַחַ֗ת הִ֥֫יא עַל־כֵּ֥ן אָמַ֑רְתִּי תָּ֥ם וְ֝רָשָׁ֗ע ה֣וּא מְכַלֶּֽה
verses.translation: It is all one; therefore I say,“The blameless are destroyed along with the guilty.”
verses.rashi: It is all one in the world, concerning which I said that He destroys both the innocent and the wicked. Now, what is it?...

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־שׁ֭וֹט יָמִ֣ית פִּתְאֹ֑ם לְמַסַּ֖ת נְקִיִּ֣ם יִלְעָֽג
verses.translation: When suddenly a scourge brings death,[God] mocks as the innocent fail.
verses.rashi: If the scourge of the Adversary’s tongue suddenly puts the righteous to death. at the calamity of the innocent that he put to death he will mock.

verses.hebrew_full: אֶ֤רֶץ נִתְּנָ֬ה בְֽיַד־רָשָׁ֗ע פְּנֵֽי־שֹׁפְטֶ֥יהָ יְכַסֶּ֑ה אִם־לֹ֖א אֵפ֣וֹא מִי־הֽוּא
verses.translation: The earth is handed over to the wicked one;The eyes of its judges are covered.If it is not [God], then who?
verses.rashi: into the hands of a wicked one Referring to the Adversary. he covers the faces of its judges from discerning and comprehending the truth of the matter. If not, then If this [interpretation] is not the truth of the matter, who is it that destroys the innocent?

verses.hebrew_full: וְיָמַ֣י קַ֭לּוּ מִנִּי־רָ֑ץ בָּ֝רְח֗וּ לֹא־רָא֥וּ טוֹבָֽה
verses.translation: My days fly swifter than a runner;They flee without seeing happiness;
verses.rashi: My days with the destruction of the other innocent people are swifter than a runner.

verses.hebrew_full: חָ֭לְפוּ עִם־אֳנִיּ֣וֹת אֵבֶ֑ה כְּ֝נֶ֗שֶׁר יָט֥וּשׂ עֲלֵי־אֹֽכֶל
verses.translation: They pass like reed-boats,Like an eagle swooping onto its prey.
verses.rashi: Ebeh The name of a rushing river. swoops Heb. יטוש, flies.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־אׇ֭מְרִי אֶשְׁכְּחָ֣ה שִׂיחִ֑י אֶעֶזְבָ֖ה פָנַ֣י וְאַבְלִֽיגָה
verses.translation: If I say, “I will forget my complaint;Abandon my sorrow and be diverted,”
verses.rashi: my speech The words of my pain. my anger Heb. פני, lit. my face. and [I will] restrain myself Heb. ואבליגה, I will strengthen myself, like (Amos 5:9), “Who strengthens (המבליג) the robbed upon the strong.”

verses.hebrew_full: יָגֹ֥רְתִּי כׇל־עַצְּבֹתָ֑י יָ֝דַ֗עְתִּי כִּי־לֹ֥א תְנַקֵּֽנִי
verses.translation: I remain in dread of all my suffering;I know that You will not acquit me.
verses.rashi: I will gather in all my sadness that it not let me cry out. I know that You will not declare me innocent of my iniquity, so as not to requite me.

verses.hebrew_full: אָנֹכִ֥י אֶרְשָׁ֑ע לָמָּה־זֶּ֝֗ה הֶ֣בֶל אִיגָֽע
verses.translation: It will be I who am in the wrong;Why then should I waste effort?
verses.rashi: why should I toil in vain to come to judgment?

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־הִתְרָחַ֥צְתִּי (במו) בְמֵי־שָׁ֑לֶג וַ֝הֲזִכּ֗וֹתִי בְּבֹ֣ר כַּפָּֽי
verses.translation: If I washed with soap,Cleansed my hands with lye,
verses.rashi: with purity Heb. בבור, with cleanliness.

verses.hebrew_full: אָ֭ז בַּשַּׁ֣חַת תִּטְבְּלֵ֑נִי וְ֝תִעֲב֗וּנִי שַׂלְמוֹתָֽי
verses.translation: You would dip me in muckTill my clothes would abhor me.
verses.rashi: You will plunge me into the ditch to be sullied and contaminated; i.e., You will find great iniquity in me.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־לֹא־אִ֣ישׁ כָּמ֣וֹנִי אֶעֱנֶ֑נּוּ נָב֥וֹא יַ֝חְדָּ֗ו בַּמִּשְׁפָּֽט
verses.translation: [God] is not someone like me whom I can answer,That we can go to law together.
verses.rashi: in debate Heb. במשפט, in words of debate. [The word] משפט is used in three expressions: 1) its beginning, viz. the clarification of the matters, 2) its middle, viz. the verdict, and 3) its end, viz. the meting out of the tortures and the collection of the debt. Sometimes Scripture writes it as referring to the beginning, sometimes to the middle, and sometimes to the end.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹ֣א יֵשׁ־בֵּינֵ֣ינוּ מוֹכִ֑יחַ יָשֵׁ֖ת יָד֣וֹ עַל־שְׁנֵֽינוּ
verses.translation: No arbiter is between usTo lay his hand on us both.
verses.rashi: arbiter discerner in Old French. He shows each one his guilt and his innocence. who will place his hand The ruling of his fear and his power. on both of us so that the stronger does not overpower the weaker.

verses.hebrew_full: יָסֵ֣ר מֵעָלַ֣י שִׁבְט֑וֹ וְ֝אֵמָת֗וֹ אַֽל־תְּבַעֲתַֽנִּי
verses.translation: If [God] would only take the rod away from meAnd call off the terror that frightens,

verses.hebrew_full: אֲֽ֭דַבְּרָה וְלֹ֣א אִירָאֶ֑נּוּ כִּ֥י לֹא־כֵ֥ן אָ֝נֹכִ֗י עִמָּדִֽי
verses.translation: Then I would speak out without fear;For I know myself not to be so.
verses.rashi: for I am not so with myself Because I fear [God], I am certain with myself that I will not be found guilty in the trial.

verses.hebrew_full: נָֽקְטָ֥ה נַפְשִׁ֗י בְּחַ֫יָּ֥י אֶעֶזְבָ֣ה עָלַ֣י שִׂיחִ֑י אֲ֝דַבְּרָ֗ה בְּמַ֣ר נַפְשִֽׁי
verses.translation: I am disgusted with life;I will give rein to my complaint,Speak in the bitterness of my soul.
verses.rashi: My soul quarrels Heb. נקטה. My soul quarrels about the fact that I am alive. That is an expression of (Ezek. 20:43), “and you shall quarrel (ונקטתם) with yourselves. I will leave my speech i.e., my trouble, to cry and lament, and I cannot forget it or restrain it.

verses.hebrew_full: אֹמַ֣ר אֶל־אֱ֭לוֹהַּ אַל־תַּרְשִׁיעֵ֑נִי ה֝וֹדִיעֵ֗נִי עַ֣ל מַה־תְּרִיבֵֽנִי
verses.translation: I say to God, “Do not condemn me;Let me know what You charge me with.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲט֤וֹב לְךָ֨ כִּֽי־תַעֲשֹׁ֗ק כִּֽי־תִ֭מְאַס יְגִ֣יעַ כַּפֶּ֑יךָ וְעַל־עֲצַ֖ת רְשָׁעִ֣ים הוֹפָֽעְתָּ
verses.translation: Does it benefit You to defraud,To despise the toil of Your hands,While smiling on the counsel of the wicked?
verses.rashi: Is it good Heb. הטוב, an interrogative form. [Is it good to You] that You should oppress and rob the righteous man of his righteousness, [while] upon the council of the wicked, who vex You, You shine Your splendor to show them a friendly countenance?

verses.hebrew_full: הַעֵינֵ֣י בָשָׂ֣ר לָ֑ךְ אִם־כִּרְא֖וֹת אֱנ֣וֹשׁ תִּרְאֶֽה
verses.translation: Do You have the eyes of flesh?Is Your vision that of a mere mortal?

verses.hebrew_full: הֲכִימֵ֣י אֱנ֣וֹשׁ יָמֶ֑יךָ אִם־שְׁ֝נוֹתֶ֗יךָ כִּ֣ימֵי גָֽבֶר
verses.translation: Are Your days the days of a mortal,Are Your years the years of a person,
verses.rashi: Are Your days like the days of a mortal to provoke him and pursue [him]?

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־תְבַקֵּ֥שׁ לַעֲוֺנִ֑י וּֽלְחַטָּאתִ֥י תִדְרֽוֹשׁ
verses.translation: That You seek my iniquityAnd search out my sin?

verses.hebrew_full: עַֽל־דַּ֭עְתְּךָ כִּֽי־לֹ֣א אֶרְשָׁ֑ע וְאֵ֖ין מִיָּדְךָ֣ מַצִּֽיל
verses.translation: You know that I am not guilty,And that there is none to deliver from Your hand.
verses.rashi: It is in Your knowledge You know.

verses.hebrew_full: יָדֶ֣יךָ עִ֭צְּבוּנִי וַֽיַּעֲשׂ֑וּנִי יַ֥חַד סָ֝בִ֗יב וַֽתְּבַלְּעֵֽנִי
verses.translation: “Your hands shaped and fashioned me,Then destroyed every part of me.
verses.rashi: shaped me Heb. עצבוני, They made me a shape, a mold, like “nor straighten (מעצבין) an infant’s limbs,” in Tractate Shabbath (22:6). But now Your hosts are together round about. and You destroy me These are the worms.

verses.hebrew_full: זְכׇר־נָ֭א כִּי־כַחֹ֣מֶר עֲשִׂיתָ֑נִי וְֽאֶל־עָפָ֥ר תְּשִׁיבֵֽנִי
verses.translation: Consider that You fashioned me like clay;Will You then turn me back into dust?
verses.rashi: You will return me in the end.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲלֹ֣א כֶ֭חָלָב תַּתִּיכֵ֑נִי וְ֝כַגְּבִנָּ֗ה תַּקְפִּיאֵֽנִי
verses.translation: You poured me out like milk,Congealed me like cheese;
verses.rashi: Did you not pour me like milk Concerning the drop from which I was born.

verses.hebrew_full: ע֣וֹר וּ֭בָשָׂר תַּלְבִּישֵׁ֑נִי וּֽבַעֲצָמ֥וֹת וְ֝גִידִ֗ים תְּשֹׂכְכֵֽנִי
verses.translation: You clothed me with skin and fleshAnd wove me of bones and sinews;

verses.hebrew_full: חַיִּ֣ים וָ֭חֶסֶד עָשִׂ֣יתָ עִמָּדִ֑י וּ֝פְקֻדָּתְךָ֗ שָֽׁמְרָ֥ה רוּחִֽי
verses.translation: You bestowed on me life and care;Your providence watched over my spirit.
verses.rashi: and Your providence The providence of Your watch, for You appointed watchers for me. watched my spirit in my mother’s womb, and afterwards...

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֭אֵלֶּה צָפַ֣נְתָּ בִלְבָבֶ֑ךָ יָ֝דַ֗עְתִּי כִּי־זֹ֥את עִמָּֽךְ
verses.translation: Yet these things You hid in Your heart;I know that You had this in mind:
verses.rashi: But You hid these in Your heart to destroy me as though You did not remember them, but... I knew that this was with You, everything as it was delineated, and You did not forget.

words.eng: Tsaf’nath. Gitt. 58ᵃ צ׳ בת פניאל שמה her name was Ts.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־חָטָ֥אתִי וּשְׁמַרְתָּ֑נִי וּ֝מֵעֲוֺנִ֗י לֹ֣א תְנַקֵּֽנִי
verses.translation: To watch me when I sinnedAnd not clear me of my iniquity;
verses.rashi: If I sinned, You watched me You laid Your eyes on me so as not to ignore [my sins] nor to forbear retaliation, and [from now on] You are not cleansing me of my iniquity but are requiting me for everything.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־רָשַׁ֡עְתִּי אַלְלַ֬י לִ֗י וְ֭צָדַקְתִּי לֹא־אֶשָּׂ֣א רֹאשִׁ֑י שְׂבַ֥ע קָ֝ל֗וֹן וּרְאֵ֥ה עׇנְיִֽי
verses.translation: Should I be guilty—the worse for me!And even when innocent, I cannot lift my head;So sated am I with shame,And drenched in my misery.
verses.rashi: sated with disgrace am I. and seeing my affliction Heb. וראה. This is not an expression of the imperative, but is an adjective. וראה עניי is badlace (?) in Old French, “that I see it all the time.” As you say (below, at the end of the Book), “וּשְׂבַע יָמִים, and I am sated with days,” like “וְשָׂבֵעַ יָמִים” (with a “kamatz” under the “sin”) so do you say; וּרְאֵה, like וְרוֹאֶה, “and seeing my affliction.”

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֭יִגְאֶה כַּשַּׁ֣חַל תְּצוּדֵ֑נִי וְ֝תָשֹׁ֗ב תִּתְפַּלָּא־בִֽי
verses.translation: It is something to be proud of to hunt me like a lion,To show Yourself wondrous through. me time and again!
verses.rashi: And it is so great that You hunt me like a lion And it is so great in Your eyes to be to You as a burden and a disappointment, that You hunt me like a lion; You spread out a net for me as though I was as mighty as a lion. and You repeatedly from day to day. pronounce a verdict upon me Heb. תתפלא, to exact and to pronounce judgment, an expression of (Num. 6:2). “shall pronounce (יפלא).”

verses.hebrew_full: תְּחַדֵּ֬שׁ עֵדֶ֨יךָ נֶגְדִּ֗י וְתֶ֣רֶב כַּ֭עַשְׂךָ עִמָּדִ֑י חֲלִיפ֖וֹת וְצָבָ֣א עִמִּֽי
verses.translation: You keep sending fresh witnesses against me,Letting Your vexation with me grow.I serve my term and am my own replacement.
verses.rashi: Your witnesses to testify about the changes of the ailments and the pains. with set times are with me Times set for these my tortures, which serve (once) and [then] repeatedly shift with changes different from these, and serve again.

verses.hebrew_full: וְלָ֣מָּה מֵ֭רֶחֶם הֹצֵאתָ֑נִי אֶ֝גְוַ֗ע וְעַ֣יִן לֹֽא־תִרְאֵֽנִי
verses.translation: “Why did You let me come out of the womb?Better had I expired before any eye saw me,

verses.hebrew_full: כַּאֲשֶׁ֣ר לֹא־הָיִ֣יתִי אֶהְיֶ֑ה מִ֝בֶּ֗טֶן לַקֶּ֥בֶר אוּבָֽל
verses.translation: Had I been as though I never was,Had I been carried from the womb to the grave.
verses.rashi: would that I was brought from the womb to the grave Would that I was brought.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲלֹֽא־מְעַ֣ט יָמַ֣י (יחדל) וַחֲדָ֑ל (ישית) וְשִׁ֥ית מִ֝מֶּ֗נִּי וְאַבְלִ֥יגָה מְּעָֽט
verses.translation: My days are few, so desist!Leave me alone, let me be diverted awhile
verses.rashi: withdraw from me Withdraw Yourself from me and from upon me, like (Isa. 65:5), “Keep to yourself” Remain by yourself and distance yourself from me.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּטֶ֣רֶם אֵ֭לֵךְ וְלֹ֣א אָשׁ֑וּב אֶל־אֶ֖רֶץ חֹ֣שֶׁךְ וְצַלְמָֽוֶת
verses.translation: Before I depart—never to return—For the land of deepest gloom;
verses.rashi: before I go While I am still alive, and the time for me to die has not yet arrived, desist from me and I will rest.

verses.hebrew_full: אֶ֤רֶץ עֵפָ֨תָה כְּמ֥וֹ אֹ֗פֶל צַ֭לְמָוֶת וְלֹ֥א סְדָרִ֗ים וַתֹּ֥פַע כְּמוֹ־אֹֽפֶל
verses.translation: A land whose light is darkness,All gloom and disarray,Whose light is like darkness.”
verses.rashi: darkness Heb. עפתה. This is a noun meaning darkness, like (Amos 4: 13) “dawn and darkness (ועיפתה).” the shadow of death it is, without orders in it (that there are no orders of man; i.e., there are no customs, for there is no civilization, and its light is like darkness). and where the light is as darkness The illuminated place [therein] is like darkness.

verses.hebrew_full: וַ֭יַּעַן צֹפַ֥ר הַֽנַּעֲמָתִ֗י וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Then Zophar the Naamathite said in reply:

verses.hebrew_full: הֲרֹ֣ב דְּ֭בָרִים לֹ֣א יֵעָנֶ֑ה וְאִם־אִ֖ישׁ שְׂפָתַ֣יִם יִצְדָּֽק
verses.translation: Is a multitude of words unanswerable?Must a loquacious person be right?
verses.rashi: Zophar’s AnswerShould not...be answered by others because of the multitude of his words?

verses.hebrew_full: בַּ֭דֶּיךָ מְתִ֣ים יַחֲרִ֑ישׁוּ וַ֝תִּלְעַ֗ג וְאֵ֣ין מַכְלִֽם
verses.translation: Your prattle may silence people;You may mock without being rebuked,
verses.rashi: Your fabrications that you invent from your heart. may silence men Heb. מתים, people.

verses.hebrew_full: וַ֭תֹּאמֶר זַ֣ךְ לִקְחִ֑י וּ֝בַ֗ר הָיִ֥יתִי בְעֵינֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: And say, “My doctrine is pure,And I have been innocent in Your sight.”
verses.rashi: You said to the Holy One, blessed be He, ‘My doctrine is pure, and I was clean in Your eyes.’

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽאוּלָ֗ם מִֽי־יִתֵּ֣ן אֱל֣וֹהַּ דַּבֵּ֑ר וְיִפְתַּ֖ח שְׂפָתָ֣יו עִמָּֽךְ
verses.translation: But would that God might speak,And talk to you directly.

verses.hebrew_full: וְיַגֶּד־לְךָ֨ תַּ֥עֲלֻמ֣וֹת חׇכְמָה֮ כִּֽי־כִפְלַ֢יִם לְֽת֫וּשִׁיָּ֥ה וְדַ֡ע כִּֽי־יַשֶּׁ֥ה לְךָ֥ אֱ֝ל֗וֹהַּ מֵעֲוֺנֶֽךָ
verses.translation: You would be told the secrets of wisdom,For there are many sides to sagacity;And know that God has overlooked for you some of your iniquity.
verses.rashi: mysteries of wisdom and you would know that the wisdom is double, i.e., the Torah that you did not fulfill. that [God] lends Heb. ישה, like (Deut.24: 10), “If you lend (תשה) your friend.” He has yet a large debt over you in the matters of your iniquity.

verses.hebrew_full: הַחֵ֣קֶר אֱל֣וֹהַּ תִּמְצָ֑א אִ֤ם עַד־תַּכְלִ֖ית שַׁדַּ֣י תִּמְצָֽא
verses.translation: Would you discover the mystery of God?Would you discover the limit of the Almighty?
verses.rashi: Can you find out the mystery of God that you think that you have fulfilled everything?

verses.hebrew_full: גׇּבְהֵ֣י שָׁ֭מַיִם מַה־תִּפְעָ֑ל עֲמֻקָּ֥ה מִ֝שְּׁא֗וֹל מַה־תֵּדָֽע
verses.translation: Higher than heaven—what can you do?Deeper than Sheol—what can you know?
verses.rashi: [In] the heights of heaven [About] something that is as high as the heights of heaven, what can you do? And the wisdom is deeper than the grave.

verses.hebrew_full: אֲרֻכָּ֣ה מֵאֶ֣רֶץ מִדָּ֑הּ וּ֝רְחָבָ֗ה מִנִּי־יָֽם
verses.translation: Its measure is longer than the earthAnd broader than the sea.
verses.rashi: its measure Heb. מדה, its measure, but here he mentions measure with a masculine noun (since he did not say, מדתה).

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־יַחֲלֹ֥ף וְיַסְגִּ֑יר וְ֝יַקְהִ֗יל וּמִ֣י יְשִׁיבֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: Should [God] pass by, or confine,Or call an assembly, who can put a stop to it?
verses.rashi: If He passes and confines with pains the one He wishes, and He assembles all His divine ministers to justify His act in having confined. who among them can hinder Him with words to find injustice in His act? And if because the One being judged is tranquil and silent for many days, that is His way.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּי־ה֭וּא יָדַ֣ע מְתֵי־שָׁ֑וְא וַיַּרְא־אָ֝֗וֶן וְלֹ֣א יִתְבּוֹנָֽן
verses.translation: For [God] is aware of deceitful people,And when seeing iniquity, discerns it.
verses.rashi: For He knows deceitful people; when He sees iniquity, He does not consider it [He sees the iniquity] that they commit for days and years, and it seems as though He does not consider it because He is slow to anger.

verses.hebrew_full: וְאִ֣ישׁ נָ֭בוּב יִלָּבֵ֑ב וְעַ֥יִר פֶּ֝֗רֶא אָדָ֥ם יִוָּלֵֽד
verses.translation: The hollow person will get understanding,When a wild ass is born a human.
verses.rashi: But an empty man a hollow man, without a heart [i.e., understanding], who did not understand His way. will gain understanding Heb. ילבב, he will gain for himself a heart [i.e., understanding] to return to his Creator and to search through his deeds—or [he will remain] a man who was like a wild donkey, accustomed to the desert, hasty, without sense. a man will be born He will teach himself to be a new man according to the order of people, and he will determine his way.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־אַ֭תָּה הֲכִינ֣וֹתָ לִבֶּ֑ךָ וּפָרַשְׂתָּ֖ אֵלָ֣יו כַּפֶּֽיךָ
verses.translation: But if you direct your mind,And spread forth your hands toward [God]—
verses.rashi: If you would aim your heart after your pains. and spread out your hands to Him in supplication.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־אָ֣וֶן בְּ֭יָדְךָ הַרְחִיקֵ֑הוּ וְאַל־תַּשְׁכֵּ֖ן בְּאֹהָלֶ֣יךָ עַוְלָֽה
verses.translation: If there is iniquity with you, remove it,And do not let injustice reside in your tent—
verses.rashi: Distance iniquity from your hand, and do not allow injustice to dwell in your tents.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּי־אָ֤ז תִּשָּׂ֣א פָנֶ֣יךָ מִמּ֑וּם וְהָיִ֥יתָ מֻ֝צָ֗ק וְלֹ֣א תִירָֽא
verses.translation: Then, free of blemish, you will hold your head high,And, when in straits,; other Heb. editions muṣṣaq, “you will be firm.” be unafraid.
verses.rashi: For then you would be sure that you could lift your face unblemished, for you would cause yourself to lift your face without any blemish. Because of this... and you shall be strong Heb. מצק, strong, like (I Sam. 2:8), “the pillars of (מצקי) the earth,”

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־אַ֭תָּה עָמָ֣ל תִּשְׁכָּ֑ח כְּמַ֖יִם עָבְר֣וּ תִזְכֹּֽר
verses.translation: You will then put your misery out of mind,Consider it as water that has flowed past.
verses.rashi: and you shall forget all your trouble. like water that has passed and gone by, so shall be the entire memory of your trouble.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: וּֽ֭מִצׇּהֳרַיִם יָק֣וּם חָ֑לֶד תָּ֝עֻ֗פָה כַּבֹּ֥קֶר תִּהְיֶֽה
verses.translation: Life will be brighter than noon; You will shine, you will be like the morning.
verses.rashi: And your luck shall rise more than midday Heb. חלד. More than the light of midday, חֶלֶד shall rise for you, i.e., luck and time, like (Ps. 39:6), “and my age (חלדי) is as nothing before You.” Another explanation: יקום חָלֶד: means, The light of your rust shall rise; i.e., your darkest place shall illuminate more than the light of midday. I heard this, but the first [explanation] does not please me (not found in some editions). your darkness Heb. תעפה. Your darkness shall be like the morning. Another explanation: תָּעֻפָה is an expression of (3:8) “the rays of (עפעפי) dawn.” Because, if it is an expression of darkness, he should have said, “תעוּפה” like תנוּפה, waving, תקוּמה rising, תרוּמה, raising, תנוּמה, slumber.

verses.hebrew_full: וּֽ֭בָטַחְתָּ כִּֽי־יֵ֣שׁ תִּקְוָ֑ה וְ֝חָפַרְתָּ֗ לָבֶ֥טַח תִּשְׁכָּֽב
verses.translation: You will be secure, for there is hope,And, entrenched, you will rest secure;

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽ֭רָבַצְתָּ וְאֵ֣ין מַחֲרִ֑יד וְחִלּ֖וּ פָנֶ֣יךָ רַבִּֽים
verses.translation: You will lie down undisturbed;The great will court your favor.

verses.hebrew_full: וְעֵינֵ֥י רְשָׁעִ֗ים תִּ֫כְלֶ֥ינָה וּ֭מָנוֹס אָבַ֣ד מִנְהֶ֑ם וְ֝תִקְוָתָ֗ם מַֽפַּֽח־נָֽפֶשׁ
verses.translation: But the eyes of the wicked pine away;Escape is cut off from them;They have only their last breath to look forward to.
verses.rashi: And the eyes of the wicked your enemies, will fail when they turn to your troubles to see, but will not achieve their desire. Every expression of כליון עינים failure of the eyes, in the holy tongue means one who looks forward to see something but does not achieve [his wish], and as (Deut. 28:32) “and languish (כלות) for them etc.” way to flee Heb. מנוס. That is a refuge and their hope What they hope to see in you will be intense grief for them.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֥עַן אִיּ֗וֹב וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Then Job said in reply:

verses.hebrew_full: אׇ֭מְנָם כִּ֣י אַתֶּם־עָ֑ם וְ֝עִמָּכֶ֗ם תָּמ֥וּת חׇכְמָֽה
verses.translation: Indeed, you are the [voice of] the people,And wisdom will die with you.
verses.rashi: wisdom shall perish When you die, all wisdom shall perish with you because all wisdom is in you, but I too have intelligence like you.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·nation

verses.hebrew_full: גַּם־לִ֤י לֵבָ֨ב כְּֽמוֹכֶ֗ם לֹא־נֹפֵ֣ל אָנֹכִ֣י מִכֶּ֑ם וְאֶת־מִי־אֵ֥ין כְּמוֹ־אֵֽלֶּה
verses.translation: But I, like you, have a mind,And am not less than you.Who does not know such things?
verses.rashi: and with whom are there none comparable to these? (Who is it who does not know like these) that the Holy One, blessed be He, has power to perform His will and to allow the tranquility of the wicked to last? You are only mocking me, and I am...

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·who?·nothing

verses.hebrew_full: שְׂחֹ֤ק לְרֵעֵ֨הוּ אֶהְיֶ֗ה קֹרֵ֣א לֶ֭אֱלוֹהַּ וַֽיַּעֲנֵ֑הוּ שְׂ֝ח֗וֹק צַדִּ֥יק תָּמִֽים
verses.translation: I have become a laughingstock to my friend—“One who calls to God and is answered,Blamelessly innocent”—a laughingstock.
verses.rashi: a laughingstock to his friend He calls to the Holy One, blessed be He, and He answers him concerning the pain of their provocation. Now what does He answer him? To give the righteous laughter. completely Heb. תָּמִים, perfectly. that the completely righteous will have laughter That is the answer that He will reply with, and I know that it is as you said (8:21), (“He will yet fill your mouth with laughter,”) for...

verses.hebrew_full: לַפִּ֣יד בּ֭וּז לְעַשְׁתּ֣וּת שַׁאֲנָ֑ן נָ֝כ֗וֹן לְמ֣וֹעֲדֵי רָֽגֶל
verses.translation: In the thought of the complacent there is contempt for calamity;It is ready for those whose foot slips.
verses.rashi: A brand of contempt The fire of Gehinnom stands for one who is tranquil in his thoughts, saying, “I will have peace.” prepared The one who is tranquil in his thoughts is destined to be among those who stumble, whose foot slips.

verses.hebrew_full: יִשְׁלָ֤יוּ אֹהָלִ֨ים לְשֹׁ֥דְדִ֗ים וּֽ֭בַטֻּחוֹת לְמַרְגִּ֣יזֵי אֵ֑ל לַאֲשֶׁ֤ר הֵבִ֖יא אֱל֣וֹהַּ בְּיָדֽוֹ
verses.translation: Robbers live untroubled in their tents,And those who provoke God are secure,Those whom God’s hands have produced.
verses.rashi: prosper I know that at times tents will prosper, namely those of the Kedarites who dwell in tents in the deserts, and who are robbers, as it is stated (Gen. 16:12): “a free man among men; his hand shall be against every man.” are secure Those who provoke God have perfection, anyone to whom the Rock brought peace with His hand, whether he is good or bad.

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽאוּלָ֗ם שְׁאַל־נָ֣א בְהֵמ֣וֹת וְתֹרֶ֑ךָּ וְע֥וֹף הַ֝שָּׁמַ֗יִם וְיַגֶּד־לָֽךְ
verses.translation: But ask the beasts, and they will teach you;The birds of the sky, they will tell you,

verses.hebrew_full: א֤וֹ שִׂ֣יחַ לָאָ֣רֶץ וְתֹרֶ֑ךָּ וִיסַפְּר֥וּ לְ֝ךָ֗ דְּגֵ֣י הַיָּֽם
verses.translation: Or speak to the earth, it will teach you;The fish of the sea, they will inform you.

verses.hebrew_full: מִ֭י לֹא־יָדַ֣ע בְּכׇל־אֵ֑לֶּה כִּ֥י יַד־יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה עָ֣שְׂתָה זֹּֽאת
verses.translation: Who among all these does not knowThat GOD’s hand has done this?

verses.hebrew_full: אֲשֶׁ֣ר בְּ֭יָדוֹ נֶ֣פֶשׁ כׇּל־חָ֑י וְ֝ר֗וּחַ כׇּל־בְּשַׂר־אִֽישׁ
verses.translation: In that hand is every living soulAnd the breath of all humankind.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲלֹא־אֹ֭זֶן מִלִּ֣ין תִּבְחָ֑ן וְ֝חֵ֗ךְ אֹ֣כֶל יִטְעַם־לֽוֹ
verses.translation: Truly, the ear tests argumentsAs the palate tastes foods.
verses.rashi: Does not the ear discern words So why did the listeners not know this, as the palate tastes to eat?

verses.hebrew_full: בִּישִׁישִׁ֥ים חׇכְמָ֑ה וְאֹ֖רֶךְ יָמִ֣ים תְּבוּנָֽה
verses.translation: Is wisdom in the agedAnd understanding in the long-lived?
verses.rashi: In elders there is wisdom etc. to know that with Him is wisdom.

verses.hebrew_full: עִ֭מּוֹ חׇכְמָ֣ה וּגְבוּרָ֑ה ל֝֗וֹ עֵצָ֥ה וּתְבוּנָֽה
verses.translation: Wisdom and courage are God’s,As are counsel and understanding.

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֣ן יַ֭הֲרוֹס וְלֹ֣א יִבָּנֶ֑ה יִסְגֹּ֥ר עַל־אִ֝֗ישׁ וְלֹ֣א יִפָּתֵֽחַ
verses.translation: Whatever [God] tears down cannot be rebuilt;Whoever is imprisoned cannot be set free.

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֤ן יַעְצֹ֣ר בַּמַּ֣יִם וְיִבָ֑שׁוּ וִ֝ישַׁלְּחֵ֗ם וְיַ֖הַפְכוּ־אָֽרֶץ
verses.translation: When the waters are held back, they dry up;When they are let loose, they tear up the land.
verses.rashi: He sends it away and it overturns the earth As His agents, as He did to the generation of Enosh, when He inundated a third of the world.

verses.hebrew_full: עִ֭מּוֹ עֹ֣ז וְתוּשִׁיָּ֑ה ל֝֗וֹ שֹׁגֵ֥ג וּמַשְׁגֶּֽה
verses.translation: Strength and resourcefulness are God’s,So are erring and causing to err.
verses.rashi: His are the misled The man who is misled. and the misleader The Adversary, who misleads.

verses.hebrew_full: מוֹלִ֣יךְ יוֹעֲצִ֣ים שׁוֹלָ֑ל וְֽשֹׁפְטִ֥ים יְהוֹלֵֽל
verses.translation: [God] thus makes counselors go about naked And causes judges to go mad;
verses.rashi: madness Heb. שולל. It is a noun, ‘madness.’ He leads them away with madness when He wishes to confuse and destroy their wisdom, as in (Isa. 59: 15), “and he who turns away from evil is considered mad (משתולל)”; (Ps. 76:6), “The stouthearted became mad אשתוללוּ.” The “tav” in משתולל and in אשתוללוּ [is inserted because] so is the structure of the Hebrew language: in a word that has a radical “shin” or “sammech,” and which is used in passive or reflexive conjugation, a “tav” is placed after the first radical, like (Ecc. 8:10), “and they will be forgotten (וישתכחו) in the city”; (Micah 6: 16), “For the statutes of Omri are kept (וישתמר)”; (Exod. 9:17), “You still exalt yourself very high (מסתולל) above My people”; (Ecc. 12:5), “and the grasshopper shall drag himself along (ויסתבל).” Me makes...fools Heb. יהולל, He makes mad, an expression of folly and foolishness.

verses.hebrew_full: מוּסַ֣ר מְלָכִ֣ים פִּתֵּ֑חַ וַיֶּאְסֹ֥ר אֵ֝ז֗וֹר בְּמׇתְנֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: Undoes the belts of kingsAnd fastens loincloths on them;
verses.rashi: He loosens the bond of kings He removed the bond of the yoke from Nebuchadnezzar and dethroned him. and He binds a girdle to their loins At his start, when He desired him, He girded his loins, and imbued him with strength for kingship.

verses.hebrew_full: מוֹלִ֣יךְ כֹּהֲנִ֣ים שׁוֹלָ֑ל וְאֵ֖תָנִ֣ים יְסַלֵּֽף
verses.translation: Makes priests go about naked And leads temple-servants, a class of temple servants; in contrast to others “the mighty.” astray;
verses.rashi: princes Heb. כהנים, like (Exod. 3:1), “the prince (כהן) of Midian”; (Gen. 41:45), “the prince (כהן) of On.”

verses.hebrew_full: מֵסִ֣יר שָׂ֭פָה לְנֶאֱמָנִ֑ים וְטַ֖עַם זְקֵנִ֣ים יִקָּֽח
verses.translation: Deprives trusted ones of speechAnd takes away the reason of elders;
verses.rashi: from trusty men Even from trusty men. (Even to those who believe in Him. Etz Chayim ms.) Sometimes his lips become confused, speaking improper words, e.g. Abraham (about whom it is written:] (Gen. 15:6), And he believed in the Lord.” And later, he stumbled [by saying,] “How will I know?”

verses.hebrew_full: שׁוֹפֵ֣ךְ בּ֭וּז עַל־נְדִיבִ֑ים וּמְזִ֖יחַ אֲפִיקִ֣ים רִפָּֽה
verses.translation: Pours disgrace upon noblesAnd loosens the belt of the mighty;
verses.rashi: and loosens the belt of the strong And He loosens the belt of the strong. He opens their belt to weaken them, like (Ps. 109:19), “and for the girdle (ולמזח) wherewith he is girded continually.” the strong Heb. אפיקים, like (below 40:18), “as strong as (אפיקי) copper; (ibid. 41:7), “He is proud of the strength of (אפיקי) His scales.”

verses.hebrew_full: מְגַלֶּ֣ה עֲ֭מֻקוֹת מִנִּי־חֹ֑שֶׁךְ וַיֹּצֵ֖א לָא֣וֹר צַלְמָֽוֶת
verses.translation: Draws mysteries out of the darknessAnd brings obscurities to light;

verses.hebrew_full: מַשְׂגִּ֣יא לַ֭גּוֹיִם וַֽיְאַבְּדֵ֑ם שֹׁטֵ֥חַ לַ֝גּוֹיִ֗ם וַיַּנְחֵֽם
verses.translation: Exalts nations, then destroys them,And expands nations, then leads them away.
verses.rashi: He makes nations great and destroys them Heb. משׂגיא. Some say, as He did to the Egyptians when they overtook Israel before Baal-zephon (Exod. 14:9). They erred and said, “You should know that He agreed with us, to drown them in the sea as we decreed, ‘Every son that is born you shall cast into the river’ (ibid. 1:22), and [they also said] that their deity Baal-zephon is mighty, seeing that no acts of judgment were performed upon it, as [they were upon) all the [other] gods of Egypt, and the Omnipresent caused them to be misled in order to drown them. But this explanation is not the main one, for were it so, it should have been dotted on the right, מַשְׁגִיא like שׁוגֶה, the misled. This is the main explanation: It means making great, an expression of שׂגיא כֹח, having great strength. He makes the nations great and causes them to prosper, and their heart becomes haughty with their prosperity, so that [the fact] that they prosper is their destruction. He spreads out He multiplies them to spread them out over the earth and to cover them.

verses.hebrew_full: מֵסִ֗יר לֵ֭ב רָאשֵׁ֣י עַם־הָאָ֑רֶץ וַ֝יַּתְעֵ֗ם בְּתֹ֣הוּ לֹא־דָֽרֶךְ
verses.translation: [God] deranges the leaders of the peopleAnd makes them wander in a trackless waste.
verses.rashi: in a wasteland not a road In a wasteland that is not a road.

verses.hebrew_full: יְמַֽשְׁשׁוּ־חֹ֥שֶׁךְ וְלֹא־א֑וֹר וַ֝יַּתְעֵ֗ם כַּשִּׁכּֽוֹר
verses.translation: They grope without light in the darkness,Made to wander as if drunk.

verses.hebrew_full: הֶן־כֹּ֭ל רָאֲתָ֣ה עֵינִ֑י שָֽׁמְעָ֥ה אׇ֝זְנִ֗י וַתָּ֥בֶן לָֽהּ
verses.translation: My eye has seen all this;My ear has heard and understood it.

verses.hebrew_full: כְּֽ֭דַעְתְּכֶם יָדַ֣עְתִּי גַם־אָ֑נִי לֹֽא־נֹפֵ֖ל אָנֹכִ֣י מִכֶּֽם
verses.translation: What you know, I know also;I am not less than you.

words.eng: also·I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: אוּלָ֗ם אֲ֭נִי אֶל־שַׁדַּ֣י אֲדַבֵּ֑ר וְהוֹכֵ֖חַ אֶל־אֵ֣ל אֶחְפָּֽץ
verses.translation: Indeed, I would speak to the Almighty;I insist on arguing with God.
verses.rashi: But I would speak I wish only to speak to Him and I wish to debate with Him.

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽאוּלָ֗ם אַתֶּ֥ם טֹפְלֵי־שָׁ֑קֶר רֹפְאֵ֖י אֱלִ֣ל כֻּלְּכֶֽם
verses.translation: But you invent lies;All of you are quacks.
verses.rashi: combine lies Heb. טפלי שקר,combine false words of falsehood. all of you are quacks Heb. רפאי אלל. Every אֱלִיל in Scripture is an expression of אַל, nought. Our Sages, however, explained that this is the cervical ligament, which [if damaged] has no cure, and it is called אֱלָל.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: מִי־יִ֭תֵּן הַחֲרֵ֣שׁ תַּחֲרִישׁ֑וּן וּתְהִ֖י לָכֶ֣ם לְחׇכְמָֽה
verses.translation: If you would only keep quietIt would be considered wisdom on your part.

words.eng: inflected m. pers. pron. meaning ‘to you’.

verses.hebrew_full: שִׁמְעוּ־נָ֥א תוֹכַחְתִּ֑י וְרִב֖וֹת שְׂפָתַ֣י הַקְשִֽׁיבוּ
verses.translation: Hear now my arguments,Listen to my pleading.
verses.rashi: the contentions The proofs.

verses.hebrew_full: הַ֭לְאֵל תְּדַבְּר֣וּ עַוְלָ֑ה וְ֝ל֗וֹ תְּֽדַבְּר֥וּ רְמִיָּֽה
verses.translation: Will you speak unjustly on God’s behalf—Speaking deceitfully
verses.rashi: Will you speak unjustly for God Since you come to contend in the place of God, it is not good that you should speak unjustly. and will you speak deceitfully for Him? Heb. ולו [usually “to him”]. For His sake will you speak deceitfully? As in (below 33:6), “Behold I am like your mouth for God (לאל),” and like (Deut. 9:28), “to the land that He spoke for them (להם),” for their benefit, and like (Exod. 14:14), “will fight for you (לכם)”; (Jud. 6:31), “Will you contend for the Baal (לבעל)?” (I find it difficult to compare these instances to here [this verse]. Rather, this is analogous to [Num. 17: 5], “through Moses concerning him [לו],” concerning Korah.)

verses.hebrew_full: הֲפָנָ֥יו תִּשָּׂא֑וּן אִם־לָאֵ֥ל תְּרִיבֽוּן
verses.translation: And showing partiality?Will you plead God’s cause?
verses.rashi: Will you show Him favor? Have you come to flatter Him by being partial to Him? for God will you debate deceitfully?

verses.hebrew_full: הֲ֭טוֹב כִּֽי־יַחְקֹ֣ר אֶתְכֶ֑ם אִם־כְּהָתֵ֥ל בֶּ֝אֱנ֗וֹשׁ תְּהָתֵ֥לּוּ בֽוֹ
verses.translation: Will it go well when you are examined?Will you fool [God] as you would a mortal?
verses.rashi: Will it be good when He searches you out and you are found to be liars? Will you mock him as one mocks a mortal by saying, “We made liars of ourselves for Your honor”?

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object
inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: הוֹכֵ֣חַ יוֹכִ֣יחַ אֶתְכֶ֑ם אִם־בַּ֝סֵּ֗תֶר פָּנִ֥ים תִּשָּׂאֽוּן
verses.translation: Surely you will be reprovedIf in your heart you show partiality.
verses.rashi: He will surely reprove you Will He not let you know your speech? When you stand before Him in judgment, will you secretly show Him partiality, saying there. “We showed You partiality”?

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: הֲלֹ֣א שְׂ֭אֵתוֹ תְּבַעֵ֣ת אֶתְכֶ֑ם וּ֝פַחְדּ֗וֹ יִפֹּ֥ל עֲלֵיכֶֽם
verses.translation: God’s threat will terrify you,And fear will seize you.
verses.rashi: Will not His majesty Heb. שאתו. [Will not] His exaltation and His awesomeness terrify you? Others define שְׂאֵתוֹ as “His burning fire.” like (II Sam. 5:21), “and David and his men burned them (וישאם),” and like (Jud. 20:38), “a great pillar of (משאת) smoke.”

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: זִֽ֭כְרֹנֵיכֶם מִשְׁלֵי־אֵ֑פֶר לְגַבֵּי־חֹ֝֗מֶר גַּבֵּיכֶֽם
verses.translation: Your briefs are empty platitudes;Your responses are unsubstantial.
verses.rashi: your memorials are compared to ashes Your memorials are compared to ashes. You expect to be compared to Abraham, who said (Genesis 18:27), “I am only dust and ashes.” are like eminences of clay Your eminences ore equal to eminences of clay, compared to Him. The word גביכם means, your height, like (Ezek. 16:24), “and you built for yourself a platform (גב).”

verses.hebrew_full: הַחֲרִ֣ישׁוּ מִ֭מֶּנִּי וַאֲדַבְּרָה־אָ֑נִי וְיַעֲבֹ֖ר עָלַ֣י מָֽה
verses.translation: Keep quiet; I will have my say,Come what may upon me.
verses.rashi: and let come upon me what may Whatever comes upon me because of the sin of my outcry, let it come upon me and do not care. For what reason must I bring up and hold...

words.eng: to speak·I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)
to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: עַל־מָ֤ה אֶשָּׂ֣א בְשָׂרִ֣י בְשִׁנָּ֑י וְ֝נַפְשִׁ֗י אָשִׂ֥ים בְּכַפִּֽי
verses.translation: How long! I will take my flesh in my teeth;I will take my life in my hands.
verses.rashi: my flesh up into my teeth to afflict myself and force myself to silence? and take my life into my hand to bring my life close to death with the agonies of silence.

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֣ן יִ֭קְטְלֵנִי (לא) ל֣וֹ אֲיַחֵ֑ל אַךְ־דְּ֝רָכַ֗י אֶל־פָּנָ֥יו אוֹכִֽיחַ
verses.translation: I may well be slain; I may have no hope;. Yet I will argue my case before [God].
verses.rashi: Behold, let Him kill me I will not separate from Him, and I will always hope for Him. Therefore, there is no rebellion or transgression in my words, but I will reason to His face about my way and my character traits ... with which I have conducted myself, to know of what He will find me guilty.

verses.hebrew_full: גַּם־הוּא־לִ֥י לִישׁוּעָ֑ה כִּי־לֹ֥א לְ֝פָנָ֗יו חָנֵ֥ף יָבֽוֹא
verses.translation: In this too is my salvation:That no one impious can come into God’s presence.
verses.rashi: He is also Just as I am wholehearted with Him, so is He also my salvation, but you will not find favor in His eyes. because a hypocrite cannot come before Him This is what was said to them (below 42:7): “because you did not speak to Me properly.”

verses.hebrew_full: שִׁמְע֣וּ שָׁ֭מוֹעַ מִלָּתִ֑י וְ֝אַחֲוָתִ֗י בְּאׇזְנֵיכֶֽם
verses.translation: Listen closely to my words;Give ear to my discourse.

verses.hebrew_full: הִנֵּה־נָ֭א עָרַ֣כְתִּי מִשְׁפָּ֑ט יָ֝דַ֗עְתִּי כִּי־אֲנִ֥י אֶצְדָּֽק
verses.translation: See now, I have prepared a case;I know that I will win it.
verses.rashi: I have set up judgement [I have prepared] my case. I have arranged my arguments in my heart viz. what to answer.

words.eng: burning·I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: מִי־ה֭וּא יָרִ֣יב עִמָּדִ֑י כִּֽי־עַתָּ֖ה אַחֲרִ֣ישׁ וְאֶגְוָֽע
verses.translation: For who is it that would challenge me?I should then keep silent and expire.
verses.rashi: I will be silent and perish If I will not contend, I will be silent and perish.

verses.hebrew_full: אַךְ־שְׁ֭תַּיִם אַל־תַּ֣עַשׂ עִמָּדִ֑י אָ֥ז מִ֝פָּנֶ֗יךָ לֹ֣א אֶסָּתֵֽר
verses.translation: But two things do not do to me,So that I need not hide from You:
verses.rashi: But do not do two things to me viz. Heavenly judgment and earthly judgment. If You come to judgment with one [of these], I will not hide from before You. If with Heavenly judgment, I will say, “I have sinned.” [because] (Prov. 28:13), “He who conceals his sins will not succeed.” If with earthly judgment, even I will not confess to concealing sin. I did not hear this (absent in some editions).

verses.hebrew_full: כַּ֭פְּךָ מֵעָלַ֣י הַרְחַ֑ק וְ֝אֵ֥מָתְךָ֗ אַֽל־תְּבַעֲתַֽנִּי
verses.translation: Remove Your hand from me,And let not Your terror frighten me.
verses.rashi: Distance Your compulsion from upon me Heb. כפי, Your compulsion, as Elihu answered him (below 33:7), “and my compulsion (ואכפי) shall not weigh heavily upon you.” Some interpret כפך as “your hand.” However, it is difficult for me to say that, because we do not find כַּף in the sense of evil, but [meaning] to protect, as in (Exod. 33:22), “And I will cover you with My hand (כפי).”

verses.hebrew_full: וּ֭קְרָא וְאָנֹכִ֣י אֶעֱנֶ֑ה אֽוֹ־אֲ֝דַבֵּ֗ר וַהֲשִׁיבֵֽנִי
verses.translation: Then summon me and I will respond,Or I will speak and You reply to me.

verses.hebrew_full: כַּמָּ֣ה לִ֭י עֲוֺנ֣וֹת וְחַטָּא֑וֹת פִּֽשְׁעִ֥י וְ֝חַטָּאתִ֗י הֹדִיעֵֽנִי
verses.translation: How many are my iniquities and sins?Advise me of my transgression and sin.

verses.hebrew_full: לָֽמָּה־פָנֶ֥יךָ תַסְתִּ֑יר וְתַחְשְׁבֵ֖נִי לְאוֹיֵ֣ב לָֽךְ
verses.translation: Why do You hide Your face,And treat me like an enemy?
verses.rashi: Why do You hide Your face from seeing the ways of my uprightness?

verses.hebrew_full: הֶעָלֶ֣ה נִדָּ֣ף תַּעֲר֑וֹץ וְאֶת־קַ֖שׁ יָבֵ֣שׁ תִּרְדֹּֽף
verses.translation: Will You harass a driven leaf,Will You pursue dried-up straw,
verses.rashi: Will You frighten Heb. תערוץ, will You scare?

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·stubble

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־תִכְתֹּ֣ב עָלַ֣י מְרֹר֑וֹת וְ֝תוֹרִישֵׁ֗נִי עֲוֺנ֥וֹת נְעוּרָֽי
verses.translation: That You decree for me bitter thingsAnd make me answer for the iniquities of my youth,
verses.rashi: disobediences Heb. מררות. The cases of obstinacy in which I obeyed Your orders, You write [down] but not the good deeds that I performed.

verses.hebrew_full: וְתָ֘שֵׂ֤ם בַּסַּ֨ד רַגְלַ֗י וְתִשְׁמ֥וֹר כׇּל־אׇרְחוֹתָ֑י עַל־שׇׁרְשֵׁ֥י רַ֝גְלַ֗י תִּתְחַקֶּֽה
verses.translation: That You put my feet in the stocksAnd watch all my ways,Hemming in my footsteps?
verses.rashi: in the stocks Heb. בסד. In Aramaic (Pesachim 28a), “The one who makes the stocks (סדנא) sits in his own stocks (בסדניה).” [This is] a large [block of] wood in which the prisoners’ feet are inserted, and in Greek it is astock(?). You engrave Heb. תתחקה. You Yourself engrave my footsteps. So did this one transgress (step—Ed. Furth]), and so he did. Like (Ezek. 43:14), “from the bottom (ומחיק) upon the ground”; [and] (verse 13), “the bottom (וחיק) shall be a cubit.” This is the foundation, affishe in Old French, to affix. (Isa. 49:16): “Behold on [My] hands have I engraved you (חקותיך).”

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֭הוּא כְּרָקָ֣ב יִבְלֶ֑ה כְּ֝בֶ֗גֶד אֲכָ֣לוֹ עָֽשׁ
verses.translation: One wastes away like a rotten thing,Like a garment eaten by moths.
verses.rashi: And it is like rot that decays The body You pursue will decay like rot, and it is beneath Your dignity to pursue it.

verses.hebrew_full: אָ֭דָם יְל֣וּד אִשָּׁ֑ה קְצַ֥ר יָ֝מִ֗ים וּֽשְׂבַֽע־רֹֽגֶז
verses.translation: Humankind, born of woman, is short-lived and sated with trouble,

verses.hebrew_full: כְּצִ֣יץ יָ֭צָא וַיִּמָּ֑ל וַיִּבְרַ֥ח כַּ֝צֵּ֗ל וְלֹ֣א יַעֲמֽוֹד
verses.translation: Blossoms like a flower and withers,Vanishes like a shadow and does not endure.

verses.hebrew_full: אַף־עַל־זֶ֭ה פָּקַ֣חְתָּ עֵינֶ֑ךָ וְאֹ֘תִ֤י תָבִ֖יא בְמִשְׁפָּ֣ט עִמָּֽךְ
verses.translation: Do You fix Your gaze on such a one?Will You go to law with me?
verses.rashi: also on this one [creature of] futility that decays have You opened Your eyes to be exact with his sins, and You bring me into judgment for my iniquities?

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: מִֽי־יִתֵּ֣ן טָ֭הוֹר מִטָּמֵ֗א לֹ֣א אֶחָֽד
verses.translation: Who can produce a pure thing out of an impure one? No one!
verses.rashi: Who can bring a clean thing from a putrid drop, and semen, which is unclean? Not one of them is clean, that he should not sin.

verses.hebrew_full: אִ֥ם־חֲרוּצִ֨ים יָמָ֗יו מִֽסְפַּר־חֳדָשָׁ֥יו אִתָּ֑ךְ חֻקָּ֥ו עָ֝שִׂ֗יתָ וְלֹ֣א יַעֲבֹֽר
verses.translation: Their days are determined;You know the number of months;You have set them limits that they cannot pass.
verses.rashi: If his days are limited If he is requited with this recompense—that his days are limited to a day set for death, and the number of months is set with You, and [that] You set this boundary—and he will no longer exist in the world, [then] this payment is sufficient for You.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object
to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: שְׁעֵ֣ה מֵעָלָ֣יו וְיֶחְדָּ֑ל עַד־יִ֝רְצֶ֗ה כְּשָׂכִ֥יר יוֹמֽוֹ
verses.translation: Turn away from them, that they may be at easeUntil, like a hireling, they finish out their day.
verses.rashi: Turn away from him during those few limited days and let him rest from pain until he desires, in his old age and the weakness of his strength, the day of his death like a hireling who desires the completion of his day’s work; for his passing from the world is decided and more definite than all passings.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י יֵ֥שׁ לָעֵ֗ץ תִּ֫קְוָ֥ה אִֽם־יִ֭כָּרֵת וְע֣וֹד יַחֲלִ֑יף וְ֝יֹנַקְתּ֗וֹ לֹ֣א תֶחְדָּֽל
verses.translation: There is hope for a tree;If it is cut down it will renew itself;Its shoots will not cease.
verses.rashi: a tree has This [tree] has hope that it will renew itself and its bough will not cease from being a branch.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־יַזְקִ֣ין בָּאָ֣רֶץ שׇׁרְשׁ֑וֹ וּ֝בֶעָפָ֗ר יָמ֥וּת גִּזְעֽוֹ
verses.translation: If its roots are old in the earth,And its stump dies in the ground,

verses.hebrew_full: מֵרֵ֣יחַ מַ֣יִם יַפְרִ֑חַ וְעָשָׂ֖ה קָצִ֣יר כְּמוֹ־נָֽטַע
verses.translation: At the scent of water it will budAnd produce branches like a sapling.
verses.rashi: and it will produce a branch Heb. קציר. This is a branch, as (Ps. 80:12), “She sent out her boughs (קצירה) to the sea.” a sapling Heb. נטע. This is a noun. It is accented on the first syllable, and is punctuated completely with a “kamatz” because it is the end of the verse, but it is derived from נֶטַע, a sapling.

verses.hebrew_full: וְגֶ֣בֶר יָ֭מוּת וַֽיֶּחֱלָ֑שׁ וַיִּגְוַ֖ע אָדָ֣ם וְאַיּֽוֹ
verses.translation: But people languish and die;Humans expire; where are they?

verses.hebrew_full: אָֽזְלוּ־מַ֭יִם מִנִּי־יָ֑ם וְ֝נָהָ֗ר יֶחֱרַ֥ב וְיָבֵֽשׁ
verses.translation: The waters of the sea fail,And the river dries up and is parched.
verses.rashi: As the waters fail from the sea From the place whence the river comes and emerges, from its source that comes to it from the sea. [Alternative explanation of Rashi: From the place whence the river comes, and they went away from its source (from) which (the water) comes to it.] and the river that comes from there is drained dry forever. So does a man lie down and not rise.

verses.hebrew_full: וְאִ֥ישׁ שָׁכַ֗ב וְֽלֹא־יָ֫ק֥וּם עַד־בִּלְתִּ֣י שָׁ֭מַיִם לֹ֣א יָקִ֑יצוּ וְלֹֽא־יֵ֝עֹ֗רוּ מִשְּׁנָתָֽם
verses.translation: So each of us lies down never to rise—And will awake only when the heavens are no more,Only then be aroused from sleep.

verses.hebrew_full: מִ֤י יִתֵּ֨ן בִּשְׁא֬וֹל תַּצְפִּנֵ֗נִי תַּ֭סְתִּירֵנִי עַד־שׁ֣וּב אַפֶּ֑ךָ תָּ֤שִֽׁית־לִ֖י חֹ֣ק וְתִזְכְּרֵֽנִי
verses.translation: O that You would hide me in Sheol,Conceal me until Your anger passes,Set me a fixed time to attend to me.

words.eng: to put·inflected pers. pron. meaning ‘to me’.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־יָמ֥וּת גֶּ֗בֶר הֲיִֽ֫חְיֶ֥ה כׇּל־יְמֵ֣י צְבָאִ֣י אֲיַחֵ֑ל עַד־בּ֝֗וֹא חֲלִיפָתִֽי
verses.translation: Can someone who dies live again?All the time of my service I waitUntil my replacement comes.
verses.rashi: All the days of my lifespan, I will hope for life.

verses.hebrew_full: תִּ֭קְרָא וְאָנֹכִ֣י אֶעֱנֶ֑ךָּ לְֽמַעֲשֵׂ֖ה יָדֶ֣יךָ תִכְסֹֽף
verses.translation: You would call and I would answer You;You would set Your heart on Your handiwork.
verses.rashi: Call This is a supplication. Call to me and I will answer you to prove my case. You desire Heb. תִכְסֹף, You desire.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־עַ֭תָּה צְעָדַ֣י תִּסְפּ֑וֹר לֹֽא־תִ֝שְׁמֹ֗ר עַל־חַטָּאתִֽי
verses.translation: Then You would not count my steps,Or keep watch over my sin.
verses.rashi: But now You are harming me. my steps You are counting. You do not wait Heb. תשמר. You do not wait for my sin to requite me, as (Gen. 37:11), “but his father awaited (שמר) the matter,” and in the language of the Mishnah (Sanh. 63b): “A person may not say to his companion, ‘Wait (שמר) for me beside such and such a pagan deity.’” [It is] an expression of waiting.

verses.hebrew_full: חָתֻ֣ם בִּצְר֣וֹר פִּשְׁעִ֑י וַ֝תִּטְפֹּ֗ל עַל־עֲוֺנִֽי
verses.translation: My transgression would be sealed up in a pouch;You would coat over my iniquity.
verses.rashi: sealed up in a bundle Sealed and preserved in a cloth bundle like silver and pearls, lest it be lost. and You have attached Yourself to my iniquity Heb. ותטפל, You have attached Yourself to my iniquity.

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֭אוּלָם הַר־נוֹפֵ֣ל יִבּ֑וֹל וְ֝צ֗וּר יֶעְתַּ֥ק מִמְּקֹמֽוֹ
verses.translation: Mountains collapse and crumble;Rocks are dislodged from their place.
verses.rashi: And surely the falling mountain gives forth produce The height of a falling mountain will give forth produce. It will produce dust, and there will be hope of deriving benefit from it. and the rock that moves from its place will also give forth produce. This is an expression of grain.

verses.hebrew_full: אֲבָנִ֤ים שָׁ֥חֲקוּ מַ֗יִם תִּשְׁטֹֽף־סְפִיחֶ֥יהָ עֲפַר־אָ֑רֶץ וְתִקְוַ֖ת אֱנ֣וֹשׁ הֶאֱבַֽדְתָּ
verses.translation: Water wears away stone;Torrents wash away earth;So you destroy a mortal’s hope,
verses.rashi: Stones which the water eroded by constantly passing over them. it washes away i.e., the flood. its aftergrowth of the stone, to be transformed into dust so that something should grow in it. As the aftergrowths of the harvest grow, so will the aftergrowths of this earth be transformed to stone. but the hope of man is not so, because as soon as he dies, it is lost forever.

verses.hebrew_full: תִּתְקְפֵ֣הוּ לָ֭נֶצַח וַֽיַּהֲלֹ֑ךְ מְשַׁנֶּ֥ה פָ֝נָ֗יו וַֽתְּשַׁלְּחֵֽהוּ
verses.translation: You overpower him forever and he perishes;You alter his visage and dispatch him.
verses.rashi: You overpower him You are stronger than he; You overcome him with Your power, and he goes away from the world.

verses.hebrew_full: יִכְבְּד֣וּ בָ֭נָיו וְלֹ֣א יֵדָ֑ע וְ֝יִצְעֲר֗וּ וְֽלֹא־יָבִ֥ין לָֽמוֹ
verses.translation: His children attain honor and he does not know it;They are humbled and he is not aware of it.
verses.rashi: become wealthy Heb. יכבדו, lit. they will become heavy. They will become heavy with silver and gold, but he will not know. and they become poor And they have little of any good, but he will not understand it.

verses.hebrew_full: אַךְ־בְּ֭שָׂרוֹ עָלָ֣יו יִכְאָ֑ב וְ֝נַפְשׁ֗וֹ עָלָ֥יו תֶּאֱבָֽל
verses.translation: He feels only the pain of his flesh,And his spirit mourns in him.
verses.rashi: But his flesh causes him pain A worm is as painful to the dead as a needle in the flesh of the living.

verses.hebrew_full: וַ֭יַּעַן אֱלִיפַ֥ז הַֽתֵּימָנִ֗י וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Eliphaz the Temanite said in reply:

verses.hebrew_full: הֶחָכָ֗ם יַעֲנֶ֥ה דַעַת־ר֑וּחַ וִימַלֵּ֖א קָדִ֣ים בִּטְנֽוֹ
verses.translation: Does a wise man answer with windy opinions,And fill his belly with the east wind?
verses.rashi: Eliphaz’s AnswerShall a wise man raise his voice with opinions of wind It is puzzling. Should one who is wise answer and raise his voice with an opinion of wind and futility?

verses.hebrew_full: הוֹכֵ֣חַ בְּ֭דָבָר לֹ֣א יִסְכּ֑וֹן וּ֝מִלִּ֗ים לֹא־יוֹעִ֥יל בָּֽם
verses.translation: Should he argue with useless talk,With words that are of no worth?
verses.rashi: To debate over a matter from which he derives no benefit Heb. יסכון. To debate over a matter from which he will derive no benefit, like (I Kings 1:2), “and she shall be to him a warmer (סכנת),” and like (Ecc. 10:9), “is warmed (יסכן) by it,” i.e., he will derive benefit from it.

words.eng: to be of use or service or profit or benefit

verses.hebrew_full: אַף־אַ֭תָּה תָּפֵ֣ר יִרְאָ֑ה וְתִגְרַ֥ע שִׂ֝יחָ֗ה לִפְנֵי־אֵֽל
verses.translation: You subvert pietyAnd restrain prayer to God.
verses.rashi: Surely you Surely you, who are very wise, will do away with fear by the multitude of your words. and increase speech Heb. ותגרע, like (below 36:27) “He will increase (יגרע) the drops of water,” and like (ibid. 15:8), “and [did] you increase (ותגרע) wisdom to yourself.”

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י יְאַלֵּ֣ף עֲוֺנְךָ֣ פִ֑יךָ וְ֝תִבְחַ֗ר לְשׁ֣וֹן עֲרוּמִֽים
verses.translation: Your sinfulness dictates your speech,So you choose crafty language.
verses.rashi: For your iniquity teaches Your evil inclination [teaches] your mouth to speak such words, but you should have chosen the tongue of the crafty.

verses.hebrew_full: יַרְשִׁיעֲךָ֣ פִ֣יךָ וְלֹא־אָ֑נִי וּ֝שְׂפָתֶ֗יךָ יַעֲנוּ־בָֽךְ
verses.translation: Your own mouth condemns you—not I;Your lips testify against you.
verses.rashi: Let your own mouth condemn you and not I That is to say: Give me an answer to my question; the result will be that your own mouth will condemn you, for it better for me that your mouth should condemn you and not I. Now this is my question:

words.eng: not·I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)
to answer·(in pause <a dir="rtl" class="refLink" href="/Klein Dictionary

verses.hebrew_full: הֲרִאישׁ֣וֹן אָ֭דָם תִּוָּלֵ֑ד וְלִפְנֵ֖י גְבָע֣וֹת חוֹלָֽלְתָּ
verses.translation: Were you the first human born?Were you created before the hills?
verses.rashi: Were you born before Adam Heb. הראישון. Were you born before Adam? This “hey” serves as the interrogative expression. Therefore, it is vowelized with a “hattaf pattah” and its meaning is: Were you born the first one, before Adam who was formed from the earth, so that you knew to fulfill all the wisdom of the commandments of the Creator?

verses.hebrew_full: הַבְס֣וֹד אֱל֣וֹהַּ תִּשְׁמָ֑ע וְתִגְרַ֖ע אֵלֶ֣יךָ חׇכְמָֽה
verses.translation: Have you listened in on the council of God?Have you sole possession of wisdom?
verses.rashi: and increase And bring down much [wisdom] from above to descend upon you?

verses.hebrew_full: מַה־יָּ֭דַעְתָּ וְלֹ֣א נֵדָ֑ע תָּ֝בִ֗ין וְֽלֹא־עִמָּ֥נוּ הֽוּא
verses.translation: What do you know that we do not know,Or understand that we do not?

verses.hebrew_full: גַּם־שָׂ֣ב גַּם־יָשִׁ֣ישׁ בָּ֑נוּ כַּבִּ֖יר מֵאָבִ֣יךָ יָמִֽים
verses.translation: Among us are gray-haired aged men,Older by far than your father.
verses.rashi: more of more days than you father.

verses.hebrew_full: הַמְעַ֣ט מִ֭מְּךָ תַּנְחוּמ֣וֹת אֵ֑ל וְ֝דָבָ֗ר לָאַ֥ט עִמָּֽךְ
verses.translation: Are God’s consolations not enough for you,And those gentle words to you?
verses.rashi: Are God’s consolations little for you with which the Rock benefited you until now for many days, and His good thing that He gave you with pleasantness and gentleness, viz. His fatness [i.e., plenty]. He gave...gently Heb. לאט, he gave gently. This is a verb, not a noun. Therefore, it is vowelized half with a “kamatz” and half with a “pattah.”

verses.hebrew_full: מַה־יִּקָּחֲךָ֥ לִבֶּ֑ךָ וּֽמַה־יִּרְזְמ֥וּן עֵינֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: How your heart has carried you away,How your eyes have failed you,
verses.rashi: teach you Heb. יקחך, teach you, as in (Deut. 32:2), “so that my teaching (לקחי) may drip like rain.” hint Heb. ירזמון, like יִרְמְזוּן ; like שַׂלְמָה שִׂמְלָה (Exod. 22:8, 26).

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־תָשִׁ֣יב אֶל־אֵ֣ל רוּחֶ֑ךָ וְהֹצֵ֖אתָ מִפִּ֣יךָ מִלִּֽין
verses.translation: That you could vent your anger on God,And let such words out of your mouth!

verses.hebrew_full: מָה־אֱנ֥וֹשׁ כִּֽי־יִזְכֶּ֑ה וְכִי־יִ֝צְדַּ֗ק יְל֣וּד אִשָּֽׁה
verses.translation: What are mortals that they can be cleared of guilt,Those born of woman, that they be in the right?
verses.rashi: What is man that he should be innocent What is his strength, that he can be innocent against the Holy One, blessed be He?

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֣ן בִּ֭קְדֹשָׁו לֹ֣א יַאֲמִ֑ין וְ֝שָׁמַ֗יִם לֹא־זַכּ֥וּ בְעֵינָֽיו
verses.translation: [God] puts no trust in the holy ones;The heavens are not guiltless in God’s sight;
verses.rashi: and the heavens the host of the heavens. pure Heb. זכו, shine. Every expression of זַךְ is splendor.

verses.hebrew_full: אַ֭ף כִּי־נִתְעָ֥ב וְֽנֶאֱלָ֑ח אִישׁ־שֹׁתֶ֖ה כַמַּ֣יִם עַוְלָֽה
verses.translation: What then of those loathsome and foul,The ones who drink wrongdoing like water!
verses.rashi: Surely Heb. אף, how much more. and impure Heb. ונאלח, abominable and mixed. Similarly (Ps. 53: 4), “Are they all dross, altogether filthy (נאלחו)?”

verses.hebrew_full: אֲחַוְךָ֥ שְֽׁמַֽע־לִ֑י וְזֶֽה־חָ֝זִ֗יתִי וַאֲסַפֵּֽרָה
verses.translation: I will hold forth; listen to me;What I have seen, I will declare—

verses.hebrew_full: אֲשֶׁר־חֲכָמִ֥ים יַגִּ֑ידוּ וְלֹ֥א כִ֝חֲד֗וּ מֵאֲבוֹתָֽם
verses.translation: That which the wise have transmitted from their ancestors,And have not withheld,
verses.rashi: That the wise men tell and confess their transgression. and do not hide their iniquity from their fathers. This is what I saw, and behold, I will tell you. Now where did I see it? Judah confessed and was not ashamed in the incident of Tamar. Reuben confessed and was not ashamed in the incident of Bilhah. And what was their reward?

verses.hebrew_full: לָהֶ֣ם לְ֭בַדָּם נִתְּנָ֣ה הָאָ֑רֶץ וְלֹא־עָ֖בַר זָ֣ר בְּתוֹכָֽם
verses.translation: To whom alone the land was given,No stranger passing among them:
verses.rashi: To them alone the land was given As a reward for this, Judah merited eternal kingship, and both took their share of the heritage, and they were not driven out of their heritage because of their iniquity, since they confessed. and no stranger passed in their midst when Moses came to bless them, as it is stated (Deut. 33:6f): “Reuben shall live and not die etc. And is this for Judah?” And no stranger passed in their midst in the heritage of the future that is apportioned in the Book of Ezekiel (48). But...

words.eng: not·to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־יְמֵ֣י רָ֭שָׁע ה֣וּא מִתְחוֹלֵ֑ל וּמִסְפַּ֥ר שָׁ֝נִ֗ים נִצְפְּנ֥וּ לֶעָרִֽיץ
verses.translation: The wicked man writhes in torment all his days;Few years are reserved for the ruthless.
verses.rashi: All the days of a wicked man who does not repent of his sin and does not confess his transgressions, all his days... he mourns and grieves over his certainty of being requited for his evil that he committed. All the number of years that were laid up for the oppressor, he mourns. The word מתחולל is an expression of mourning, but I did not hear this.

verses.hebrew_full: קוֹל־פְּחָדִ֥ים בְּאׇזְנָ֑יו בַּ֝שָּׁל֗וֹם שׁוֹדֵ֥ד יְבוֹאֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: Frightening sounds fill his ears;When he is at ease a robber falls upon him.
verses.rashi: The voice of terrors is in his ears saying that the time of his retribution has arrived. in peace And when he lives in peace, then the robber will come upon him.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־יַאֲמִ֣ין שׁ֭וּב מִנִּי־חֹ֑שֶׁךְ (וצפו) וְצָפ֖וּי ה֣וּא אֱלֵי־חָֽרֶב
verses.translation: He is never sure he will come back from the dark;A sword stares him in the face.
verses.rashi: and he is destined to the sword Heb. וצפוי. He is destined to the sword, and his eye to wander for bread—where should he find it?

verses.hebrew_full: נֹ֘דֵ֤ד ה֣וּא לַלֶּ֣חֶם אַיֵּ֑ה יָדַ֓ע כִּי־נָכ֖וֹן בְּיָד֣וֹ יֽוֹם־חֹֽשֶׁךְ
verses.translation: He wanders about for bread—where is it?He knows that the day of darkness has been readied for him.
verses.rashi: He wanders i.e., he is assured of wandering. He knows in fact. that...is ready and prepared to come to his hand, i.e., to him, and with him is the day of darkness. at his hand Like (Gen. 32: 14), “from what came into his hand.”

verses.hebrew_full: יְֽ֭בַעֲתֻהוּ צַ֣ר וּמְצוּקָ֑ה תִּ֝תְקְפֵ֗הוּ כְּמֶ֤לֶךְ עָתִ֬יד לַכִּידֽוֹר
verses.translation: Troubles terrify him, anxiety overpowers him,Like a king expecting a siege.
verses.rashi: it overpowers him The anguish is stronger than him and it will prevail over him. like a king destined for the inferno Heb. לכידור. I have not found anything similar to it, but it may be interpreted by means of the interchangeable letters, “resh” with “dalet,” like (Gen. 10:3), “Ashkenaz and Riphath” in the Torah, and in Chronicles (I, 1:6): “Ashkenaz and Diphath;” and similarly (Gen. 10: 4). “Kittim and Dodanim” Here too, לַכִּידוֹר is equivalent to לַכִּידוֹד, to the king who is destined to burn (ליקוד) in the fire of Gehinnom; i.e., Sennacherib, about whom the prophet prophesied (Isa. 30: 33): “For Tophteh has been set up from yesterday, that too has been prepared for the king,” to burn his legions; (Isa. 10:16), “a burning shall burn.” Similar to this (below 41:11), “sparks of (כידודי) fire go forth”; either brands or flames.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־נָטָ֣ה אֶל־אֵ֣ל יָד֑וֹ וְאֶל־שַׁ֝דַּ֗י יִתְגַּבָּֽר
verses.translation: For he has raised his arm against GodAnd played the hero against the Almighty.

verses.hebrew_full: יָר֣וּץ אֵלָ֣יו בְּצַוָּ֑אר בַּ֝עֲבִ֗י גַּבֵּ֥י מָגִנָּֽיו
verses.translation: He runs at [God] defiantly With his thickly bossed shield.
verses.rashi: He runs towards Him He would run toward the Creator with his neck high and upright, in order to provoke Him. the thickness of the body of his shields With the power of his strength. the body of an expression of height, as (Ezek 16:24), “and you built yourself a platform (גב).”

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־כִסָּ֣ה פָנָ֣יו בְּחֶלְבּ֑וֹ וַיַּ֖עַשׂ פִּימָ֣ה עֲלֵי־כָֽסֶל
verses.translation: His face is covered with fatAnd his loins with blubber.
verses.rashi: collops of fat on his loins He made a mouth on his loins, for his fat and the thickness of his blubber is folded on his loins, and they look like a sort of mouth.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּשְׁכּ֤וֹן עָ֘רִ֤ים נִכְחָד֗וֹת בָּ֭תִּים לֹא־יֵ֣שְׁבוּ לָ֑מוֹ אֲשֶׁ֖ר הִתְעַתְּד֣וּ לְגַלִּֽים
verses.translation: He dwells in cities doomed to ruin,In houses that shall not be lived in,That are destined to become heaps of rubble.
verses.rashi: And he dwelt in ruined cities So is the custom of haughty people, to build ruins for a name.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹֽא־יֶ֭עְשַׁר וְלֹא־יָק֣וּם חֵיל֑וֹ וְלֹא־יִטֶּ֖ה לָאָ֣רֶץ מִנְלָֽם
verses.translation: He will not be rich;His wealth will not endure;His produce shall not bend to the earth.
verses.rashi: and his possessions will not remain They will not be preserved for long. neither will...bend Heb. יטה, [like] ינטה, like (I Sam. 8:3), “and they turned (ויטו) after gain.” They themselves turned (וינטו). It means that they did not bend from heaven to earth. the gratification of their desire Heb. מנלם, the granting of their desire and the fulfillment of their plans. It is like two words, מן להם. מן means their preparation, like (Dan. 1:5), “And the king prepared (וימן) for them.” Similar to it is (I Sam. 13:21), “a file (הפצירה פים),” in two words. [The analogy is obscure.] (Isa. 33:7), “Behold I will appear to them (אראלם),” which Jonathan renders: I will appear to them. So it appears to me, and Menachem supports me. Another explanation: מִנְלָם is an expression of (Isa. 33:1), “when you finish (כנלתך) dealing treacherously,” in which the “nun” is a radical that sometimes is omitted, an expression of completion. It will not bend to the ground to be freed from a decree of destruction.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹֽא־יָס֨וּר מִנִּי־חֹ֗שֶׁךְ יֹ֭נַקְתּוֹ תְּיַבֵּ֣שׁ שַׁלְהָ֑בֶת וְ֝יָס֗וּר בְּר֣וּחַ פִּֽיו
verses.translation: He will never get away from the darkness;Flames will sear his shoots;He will pass away by the breath of God’s mouth.
verses.rashi: and he will turn away from the world. by the breath of His mouth Of the Ruler of the world. This is an ellipsis, Others interpret this to mean: because of the breath of his mouth that was guilty of speaking lies.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־יַאֲמֵ֣ן בַּשָּׁ֣ו נִתְעָ֑ה כִּי־שָׁ֝֗וְא תִּהְיֶ֥ה תְמוּרָתֽוֹ
verses.translation: He will not be trusted;He will be misled by falsehood,And falsehood will be his recompense.
verses.rashi: will not believe He who was misled by futility will not believe that his recompense and his toil shall be futility, like (Ruth 4:7), “and concerning exchange (התמורה).”

verses.hebrew_full: בְּֽלֹא־י֭וֹמוֹ תִּמָּלֵ֑א וְ֝כִפָּת֗וֹ לֹ֣א רַעֲנָֽנָה
verses.translation: He will wither before his time,His boughs never having flourished.
verses.rashi: Before his time his recompense will be complete [and will cease] from growing and prospering anymore, like (Gen. 50:3), “were fulfilled for him,” i.e., they were completed. and his branches His bent-over branches. All branches of a tree resemble a dome.

verses.hebrew_full: יַחְמֹ֣ס כַּגֶּ֣פֶן בִּסְר֑וֹ וְיַשְׁלֵ֥ךְ כַּ֝זַּ֗יִת נִצָּתֽוֹ
verses.translation: He will drop his unripe grapes like a vine;He will shed his blossoms like an olive tree.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־עֲדַ֣ת חָנֵ֣ף גַּלְמ֑וּד וְ֝אֵ֗שׁ אָכְלָ֥ה אׇהֳלֵי־שֹֽׁחַד
verses.translation: For the company of the impious is desolate;Fire consumes the tents of the briber;

verses.hebrew_full: הָרֹ֣ה עָ֭מָל וְיָ֣לֹֽד אָ֑וֶן וּ֝בִטְנָ֗ם תָּכִ֥ין מִרְמָֽה
verses.translation: For they have conceived mischief, given birth to evil,And their womb has produced deceit.
verses.rashi: They conceive trouble and bear iniquity They commit evil and are recompensed. The recompense of the birth is in accordance with the conception. prepares makes ready. deceit Their desire shall be converted for them into deceit.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֥עַן אִיּ֗וֹב וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Job said in reply:

verses.hebrew_full: שָׁמַ֣עְתִּי כְאֵ֣לֶּה רַבּ֑וֹת מְנַחֲמֵ֖י עָמָ֣ל כֻּלְּכֶֽם
verses.translation: I have often heard such things;You are all mischievous comforters.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲקֵ֥ץ לְדִבְרֵי־ר֑וּחַ א֥וֹ מַה־יַּ֝מְרִֽיצְךָ֗ כִּ֣י תַֽעֲנֶֽה
verses.translation: Have windy words no limit?What afflicts you that you speak on?
verses.rashi: Job’s Replywill bring clarity to you Heb. ימרצך. Your mouth will make you evaluate to think clear words with proper basis.

words.eng: interr pron·to be or make sick

verses.hebrew_full: גַּ֤ם אָנֹכִי֮ כָּכֶ֢ם אֲדַ֫בֵּ֥רָה ל֤וּ יֵ֪שׁ נַפְשְׁכֶ֡ם תַּ֤חַת נַפְשִׁ֗י אַחְבִּ֣ירָה עֲלֵיכֶ֣ם בְּמִלִּ֑ים וְאָנִ֥יעָה עֲ֝לֵיכֶ֗ם בְּמ֣וֹ רֹאשִֽׁי
verses.translation: I would also talk like youIf you were in my place;I would barrage you with words,I would wag my head over you.
verses.rashi: could speak like you I know how to chide as you do. if your soul were in pain instead of my soul, I too would join words together against you and shake my head at you in your cry, and encourage you with my mouth, saying, “Be still, have courage, and endure your sufferings,” just as you are saying to me.

verses.hebrew_full: אֲאַמִּצְכֶ֥ם בְּמוֹ־פִ֑י וְנִ֖יד שְׂפָתַ֣י יַחְשֹֽׂךְ
verses.translation: I would encourage you with advice, My moving lips would bring relief.
verses.rashi: and the movement of my lips which I move and [with which I] complain now and cry, would then stop, and I would no longer cry or lament, but now...

verses.hebrew_full: אִֽם־אֲ֭דַבְּרָה לֹא־יֵחָשֵׂ֣ךְ כְּאֵבִ֑י וְ֝אַחְדְּלָ֗ה מַה־מִּנִּ֥י יַהֲלֹֽךְ
verses.translation: If I speak, my pain will not be relieved,And if I do not—what have I lost?
