verses.rashi: Should I speak etc.

verses.hebrew_full: אַךְ־עַתָּ֥ה הֶלְאָ֑נִי הֲ֝שִׁמּ֗וֹתָ כׇּל־עֲדָתִֽי
verses.translation: Now [God] has truly worn me out;You have destroyed my whole community.
verses.rashi: you have silenced Heb. השמות, you have silenced, like (Dan. 4:16), “was appalled (אשתומם),” and like (Jer. 2:12), “O heavens, be astonished (שמו).” Be silent and wonder.

verses.hebrew_full: וַֽ֭תִּקְמְטֵנִי לְעֵ֣ד הָיָ֑ה וַיָּ֥קׇם בִּ֥י כַ֝חֲשִׁ֗י בְּפָנַ֥י יַעֲנֶֽה
verses.translation: You have shriveled me;My gauntness serves as a witness,And testifies against me.
verses.rashi: You have shriveled me up; it has become a witness You have shriveled me up and blackened me for a witness, for it has become a witness to my iniquity, to accuse me.

verses.hebrew_full: אַפּ֤וֹ טָרַ֨ף וַֽיִּשְׂטְמֵ֗נִי חָרַ֣ק עָלַ֣י בְּשִׁנָּ֑יו צָרִ֓י יִלְטֹ֖שׁ עֵינָ֣יו לִֽי
verses.translation: By God’s anger I am torn and persecuted;With teeth gnashing at me,My Foe stabs me with a stare.
verses.rashi: My Adversary Satan is the adversary. sharpens Heb. ילטוש, sharpens, like (Gen. 4:22), “who sharpened (לטש) every instrument etc.”

verses.hebrew_full: פָּעֲר֬וּ עָלַ֨י בְּפִיהֶ֗ם בְּ֭חֶרְפָּה הִכּ֣וּ לְחָיָ֑י יַ֝֗חַד עָלַ֥י יִתְמַלָּאֽוּן
verses.translation: They open wide their mouths at me;Reviling me, they strike my cheeks;They inflame themselves against me.
verses.rashi: They open their mouth at me Heb. פערו, they open, like (Isa. 5:14), “and opened (ופערה) its mouth.” they assemble Heb. יתמלאון, they gather, and there are many similar words in Scripture, e.g. (Jer. 12:6), “have called a gang (מלא) after you”; (Isa. 31:4), “although a band (מלא) of shepherds gather against him,” but it does not deviate from its original meaning because every filling (מלוי) is a gathering together.

verses.hebrew_full: יַסְגִּירֵ֣נִי אֵ֭ל אֶ֣ל עֲוִ֑יל וְעַל־יְדֵ֖י רְשָׁעִ֣ים יִרְטֵֽנִי
verses.translation: God hands me over to an evildoer,Thrusts me into the clutches of the wicked.
verses.rashi: God delivers me to a fool The Holy One, blessed be He, delivered me to a fool, a scorner, a mocker, to be scornful of me. and heals me through the hands of the wicked Heb. ירטני. The bandage of His consolations and my healing He delivered to the wicked, who only provoke. Another explanation: יַרְטֵנִי is like יַרְצֵנִי, He placates me, an expression of placating and consolation

words.eng: young male or man or boy

verses.hebrew_full: שָׁ֘לֵ֤ו הָיִ֨יתִי וַֽיְפַרְפְּרֵ֗נִי וְאָחַ֣ז בְּ֭עׇרְפִּי וַֽיְפַצְפְּצֵ֑נִי וַיְקִימֵ֥נִי ל֝֗וֹ לְמַטָּרָֽה
verses.translation: I had been untroubled, and [God] broke me in pieces—Taking me by the scruff and shattering me,Setting me up as a target,
verses.rashi: but he crumbled me to pieces Heb. ויפרפני. That fool [crumbled me]. [It is] an expression of crumbs (פרורין). and shattered me Heb. ויפצפצני, an expression of something being broken into many fragments with a hard blow, like (Jer. 13:14), “And I will dash them (ונפצתים), one against the other”; (Ps. 137:90), “and dash (ונפץ) your little ones against the rock. “

verses.hebrew_full: יָ֘סֹ֤בּוּ עָלַ֨י רַבָּ֗יו יְפַלַּ֣ח כִּ֭לְיוֹתַי וְלֹ֣א יַחְמֹ֑ל יִשְׁפֹּ֥ךְ לָ֝אָ֗רֶץ מְרֵרָתִֽי
verses.translation: As archers surrounded me;My kidneys were pierced; I was shown no mercy;My bile spilled onto the ground.
verses.rashi: His archers רביו, the one who shoots his arrows, as in (Gen. 21:20), “and he became an archer (רבה קשת).” my gall Heb. מְרֵירָתִי, my gall.

verses.hebrew_full: יִפְרְצֵ֣נִי פֶ֭רֶץ עַל־פְּנֵי־פָ֑רֶץ יָרֻ֖ץ עָלַ֣י כְּגִבּֽוֹר
verses.translation: [God] breached me, breach after breach,Rushing at me like a warrior.

words.eng: to break through or down or over

verses.hebrew_full: שַׂ֣ק תָּ֭פַרְתִּי עֲלֵ֣י גִלְדִּ֑י וְעֹלַ֖לְתִּי בֶעָפָ֣ר קַרְנִֽי
verses.translation: I sewed sackcloth over my skin;I buried my glory in the dust.
verses.rashi: my scab Heb. גלדי, a dry wound that forms a crust, and [in] the language of the Mishnah (Rosh Hashanah 27b): He scraped it (the shofar) down to a thin crust (גלדו). and sullied Heb. ועללתי, and I sullied, like (Jud. 19:25), “and abused (ויתעללו) her,” an expression of disgrace and abasement, and like (Lam. 3:51), “sullies (עוללה) my soul.” my radiance Heb. קרני, my radiance, an expression of (Habakkuk 3:4): “rays of light (קרנים) came to them from his hand.”

verses.hebrew_full: פָּנַ֣י (חמרמרה) חֳ֭מַרְמְרוּ מִנִּי־בֶ֑כִי וְעַ֖ל עַפְעַפַּ֣י צַלְמָֽוֶת
verses.translation: My face is red with weeping;Darkness covers my eyes
verses.rashi: became shriveled Heb. חמרמרו, like (Chullin 3:3), “and its internal organs became shriveled (ונחמרו),” in the language of the Mishnah. Similarly (Lam. 1:20), “my intestines have become shriveled (חמרמרו).” the shadow of death it became darkened from weeping and tears.

verses.hebrew_full: עַ֭ל לֹא־חָמָ֣ס בְּכַפָּ֑י וּֽתְפִלָּתִ֥י זַכָּֽה
verses.translation: For no injustice on my partAnd for the purity of my prayer!
verses.rashi: and my prayer is pure I did not curse my friend nor did I think any evil against him.

verses.hebrew_full: אֶ֭רֶץ אַל־תְּכַסִּ֣י דָמִ֑י וְֽאַל־יְהִ֥י מָ֝ק֗וֹם לְזַעֲקָתִֽי
verses.translation: Earth, do not cover my blood;Let there be no resting place for my outcry!
verses.rashi: and let there be no place in the earth to absorb my cry, but it should ascend to heaven.

verses.hebrew_full: גַּם־עַ֭תָּה הִנֵּה־בַשָּׁמַ֣יִם עֵדִ֑י וְ֝שָׂהֲדִ֗י בַּמְּרֹמִֽים
verses.translation: Surely now my witness is in heaven;The One who can testify for me is on high.
verses.rashi: behold my Witness is in heaven My Creator, Who knows my ways.

verses.hebrew_full: מְלִיצַ֥י רֵעָ֑י אֶל־אֱ֝ל֗וֹהַּ דָּלְפָ֥ה עֵינִֽי
verses.translation: O my advocates, my fellows,Before God my eyes shed tears;
verses.rashi: pours out Heb. דלפה, tears and sprays tears, an expression of dripping (דלוף).

verses.hebrew_full: וְיוֹכַ֣ח לְגֶ֣בֶר עִם־אֱל֑וֹהַּ וּֽבֶן־אָדָ֥ם לְרֵעֵֽהוּ
verses.translation: Let arbitration be made between a person and GodAs between one mortal and another.
verses.rashi: Would that a man could reason If only He would do this for me, that He would give me a place so that a man could reason with God, to liken the two contentions: the contention of man with his Maker, and the contention of man with his fellow.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־שְׁנ֣וֹת מִסְפָּ֣ר יֶאֱתָ֑יוּ וְאֹ֖רַח לֹא־אָשׁ֣וּב אֶהֱלֹֽךְ
verses.translation: For a few more years will pass,And I shall go the way of no return.
verses.rashi: For numbered [years] The limited number of the days of my life. will come Will arrive, and then I will go on a way from which I will not return.

verses.hebrew_full: רוּחִ֣י חֻ֭בָּלָה יָמַ֥י נִזְעָ֗כוּ קְבָרִ֥ים לִֽי
verses.translation: My spirit is crushed, my days run out;The graveyard waits for me.
verses.rashi: flicker Heb. נזעכו have sprung away, like (above 6:17) “they jump (נדעכו).” graves are ready for me I am ready for the grave.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־לֹ֣א הֲ֭תֻלִים עִמָּדִ֑י וּ֝בְהַמְּרוֹתָ֗ם תָּלַ֥ן עֵינִֽי
verses.translation: Surely mockers keep me company,And with their provocations I close my eyes.
verses.rashi: even if there were no [Even if] this had not befallen me, that scornful people had gathered with me in my consolations to mock me. in their provocation Heb. ובהמרותם, like (Ezek. 20:8, 13, 21), “And they provoked (וימרו) me”; (Deut. 9:7), “you have been provoking (ממרים).” [This is] an expression meaning: in their provocation. my eye...abide My eye, my heart, and my thought abide in them.

verses.hebrew_full: שִֽׂימָה־נָּ֭א עׇרְבֵ֣נִי עִמָּ֑ךְ מִ֥י ה֝֗וּא לְיָדִ֥י יִתָּקֵֽעַ
verses.translation: Come now, stand surety for me!Who will give a hand in my behalf?
verses.rashi: Pay heed now O Creator. give me surety by shaking the hands to debate together, for who is it of these who will shake my hand that it should be good for me?

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־לִ֭בָּם צָפַ֣נְתָּ מִשָּׂ֑כֶל עַל־כֵּ֝֗ן לֹ֣א תְרֹמֵֽם
verses.translation: You have hidden understanding from their minds;Therefore You must not exalt [them].
verses.rashi: For Behold. You have hidden their hearts from understanding Therefore, Your glory will not be exalted through them.

words.eng: Tsaf’nath. Gitt. 58ᵃ צ׳ בת פניאל שמה her name was Ts.

verses.hebrew_full: לְ֭חֵלֶק יַגִּ֣יד רֵעִ֑ים וְעֵינֵ֖י בָנָ֣יו תִּכְלֶֽנָה
verses.translation: One informs on friends for a share [of their property],And the eyes of one’s children pine away.
verses.rashi: flattery Heb. לְחֵלֶק, flattery (חלקלקות). speaks Each one of them [speaks] to his friend, and this will come to them: their children’s eyes will fail.

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽ֭הִצִּיגַנִי לִמְשֹׁ֣ל עַמִּ֑ים וְתֹ֖פֶת לְפָנִ֣ים אֶהְיֶֽה
verses.translation: [God] made me a byword among people;I have become like Topheth of old.
verses.rashi: and...a drum Heb. תפת, like תּוֹף, a drum. before them lit. to the face; [i.e., to the face] of the people, I am [a drum].

verses.hebrew_full: וַתֵּ֣כַהּ מִכַּ֣עַשׂ עֵינִ֑י וִיצֻרַ֖י כַּצֵּ֣ל כֻּלָּֽם
verses.translation: My eyes fail from vexation;All shapes seem to me like shadows.
verses.rashi: and my limbs namely, the limbs with which I was formed.

verses.hebrew_full: יָשֹׁ֣מּוּ יְשָׁרִ֣ים עַל־זֹ֑את וְ֝נָקִ֗י עַל־חָנֵ֥ף יִתְעֹרָֽר
verses.translation: The upright are amazed at this;The pure are aroused against the impious.
verses.rashi: will be appalled they will wonder. and an innocent one If there is a righteous [man] who hears the mockery of these hypocrites. will be aroused to quarrel with you.

verses.hebrew_full: וְיֹאחֵ֣ז צַדִּ֣יק דַּרְכּ֑וֹ וּֽטְהׇר־יָ֝דַ֗יִם יֹסִ֥יף אֹֽמֶץ
verses.translation: The righteous hold to their way;They whose hands are pure grow stronger.
verses.rashi: Yet the righteous holds on his way i.e., every righteous man holds on his way to stand up against the company of the hypocrites.

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽאוּלָ֗ם כֻּלָּ֣ם תָּ֭שֻׁבוּ וּבֹ֣אוּ נָ֑א וְלֹֽא־אֶמְצָ֖א בָכֶ֣ם חָכָֽם
verses.translation: But all of you, come back now;I shall not find anyone wise among you.
verses.rashi: But all of them I say to you, “Return now and come now.” and I will not find any wise man among you insofar as you mock my lamentation.

verses.hebrew_full: יָמַ֣י עָ֭בְרוּ זִמֹּתַ֣י נִתְּק֑וּ מ֖וֹרָשֵׁ֣י לְבָבִֽי
verses.translation: My days are done, my tendons severed,The strings of my heart.
verses.rashi: My days etc. Behold, the days of my prosperity, to which I became accustomed, have passed. my purposes Which I had expected to last with prosperity. the thoughts of my heart have been broken off Heb. מורשי, lit. the inheritances of. The thoughts which my heart gave me to possess, for my heart said that I would inherit good because of my [righteous] way.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through
to pull or tear or draw off or away or apart

verses.hebrew_full: לַ֭יְלָה לְי֣וֹם יָשִׂ֑ימוּ א֝֗וֹר קָר֥וֹב מִפְּנֵי־חֹֽשֶׁךְ
verses.translation: They say that night is day,That light is here—in the face of darkness.
verses.rashi: They turn night into day These mockers turn night into day for me, for I cannot sleep at night because of the trouble of the mockings. the light is short lit. קרוב, near, and short for me because of the trouble of the darkness of the night. The light is short; when the sun sets, it appears to me as though a short time ago the day dawned, was shortened, and left. This is like (Gen. 19:20), “this city is recent (קרובה)” it was newly settled recently and like (below 20:5), “That the triumph of the wicked is short (מקרוב),” and like (Deut. 32:17), “new ones that came up of late (מקרוב).”

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־אֲ֭קַוֶּה שְׁא֣וֹל בֵּיתִ֑י בַּ֝חֹ֗שֶׁךְ רִפַּ֥דְתִּי יְצוּעָֽי
verses.translation: If I must look forward to Sheol as my home,And make my bed in the dark place,
verses.rashi: Since I hope for the grave Heb. אם, like (above 14:5), “If (אם) his days are limited” [See Rashi ad loc.], i.e., since I hope for the grave as my house. in the darkness I have spread out my couch I have spread out my couch in the grave.

verses.hebrew_full: לַשַּׁ֣חַת קָ֭רָאתִי אָ֣בִי אָ֑תָּה אִמִּ֥י וַ֝אֲחֹתִ֗י לָרִמָּֽה
verses.translation: Say to the Pit, “You are my father,”To the maggots, “Mother,” “Sister”—
verses.rashi: To the pit I have called, ‘You are my father,’ to lodge there many days as though it was my father, since this is my hope.

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֭אַיֵּה אֵפ֣וֹ תִקְוָתִ֑י וְ֝תִקְוָתִ֗י מִ֣י יְשׁוּרֶֽנָּה
verses.translation: Where, then, is my hope?Who can see hope for me?
verses.rashi: Where then is my hope [Where then is] this [my hope]? Why is it deferred, and who will see it to hasten it to me?

verses.hebrew_full: בַּדֵּ֣י שְׁאֹ֣ל תֵּרַ֑דְנָה אִם־יַ֖חַד עַל־עָפָ֣ר נָֽחַת
verses.translation: Will it descend to Sheol?Shall we go down together to the dust?
verses.rashi: The limbs of the grave shall descend Those limbs [of my body] that will ultimately descend to the grave, for indeed they will all rest on the dust. they rest on the dust Heb. נחת, will lodge, an expression of (Num. 11:26), “the spirit rested (ותנח) upon them.”

verses.hebrew_full: וַ֭יַּעַן בִּלְדַּ֥ד הַשֻּׁחִ֗י וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Then Bildad the Shuhite said in reply:

verses.hebrew_full: עַד־אָ֤נָה תְּשִׂימ֣וּן קִנְצֵ֣י לְמִלִּ֑ין תָּ֝בִ֗ינוּ וְאַחַ֥ר נְדַבֵּֽר
verses.translation: How long? Put an end to talk!Consider, and then we shall speak.
verses.rashi: Bildad’s reply“How long?” will you prolong the discussion? Consider Be silent to understand and to hear what we will speak.

verses.hebrew_full: מַ֭דּוּעַ נֶחְשַׁ֣בְנוּ כַבְּהֵמָ֑ה נִ֝טְמִ֗ינוּ בְּעֵינֵיכֶֽם
verses.translation: Why are we thought of as brutes,Regarded by you as stupid?
verses.rashi: and reputed dull in your sight Heb. נטמינו. We are considered in your eyes as stopped up. The word has no radical besides the “teth” “mem,” like טַמוֹנוּן פְלִשְתָּאֵי, which is the Aramaic translation of סִתְּמוּם פלשתים “the Philistines stopped them up” (Gen. 26:15). Some explain [this] as an expression of hiding (טמינה), but they are in error, because were that the case, the word would require two “nun”s, one for the radical and one for the plural suffix.

verses.hebrew_full: טֹרֵ֥ף נַפְשׁ֗וֹ בְּאַ֫פּ֥וֹ הַֽ֭לְמַעַנְךָ תֵּעָ֣זַב אָ֑רֶץ וְיֶעְתַּק־צ֝֗וּר מִמְּקֹמֽוֹ
verses.translation: You who tear yourself to pieces in anger—Will earth’s order be disrupted for your sake?Will rocks be dislodged from their place?
verses.rashi: One who destroys his soul He turns to Job, “To you I say, the one who destroys his soul in his anger and in his wrath.” for your sake For the sake of your innocence shall the earth be abandoned by its standard of justice? and shall the Rock be removed The Creator. from His place From His knowledge and from His nature.

verses.hebrew_full: גַּ֤ם א֣וֹר רְשָׁעִ֣ים יִדְעָ֑ךְ וְלֹא־יִ֝גַּ֗הּ שְׁבִ֣יב אִשּֽׁוֹ
verses.translation: Indeed, the light of the wicked fails;The flame of his fire does not shine.
verses.rashi: Yea Even now, according to your cry, this will be in force for generations. the light of the wicked shall be put out it shall spring from its place. spark Etinceler in French.

verses.hebrew_full: א֭וֹר חָשַׁ֣ךְ בְּאׇהֳל֑וֹ וְ֝נֵר֗וֹ עָלָ֥יו יִדְעָֽךְ
verses.translation: The light in his tent darkens;His lamp fails him.

verses.hebrew_full: יֵ֭צְרוּ צַעֲדֵ֣י אוֹנ֑וֹ וְֽתַשְׁלִיכֵ֥הוּ עֲצָתֽוֹ
verses.translation: His iniquitous strides are hobbled;His schemes overthrow him.
verses.rashi: shall be straitened They shall be short. The steps of The steps of his strength. shall cast him down His counsel that he advised, which will not be fulfilled [will cast him down].

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־שֻׁלַּ֣ח בְּרֶ֣שֶׁת בְּרַגְלָ֑יו וְעַל־שְׂ֝בָכָ֗ה יִתְהַלָּֽךְ
verses.translation: He is led by his feet into the net;He walks onto the toils.
verses.rashi: For his end will be that... he shall be sent into a net His feet will be sent to step on a net and be caught. and he shall walk on the toils Heb. שבכה. Every שבכה is made like a net, and that is the coif that is on the head of women.

verses.hebrew_full: יֹאחֵ֣ז בְּעָקֵ֣ב פָּ֑ח יַחֲזֵ֖ק עָלָ֣יו צַמִּֽים
verses.translation: The trap seizes his heel;The noose tightens on him.
verses.rashi: shall seize A trap shall seize him by the heel.

verses.hebrew_full: טָמ֣וּן בָּאָ֣רֶץ חַבְל֑וֹ וּ֝מַלְכֻּדְתּ֗וֹ עֲלֵ֣י נָתִֽיב
verses.translation: The rope for him lies hidden on the ground;His snare, on the path.
verses.rashi: hidden...in the ground under his feet. A noose...for him The rope of his trap, with which he will be caught. and a trap for him is hidden on the path just as they hide the noose of the trap for birds.

verses.hebrew_full: סָ֭בִיב בִּעֲתֻ֣הוּ בַלָּה֑וֹת וֶהֱפִיצֻ֥הוּ לְרַגְלָֽיו
verses.translation: Terrors assault him on all sidesAnd send his feet flying.
verses.rashi: terrors Demons. and beat him off his fee They knock him down to the ground.

verses.hebrew_full: יְהִי־רָעֵ֥ב אֹנ֑וֹ וְ֝אֵ֗יד נָכ֥וֹן לְצַלְעֽוֹ
verses.translation: His progeny hunger;Disaster awaits his wife.
verses.rashi: His son Heb. אֹנוֹ, lit, his strength. for his wife Heb. לצלעוֹ lit. his rib [alluding to Eve].

verses.hebrew_full: יֹ֭אכַל בַּדֵּ֣י עוֹר֑וֹ יֹאכַ֥ל בַּ֝דָּ֗יו בְּכ֣וֹר מָֽוֶת
verses.translation: The tendons under his skin are consumed;Death’s first-born consumes his tendons.
verses.rashi: He shall eat the branches of his body And he shall devour all the rest of his branches, viz. his sons and daughters. the prince of death Heb. בכור מות, as in (Ps. 89:28), “Also I will make him a prince (בכור).”

verses.hebrew_full: יִנָּתֵ֣ק מֵ֭אׇהֳלוֹ מִבְטַח֑וֹ וְ֝תַצְעִדֵ֗הוּ לְמֶ֣לֶךְ בַּלָּהֽוֹת
verses.translation: He is torn from the safety of his tent;Terror marches him to the king.
verses.rashi: He shall be torn away from his wife Heb. מאהלו, lit. from his tent. who trusted him Heb. מבטחו, his trust, who trusted him. (His trust), that she trusted him. she will cause him to walk. yet she will cause him to walk and will send him away (from her) to the grave, to the king of the demons.

words.eng: to pull or tear or draw off or away or apart

verses.hebrew_full: תִּשְׁכּ֣וֹן בְּ֭אׇהֳלוֹ מִבְּלִיל֑וֹ יְזֹרֶ֖ה עַל־נָוֵ֣הוּ גׇפְרִֽית
verses.translation: It lodges in his desolate tent;Sulfur is strewn upon his home.
verses.rashi: She shall dwell in his tent as a widow. not being his (that she will not be his) because he is dead, and ultimately brimstone shall be scattered on his dwelling.

verses.hebrew_full: מִ֭תַּחַת שׇׁרָשָׁ֣יו יִבָ֑שׁוּ וּ֝מִמַּ֗עַל יִמַּ֥ל קְצִירֽוֹ
verses.translation: His roots below dry up,And above, his branches wither.

verses.hebrew_full: זִֽכְרוֹ־אָ֭בַד מִנִּי־אָ֑רֶץ וְלֹא־שֵׁ֥ם ל֝֗וֹ עַל־פְּנֵי־חֽוּץ
verses.translation: All mention of him vanishes from the earth;He has no name abroad.

verses.hebrew_full: יֶ֭הְדְּפֻהוּ מֵא֣וֹר אֶל־חֹ֑שֶׁךְ וּֽמִתֵּבֵ֥ל יְנִדֻּֽהוּ
verses.translation: He is thrust from light to darkness,Driven from the world.
verses.rashi: They shall drive him from heaven.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹ֘א־נִ֤ין ל֣וֹ וְלֹא־נֶ֣כֶד בְּעַמּ֑וֹ וְאֵ֥ין שָׂ֝רִ֗יד בִּמְגוּרָֽיו
verses.translation: He has no seed or breed among his people,No survivor where he once lived.
verses.rashi: grandson Heb. נכד, a son of the son.

verses.hebrew_full: עַל־י֭וֹמוֹ נָשַׁ֣מּוּ אַחֲרֹנִ֑ים וְ֝קַדְמֹנִ֗ים אָ֣חֲזוּ שָֽׂעַר
verses.translation: Generations to come will be appalled at his fate,As the previous ones are seized with horror.
verses.rashi: the later people They wondered when they heard of the misfortune that befell him. quaking Heb. שָׂעַר, tempest. This cannot be of the form of שֵׂעָר, hair, because the accent in שָׂעַר is not (on the “sin,” but on the “ayin,”) and it is not converted to a “kamatz” when it is cantillated with an “ethnachta” or a “sof pasuk.” I did not hear this.

verses.hebrew_full: אַךְ־אֵ֭לֶּה מִשְׁכְּנ֣וֹת עַוָּ֑ל וְ֝זֶ֗ה מְק֣וֹם לֹֽא־יָדַֽע־אֵֽל
verses.translation: “These were the haunts of the wicked;Here was the place of someone who knew not God.”
verses.rashi: These are only the dwellings of the unrighteous This is their fate.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֥עַן אִיּ֗וֹב וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Job said in reply:

verses.hebrew_full: עַד־אָ֭נָה תּוֹגְי֣וּן נַפְשִׁ֑י וּֽתְדַכּאוּנַ֥נִי בְמִלִּֽים
verses.translation: How long will you grieve my spirit,And crush me with words?
verses.rashi: Job’s Replywill you grieve Heb. תגיון, an expression of grief (תוגה).

verses.hebrew_full: זֶ֤ה עֶ֣שֶׂר פְּ֭עָמִים תַּכְלִימ֑וּנִי לֹֽא־תֵ֝בֹ֗שׁוּ תַּהְכְּרוּ־לִֽי
verses.translation: Time and again you humiliate me,And are not ashamed to abuse me.
verses.rashi: ten times There are ten chapters until here.

words.eng: to deal wrongly with·inflected pers. pron. meaning ‘to me’.

verses.hebrew_full: וְאַף־אׇמְנָ֥ם שָׁגִ֑יתִי אִ֝תִּ֗י תָּלִ֥ין מְשׁוּגָתִֽי
verses.translation: If indeed I have erred,My error remains with me.
verses.rashi: stay with me I am the one who receives blows for my error.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־אׇ֭מְנָם עָלַ֣י תַּגְדִּ֑ילוּ וְתוֹכִ֥יחוּ עָ֝לַ֗י חֶרְפָּתִֽי
verses.translation: Though you are overbearing toward me,Reproaching me with my disgrace,
verses.rashi: If indeed you are added upon me to my trouble. and prove against me my reproach You show and prove my reproach to my face.

verses.hebrew_full: דְּֽעוּ־אֵ֭פוֹ כִּֽי־אֱל֣וֹהַּ עִוְּתָ֑נִי וּ֝מְצוּד֗וֹ עָלַ֥י הִקִּֽיף
verses.translation: Yet know that God has wronged me,And has thrown up siege works around me.

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֤ן אֶצְעַ֣ק חָ֭מָס וְלֹ֣א אֵעָנֶ֑ה אֲ֝שַׁוַּ֗ע וְאֵ֣ין מִשְׁפָּֽט
verses.translation: I cry, “Violence!” but am not answered;I shout, but can get no justice.
verses.rashi: I cry out concerning violence but I am not answered.

verses.hebrew_full: אׇרְחִ֣י גָ֭דַר וְלֹ֣א אֶעֱב֑וֹר וְעַ֥ל נְ֝תִיבוֹתַ֗י חֹ֣שֶׁךְ יָשִֽׂים
verses.translation: My way has been barred; I cannot pass;Darkness has been laid upon my path.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: כְּ֭בוֹדִי מֵעָלַ֣י הִפְשִׁ֑יט וַ֝יָּ֗סַר עֲטֶ֣רֶת רֹאשִֽׁי
verses.translation: My glory has been stripped from me,The crown removed from my head.

verses.hebrew_full: יִתְּצֵ֣נִי סָ֭בִיב וָאֵלַ֑ךְ וַיַּסַּ֥ע כָּ֝עֵ֗ץ תִּקְוָתִֽי
verses.translation: [God] tears down every part of me; I perish;My hopes are uprooted like a tree.
verses.rashi: he has uprooted Heb. ויסע, an expression of uprooting.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֣חַר עָלַ֣י אַפּ֑וֹ וַיַּחְשְׁבֵ֖נִי ל֣וֹ כְצָרָֽיו
verses.translation: God’s anger is kindled against me;I am regarded as a foe.

verses.hebrew_full: יַ֤חַד יָ֘בֹ֤אוּ גְדוּדָ֗יו וַיָּסֹ֣לּוּ עָלַ֣י דַּרְכָּ֑ם וַיַּחֲנ֖וּ סָבִ֣יב לְאׇהֳלִֽי
verses.translation: Heavenly troops advance together;They build their road toward meAnd encamp around my tent.
verses.rashi: they build their road Heb. ויסלו, an expression of a highway (מסלה).

verses.hebrew_full: אַ֭חַי מֵעָלַ֣י הִרְחִ֑יק וְ֝יֹדְעַ֗י אַךְ־זָ֥רוּ מִמֶּֽנִּי
verses.translation: My kin have been alienated from me;My acquaintances disown me.
verses.rashi: became strange Heb. אך זרו, an expression of cruelty.

verses.hebrew_full: חָדְל֥וּ קְרוֹבָ֑י וּֽמְיֻדָּעַ֥י שְׁכֵחֽוּנִי
verses.translation: My relatives are gone;My friends have forgotten me.

verses.hebrew_full: גָּ֘רֵ֤י בֵיתִ֣י וְ֭אַמְהֹתַי לְזָ֣ר תַּחְשְׁבֻ֑נִי נׇ֝כְרִ֗י הָיִ֥יתִי בְעֵינֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: My dependents and maidservants regard me as a stranger;I am an outsider to them.

verses.hebrew_full: לְעַבְדִּ֣י קָ֭רָאתִי וְלֹ֣א יַעֲנֶ֑ה בְּמוֹ־פִ֝֗י אֶתְחַנֶּן־לֽוֹ
verses.translation: I summon my servant but he does not respond;I must myself entreat him.

words.eng: to be gracious·unto him; v. ל־.

verses.hebrew_full: ר֭וּחִי זָ֣רָה לְאִשְׁתִּ֑י וְ֝חַנֹּתִ֗י לִבְנֵ֥י בִטְנִֽי
verses.translation: My odor is repulsive to my wife;I am loathsome to my children.
verses.rashi: to the children of my body To those who I raised in my house as though they were my own children.

verses.hebrew_full: גַּם־עֲ֭וִילִים מָ֣אֲסוּ בִ֑י אָ֝ק֗וּמָה וַיְדַבְּרוּ־בִֽי
verses.translation: Even youngsters disdain me;When I rise, they speak against me.
verses.rashi: children despised me I was despised in the eyes of children, all the more so in the eyes of princes.

words.eng: also·young male or man or boy

verses.hebrew_full: תִּ֭עֲבוּנִי כׇּל־מְתֵ֣י סוֹדִ֑י וְזֶה־אָ֝הַ֗בְתִּי נֶהְפְּכוּ־בִֽי
verses.translation: All my bosom friends detest me;Those I love have turned against me.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּעוֹרִ֣י וּ֭בִבְשָׂרִי דָּבְקָ֣ה עַצְמִ֑י וָ֝אֶתְמַלְּטָ֗ה בְּע֣וֹר שִׁנָּֽי
verses.translation: My bones stick to my skin and flesh;I escape with the skin of my teeth.
verses.rashi: My bones cleaved because I am emaciated from thickness of flesh. by the skin of my teeth All his flesh was afflicted with boils and worms except his gums.

verses.hebrew_full: חׇנֻּ֬נִי חׇנֻּ֣נִי אַתֶּ֣ם רֵעָ֑י כִּ֥י יַד־אֱ֝ל֗וֹהַּ נָ֣גְעָה בִּֽי
verses.translation: Pity me, pity me! You are my friends;For the hand of God has struck me!

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: לָ֭מָּה תִּרְדְּפֻ֣נִי כְמוֹ־אֵ֑ל וּ֝מִבְּשָׂרִ֗י לֹ֣א תִשְׂבָּֽעוּ
verses.translation: Why do you pursue me like God,Maligning me insatiably?

verses.hebrew_full: מִי־יִתֵּ֣ן אֵ֭פוֹ וְיִכָּתְב֣וּן מִלָּ֑י מִי־יִתֵּ֖ן בַּסֵּ֣פֶר וְיֻחָֽקוּ
verses.translation: O that my words were written down;Would they were inscribed in a record,

verses.hebrew_full: בְּעֵט־בַּרְזֶ֥ל וְעֹפָ֑רֶת לָ֝עַ֗ד בַּצּ֥וּר יֵחָצְבֽוּן
verses.translation: Incised on a rock foreverWith iron stylus and lead!
verses.rashi: With an iron pen they should be inscribed in the rock, and afterwards they run lead over them to give the letters a black appearance, to make them recognizable. This is the practice of those who inscribe on stone. It cannot be explained to mean a lead pen because lead is soft in comparison with stone.

verses.hebrew_full: וַאֲנִ֣י יָ֭דַעְתִּי גֹּ֣אֲלִי חָ֑י וְ֝אַחֲר֗וֹן עַל־עָפָ֥ר יָקֽוּם
verses.translation: But I know that my Vindicator lives,And in the end will testify on earth—
verses.rashi: But I know that my Redeemer lives This “vav” refers to the above. You persecute me, but I know that my Redeemer lives to requite you, and He will endure and rise. and the last on the earth, He will endure After all earth dwellers will perish, He will endure last.

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: וְאַחַ֣ר ע֭וֹרִי נִקְּפוּ־זֹ֑את וּ֝מִבְּשָׂרִ֗י אֶחֱזֶ֥ה אֱלֽוֹהַּ
verses.translation: This, after my skin will have been peeled off.But I would behold God while still in my flesh,
verses.rashi: And after my skin, they have cut into this Yet they do not pay heed to my Redeemer, but after the plague of my skin, they cut, strike, and pierce. This vexation and persecution that I mentioned, which is to me like one cutting into my skin, like (Isa. 10:34): “And the thickets of the forests shall be cut off (ונקף).” and from my flesh I see judgments. judgment Heb. אלוה, an expression of judgment and chastisements.

verses.hebrew_full: אֲשֶׁ֤ר אֲנִ֨י אֶחֱזֶה־לִּ֗י וְעֵינַ֣י רָא֣וּ וְלֹא־זָ֑ר כָּל֖וּ כִלְיֹתַ֣י בְּחֵקִֽי
verses.translation: I myself, not another, would do so;Would see with my own eyes:My heart pines within me.

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֣י תֹ֭אמְרוּ מַה־נִּרְדׇּף־ל֑וֹ וְשֹׁ֥רֶשׁ דָּ֝בָ֗ר נִמְצָא־בִֽי
verses.translation: You say, “How do we persecute him?The root of the matter is in him.”
verses.rashi: Shall you say, ‘Why should we persecute him?’ Like (Deut. 7:17), “Will you say in your heart,” in which [כי] is an expression of a question. That is to say, “Will you lay your heart to the matter to have pity and say, ‘Why should we persecute him?’” and the root of the matter that is found in him—what is it? This favor will never enter your mind [to determine] the root of the matter for which he suffers with pains.

words.eng: interr pron·to be behind·unto him; v. ל־.

verses.hebrew_full: גּ֤וּרוּ לָכֶ֨ם מִפְּנֵי־חֶ֗רֶב כִּֽי־חֵ֭מָה עֲוֺנ֣וֹת חָ֑רֶב לְמַ֖עַן תֵּדְע֣וּן (שדין) שַׁדּֽוּן
verses.translation: Be in fear of the sword,For [your] fury is iniquity worthy of the sword;Know there is a judgment!
verses.rashi: for the wrath For you are committing much iniquity that will bring about the sword. in order that you know and understand the secret of the retribution that will come upon the wicked. Also, the mesorah is authoritative and שדין is written, as though it would say that there is justice (שיש דין) in the world, like (Jud. 5:7), “I, Deborah, arose (שַׁקַמְתִּי), [like שֶׁקַמְתִּי].” I saw this in Tanchuma (Mishpatim 3)

words.eng: inflected m. pers. pron. meaning ‘to you’.

verses.hebrew_full: וַ֭יַּעַן צֹפַ֥ר הַֽנַּעֲמָתִ֗י וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Zophar the Naamathite said in reply:

verses.hebrew_full: לָ֭כֵן שְׂעִפַּ֣י יְשִׁיב֑וּנִי וּ֝בַעֲב֗וּר ח֣וּשִׁי בִֽי
verses.translation: In truth, my thoughts urge me to answer(It is because of my feelings
verses.rashi: Zophar’s answermy thoughts will cause me to answer My thoughts will answer me with an answer in my mouth, and because I keep my peace and remain silent, and I put my silence within me, my thoughts will give me an answer to reply. The chastisement of my disgrace I hear from you the chastisement of my disgrace, that you disgrace me. Therefore, a word of spirit shall answer me out of my understanding, to reply to you. my silence Heb. חושי, my silence, like (Ecc. 3:7), “a time to be silent (לחשות).”

verses.hebrew_full: מוּסַ֣ר כְּלִמָּתִ֣י אֶשְׁמָ֑ע וְ֝ר֗וּחַ מִֽבִּינָתִ֥י יַעֲנֵֽנִי
verses.translation: When I hear reproof that insults me);A spirit out of my understanding makes me reply:

verses.hebrew_full: הֲזֹ֣את יָ֭דַעְתָּ מִנִּי־עַ֑ד מִנִּ֤י שִׂ֖ים אָדָ֣ם עֲלֵי־אָֽרֶץ
verses.translation: Do you not know this, that from time immemorial,Since humankind was set on earth,
verses.rashi: Do...this Heb. הזאת. This is a wonder. Do you know the manner in which this has been conducted throughout all generations since time immemorial?

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י רִנְנַ֣ת רְ֭שָׁעִים מִקָּר֑וֹב וְשִׂמְחַ֖ת חָנֵ֣ף עֲדֵי־רָֽגַע
verses.translation: The joy of the wicked has been brief,The happiness of the impious man, fleeting?
verses.rashi: that the triumph of the wicked throughout all its duration, has come but recently, and will not last for any length of time. and the joy of the flatterers is but a moment in a short time, it will end.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־יַעֲלֶ֣ה לַשָּׁמַ֣יִם שִׂיא֑וֹ וְ֝רֹאשׁ֗וֹ לָעָ֥ב יַגִּֽיעַ
verses.translation: Though he grows as high as the sky,His head reaching the clouds,

verses.hebrew_full: כְּֽ֭גֶלְלוֹ לָנֶ֣צַח יֹאבֵ֑ד רֹ֝אָ֗יו יֹאמְר֥וּ אַיּֽוֹ
verses.translation: He perishes forever, like his dung;Those who saw him will say, “Where is he?”
verses.rashi: his dung Heb. כְּגֶלְלוֹ, dung of excrement.

verses.hebrew_full: כַּחֲל֣וֹם יָ֭עוּף וְלֹ֣א יִמְצָא֑וּהוּ וְ֝יֻדַּ֗ד כְּחֶזְי֥וֹן לָֽיְלָה
verses.translation: He flies away like a dream and cannot be found;He is banished like a night vision.
verses.rashi: and they will not find him i.e., his friends.

verses.hebrew_full: עַ֣יִן שְׁ֭זָפַתּוּ וְלֹ֣א תוֹסִ֑יף וְלֹֽא־ע֝֗וֹד תְּשׁוּרֶ֥נּוּ מְקוֹמֽוֹ
verses.translation: Eyes that glimpsed him do so no more;They cannot see him in his place any longer.
verses.rashi: that glimpsed him That saw him.

verses.hebrew_full: בָּ֭נָיו יְרַצּ֣וּ דַלִּ֑ים וְ֝יָדָ֗יו תָּשֵׁ֥בְנָה אוֹנֽוֹ
verses.translation: His children ingratiate themselves with the poor;His own hands must give back his wealth.
verses.rashi: His sons will placate the poor This reply was stated in reference to the people of Sodom, who were robbers and were cruel to the poor. and his hands will return He will return the plunder of his hands. his plunder The power of his force.

verses.hebrew_full: עַ֭צְמוֹתָיו מָלְא֣וּ עֲלוּמָ֑ו וְ֝עִמּ֗וֹ עַל־עָפָ֥ר תִּשְׁכָּֽב
verses.translation: His bones, still full of vigor,Lie down in the dust with him.
verses.rashi: His bones are full of his youth The strength of his youth. and it will lie on the dust with him Because he will die suddenly with his strength.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־תַּמְתִּ֣יק בְּפִ֣יו רָעָ֑ה יַ֝כְחִידֶ֗נָּה תַּ֣חַת לְשֹׁנֽוֹ
verses.translation: Though evil is sweet to his taste,And he conceals it under his tongue;
verses.rashi: be sweet So is the custom of a wicked man: if evil is sweet in his mouth and he does not see now the moment for it to take effect. though he hide it under his tongue until the time comes when it will prosper.

words.eng: mother·to be or become sweet or pleasing

verses.hebrew_full: יַחְמֹ֣ל עָ֭לֶיהָ וְלֹ֣א יַעַזְבֶ֑נָּה וְ֝יִמְנָעֶ֗נָּה בְּת֣וֹךְ חִכּֽוֹ
verses.translation: Though he saves it, does not let it go,Holds it inside his mouth,

verses.hebrew_full: לַ֭חְמוֹ בְּמֵעָ֣יו נֶהְפָּ֑ךְ מְרוֹרַ֖ת פְּתָנִ֣ים בְּקִרְבּֽוֹ
verses.translation: His food in his bowels turnsInto asps’ venom within him.
verses.rashi: His food...within him on the day of his misfortune will turn into the venom of cobras.

verses.hebrew_full: חַ֣יִל בָּ֭לַע וַיְקִאֶ֑נּוּ מִ֝בִּטְנ֗וֹ יֹרִשֶׁ֥נּוּ אֵֽל
verses.translation: The riches he swallows he vomits;God empties it out of his stomach.
verses.rashi: wealth Heb. חיל, money.

verses.hebrew_full: רֹאשׁ־פְּתָנִ֥ים יִינָ֑ק תַּ֝הַרְגֵ֗הוּ לְשׁ֣וֹן אֶפְעֶֽה
verses.translation: He sucks the poison of asps;The tongue of the viper kills him.
verses.rashi: carpet viper Heb. אֶפְעֶה, a fiery serpent.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־יֵ֥רֶא בִפְלַגּ֑וֹת נַהֲרֵ֥י נַ֝חֲלֵ֗י דְּבַ֣שׁ וְחֶמְאָֽה
verses.translation: Let him not enjoy the streams,The rivers of honey, the brooks of cream.
verses.rashi: the rivulets, the flowing rivers of the Garden of Eden, which are as sweet as honey and cream.

verses.hebrew_full: מֵשִׁ֣יב יָ֭גָע וְלֹ֣א יִבְלָ֑ע כְּחֵ֥יל תְּ֝מוּרָת֗וֹ וְלֹ֣א יַעֲלֹֽס
verses.translation: He will give back the goods unswallowed;The value of the riches, undigested.
verses.rashi: He will return that which he labored for The loot that he stole. when his gain becomes vast wealth When his gain becomes vast wealth, then he will lose it. he will not rejoice Heb. יעלס, with it. Similarly, יַעֲלֹז, and so יַעַלֹץ ; the three of them are interchangeable.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־רִ֭צַּץ עָזַ֣ב דַּלִּ֑ים בַּ֥יִת גָּ֝זַ֗ל וְלֹ֣א יִבְנֵֽהוּ
verses.translation: Because he crushed and tortured the poor,He will not build up the house he took by force.
verses.rashi: When he oppressed, he abandoned the poor After he oppressed the poor, he abandoned the poor and was banished from the world and went his way, and they return and collect their stolen items from him. [The expression] כִּי רָצַץ [means] when he oppressed, like (Exod. 18:16), “Whenever (כי) they have a concern, they come to me,” which means when they have.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י לֹא־יָדַ֣ע שָׁלֵ֣ו בְּבִטְנ֑וֹ בַּ֝חֲמוּד֗וֹ לֹ֣א יְמַלֵּֽט
verses.translation: He will not see his children tranquil;He will not preserve one of his dear ones. and ḥamud, cf. Hos. 9.16.
verses.rashi: For he did not know [He] never [knew] his stomach to be at rest. His stomach always says to him, “Rob and bring [food] to fill me up.” Therefore... with his beauty With the flesh of his coveted beauty. he will not save anything because it will continuously diminish. Similar to this is (Ps. 39: 11): “You make his beauty melt away like a moth.”

verses.hebrew_full: אֵין־שָׂרִ֥יד לְאׇכְל֑וֹ עַל־כֵּ֝֗ן לֹא־יָחִ֥יל טוּבֽוֹ
verses.translation: With no survivor to enjoy it,His fortune will not prosper.
verses.rashi: Nothing remains of his food He left nothing over from his food to share with the poor. therefore his goods shall not prosper Heb. יחיל, shall not prosper, as in (Ps. 10:5), “His ways prosper (יחילו) .” He speaks of the people of Sodom, who were stingy with travelers, for so did Ezekiel testify (16:49) concerning Sodom: “proud with plenty of food etc. and yet, she did not strengthen the hand of the poor and needy.” And our Sages explained (Sanh. 92a) אין שריד לאכלוֹ to mean that he did not allow Torah scholars to sit at his table, like (Joel 3:5): “and among the survivors whom the Lord calls.”

verses.hebrew_full: בִּמְלֹ֣אות שִׂ֭פְקוֹ יֵ֣צֶר ל֑וֹ כׇּל־יַ֖ד עָמֵ֣ל תְּבֹאֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: When he has all he wants, trouble will come;Misfortunes of all kinds will batter him.
verses.rashi: When his desire has been filled sufficiently when the desire of his heart has been filled sufficiently, then... he will be in straits Trouble will come upon him. סִפְקוֹ is an expression of sufficiency, as is translated into Aramaic (Gen. 24:22): “When the camels finished (ספיקו) drinking”; (Num. 11:22): “so that it may be enough for them (היספקון).” the hand of every laborer will come upon him The hand of the poor shall overwhelm him and come upon him.

verses.hebrew_full: יְהִ֤י לְמַלֵּ֬א בִטְנ֗וֹ יְֽשַׁלַּח־בּ֭וֹ חֲר֣וֹן אַפּ֑וֹ וְיַמְטֵ֥ר עָ֝לֵ֗ימוֹ בִּלְחוּמֽוֹ
verses.translation: Let that fill his belly;Let [God] loose burning anger at him,And God’s weapons rain down upon him.
verses.rashi: It shall come to pass that to fill his belly, He shall send upon him His burning wrath The sending of His burning wrath will be [enough] to fill his belly. This is not an expression of a curse but an expression of prophecy and of the future. and rain upon them The Holy One, blessed be He. with His battle With the fury of His battle, coals, fire, and brimstone, as the matter is stated (Gen. 19: 24): “And the Lord rained upon Sodom etc.”

words.eng: to send·inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: יִ֭בְרַח מִנֵּ֣שֶׁק בַּרְזֶ֑ל תַּ֝חְלְפֵ֗הוּ קֶ֣שֶׁת נְחוּשָֽׁה
verses.translation: Fleeing from iron arrows,He is shot through from a bow of bronze.
verses.rashi: shall pierce him Heb. תַּחְלְפֵהוּ The arrows that the bow will shoot will pierce his body, going through from one side to the other. Another version: תַּחְלְפֵהוּ is like תַּחְליִפֵהוּ (sic), a copper bow shall confront him.

verses.hebrew_full: שָׁלַף֮ וַיֵּצֵ֢א מִגֵּ֫וָ֥ה וּ֭בָרָק מִֽמְּרֹרָת֥וֹ יַהֲלֹ֗ךְ עָלָ֥יו אֵמִֽים
verses.translation: Brandished and run through his body,The blade, through his gall,Strikes terror into him.
verses.rashi: drew The one who drew the weapon upon him. and it emerged [i.e.,] the weapon. and its glittering blade Splendeur, or splendor (?) in French, like (Ezek. 21: 15), “in order that it glitter (ברק) .” Fourbir in Old French, to polish. from its bitterness [From the bitterness] of the weapon it shall come forth, and from there shall be terrors and fear upon the wicked.

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־חֹשֶׁךְ֮ טָמ֢וּן לִצְפּ֫וּנָ֥יו תְּ֭אׇכְלֵהוּ אֵ֣שׁ לֹא־נֻפָּ֑ח יֵ֖רַע שָׂרִ֣יד בְּאׇהֳלֽוֹ
verses.translation: Utter darkness waits for his treasured ones;A fire, fanned by no one, will consume him;Who survives in his tent will be crushed.
verses.rashi: for what awaits him For the hidden things that lie in store for him. fire that was not blown The fire of Gehinnom. harm shall befall the survivor in his tent Harm will come upon the survivor who will remain in his tent. The entire portion deals with the people of Sodom. “Harm shall befall the survivor in his tent” means that even Lot who escaped from them—harm befell his tent, i.e., his wife, who became a pillar of salt. I found this in the midrash of Rabbi Tanchuma (Buber, Vayera 8).

verses.hebrew_full: יְגַלּ֣וּ שָׁמַ֣יִם עֲוֺנ֑וֹ וְ֝אֶ֗רֶץ מִתְקוֹמָ֘מָ֥ה לֽוֹ
verses.translation: Heaven will expose his iniquity;Earth will rise up against him.
verses.rashi: shall rise up [This is] an expression of an enemy who rises up against him.

verses.hebrew_full: יִ֭גֶל יְב֣וּל בֵּית֑וֹ נִ֝גָּר֗וֹת בְּי֣וֹם אַפּֽוֹ
verses.translation: His household will be cast forth by a flood,Spilled out on the day of God’s wrath.
verses.rashi: shall go into exile Heb. יגל, an expression of exile. shall flow Heb. נגרות. His treasures shall be given to plunder and despoiling, like (Micah 1:4), “like water that flows (המגרים) to the earth” [sic].

verses.hebrew_full: זֶ֤ה חֵלֶק־אָדָ֣ם רָ֭שָׁע מֵאֱלֹהִ֑ים וְנַחֲלַ֖ת אִמְר֣וֹ מֵאֵֽל
verses.translation: This is the wicked man’s portion from God,The lot God has ordained for him.
verses.rashi: appointed him by God Heb. אִמְרוֹ. What was said about him from before the Omnipresent. The word אִמְרוֹ is in the masculine gender, from the form of (Ps. 5:2), “Give ear to my words (אַמָרַי).” אִמְרָתוֹ appears (ibid. 147:15) in the feminine gender, from the form of (ibid. 12:7) “pure words (אַמָרוֹת).”

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֥עַן אִיּ֗וֹב וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Job said in reply:

verses.hebrew_full: שִׁמְע֣וּ שָׁ֭מוֹעַ מִלָּתִ֑י וּתְהִי־זֹ֝֗את תַּנְח֥וּמֹֽתֵיכֶֽם
verses.translation: Listen well to what I say,And let that be your consolation.
verses.rashi: Job’s answerand this shall be your consolations The fact that you will keep your peace and listen to me this will be my consolation from you.

verses.hebrew_full: שָׂ֭אוּנִי וְאָנֹכִ֣י אֲדַבֵּ֑ר וְאַחַ֖ר דַּבְּרִ֣י תַלְעִֽיג
verses.translation: Bear with me while I speak,And after I have spoken, you may mock.
verses.rashi: Bear with me Heb. שאוני, endure me.

verses.hebrew_full: הֶ֭אָנֹכִי לְאָדָ֣ם שִׂיחִ֑י וְאִם־מַ֝דּ֗וּעַ לֹֽא־תִקְצַ֥ר רוּחִֽי
verses.translation: Is my complaint directed toward a human?Why should I not lose my patience?
verses.rashi: Am I speaking to man Why should you make an issue of my complaint? Am I complaining to a man like me that he should listen to me? Why then should I not be short of breath to cry out from my straits?

verses.hebrew_full: פְּנוּ־אֵלַ֥י וְהָשַׁ֑מּוּ וְשִׂ֖ימוּ יָ֣ד עַל־פֶּֽה
verses.translation: Look at me and be appalled,And clap your hand to your mouth.
verses.rashi: Turn to me to listen. and be silent Be astonished at my words. and place your hand upon your mouth because you will not know what to answer.

verses.hebrew_full: וְאִם־זָכַ֥רְתִּי וְנִבְהָ֑לְתִּי וְאָחַ֥ז בְּ֝שָׂרִ֗י פַּלָּצֽוּת
verses.translation: When I think of it I am terrified;My body is seized with shuddering.
verses.rashi: Now when I remember the thing I wish to say, “Why do the wicked live?” I become frightened for I see the wicked men of the Flood.

verses.hebrew_full: מַ֭דּוּעַ רְשָׁעִ֣ים יִֽחְי֑וּ עָ֝תְק֗וּ גַּם־גָּ֥בְרוּ חָֽיִל
verses.translation: Why do the wicked live on,Prosper and grow wealthy?

verses.hebrew_full: זַרְעָ֤ם נָכ֣וֹן לִפְנֵיהֶ֣ם עִמָּ֑ם וְ֝צֶאֱצָאֵיהֶ֗ם לְעֵינֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: Their children are with them always,And they see their children’s children.

verses.hebrew_full: בָּתֵּיהֶ֣ם שָׁל֣וֹם מִפָּ֑חַד וְלֹ֤א שֵׁ֖בֶט אֱל֣וֹהַּ עֲלֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: Their homes are secure, without fear;They do not feel the rod of God.
verses.rashi: peace from fear Demons had no power over them.

verses.hebrew_full: שׁוֹר֣וֹ עִ֭בַּר וְלֹ֣א יַגְעִ֑ל תְּפַלֵּ֥ט פָּ֝רָת֗וֹ וְלֹ֣א תְשַׁכֵּֽל
verses.translation: Their bull breeds and does not fail;Their cow calves and never miscarries;
verses.rashi: His bull impregnates a female. and does not fail He does not eject into her inferior sperm, which would be cast out of her body without impregnating her. Every expression of געילה means casting out, and so it is in the language of the Mishnah (Avodah Zarah 75a), One must purge them (מגעילן) with boiling water. Similarly (II Sam. 1:21), “for there the shield of the mighty was rejected (נגעל).” Its anointing was cast out, and it was as though it had not been anointed with oil, for they were accustomed to anointing the leather shields in order that it be smooth and cause the spear or javelin that strikes it to glide off. It is like (Isa. 21:5), “princes, anoint a shield.” his cow bears young She gives birth to her young at the proper time. and does not abort before its time.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: יְשַׁלְּח֣וּ כַ֭צֹּאן עֲוִילֵיהֶ֑ם וְ֝יַלְדֵיהֶ֗ם יְרַקֵּדֽוּן
verses.translation: They let their infants run loose like sheep,And their children skip about.
verses.rashi: They send forth their infants like sheep etc. At the time of his birth, he would go and dance immediately, and if he would meet a demon, he would battle with him, and his mother would say to him, “Bring me a pair of scissors and I will cut your umbilical cord,” in Haggadath Bereishith, “Go and light a candle for me,” in Gen. Rabbah (36:1).

words.eng: young male or man or boy

verses.hebrew_full: יִ֭שְׂאוּ כְּתֹ֣ף וְכִנּ֑וֹר וְ֝יִשְׂמְח֗וּ לְק֣וֹל עוּגָֽב
verses.translation: They sing to the music of hand-drum and lute,And revel to the tune of the pipe;
verses.rashi: They raise They would raise their voice in song with the tambourine and the harp. The flute (עוגב) is only an expression of sensual laughter (עגבות) (aboyement in French, barking.) So I heard.

verses.hebrew_full: (יבלו) יְכַלּ֣וּ בַטּ֣וֹב יְמֵיהֶ֑ם וּ֝בְרֶ֗גַע שְׁא֣וֹל יֵחָֽתּוּ
verses.translation: They spend their days in happiness,And go down to Sheol in peace.
verses.rashi: and in a moment they descend to the grave When his dying day arrived, he would die in a moment, easily, without suffering. they descend Heb. יחתו, they descend, which is in Targum נחיתוּ.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּאמְר֣וּ לָ֭אֵל ס֣וּר מִמֶּ֑נּוּ וְדַ֥עַת דְּ֝רָכֶ֗יךָ לֹ֣א חָפָֽצְנוּ
verses.translation: They say to God, “Leave us alone,We do not want to learn Your ways;

verses.hebrew_full: מַה־שַּׁדַּ֥י כִּֽי־נַעַבְדֶ֑נּוּ וּמַה־נּ֝וֹעִ֗יל כִּ֣י נִפְגַּע־בּֽוֹ
verses.translation: What is Shaddai that we should render service?What will we gain by offering prayer?”
verses.rashi: and what will it avail us What benefit do we have if we pray to Him? We do not need Him for the drops of rain, because “a cloud would ascend from the earth” (Gen. 2:6).

words.eng: to encounter·inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֤ן לֹ֣א בְיָדָ֣ם טוּבָ֑ם עֲצַ֥ת רְ֝שָׁעִ֗ים רָ֣חֲקָה מֶֽנִּי
verses.translation: Their happiness is not their own doing.(The thoughts of the wicked are beyond me!)
verses.rashi: Behold, is not their prosperity in their hand This is a question: Is not all their prosperity in their hand? The counsel of the wicked has distanced itself from me Job was boasting that he saw them [the wicked] and was not counted among them.

verses.hebrew_full: כַּמָּ֤ה נֵר־רְשָׁ֘עִ֤ים יִדְעָ֗ךְ וְיָבֹ֣א עָלֵ֣ימוֹ אֵידָ֑ם חֲ֝בָלִ֗ים יְחַלֵּ֥ק בְּאַפּֽוֹ
verses.translation: How seldom does the lamp of the wicked fail,Does the calamity they deserve befall them,Are their lots apportioned in anger!
verses.rashi: How long? May the lamp of the wicked be snuffed out This refers back to the above, to (verse 7) “Why do the wicked live on?” How long will You lengthen this time [the lifetime of the wicked]? Then he continues and curses them, “May the lamp of the wicked be snuffed out...” lots Heb. חבלים, the lot that they deserve.

verses.hebrew_full: יִֽהְי֗וּ כְּתֶ֥בֶן לִפְנֵי־ר֑וּחַ וּ֝כְמֹ֗ץ גְּנָבַ֥תּוּ סוּפָֽה
verses.translation: Let them become like straw in the wind,Like chaff carried off by a storm.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱל֗וֹהַּ יִצְפֹּן־לְבָנָ֥יו אוֹנ֑וֹ יְשַׁלֵּ֖ם אֵלָ֣יו וְיֵדָֽע
verses.translation: [You say,] “God is reserving his punishment for his children”;Let it be paid back to him that he may feel it,
verses.rashi: Should God lay away his violence for his sons This is a question. Is this just, that this one should sin all his life and die in peace, and his violence and his wickedness the Holy One, blessed be He, should lay away for his sons, to punish them? Let Him requite him so that he should know it is a praise to the Holy One, blessed be He, that He Himself requite the wicked man his recompense so that he understand that he dealt wickedly.

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace

verses.hebrew_full: יִרְא֣וּ עֵינָ֣ו כִּיד֑וֹ וּמֵחֲמַ֖ת שַׁדַּ֣י יִשְׁתֶּֽה
verses.translation: Let his eyes see his ruin,And let him drink the wrath of Shaddai!
verses.rashi: Let his eyes see his ruin Heb. כידו, his ruin, but I cannot find a comparison in the Bible. However, according to the context of the verse, it appears to mean ruin, and so, many did not find a comparison to it. (כידו An expression of אֵידוֹ, his ruin. The “aleph” is substituted by a “chaff.”)

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י מַה־חֶפְצ֣וֹ בְּבֵית֣וֹ אַחֲרָ֑יו וּמִסְפַּ֖ר חֳדָשָׁ֣יו חֻצָּֽצוּ
verses.translation: For what does he care about the fate of his family,When his number of months runs out?
verses.rashi: For what does He care about his house after him since the number of his months has been cut off For what does he care (saklirir in Old French) or desire in his house after his death, to worry in his lifetime about the retribution destined to befall them, since the number of his months has been cut off and they will end before the evil that the Omnipresent promised them, viz. (Gen. 6:3) “therefore his days shall be 120 years.” This is what I said to you (verse 5), “Turn to me and be silent.”

verses.hebrew_full: הַלְאֵ֥ל יְלַמֶּד־דָּ֑עַת וְ֝ה֗וּא רָמִ֥ים יִשְׁפּֽוֹט
verses.translation: Can God be instructed in knowledge,The One who judges from such heights?
verses.rashi: Can anyone teach knowledge for God Is there anyone among you who is in the place of God, who can teach the knowledge of what this standard is [i.e., the standard of God’s judgment]? But He judges high-handedly Heb. והוא רמים ישפוט. [He punishes the exalted.] Those like me, and the righteous and the esteemed, He chastises, judges, and requites, like (below 24:18), “and he will not turn in the way of the vineyards,” stated in regard to Noah and Methuselah in the Aggadah of [chapter] Chelek (Sanh. 108a). Another explanation: Can anyone teach God knowledge? That is, do I have to teach Him knowledge to judge fairly? He Himself knows that it is so, but He passes high- handed judgments with His loftiness and His greatness, and He does not care to be exact. I heard this one but not the first one.

verses.hebrew_full: זֶ֗ה יָ֭מוּת בְּעֶ֣צֶם תֻּמּ֑וֹ כֻּ֝לּ֗וֹ שַׁלְאֲנַ֥ן וְשָׁלֵֽיו
verses.translation: This one dies in robust health,All tranquil and untroubled,
verses.rashi: This one The wicked man. dies (Is punished.) in his full strength in the appearance of his perfection, lacking nothing. tranquil Heb. שַׁלְאֲנָן, like שַׁאֲנָן.

verses.hebrew_full: עֲ֭טִינָיו מָלְא֣וּ חָלָ֑ב וּמֹ֖חַ עַצְמוֹתָ֣יו יְשֻׁקֶּֽה
verses.translation: With the pails full of milk,The bones’ marrow juicy.
verses.rashi: His pails are full of milk Heb. עטיניו. This is the language of the Mishnah (Menahoth 86a): “He packs it (עוטנו) in the olive press.” They gather the olives together, and their oil forms globules and gathers in its midst in order to be ready when he presses it in the press, and that vessel in which they gather it in order for its oil to gather within it is called “ma’atan.” Here too, his milk and his moisture and fat are called “atinav.”

verses.hebrew_full: וְזֶ֗ה יָ֭מוּת בְּנֶ֣פֶשׁ מָרָ֑ה וְלֹֽא־אָ֝כַ֗ל בַּטּוֹבָֽה
verses.translation: While that one dies embittered,Never having tasted happiness.
verses.rashi: And this one dies with a bitter spirit Those that are exalted and holy to Him—them He requites immediately.

verses.hebrew_full: יַ֭חַד עַל־עָפָ֣ר יִשְׁכָּ֑בוּ וְ֝רִמָּ֗ה תְּכַסֶּ֥ה עֲלֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: They both lie in the dustAnd are covered with worms.
verses.rashi: Together on the earth they lie After their death, it is not recognizable to the people who is good and who is bad, because this one is like that one; they lie on the earth together.

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֣ן יָ֭דַעְתִּי מַחְשְׁבֽוֹתֵיכֶ֑ם וּ֝מְזִמּ֗וֹת עָלַ֥י תַּחְמֹֽסוּ
verses.translation: Oh, I know your thoughts,And the tactics you will devise against me.
verses.rashi: Lo, I know your thoughts and the devices that you wrongfully think against me You do wrong to think injustice.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י תֹאמְר֗וּ אַיֵּ֥ה בֵית־נָדִ֑יב וְ֝אַיֵּ֗ה אֹ֤הֶל מִשְׁכְּנ֬וֹת רְשָׁעִֽים
verses.translation: You will say, “Where is the house of the noble—And where the tent in which the wicked dwelled?”
verses.rashi: For etc. Because of the evil that has befallen me, you say, “Look, where is the house of this man who was a prince, and where is the tent and the dwellings of the other wicked men?” That is to say that he is like them. Because of his wickedness, his house was lost. (Now why do you rely on your tranquility?)

verses.hebrew_full: הֲלֹ֣א שְׁ֭אֶלְתֶּם ע֣וֹבְרֵי דָ֑רֶךְ וְ֝אֹתֹתָ֗ם לֹ֣א תְנַכֵּֽרוּ
verses.translation: You must have consulted the wayfarers;You cannot deny their evidence.
verses.rashi: Have you not asked etc. All these parables you are composing about me. and their signs which they made known to you, you shall not make strange to yourselves by not paying attention to them, like (Gen. 42:7), “But he made himself a stranger (ויתנכר) to them.”

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י לְי֣וֹם אֵ֭יד יֵחָ֣שֶׂךְ רָ֑ע לְי֖וֹם עֲבָר֣וֹת יוּבָֽלוּ
verses.translation: For the evildoer is spared on the day of calamity,On the day when wrath is led forth.
verses.rashi: That They told you that... the wicked man is reserved for the day of calamity The wicked man is withheld and spared from evil and reserved for the day that is appointed for his calamity, like (below 38:23), “That I have saved for a time of trouble.” I laid them away and spared them to let them know until the day of battle and war.

verses.hebrew_full: מִֽי־יַגִּ֣יד עַל־פָּנָ֣יו דַּרְכּ֑וֹ וְהוּא־עָ֝שָׂ֗ה מִ֣י יְשַׁלֶּם־לֽוֹ
verses.translation: Who will upbraid him to his face?Who will requite him for what he has done?
verses.rashi: Who will tell His way to His face Who is the great king who will tell His way to His face and will not fear Him? How exalted He is! That is the Holy One, blessed be He, Who did. and who will requite Him Who will requite Him with the recompense for His wickedness?

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace·unto him; v. ל־.

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֭הוּא לִקְבָר֣וֹת יוּבָ֑ל וְֽעַל־גָּדִ֥ישׁ יִשְׁקֽוֹד
verses.translation: He is brought to the grave,While a watch is kept at his tomb.
verses.rashi: But he will be borne to the grave He will ultimately be borne to the grave. and he will hasten to be beside the stacks of grain when he is buried in the fields beside the stacks of grain.

verses.hebrew_full: מָֽתְקוּ־ל֗וֹ רִגְבֵ֫י־נָ֥חַל וְ֭אַחֲרָיו כׇּל־אָדָ֣ם יִמְשׁ֑וֹךְ וּ֝לְפָנָ֗יו אֵ֣ין מִסְפָּֽר
verses.translation: The clods of the wadi are sweet to him,Everyone follows behind him,Innumerable are those who precede him.
verses.rashi: clods balast in French (which the sailors bring into the holds of the ships in order to weigh them down.) as there were innumerable ones before him Those who draw after the same way. All these devices you think against me.

words.eng: to be or become sweet or pleasing·unto him; v. ל־.

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֭אֵיךְ תְּנַחֲמ֣וּנִי הָ֑בֶל וּ֝תְשׁ֥וּבֹתֵיכֶ֗ם נִשְׁאַר־מָֽעַל
verses.translation: Why then do you offer me empty consolation?Of your replies only the perfidy remains.
verses.rashi: Now how do you comfort me with futility Since only betrayal remains of your replies, the replies of wisdom are forgotten and lost from you, and of your replies, [those] left in your hands are only replies of betrayal. betrayal This word (מעל) is a noun, which is why it is voweled with a kamatz, and the accent is on the first syllable.

verses.hebrew_full: וַ֭יַּעַן אֱלִיפַ֥ז הַֽתֵּמָנִ֗י וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Eliphaz the Temanite said in reply:

verses.hebrew_full: הַלְאֵ֥ל יִסְכׇּן־גָּ֑בֶר כִּֽי־יִסְכֹּ֖ן עָלֵ֣ימוֹ מַשְׂכִּֽיל
verses.translation: Can an individual be of use to God,An intellect provide benefit?
verses.rashi: Eliphaz’s replyDoes a man benefit God Will a man avail and benefit for the need and the benefit of the Creator when he teaches the people knowledge and wisdom? The first יִסְכָּן is an expression of benefit [or pleasure], like (I Kings 1:2), “and she shall be to him a warmer (סֹכֶנֶת).” The second is an expression of teaching [or accustoming], like (Num. 22:30), “Have I ever been in the habit (ההסכן הסכנתי)?” The former may also be interpreted as an expression of teaching, thus: Does a man learn for the good of the Creator and for His need when he learns wisdom?

words.eng: to be of use or service or profit or benefit·adj
burning·to be of use or service or profit or benefit

verses.hebrew_full: הַחֵ֣פֶץ לְ֭שַׁדַּי כִּ֣י תִצְדָּ֑ק וְאִם־בֶּ֝֗צַע כִּֽי־תַתֵּ֥ם דְּרָכֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: Does Shaddai gain if you are righteous,Or profit if your conduct is blameless?
verses.rashi: Does the Almighty care Like (above 21:21), “For what does He care (חפצו) about his house after him?” What pleasure or concern does He have if you are righteous in your deeds, that you reason with Him that He come with you to contest your words? or...any gain i.e., monetary gain for Him if you perfect your ways?

verses.hebrew_full: הֲֽ֭מִיִּרְאָ֣תְךָ יֹכִיחֶ֑ךָ יָב֥וֹא עִ֝מְּךָ֗ בַּמִּשְׁפָּֽט
verses.translation: Is it because of your piety that you are arraigned,And entered into God’s judgment?
verses.rashi: Will He reason with you because He fears you? Or, out of fear, that He fears you, will He come and reason with you?

verses.hebrew_full: הֲלֹ֣א רָעָתְךָ֣ רַבָּ֑ה וְאֵֽין־קֵ֝֗ץ לַעֲוֺנֹתֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: You know that your wickedness is great,And that your iniquities have no limit.
verses.rashi: Is not [Is it not so] that He knows that your evil is great?

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־תַחְבֹּ֣ל אַחֶ֣יךָ חִנָּ֑ם וּבִגְדֵ֖י עֲרוּמִּ֣ים תַּפְשִֽׁיט
verses.translation: You exact pledges from your fellows without reason,And leave them naked, stripped of their clothes;

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־מַ֭יִם עָיֵ֣ף תַּשְׁקֶ֑ה וּ֝מֵרָעֵ֗ב תִּֽמְנַֽע־לָֽחֶם
verses.translation: You do not give the thirsty water to drink;You deny bread to the hungry.

verses.hebrew_full: וְאִ֣ישׁ זְ֭רוֹעַ ל֣וֹ הָאָ֑רֶץ וּנְשׂ֥וּא פָ֝נִ֗ים יֵ֣שֶׁב בָּֽהּ
verses.translation: The land belongs to the strong;The privileged occupy it.
verses.rashi: Does a strong man own the earth This is a question. Because you were strong, should you inherit the earth? remain therein Is he fit to remain in existence?

verses.hebrew_full: אַ֭לְמָנוֹת שִׁלַּ֣חְתָּ רֵיקָ֑ם וּזְרֹע֖וֹת יְתֹמִ֣ים יְדֻכָּֽא
verses.translation: You have sent away widows empty-handed;The strength of the fatherless is broken.

verses.hebrew_full: עַל־כֵּ֭ן סְבִיבוֹתֶ֣יךָ פַחִ֑ים וִ֝יבַהֶלְךָ֗ פַּ֣חַד פִּתְאֹֽם
verses.translation: Therefore snares are all around you,And sudden terrors frighten you,

verses.hebrew_full: אוֹ־חֹ֥שֶׁךְ לֹֽא־תִרְאֶ֑ה וְֽשִׁפְעַת־מַ֥יִם תְּכַסֶּֽךָּ
verses.translation: Or darkness, so you cannot see;A flood of waters covers you.
verses.rashi: or darkness so that you do not see That is to say, You shall not put your eyes and your trust in strength, and say...

verses.hebrew_full: הֲֽלֹא־אֱ֭לוֹהַּ גֹּ֣בַהּ שָׁמָ֑יִם וּרְאֵ֤ה רֹ֖אשׁ כּוֹכָבִ֣ים כִּי־רָֽמּוּ
verses.translation: God is in the heavenly heights;See the highest stars, how lofty!
verses.rashi: Is not God in the height of heaven and will not lower Himself to see. and behold the topmost of the stars, which are lofty Heb. וראה, like (above 10:15), “and seeing (וראה) my affliction” at the end of the fifth reply. That is to say, ‘And You gaze upon the topmost stars because they are lofty.’ Therefore, you said...

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽ֭אָמַרְתָּ מַה־יָּ֣דַֽע אֵ֑ל הַבְעַ֖ד עֲרָפֶ֣ל יִשְׁפּֽוֹט
verses.translation: You say, “What can God know?Is it possible to govern through the dense cloud?
verses.rashi: ‘What does God know?’ of what occurs in the dark? Does He judge through the dark cloud? Does He see and judge facing this darkness? Behold...

verses.hebrew_full: עָבִ֣ים סֵֽתֶר־ל֭וֹ וְלֹ֣א יִרְאֶ֑ה וְח֥וּג שָׁ֝מַ֗יִם יִתְהַלָּֽךְ
verses.translation: The clouds form an opaque screenAs [God] moves about the circuit of heaven.”
verses.rashi: Thick clouds are a concealment before Him, and He cannot see. and He walks on the circle of the heavens and does not know what is on the earth. circle Heb. חוג, the circle of the compass of the heavens, like (Isa. 44:13), “and with a compass (ובמחוגה) he rounds it,” compas in French

verses.hebrew_full: הַאֹ֣רַח עוֹלָ֣ם תִּשְׁמ֑וֹר אֲשֶׁ֖ר דָּרְכ֣וּ מְתֵי־אָֽוֶן
verses.translation: Have you observed the immemorial pathThat evildoers have trodden;
verses.rashi: the old way The way of the ancients who were in days of yore. Will you keep Have you kept in your heart to remember what happened to them?

verses.hebrew_full: אֲשֶֽׁר־קֻמְּט֥וּ וְלֹא־עֵ֑ת נָ֝הָ֗ר יוּצַ֥ק יְסוֹדָֽם
verses.translation: How they were shriveled up before their timeAnd their foundation poured out like a river?
verses.rashi: Who were cut off when it was not their time They were cut off before their time. a river And the river of the Flood or the brimstone and fire of Sodom was poured into their foundation.

verses.hebrew_full: הָאֹמְרִ֣ים לָ֭אֵל ס֣וּר מִמֶּ֑נּוּ וּמַה־יִּפְעַ֖ל שַׁדַּ֣י לָֽמוֹ
verses.translation: They said to God, “Leave us alone;What can Shaddai do about it?”

verses.hebrew_full: וְה֤וּא מִלֵּ֣א בָתֵּיהֶ֣ם ט֑וֹב וַעֲצַ֥ת רְ֝שָׁעִ֗ים רָ֣חֲקָה מֶֽנִּי
verses.translation: But it was this One who filled their houses with good things.(The thoughts of the wicked are beyond me!)

verses.hebrew_full: יִרְא֣וּ צַדִּיקִ֣ים וְיִשְׂמָ֑חוּ וְ֝נָקִ֗י יִלְעַג־לָֽמוֹ
verses.translation: The righteous, seeing the fate of the wicked, rejoiced;The innocent laughed with scorn.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־לֹ֣א נִכְחַ֣ד קִימָ֑נוּ וְ֝יִתְרָ֗ם אָ֣כְלָה אֵֽשׁ
verses.translation: Surely their substance was destroyed,And their remnant consumed by fire.
verses.rashi: Whether their substance was cut off Will you watch and see whether their substance was cut off. their substance Heb קימנו, like קומתו, his substance; i.e., of that generation, like (Hosea 12:5), “in Bethel he shall fed Him, and there He shall speak with him (עמנו),” like עִמוֹ. consumed by the fire They were judged with boiling water. Rabbi Tanchuma expounded that they were tall and did not die in the depths. Then boiling water fell upon them from above and this is the meaning of יִתְרָם, their unusually tall height. So I heard.

verses.hebrew_full: הַסְכֶּן־נָ֣א עִמּ֣וֹ וּשְׁלָ֑ם בָּ֝הֶ֗ם תְּֽבוֹאַתְךָ֥ טוֹבָֽה
verses.translation: Be close to [God] and wholehearted;Good things will come to you thereby.
verses.rashi: Accustom yourself Accustom yourself to be (wholehearted) with him. and you will be complete And you will be complete, lacking nothing. with them With these things.

words.eng: to be of use or service or profit or benefit·I (we) pray
to be in a covenant of peace

verses.hebrew_full: קַח־נָ֣א מִפִּ֣יו תּוֹרָ֑ה וְשִׂ֥ים אֲ֝מָרָ֗יו בִּלְבָבֶֽךָ
verses.translation: Accept instruction from God’s mouth;Lay up those words in your heart.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־תָּשׁ֣וּב עַד־שַׁ֭דַּי תִּבָּנֶ֑ה תַּֽרְחִ֥יק עַ֝וְלָ֗ה מֵאׇהֳלֶֽךָ
verses.translation: If you return to Shaddai you will be restored,If you banish iniquity from your tent;

verses.hebrew_full: וְשִׁית־עַל־עָפָ֥ר בָּ֑צֶר וּכְצ֖וּר נְחָלִ֣ים אוֹפִֽיר
verses.translation: If you regard treasure as dirt,Ophir-gold as stones of the wadi,
verses.rashi: Then you shall make a fortification on the earth By returning to Him, you will establish on the earth strength and fortifications. and on the rocks of the valleys On the lofty rocks in the deep valleys, you shall have a collection of an accumulation of Ophir. This is the treasure of the kings, for they would gather and store gold that came from Ophir.

verses.hebrew_full: וְהָיָ֣ה שַׁדַּ֣י בְּצָרֶ֑יךָ וְכֶ֖סֶף תּוֹעָפ֣וֹת לָֽךְ
verses.translation: And Shaddai be your treasureAnd precious silver for you,
verses.rashi: And the Almighty shall be of your adversaries Heb. והיה שד-י בצריך. If this word is holy [i.e., is a reference to the Almighty], I am bewildered at its interpretation, because its meaning is a curse, that He will be counted with his enemies, as in (Jud. 11:35), “and you have become one of those who trouble me (בעכרי),” with those who trouble me. By necessity, we will adjudge it as one of the elliptical verses, [meaning: And the Almighty shall be the Judge over your adversaries. But if we can interpret it as profane, as an expression of a judge and of strength, the following is its interpretation:] And your fortifications (בצריך) shall be strong (שד-י), like “Then you shall make a fortification (בצר) on the earth.” That is, powerful wealth will be fortified, and according to the context, that is indeed its interpretation, as evidenced by the end of the verse, “and you shall have abundant (תועפות) silver,” an expression of strength, like (Num. 23:22), “his is like the power of (כתועפות) a wild ox.”

verses.hebrew_full: כִּי־אָ֭ז עַל־שַׁדַּ֣י תִּתְעַנָּ֑ג וְתִשָּׂ֖א אֶל־אֱל֣וֹהַּ פָּנֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: When you seek the favor of Shaddai,And lift up your face to God,
verses.rashi: and lift your countenance to God You will not come to lift your face except to ask Him for your needs.

verses.hebrew_full: תַּעְתִּ֣יר אֵ֭לָיו וְיִשְׁמָעֶ֑ךָּ וּנְדָרֶ֥יךָ תְשַׁלֵּֽם
verses.translation: You will offer prayer, and be heard,And you will pay your vows.
verses.rashi: and you will pay your wows You will be confident that your sacrifices, the payments of your vows, will be accepted.

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽתִגְזַר־אֹ֭מֶר וְיָ֣קׇם־לָ֑ךְ וְעַל־דְּ֝רָכֶ֗יךָ נָ֣גַהּ אֽוֹר
verses.translation: You will decree and it will be fulfilled,And light will shine upon your affairs.
verses.rashi: and it will be accomplished for you Heb. ויקם, lit. and it will rise, like וְיָקוּם לָךְ.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־הִ֭שְׁפִּילוּ וַתֹּ֣אמֶר גֵּוָ֑ה וְשַׁ֖ח עֵינַ֣יִם יוֹשִֽׁעַ
verses.translation: When others sink low, you will say it is pride;For [God] saves the humble.
verses.rashi: If they are humble If you see your generation humble, pledge that it be uplifted, and it will be uplifted. a humble person [One who is humbled] by the trouble that befalls [him] because of his iniquity, God will save through your hands and through your prayer.

verses.hebrew_full: יְמַלֵּ֥ט אִֽי־נָקִ֑י וְ֝נִמְלַ֗ט בְּבֹ֣ר כַּפֶּֽיךָ
verses.translation: The guilty will be delivered—Through the cleanness of your hands.
verses.rashi: He shall deliver one who is not innocent Heb. אי נקי, synonymous with אין נקִי, not innocent, like (I Sam. 4:21), “Ichabod (איכבוד),” meaning, there is no glory (אין כבוד). And so (Prov. 31:4), “neither is drunkenness for rulers (אי שכר),” there is no drunkenness (אין שכר). It does not befit them to become intoxicated. Menachem joined the three into one group (Machbereth Menachem p. 22). Although they are two words (?), they explain them thus. And that one who is not innocent will be delivered because of the purity of your hands. So I heard. I heard another version that was being said in the name of Rabbi Jacob: This is an expression of a man who lives in pain and sighing, and the “yud” is superfluous in the word, like (Ps. 123:1), “Who dwells (הישבי) in heaven.” But I did not hear this from the rabbi’s mouth.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֥עַן אִיּ֗וֹב וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Job said in reply:

verses.hebrew_full: גַּם־הַ֭יּוֹם מְרִ֣י שִׂחִ֑י יָ֝דִ֗י כָּבְדָ֥ה עַל־אַנְחָתִֽי
verses.translation: Today again my complaint is bitter;My strength is spent on account of my groaning.
verses.rashi: Job’s ReplyEven today after all these consolations. my speech is bitter [The bitterness of my speech] remains in its place, for there is no consolation in your words. my wound is heavier The wound of my plague is stronger than my sigh, for I do not complain and cry about my wound.

verses.hebrew_full: מִֽי־יִתֵּ֣ן יָ֭דַעְתִּי וְאֶמְצָאֵ֑הוּ אָ֝ב֗וֹא עַד־תְּכוּנָתֽוֹ
verses.translation: Would that I knew how to reach [God],How to get to the heavenly dwelling-place.
verses.rashi: and I would find Him My Judge. the place prepared for Him The place prepared for His throne.

verses.hebrew_full: אֶעֶרְכָ֣ה לְפָנָ֣יו מִשְׁפָּ֑ט וּ֝פִ֗י אֲמַלֵּ֥א תוֹכָחֽוֹת
verses.translation: I would set out my case,And fill my mouth with arguments.
verses.rashi: case [i.e., my] argument.

verses.hebrew_full: אֵ֭דְעָה מִלִּ֣ים יַעֲנֵ֑נִי וְ֝אָבִ֗ינָה מַה־יֹּ֥אמַר לִֽי
verses.translation: I would learn what answers were waiting for me,And know how the reply would be.
verses.rashi: I would know the words that He would answer me Even if He stops me from speaking before Him, perhaps He would speak with me and I would know the words He would answer me.

verses.hebrew_full: הַבְּרׇב־כֹּ֭חַ יָרִ֣יב עִמָּדִ֑י לֹ֥א אַךְ־ה֝֗וּא יָשִׂ֥ם בִּֽי
verses.translation: Would [God] contend with me overbearingly?Surely I would not be accused!
verses.rashi: with much power of words and rebukes for His sins [other editions read: my sins] will He contend with me? not...coercion smiting and coercion. He will not cast His coercion and His awe upon me. (It may also be interpreted:) Nothing but [this] will He cast on me, for He will not cast on me anything but the contest of the rebukes for my sins; [that,] but not false accusations.

verses.hebrew_full: שָׁ֗ם יָ֭שָׁר נוֹכָ֣ח עִמּ֑וֹ וַאֲפַלְּטָ֥ה לָ֝נֶ֗צַח מִשֹּׁפְטִֽי
verses.translation: There the upright would be cleared by divine decree,And I would escape forever from my judge.
verses.rashi: There in the place of our contest. integrity The propriety of my deeds will be proven, revealed, and it will appear with Him, i.e., before Him. proven and there I will extricate [myself] forever because He will find no iniquity in me.

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֤ן קֶ֣דֶם אֶהֱלֹ֣ךְ וְאֵינֶ֑נּוּ וְ֝אָח֗וֹר וְֽלֹא־אָבִ֥ין לֽוֹ
verses.translation: But if I go East—[God] is not there;West—I still appear to be alone;
verses.rashi: Behold...east it refers to the above (verse 3), “Would that I knew etc.” east as it is stated (Exod. 27: 13): “on the east side (קדמה), eastward.” and to the west Heb. ואחור. Since the east is the front of the world (קדמה), the west is found to be the back of the world (אחור).

verses.hebrew_full: שְׂמֹ֣אול בַּעֲשֹׂת֣וֹ וְלֹא־אָ֑חַז יַעְטֹ֥ף יָ֝מִ֗ין וְלֹ֣א אֶרְאֶֽה
verses.translation: North—I cannot discern One who is concealed;South—also there hidden from my sight.
verses.rashi: north Heb. שמאוֹל, lit. left. when He made it When He created it, He did not make the place of His throne there, that I should see it there. see Heb. אחז, the Aramaic translation of אֶרְאֶה, I will see. Proof of this is that the accent is under the “aleph.” Now, if it were an expression of holding (אחיזה), the accent would be at the end of the word, under the “cheth.” He wraps up the south He covers the face of the south before me in order that I should not see Him.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־יָ֭דַע דֶּ֣רֶךְ עִמָּדִ֑י בְּ֝חָנַ֗נִי כַּזָּהָ֥ב אֵצֵֽא
verses.translation: But [God] knows the way I take;Would it be assayed, I should emerge pure as gold.
verses.rashi: Because He knows the way that is with me therefore, He does not come to contend with me. He has tested me that I will emerge like gold if He contends with me.

verses.hebrew_full: בַּ֭אֲשֻׁרוֹ אָחֲזָ֣ה רַגְלִ֑י דַּרְכּ֖וֹ שָׁמַ֣רְתִּי וְלֹא־אָֽט
verses.translation: I have followed in God’s tracks,Kept that way without swerving,
verses.rashi: to His path Heb. באשרו, to His path, like (below 31:7), “If my foot (אשרי) has turned away from the road”; (Ps. 40:3), “He has established my paths (אשרי).” and do not turn away I am not accustomed to turn away.

verses.hebrew_full: מִצְוַ֣ת שְׂ֭פָתָיו וְלֹ֣א אָמִ֑ישׁ מֵ֝חֻקִּ֗י צָפַ֥נְתִּי אִמְרֵי־פִֽיו
verses.translation: I have not deviated from what God’s lips commanded;I have treasured those words more than my daily bread.
verses.rashi: more than my daily bread I kept the words of His mouth More than my daily food I was eager to keep the words of His mouth. [The expression חקי] is like (Prov. 30:8), “my daily bread (לחם חקי),” my food.

verses.hebrew_full: וְה֣וּא בְ֭אֶחָד וּמִ֣י יְשִׁיבֶ֑נּוּ וְנַפְשׁ֖וֹ אִוְּתָ֣ה וַיָּֽעַשׂ
verses.translation: Single-minded, not readily dissuaded,[God] desires, and it comes to pass.
verses.rashi: But He Since He is One in the world and knows the knowledge and the thoughts of the creatures, and what they have to reply to Him, therefore replies are prepared before Him. and who will answer Him Therefore, what His soul desires, He does.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֭י יַשְׁלִ֣ים חֻקִּ֑י וְכָהֵ֖נָּה רַבּ֣וֹת עִמּֽוֹ
verses.translation: For this One will bring my term to an end,But there are many more such who are held in reserve.
verses.rashi: For He will complete my sentence I know that He will not draw back His hand until He completes the sentence of His decrees, the retribution that He has decreed against me. and there are many such things with Him For He did many such things.

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace

verses.hebrew_full: עַל־כֵּ֭ן מִפָּנָ֣יו אֶבָּהֵ֑ל אֶ֝תְבּוֹנֵ֗ן וְאֶפְחַ֥ד מִמֶּֽנּוּ
verses.translation: Therefore I am terrified at God’s presence;When I consider, I feel dread.
verses.rashi: Therefore Since He does not requite man according to his ways. I am startled by Him and when I ponder His ways, I am frightened of Him.

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֭אֵל הֵרַ֣ךְ לִבִּ֑י וְ֝שַׁדַּ֗י הִבְהִילָֽנִי
verses.translation: God has made me fainthearted;Shaddai has terrified me.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־לֹ֣א נִ֭צְמַתִּי מִפְּנֵי־חֹ֑שֶׁךְ וּ֝מִפָּנַ֗י כִּסָּה־אֹֽפֶל
verses.translation: Yet I am not cut off by the darkness;The thick gloom has been concealed from me.
verses.rashi: Because I was not cut off from before the darkness Because of this I am startled; because I was not completely cut off and laid permanently in the grave before this darkness that has come upon me, and because He did not cover this thick darkness from before me until my death. and [because He did not] cover the thick darkness from before me This refers back to “because not” at the beginning of the verse.

verses.hebrew_full: מַדּ֗וּעַ מִ֭שַּׁדַּי לֹא־נִצְפְּנ֣וּ עִתִּ֑ים וְ֝יֹדְעָ֗ו לֹא־חָ֥זוּ יָמָֽיו
verses.translation: Why are times for judgment not reserved by Shaddai?Even those held close cannot foresee God’s actions.
verses.rashi: Why were the times not hidden from the Almighty? This is a plaint. Why were the times of man’s days not hidden from the Holy One, blessed be He, that He should not know the day of man’s death? Perhaps He would not hasten the retribution, and the day of death would precede it. And He Who knows him should not see his days Of the day of man, like (Gen. 29:14), “a month of days (ימים),” (which is translated into Aramaic as “from time to time.”) [Another explanation is:] Why are those who are close and who love the Holy One, blessed be He, unable to see the end of His ways and His traits? This [word] ימיו, His days, denotes the way He acted in His time, like (below 27:6), “my heart will not reproach me because of my days.” [See Rashi ad loc.] Now what are the puzzling traits?

verses.hebrew_full: גְּבֻל֥וֹת יַשִּׂ֑יגוּ עֵ֥דֶר גָּ֝זְל֗וּ וַיִּרְעֽוּ
verses.translation: People remove boundary-stones;They carry off flocks and pasture them;
verses.rashi: [There are some who] remove the landmarks Those who habitually remove the landmarks, who purchase fields adjoining the fields of others, and at night widen their [own] borders by placing their fences at night within the territory of their neighbors. (This is the meaning of removing the landmarks, which is translated into Aramaic as מְשַׁנֶּה, changing, because they would change the boundaries.) And they violently take away a flock and pasture it.

verses.hebrew_full: חֲמ֣וֹר יְתוֹמִ֣ים יִנְהָ֑גוּ יַ֝חְבְּל֗וּ שׁ֣וֹר אַלְמָנָֽה
verses.translation: They lead away the donkeys of the fatherless,And seize the widow’s bull as a pledge;
verses.rashi: They lead away the orphan’s donkey and commit all the evils stated in the chapter. The end of the matter is that “God does not impute it for unseemliness” (verse 12), to let them know their iniquity and to furnish them with their recompense.

verses.hebrew_full: יַטּ֣וּ אֶבְיֹנִ֣ים מִדָּ֑רֶךְ יַ֥חַד חֻ֝בְּא֗וּ עֲנִיֵּי־אָֽרֶץ
verses.translation: They chase the needy off the roads;All the poor of the land are forced into hiding.
verses.rashi: the poor of the land hide together because they fear them.

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֤ן פְּרָאִ֨ים בַּ֥מִּדְבָּ֗ר יָצְא֣וּ בְּ֭פׇעֳלָם מְשַׁחֲרֵ֣י לַטָּ֑רֶף עֲרָבָ֥ה ל֥וֹ לֶ֝֗חֶם לַנְּעָרִֽים
verses.translation: Like the wild asses of the wilderness,They go about their tasks, seeking food;The wilderness provides each with food for their young;
verses.rashi: And he says: Behold, [as] wild donkeys in the desert who are used to the desert, to lie in wait for the passersby. they go out to their work and what is the work? seeking prey They seek to prey. the wilderness yields bread for his young men For the wicked man’s servants and young men, the wilderness and the desert supply food for their needs, for from there he seizes and eats and steals what they eat. for...young men They are the ones who go with him to rob, as in (Gen. 14:24), “only what the young men have eaten.”

verses.hebrew_full: בַּ֭שָּׂדֶה בְּלִיל֣וֹ (יקצירו) יִקְצ֑וֹרוּ וְכֶ֖רֶם רָשָׁ֣ע יְלַקֵּֽשׁוּ
verses.translation: They harvest fodder in the field,And glean the late grapes in the vineyards of the wicked.
verses.rashi: In the field of others. his produce Heb. בְּלִילוֹ [like] יְבוּלוּ, his produce. they reap to steal it. and the wicked gather the plants of the vineyard Heb. יְלַקֵּשׁוּ. And [from] the vineyards of others these wicked men take their fruit; espreinment in Old French. They take their לֶקֶש. לֶקֶש is an expression of a growth of produce, as in (Amos 7:1), at the beginning of the shooting up of the grain, which Jonathan renders. צִמוּחַ, growing. And so did Rabbi Eleazar [Hakalir] establish (in the Hoshanah “Soil from curse”): produce לֶקֶש) from locusts. (Another interpretation: and they delay the vineyard of the wicked. They delay it from destruction, because they eat up [the vineyards] of the poor and the orphans. יְלַקֵּשׁוּ is like (below 29:23), “and they opened their mouth (למלקוש) for a long time,” and like (Gen. 30:42), “and the late ones (לקישיא) were Laban’s.”

verses.hebrew_full: עָר֣וֹם יָ֭לִינוּ מִבְּלִ֣י לְב֑וּשׁ וְאֵ֥ין כְּ֝ס֗וּת בַּקָּרָֽה
verses.translation: They pass the night naked for lack of clothing,They have no covering against the cold;
verses.rashi: They make them lie all night naked They make the poor lie all night. without clothing and without a cover because they took their cover.

verses.hebrew_full: מִזֶּ֣רֶם הָרִ֣ים יִרְטָ֑בוּ וּֽמִבְּלִ֥י מַ֝חְסֶ֗ה חִבְּקוּ־צֽוּר
verses.translation: They are drenched by the mountain rains,And huddle against the rock for lack of shelter.
verses.rashi: From the stream that flows from the mountains, these naked people became wet and damp because they have no cover. They have no refuge under which to shelter themselves. they embrace the rocks They hide in the crevices in the rocks, and even there the stream flows down and sprays on them, soaking them. רָטוֹב is the Aramaic for לַח wet, and whoever is not dried is called לַח. moist (moite in Old French).

verses.hebrew_full: יִ֭גְזְלוּ מִשֹּׁ֣ד יָת֑וֹם וְֽעַל־עָנִ֥י יַחְבֹּֽלוּ
verses.translation: They snatch the fatherless infant from the breast,And seize the child of the poor as a pledge.
verses.rashi: from the breast of the orphan The sucking of the orphan, the source of his food and sustenance, as in (Isa. 60:16), “and the breast of kings you shall suck.” they take a pledge Heb. יחבלו, an expression of (Exod. 22:25), “If you take a pledge (חבל תחבל)”; (Ezek.18:16), “he did not take a pledge (חבל לא חבל).”

verses.hebrew_full: עָר֣וֹם הִ֭לְּכוּ בְּלִ֣י לְב֑וּשׁ וּ֝רְעֵבִ֗ים נָ֣שְׂאוּ עֹֽמֶר
verses.translation: They go about naked for lack of clothing,And, hungry, carry sheaves;
verses.rashi: They made go They made him go without clothing. and [from] the hungry they carried off the sheaves And from the hungry they took the sheaves of gleaning [which are left for the poor]. It may also be interpreted thus: And those who were carrying the sheaves are hungry, because they were robbed of them and so they remain hungry.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּין־שׁוּרֹתָ֥ם יַצְהִ֑ירוּ יְקָבִ֥ים דָּ֝רְכ֗וּ וַיִּצְמָֽאוּ
