verses.translation: Between rows [of olive trees] they make oil,And, thirsty, they tread the winepresses.
verses.rashi: Between their rows they make oil The owners of the olive trees make oil. they tread the winepresses The owners of the vineyards. but they are thirsty after a while, because these wicked men rob them of them [the wine]. Between their rows It seems to me that they pile up the olives in rows in the oil press and the oil flows between them.

verses.hebrew_full: מֵ֘עִ֤יר מְתִ֨ים יִנְאָ֗קוּ וְנֶֽפֶשׁ־חֲלָלִ֥ים תְּשַׁוֵּ֑עַ וֶ֝אֱל֗וֹהַּ לֹֽא־יָשִׂ֥ים תִּפְלָֽה
verses.translation: Mortals groan in the city;The souls of the dying cry out;Yet God does not regard it as a reproach.
verses.rashi: From the city people groan The people and the dignitaries of the city groan because of those robbers. does not impute it for unseemliness to be strict with them and to take revenge.

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֤מָּה הָיוּ֮ בְּֽמֹרְדֵ֫י־א֥וֹר לֹא־הִכִּ֥ירוּ דְרָכָ֑יו וְלֹ֥א יָ֝שְׁב֗וּ בִּנְתִיבֹתָֽיו
verses.translation: They are rebels against the light;They are strangers to its ways,And do not stay in its path.
verses.rashi: They These wicked people are the generation of the Flood, who rebelled because of אור, because of the rain; because it would ascend from the earth, they needed nothing.

words.eng: to rebel·to be or become light

verses.hebrew_full: לָא֡וֹר יָ֘ק֤וּם רוֹצֵ֗חַ יִֽקְטׇל־עָנִ֥י וְאֶבְי֑וֹן וּ֝בַלַּ֗יְלָה יְהִ֣י כַגַּנָּֽב
verses.translation: The murderer arises in the evening, Aramaic ʻorta, “evening”; in contrast to others “with the light.” To kill the poor and needy,And at night he acts the thief.
verses.rashi: Should one rise in daylight If they would rise by day, they would slay the wayfarers. and at night he would be like a thief to dig under houses.

verses.hebrew_full: וְעֵ֤ין נֹאֵ֨ף שָׁ֤מְרָֽה־נֶ֣שֶׁף לֵ֭אמֹר לֹא־תְשׁוּרֵ֣נִי עָ֑יִן וְסֵ֖תֶר פָּנִ֣ים יָשִֽׂים
verses.translation: The eyes of the adulterer watch for twilight,Thinking, “No one will glimpse me then.”He masks his face.
verses.rashi: And the eye of the adulterer And the adulterer among them would wait until evening, saying... a mask And he puts a mask on his face to commit a transgression, for the generation of the Flood was suspected of all these: of robbery, for it is stated (Gen. 6:11): “and the earth was filled with violence,” and of immorality, for it is stated (ibid. verse 12): “for all flesh had corrupted [its way on the earth].”

verses.hebrew_full: חָתַ֥ר בַּחֹ֗שֶׁךְ בָּ֫תִּ֥ים יוֹמָ֥ם חִתְּמוּ־לָ֗מוֹ לֹא־יָ֥דְעוּ אֽוֹר
verses.translation: In the dark they break into houses;By day they shut themselves in;They do not know the light.
verses.rashi: by day, they lock themselves in Those who are weaker than they, too weak to commit robbery by day, would dig under houses at night; by day they were sealed and closed up in their houses. they know no light They did not recognize the light because it was not [shining at] the time of their prowling.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י יַחְדָּ֨ו בֹּ֣קֶר לָ֣מוֹ צַלְמָ֑וֶת כִּֽי־יַ֝כִּ֗יר בַּלְה֥וֹת צַלְמָֽוֶת
verses.translation: For all of them morning is darkness;It is then that they discern the terror of darkness.
verses.rashi: For together morning is to them [as] the shadow of death For by day they were locked in, and at night they would go to their tunnel, and they were not at all afraid of the demons. for he recognizes the terrors of the shadow of death Everyone could recognize the terrifying demons, and he did not fear them. Our Sages, however, explained: By day, they would seal for themselves—By day they would chart the location of the cellar of the house and a rich man’s treasurehouse, for they would deposit balsamum oil with wealthy householders, knowing that he (sic) would put it in his (sic) treasurehouse, and at night they would smell and go there and dig into there.

verses.hebrew_full: קַל־ה֤וּא עַל־פְּנֵי־מַ֗יִם תְּקֻלַּ֣ל חֶלְקָתָ֣ם בָּאָ֑רֶץ לֹא־יִ֝פְנֶ֗ה דֶּ֣רֶךְ כְּרָמִֽים
verses.translation: May they be flotsam on the face of the water;May their portion in the land be cursed;May none turn aside by way of their vineyards.
verses.rashi: He is light upon the face of the water When the righteous men, Noah and Methuselah, would rebuke them, they would say, “we are light [enough] to swim on the water, and we have papyrus and light materials from which to make boats and to float.” their portion in the earth is cursed; he does not turn by the way of the vineyards They would curse their portion in the earth, saying that they would not turn by the way of the good, in the way of the righteous, who are lofty and dwell in the height or the world. Vineyards represent the righteous men and the leaders of the generation, as in (Hosea 2:17), “And I will give her vineyards from there,” which Jonathan renders: her leaders. They would curse their portion in the earth, saying that they would not turn in the way of the good; they would swear that they would not turn their way. Another explanation: תקלל חלקתם בארץ Their portion in the earth was spoiled, for they would destroy their seed in vain, as in (Gen. 6:12), “for all flesh had corrupted its way.” תקלל is an expression of spoiling (קלקול), as in (I Sam. 3:13), “for the iniquity [of the matter] that he knew that his sons were bringing disgrace (מקללים) upon them.” Another explanation: Their portion shall be cursed—the following generations will curse by them: He who requited the generation of the Flood. So I heard. (Gen. Rabbah 30:2). Another explanation: their portion is cursed; he does not turn by the way of the vineyards They would not accept reproof from the good men. Another explanation: They were swift to sail in ships to kill and to plunder property, but in the way of the land that was inhabited by humans and planted with vineyards they did not care to go.

verses.hebrew_full: צִיָּ֤ה גַם־חֹ֗ם יִגְזְל֥וּ מֵֽימֵי־שֶׁ֗לֶג שְׁא֣וֹל חָטָֽאוּ
verses.translation: May drought and heat snatch away their snow waters,And Sheol, those who have sinned.
verses.rashi: Dryness and heat they steal away Because of their evil, they caused for themselves that the world should change for one year from its six seasons: sowing, reaping, heat, cold, summer, and winter. snow water Like: and snow water. This too they stole from the world, because they sinned so that they descended to the grave. In some words, the “vavin” are missing, like (Jer. 11:19), “And I was like a lamb, a bull,” which is interpreted as, a lamb and a bull. There are many more in Machbereth Menachem ben Saruk (p. 26).

verses.hebrew_full: יִשְׁכָּ֘חֵ֤הוּ רֶ֨חֶם מְתָ֘ק֤וֹ רִמָּ֗ה ע֥וֹד לֹֽא־יִזָּכֵ֑ר וַתִּשָּׁבֵ֖ר כָּעֵ֣ץ עַוְלָֽה
verses.translation: May the womb forget him;May he be sweet to the worms;May he be no longer remembered;May wrongdoers be broken like a tree.
verses.rashi: The womb forgets him [i.e., the womb of] his mother. Rabbi Tanchum expounded that the generation of the Flood would take their children and place them underneath themselves in order to stop up the windows of the deep, and even his mother would have no mercy on him. he is sweet to the worms to die, and for the worms to eat their (sic) flesh. he will no longer be remembered In one year they were [to be] uprooted, but Job was complaining about the reprieve that was given them [the wicked] from the start, for many died and did not see this retribution.

words.eng: to be or become sweet or pleasing

verses.hebrew_full: רֹעֶ֣ה עֲ֭קָרָה לֹ֣א תֵלֵ֑ד וְ֝אַלְמָנָ֗ה לֹ֣א יְיֵטִֽיב
verses.translation: May he consort with an infertile woman who bears no child,Leave his widow deprived of good.
verses.rashi: He feeds the barren woman who will not bear so was their custom: One would marry two wives, one for coitus and one for children. The one intended for coitus he would give a potion of roots to drink in order that she should never give birth, and he would feed her delicacies, bathe her, and adorn her, so that she should appear beautiful. The one intended for children he would dress in widow’s garments and starve. This is the meaning of his statement that the barren woman who will not give birth he would feed and adorn, but the “widow,” who was intended for children, he would not adorn.

verses.hebrew_full: וּמָשַׁ֣ךְ אַבִּירִ֣ים בְּכֹח֑וֹ יָ֝ק֗וּם וְֽלֹא־יַאֲמִ֥ין בַּחַיִּֽין
verses.translation: Though he has the strength to seize bulls,May he live with no assurance of survival.
verses.rashi: And He draws The Holy One, blessed be He, [draws] those mighty men with His strength to retribution and to sustenance by the reprieve that He granted them. he rises one day. and does not believe in his life Heb. בחיין, like בחיים ; (Gen. 7:11): “on that day all the fountains of the deep were torn apart etc.”

verses.hebrew_full: יִתֶּן־ל֣וֹ לָ֭בֶטַח וְיִשָּׁעֵ֑ן וְ֝עֵינֵ֗יהוּ עַל־דַּרְכֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: Yet [God] gives him the security on which he relies,And keeps watch over his affairs.
verses.rashi: He grants him safety until the day of their catastrophe and they rely [on it].

verses.hebrew_full: ר֤וֹמּוּ מְּעַ֨ט וְֽאֵינֶ֗נּוּ וְֽהֻמְּכ֗וּ כַּכֹּ֥ל יִקָּפְצ֑וּן וּכְרֹ֖אשׁ שִׁבֹּ֣לֶת יִמָּֽלוּ
verses.translation: Exalted for a while, let them be gone;Be brought low, and shrivel like mallows,And wither like the heads of grain.
verses.rashi: They are taken away in a second They are taken away from the world in a fleeting second. and they are crushed crushed and impoverished. like all they are gathered in Like all things that are gathered in and taken away at the time of the harvest, vintage, and olive harvest; just as those are gathered in, so were they destroyed instantly.

verses.hebrew_full: וְאִם־לֹ֣א אֵ֭פוֹ מִ֣י יַכְזִיבֵ֑נִי וְיָשֵׂ֥ם לְ֝אַ֗ל מִלָּתִֽי
verses.translation: Surely no one can confute me,Or prove that I am wrong.
verses.rashi: And if it is not so, then [If it is not] as I said, let one of you come and prove me a liar. and make my words as nought [That is, my words] with which I criticize Him to know His standards, that He is slow to anger with the wicked, but He chastises those who love Him as I do, and He does not put them to death, as he complains above (23:17): “Because I was not cut off from before the darkness,” but those who remove the landmarks and commit all these [crimes] were not smitten with suffering.

verses.hebrew_full: וַ֭יַּעַן בִּלְדַּ֥ד הַשֻּׁחִ֗י וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Bildad the Shuhite said in reply:

verses.hebrew_full: הַמְשֵׁ֣ל וָפַ֣חַד עִמּ֑וֹ עֹשֶׂ֥ה שָׁ֝ל֗וֹם בִּמְרוֹמָֽיו
verses.translation: Dominion and dread are God’s,Who imposes peace on high.
verses.rashi: Bildad’s replyDominion and fear Directed towards what Job said (23:4), “I would set out my case before Him,” he says to him, “Dominion and fear are with Him.” “Dominion” refers to Michael, and “fear” refers to Gabriel. Can you answer even one of them? He makes peace in His heights When the constellations ascend, each one thinks, “I will ascend first,” and because he does not see what is before him, he is not envious. So I heard. [Tanchuma Buber ad loc.] Another explanation: He makes peace in His heights—Fire and water are mixed, and the water does not extinguish the fire; I did not hear this.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲיֵ֣שׁ מִ֭סְפָּר לִגְדוּדָ֑יו וְעַל־מִ֝֗י לֹא־יָק֥וּם אוֹרֵֽהוּ
verses.translation: Can the celestial troops be numbered?On whom does God’s light not shine?

verses.hebrew_full: וּמַה־יִּצְדַּ֣ק אֱנ֣וֹשׁ עִם־אֵ֑ל וּמַה־יִּ֝זְכֶּ֗ה יְל֣וּד אִשָּֽׁה
verses.translation: How can a mortal be in the right before God?How can one born of woman be cleared of guilt?
verses.rashi: How then can man be just... that you said (23:10), “He has tested me that I will emerge like gold.”

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֣ן עַד־יָ֭רֵחַ וְלֹ֣א יַאֲהִ֑יל וְ֝כוֹכָבִ֗ים לֹא־זַכּ֥וּ בְעֵינָֽיו
verses.translation: Even the moon is not bright,And the stars are not pure in God’s sight.
verses.rashi: He removes the moon Heb. עד, like (28:8), “neither did a lion pass (עדה) over it.” The meaning is that even the moon will be removed from its [source of] light in the future and it will not shine, as it is written (Isa. 24:23): “And the moon shall be ashamed etc.” And the stars will not be pure in His eyes in the future. How much less can man, who is a worm, be just with God! and will not shine Heb. יאהיל, like (Isa. 13:10), “shall not allow their light to illuminate (יהלו),” and this “alef” serves the same function as (I Sam. 2:33), “and to sadden (ולאדיב) your heart,” in which the “alef” is superfluous. Another explanation: יַאֲהִיל, you will not have shadow. When it does not shine, no shadows are cast. I prefer the former, but I heard both.

verses.hebrew_full: אַ֭ף כִּֽי־אֱנ֣וֹשׁ רִמָּ֑ה וּבֶן־אָ֝דָ֗ם תּוֹלֵעָֽה
verses.translation: How much less a mortal, a worm,One born of humans, a maggot.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֥עַן אִיּ֗וֹב וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Then Job said in reply:

verses.hebrew_full: מֶה־עָזַ֥רְתָּ לְלֹא־כֹ֑חַ ה֝וֹשַׁ֗עְתָּ זְר֣וֹעַ לֹא־עֹֽז
verses.translation: You would help without having the strength;You would deliver with arms that have no power.

verses.hebrew_full: מַה־יָּ֭עַצְתָּ לְלֹ֣א חׇכְמָ֑ה וְ֝תֻשִׁיָּ֗ה לָרֹ֥ב הוֹדָֽעְתָּ
verses.translation: Without having the wisdom, you offer adviceAnd freely give your counsel.

verses.hebrew_full: אֶת־מִ֭י הִגַּ֣דְתָּ מִלִּ֑ין וְנִשְׁמַת־מִ֝֗י יָצְאָ֥ה מִמֶּֽךָּ
verses.translation: To whom have you addressed words?Whose breath issued from you?
verses.rashi: Job’s Replyand whose breath emerged from you? Who put into you this breath that emerged from your mouth? Who does not know this?

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·who?

verses.hebrew_full: הָרְפָאִ֥ים יְחוֹלָ֑לוּ מִתַּ֥חַת מַ֝֗יִם וְשֹׁכְנֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: The shades trembleBeneath the waters and their denizens.
verses.rashi: Gehinnom Gehinnom, which weakens the creatures. is hollow Its seven hollows. beneath the water and its denizens in the deepest of the depths, but that too...

verses.hebrew_full: עָר֣וֹם שְׁא֣וֹל נֶגְדּ֑וֹ וְאֵ֥ין כְּ֝ס֗וּת לָאֲבַדּֽוֹן
verses.translation: Sheol stands naked;Abaddon has no cover.
verses.rashi: Sheol is naked before Him to know and to see all that is within it.

verses.hebrew_full: נֹטֶ֣ה צָפ֣וֹן עַל־תֹּ֑הוּ תֹּ֥לֶה אֶ֝֗רֶץ עַל־בְּלִי־מָֽה
verses.translation: [God] is the One who stretched out Zaphon over chaos,Who suspended earth over emptiness.

verses.hebrew_full: צֹרֵֽר־מַ֥יִם בְּעָבָ֑יו וְלֹֽא־נִבְקַ֖ע עָנָ֣ן תַּחְתָּֽם
verses.translation: The waters were wrapped up in God’s clouds;Yet no cloud burst under their weight.
verses.rashi: and the cloud does not split It never [split] so that its water [i.e., rain] should fall together. beneath it Beneath the water.

verses.hebrew_full: מְאַחֵ֥ז פְּנֵֽי־כִסֵּ֑ה פַּרְשֵׁ֖ז עָלָ֣יו עֲנָנֽוֹ
verses.translation: [God] shuts off the view of the heavenly throne,Spreading a cloud over it,
verses.rashi: He closes in with walls the face of His throne of glory, like (Ps. 18:12), “He made darkness His secret place.” He spreads over the throne His cloud (Ezek. 1:22), “And there was a likeness over the heads of the living creatures, of an expanse.”

verses.hebrew_full: חֹֽק־חָ֭ג עַל־פְּנֵי־מָ֑יִם עַד־תַּכְלִ֖ית א֣וֹר עִם־חֹֽשֶׁךְ
verses.translation: Drawing a boundary on the surface of the waters,At the extreme where light and darkness meet.
verses.rashi: He encircled a boundary (compas in French), an expression of (Isa. 44: 13), “and with a compass (ובמחוגה) he rounds it.” [He surrounded it with sand for the sand to be a circle for it. Does not appear in all editions.] The word חֹק denotes a perpetual boundary. The sea will not cross that circle until light and dark come to an end.

verses.hebrew_full: עַמּוּדֵ֣י שָׁמַ֣יִם יְרוֹפָ֑פוּ וְ֝יִתְמְה֗וּ מִגַּעֲרָתֽוֹ
verses.translation: The pillars of heaven tremble,Astounded at God’s blasts.
verses.rashi: trembled when He created them, and He caused them to congeal. They were trembling, and He rebuked them, and they stood dry and strong. astonished (wondering and ascending. Other editions: wondering and standing) in one place.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּ֭כֹחוֹ רָגַ֣ע הַיָּ֑ם (ובתובנתו) וּ֝בִתְבוּנָת֗וֹ מָ֣חַץ רָֽהַב
verses.translation: With power [God] stilled the sea;With skill [God] struck down Rahab.
verses.rashi: the sea wrinkled Like (above 7:5), “my skin wrinkled.” Many wrinkles were formed. (So, when He said (Gen. 1:9), “Let the waters...gather,” they were gathered from their straightness and were wrinkled into many wrinkles) until they gathered into one place, which was prepared for them. He smote Rahab The Egyptians, who are called רהב, haughty.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּ֭רוּחוֹ שָׁמַ֣יִם שִׁפְרָ֑ה חֹלְלָ֥ה יָ֝ד֗וֹ נָחָ֥שׁ בָּרִֽחַ
verses.translation: With a wind [God] calmed the heavens;God’s hand pierced the Elusive Serpent.
verses.rashi: By His breath He made the heavens a tent Heb. שפרה. With His words and with the breath of His mouth, He made a tent, like (Jer. 43:10), “and he shall spread his royal pavilion (שפרירו).” His hand caused pain to Pharaoh, who was called the barlike serpent (Isa. 26:1). חללה is an expression of pain and trouble. Likewise, elsewhere (ibid. 51:9): “are you not the one that hewed Rahab and slew (מחוללת), the sea monster?” Another explanation: His hand founded the leviathan, as in (Prov. 8: 25), “before the hills I was created (חוללתי).”

verses.hebrew_full: הֶן־אֵ֤לֶּה קְצ֬וֹת דְּרָכָ֗ו וּמַה־שֵּׁ֣מֶץ דָּ֭בָר נִשְׁמַע־בּ֑וֹ וְרַ֥עַם גְּ֝בוּרֹתָ֗ו מִ֣י יִתְבּוֹנָֽן
verses.translation: These are but glimpses of God’s rule,The mere whisper we are able to perceive;Who can absorb the thunder of God’s mighty deeds?
verses.rashi: the outskirts of His ways The easiest and the smallest in comparison to His other qualities. and what implications of any matter can be understood What hint of anything can a man understand from His deeds? Another explanation: שֶמֶץ is like (Exod. 32:25), “to the scandal (לשמצה),” uncomplimentary.

words.eng: v·inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֣סֶף אִ֭יּוֹב שְׂאֵ֥ת מְשָׁל֗וֹ וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Job again took up his theme and said:
verses.rashi: Then Job again His friends refrained from answering as Elihu reprimanded them, for they stood and replied no more (32:3).

verses.hebrew_full: חַי־אֵ֭ל הֵסִ֣יר מִשְׁפָּטִ֑י וְ֝שַׁדַּ֗י הֵמַ֥ר נַפְשִֽׁי
verses.translation: By God who has deprived me of justice!By Shaddai who has embittered my life!
verses.rashi: As God lives I swear that indeed He took away my right. From here Rabbi Joshua derived that Job served the Omnipresent out of love. No one swears by the king’s life unless he loves the king.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּי־כׇל־ע֣וֹד נִשְׁמָתִ֣י בִ֑י וְר֖וּחַ אֱל֣וֹהַּ בְּאַפִּֽי
verses.translation: As long as there is life in me,And God’s breath is in my nostrils,

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־תְּדַבֵּ֣רְנָה שְׂפָתַ֣י עַוְלָ֑ה וּ֝לְשׁוֹנִ֗י אִם־יֶהְגֶּ֥ה רְמִיָּֽה
verses.translation: My lips will speak no wrong,Nor my tongue utter deceit.

verses.hebrew_full: חָלִ֣ילָה לִּי֮ אִם־אַצְדִּ֢יק אֶ֫תְכֶ֥ם עַד־אֶגְוָ֑ע לֹֽא־אָסִ֖יר תֻּמָּתִ֣י מִמֶּֽנִּי
verses.translation: Far be it from me to say you are right;Until I die I will maintain my integrity.
verses.rashi: that I should justify you That I should say that you are right in condemning me. I will not give up my innocence I will not concede to your words and say that I am not innocent.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: בְּצִדְקָתִ֣י הֶ֭חֱזַקְתִּי וְלֹ֣א אַרְפֶּ֑הָ לֹא־יֶחֱרַ֥ף לְ֝בָבִ֗י מִיָּמָֽי
verses.translation: I persist in my righteousness and will not yield;I shall be free of reproach as long as I live.
verses.rashi: I have maintained my righteousness To say that I am righteous. my heart will not reproach me because of my days, i.e., because of any manner or trait with which I deported myself in my days. Therefore, perforce, my enemy shall be like a wicked man.

verses.hebrew_full: יְהִ֣י כְ֭רָשָׁע אֹ֣יְבִ֑י וּמִתְקוֹמְמִ֥י כְעַוָּֽל
verses.translation: May my enemy be as the wicked;My assailant, as the wrongdoer.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י מַה־תִּקְוַ֣ת חָ֭נֵף כִּ֣י יִבְצָ֑ע כִּ֤י יֵ֖שֶׁל אֱל֣וֹהַּ נַפְשֽׁוֹ
verses.translation: For what hope has the impious man when he is cut down,When God takes away his life?
verses.rashi: For what is the hope of a flatterer For why should I be a wicked man and a robber? What is the end of a wicked man who takes money by force? when God casts off Heb. ישל. When the Omnipresent casts his soul off him. This is an expression of (Exod. 3:5), “Remove (של) your shoes”; (Deut. 19:5), “and the iron [head] slips off (ונשל).”

verses.hebrew_full: הֲֽ֭צַעֲקָתוֹ יִשְׁמַ֥ע אֵ֑ל כִּֽי־תָב֖וֹא עָלָ֣יו צָרָֽה
verses.translation: Will God hear his cryWhen trouble comes upon him,

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־עַל־שַׁדַּ֥י יִתְעַנָּ֑ג יִקְרָ֖א אֱל֣וֹהַּ בְּכׇל־עֵֽת
verses.translation: When he seeks the favor of Shaddai,Calls upon God at all times?
verses.rashi: Or will he delight with the Almighty The double interrogative is like all double interrogatives. the first one is customarily prefixed by a “hey” and the second one by אִם ; e.g., (Amos 6:12), “Will horses run (הירצון) on the rock, or (אִם) will one plow with cattle?” (Job 6:30), “Is there (היש) injustice in my tongue, or (אִם) will my palate not understand wickedness?” Here, too, will God hearken to his cry...or will he delight with the Almighty? Will one who is a wicked man or a flatterer perhaps be able to delight and rely on the salvations of the Holy One, blessed be He, and call to Him in time of trouble? This is a question.

verses.hebrew_full: אוֹרֶ֣ה אֶתְכֶ֣ם בְּיַד־אֵ֑ל אֲשֶׁ֥ר עִם־שַׁ֝דַּ֗י לֹ֣א אֲכַחֵֽד
verses.translation: I will teach you what is in God’s power,And what is with Shaddai I will not conceal.
verses.rashi: I will instruct you what the standard is that is in God’s hand, and the way... what is with the Almighty I will not conceal Like (above 23:10), “Because He knows the way that is with me.”

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: הֵן־אַתֶּ֣ם כֻּלְּכֶ֣ם חֲזִיתֶ֑ם וְלָמָּה־זֶּ֝֗ה הֶ֣בֶל תֶּהְבָּֽלוּ
verses.translation: All of you have seen it,So why talk nonsense?
verses.rashi: all of you have seen that this is the portion of a wicked man. now why do you become altogether vain? To be wicked and hypocritical.

words.eng: they·sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: זֶ֤ה חֵלֶק־אָדָ֖ם רָשָׁ֥ע עִם־אֵ֑ל וְֽנַחֲלַ֥ת עָ֝רִיצִ֗ים מִשַּׁדַּ֥י יִקָּֽחוּ
verses.translation: This is the evil man’s portion from God,The lot that the ruthless receive from Shaddai:

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־יִרְבּ֣וּ בָנָ֣יו לְמוֹ־חָ֑רֶב וְ֝צֶאֱצָאָ֗יו לֹ֣א יִשְׂבְּעוּ־לָֽחֶם
verses.translation: Should he have many children—they are marked for the sword;His descendants will never have their fill of bread;
verses.rashi: If his sons increase they will increase for the sword. in death They will die amidst suffering and sicknesses, until his widows will be happy at their death and will not weep.

verses.hebrew_full: שְׂ֭רִידָו בַּמָּ֣וֶת יִקָּבֵ֑רוּ וְ֝אַלְמְנֹתָ֗יו לֹ֣א תִבְכֶּֽינָה
verses.translation: Those who survive him will be buried in a plague,And their widows will not weep;

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־יִצְבֹּ֣ר כֶּעָפָ֣ר כָּ֑סֶף וְ֝כַחֹ֗מֶר יָכִ֥ין מַלְבּֽוּשׁ
verses.translation: Should he pile up silver like dust,Lay up clothing like dirt—

verses.hebrew_full: יָ֭כִין וְצַדִּ֣יק יִלְבָּ֑שׁ וְ֝כֶ֗סֶף נָקִ֥י יַחֲלֹֽק
verses.translation: He may lay it up, but the righteous will wear it,And the innocent will share the silver.
verses.rashi: and He will distribute [his] money to the innocent The Holy One, blessed be He, will distribute the wicked man’s money to an innocent one.

verses.hebrew_full: בָּנָ֣ה כָעָ֣שׁ בֵּית֑וֹ וּ֝כְסֻכָּ֗ה עָשָׂ֥ה נֹצֵֽר
verses.translation: The house he built is like a bird’s nest,Like the booth a watchman makes.
verses.rashi: He builds...as a moth As a moth, who builds his hole which does not endure, and like a booth that the watchman of the fig tree and the vineyard makes, which is not permanent. (Another explanation: that the watchman of the vineyard, who does not watch it at all times, made. I heard this. Absent in certain editions.)

verses.hebrew_full: עָשִׁ֣יר יִ֭שְׁכַּב וְלֹ֣א יֵאָסֵ֑ף עֵינָ֖יו פָּקַ֣ח וְאֵינֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: He lies down, a rich man, with [his wealth] intact;When he opens his eyes it is gone.
verses.rashi: He dies rich He will die with his riches, but sometimes he will not be gathered in to burial.

verses.hebrew_full: תַּשִּׂיגֵ֣הוּ כַ֭מַּיִם בַּלָּה֑וֹת לַ֝֗יְלָה גְּנָבַ֥תּוּ סוּפָֽה
verses.translation: Terror overtakes him like a flood;A storm wind makes off with him by night.
verses.rashi: Terrors Demons. a tempest snatches him away and casts him away in disgrace.

verses.hebrew_full: יִשָּׂאֵ֣הוּ קָדִ֣ים וְיֵלַ֑ךְ וִ֝ישָׂעֲרֵ֗הוּ מִמְּקֹמֽוֹ
verses.translation: The east wind carries him far away, and he is gone;It sweeps him from his place.

verses.hebrew_full: וְיַשְׁלֵ֣ךְ עָ֭לָיו וְלֹ֣א יַחְמֹ֑ל מִ֝יָּד֗וֹ בָּר֥וֹחַ יִבְרָֽח
verses.translation: Then it hurls itself at him without mercy;He tries to escape from its force.
verses.rashi: And he will cast upon him i.e., the one who casts evil upon him will show no compassion. from his hand of the wicked man, and from his strength. from his place. he will flee His counselor, who assisted him from the start, will flee from assisting him at the time of his downfall, as Charbonah did to Haman (Esther 7:9).

verses.hebrew_full: יִשְׂפֹּ֣ק עָלֵ֣ימוֹ כַפֵּ֑ימוֹ וְיִשְׁרֹ֥ק עָ֝לָ֗יו מִמְּקֹמֽוֹ
verses.translation: It claps its hands at him,And whistles at him from its place.
verses.rashi: They clap their hands over them Those who knew [him] long ago and now see his ruin.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י יֵ֣שׁ לַכֶּ֣סֶף מוֹצָ֑א וּ֝מָק֗וֹם לַזָּהָ֥ב יָזֹֽקּוּ
verses.translation: There is a mine for silver,And a place where gold is refined.
verses.rashi: For silver has a mine This, too, is another reason for his first statement that he said (27:6), “I have maintained my righteousness,” for why should I be wicked? If for silver or gold, each one has a source and an end, but from where does wisdom come? This is the end of the chapter; that is the most precious of all. Therefore, all my life, I set my heart on studying it. and...a place Gold has [a place] whence they refine and smelt it.

words.eng: act or place of going out or forth

verses.hebrew_full: בַּ֭רְזֶל מֵעָפָ֣ר יֻקָּ֑ח וְ֝אֶ֗בֶן יָצ֥וּק נְחוּשָֽׁה
verses.translation: Iron is taken out of the earth,And copper smelted from rock.
verses.rashi: and copper is smelted from stone From the mountain the smith smelts the copper. Hence, everything has a beginning and an end, for the whole world will end with all that is therein.

verses.hebrew_full: קֵ֤ץ שָׂ֤ם לַחֹ֗שֶׁךְ וּֽלְכׇל־תַּ֭כְלִית ה֣וּא חוֹקֵ֑ר אֶ֖בֶן אֹ֣פֶל וְצַלְמָֽוֶת
verses.translation: Bounds for darkness are set,Every limit is probed,To rocks in deepest darkness.
verses.rashi: He made an end The Omnipresent [made an end] when there will be darkness, for the heavens shall vanish and the earth shall rot away, and He fathoms the end of everything. a stone A stone whence darkness and the shadow of death emanate. A stone is the place of the source of trouble. For this reason, a place of trouble is called a stone, like a man who dashes his foot against a stone, who suffers pain. So I heard. This is like (Isa. 34:11), “a line of waste and stones of destruction”; (Dan. 2:45), “that from the mountain a stone was cut.”

verses.hebrew_full: פָּ֤רַץ נַ֨חַל מֵֽעִם־גָּ֗ר הַֽנִּשְׁכָּחִ֥ים מִנִּי־רָ֑גֶל דַּ֖לּוּ מֵאֱנ֣וֹשׁ נָֽעוּ
verses.translation: A shaft was forged where no one lives,[In places] forgotten by wayfarers,Destitute of mortals, far removed.
verses.rashi: A stream burst forth on Sodom and Gemorrah, streams of fire and brimstone. from the place of its flow Heb. מעם גר, from the place from which it gushes and flows (נוגר). those who cause the foot to be forgotten These are the people of Sodom, who caused the feet of wayfarers to be forgotten from their land, for they would lay the wayfarer in a bed. If he was too long, they would cut off his feet, and if he was too short, they would stretch him. In this manner it is explained in the Aggadah. they are lifted up through the bursting forth of the stream, and they are moved away from man.

verses.hebrew_full: אֶ֗רֶץ מִמֶּ֥נָּה יֵֽצֵא־לָ֑חֶם וְ֝תַחְתֶּ֗יהָ נֶהְפַּ֥ךְ כְּמוֹ־אֵֽשׁ
verses.translation: Earth, out of which food grows,Is changed below as if into fire.
verses.rashi: A land—that from [it] bread emerged its place was as though turned into fire.

verses.hebrew_full: מְקוֹם־סַפִּ֥יר אֲבָנֶ֑יהָ וְעַפְרֹ֖ת זָהָ֣ב לֽוֹ
verses.translation: Its rocks are a source of sapphires;It contains gold dust too.
verses.rashi: a place of sapphire its stones were.

verses.hebrew_full: נָ֭תִיב לֹא־יְדָ֣עוֹ עָ֑יִט וְלֹ֥א שְׁ֝זָפַ֗תּוּ עֵ֣ין אַיָּֽה
verses.translation: No bird of prey knows the path to it;The falcon’s eye has not gazed upon it.
verses.rashi: A path unknown to brigands Heb. עיט. A band of robbers never passed by them. and which the eye of a vulture did not see Since it sees more than other birds, and is therefore called רָאָה, because it stands in Babylon and sees a carcass in Eretz Israel. That is how it is explained in the Aggadah of Hullin (632); i.e., spies never discerned it.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹֽא־הִדְרִיכ֥וּהוּ בְנֵי־שָׁ֑חַץ לֹֽא־עָדָ֖ה עָלָ֣יו שָֽׁחַל
verses.translation: The proud beasts have not reached it;The lion has not crossed it.
verses.rashi: neither did... pass by it Heb. עדה, passed, as we translate (Gen. 15 17), “which passed between these parts,” דִּעֲדָה. Proud beasts Heb. (שַחַץ) and lion (שַחַל) are some of the seven names of the lion (Sanh. 95a: The lion has seven names.) Now what did the Holy One, blessed be He, do?...

verses.hebrew_full: בַּ֭חַלָּמִישׁ שָׁלַ֣ח יָד֑וֹ הָפַ֖ךְ מִשֹּׁ֣רֶשׁ הָרִֽים
verses.translation: The hand is set against the flinty rock,And mountains are overturned by the roots.
verses.rashi: upon the flinty rock and turned over the roots of the mountains from top to bottom.

verses.hebrew_full: בַּ֭צּוּרוֹת יְאֹרִ֣ים בִּקֵּ֑עַ וְכׇל־יְ֝קָ֗ר רָאֲתָ֥ה עֵינֽוֹ
verses.translation: Channels are carved through rock,And every precious thing can be seen;
verses.rashi: In the rocks, He split rivers He refers back to the beginning of his topic, that he said that everything has a source. He split the rocks for the source of the rivers, and so, “His eye saw every precious thing,” whence it would emerge for the creatures, [as He did for Israel; He struck a rock and its water flowed.]

verses.hebrew_full: מִ֭בְּכִי נְהָר֣וֹת חִבֵּ֑שׁ וְ֝תַעֲלֻמָ֗הּ יֹ֣צִא אֽוֹר
verses.translation: The sources of the streams are dammed upSo all that is hidden may be brought to light.
verses.rashi: He bound up the depths of the rivers Heb. מבכי. In the Creation of the world, when the lower waters wept (בכו). the depths Heb. מבכי, like (below 38:16), “the depths of (נבכי) the sea.” bound up Heb. חבש, like (Gen. 22:3), “and saddled (ויחבש) his donkey.” I did not hear this. [Other editions: Because of the weeping etc. Because of the weeping of Israel, who were weeping, saying, “What will we drink,” He bound up and fixed for them water from the rock. (Lev. 8:13),”And he fixed hats (ויחבש) for them,” is translated into Aramaic as ואתקין.] and [from] Taalumah He brings out light There is a window in the firmament, named Taalumah, from which the sun develops in its orbit [in Pirke d’Rabbi Eliezer] (ch. 6). That is to say that everything has a source and an end; only wisdom has no source except from His mouth, and never an end.

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽ֭הַחׇכְמָה מֵאַ֣יִן תִּמָּצֵ֑א וְאֵ֥י זֶ֝֗ה מְק֣וֹם בִּינָֽה
verses.translation: But where can wisdom be found;Where is the source of understanding?
verses.rashi: But wisdom i.e., Torah.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־יָדַ֣ע אֱנ֣וֹשׁ עֶרְכָּ֑הּ וְלֹ֥א תִ֝מָּצֵ֗א בְּאֶ֣רֶץ הַחַיִּֽים
verses.translation: No mortal can set a value on it;It cannot be found in the land of the living.
verses.rashi: and it will not be found in the land of the living with one who enlivens himself, but with one who “puts himself to death” with toil and hunger.

verses.hebrew_full: תְּה֣וֹם אָ֭מַר לֹ֣א בִי־הִ֑יא וְיָ֥ם אָ֝מַ֗ר אֵ֣ין עִמָּדִֽי
verses.translation: The deep says, “It is not in me”;The sea says, “I do not have it.”
verses.rashi: The deep says, “It is not in me,” If you ask those who dive into the deep, who deal with pearls, with the source of pearls and with gold and silver mines from the place of the depths of the earth, they will tell you, “It is not in me.” I am not well-versed in halachic instruction. and the sea says And those who go down to the sea for business will say to you, “Wisdom is not with me.” Why? Because they cannot purchase it like other merchandise, to give of their wealth for its price. And in the Aggadah (see Shabbath 89a), it is stated that at the time of the giving of the Torah, Satan came before the Holy One, blessed be He, and said to Him, “Where is the Torah?” He replied, “With the son of Amram.” He came to Moses, and said to him, “Where is the Torah?” “With the sea.” He came to the sea and asked it, “Where is the Torah?” “It is not in me. Go to the son of Amram.” He came to the son of Amram and said to him, “Where is the Torah?” “With the deep.” He came to the deep and asked, “Where is the Torah?” It replied, “It is not with me. Go to the son of Amram.” He came to the son of Amram and asked, “Where is the Torah?” “With the Holy One, blessed be He.” This is what the deep says, “It is not in me,” and the sea says, “It is not with me.”

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־יֻתַּ֣ן סְג֣וֹר תַּחְתֶּ֑יהָ וְלֹ֥א יִ֝שָּׁקֵ֗ל כֶּ֣סֶף מְחִירָֽהּ
verses.translation: It cannot be bartered for gold;Silver cannot be paid out as its price.
verses.rashi: Fine gold cannot be given Heb. סְגוּר. This is synonymous with זהב סגוּר, lit. closed gold. This is fine gold, for when it is open, all gold establishments close.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־תְ֭סֻלֶּה בְּכֶ֣תֶם אוֹפִ֑יר בְּשֹׁ֖הַם יָקָ֣ר וְסַפִּֽיר
verses.translation: The finest gold of Ophir cannot be weighed against it,Nor precious onyx, nor sapphire.
verses.rashi: It cannot be praised Heb. תסלה, an expression of (Ps. 68:5), “Extol Him (סלו) Who rides in Aravoth.” He who praises it saying, “It is as esteemed as the gold of Ophir and as precious onyx,”—this is no praise for it. Similar to this is (Lam. 4:2), “praised (המסלאים) with fine gold.”

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־יַעַרְכֶ֣נָּה זָ֭הָב וּזְכוֹכִ֑ית וּתְמ֖וּרָתָ֣הּ כְּלִי־פָֽז
verses.translation: Gold or glass cannot match its value,Nor vessels of fine gold be exchanged for it.
verses.rashi: gold Pure gold that shines like pearls. its exchange is not a pure golden vessel.

verses.hebrew_full: רָאמ֣וֹת וְ֭גָבִישׁ לֹ֣א יִזָּכֵ֑ר וּמֶ֥שֶׁךְ חׇ֝כְמָ֗ה מִפְּנִינִֽים
verses.translation: Coral and crystal cannot be mentioned with it;A pouch of wisdom is better than rubies.
verses.rashi: Coral and crystal They are names of precious stones in the sea. cannot be mentioned in comparison to it.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹֽא־יַ֭עַרְכֶנָּה פִּטְדַת־כּ֑וּשׁ בְּכֶ֥תֶם טָ֝ה֗וֹר לֹ֣א תְסֻלֶּֽה
verses.translation: Topaz from Cush cannot match its value;Pure gold cannot be weighed against it.
verses.rashi: The topaz of Kush It is a precious stone, as in (Exod. 28:17), “ruby, topaz (פטדה).” with...jewelry Heb. בכתם. That is a collection of jewelry for women’s adornments, and this collection is called כֶּתֶם in Hebrew, and “al halls” in Arabic. That is (Prov. 25:12) “Like a golden earring and jewelry of the finest gold (וחלי כתם),” and similarly (Hosea 2:15), “and adorned herself with her earrings and her jewelry (וחליתה).” Others interpret כֶּתֶם like כֶּתֶר, a crown, but it is incorrect because we do not find a verb with this root, as we find with כֶּתֶר. מַכְתִּיר, to crown or surround, כותרות capitals.

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽ֭הַחׇכְמָה מֵאַ֣יִן תָּב֑וֹא וְאֵ֥י זֶ֝֗ה מְק֣וֹם בִּינָֽה
verses.translation: But whence does wisdom come?Where is the source of understanding?

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽ֭נֶעֶלְמָה מֵעֵינֵ֣י כׇל־חָ֑י וּמֵע֖וֹף הַשָּׁמַ֣יִם נִסְתָּֽרָה
verses.translation: It is hidden from the eyes of all living,Concealed from the fowl of heaven.
verses.rashi: and from the winged creatures of the heavens The angels who fly.

verses.hebrew_full: אֲבַדּ֣וֹן וָ֭מָוֶת אָ֣מְר֑וּ בְּ֝אׇזְנֵ֗ינוּ שָׁמַ֥עְנוּ שִׁמְעָֽהּ
verses.translation: Abaddon and Death say,“We have only a report of it.”
verses.rashi: Destruction and death said Those who destroy themselves and kill themselves over it said, “With our ears we heard its report that if one toils with it, it endures with him.” And others say as in the Aggadah that I explained above, that Satan came before the Omnipresent and asked Him, “Where is the Torah?” He said to him etc. That is what is written, “Destruction and death said, ‘With our ears we heard its report that it was given to Israel.’ But...”

verses.hebrew_full: אֱ֭לֹהִים הֵבִ֣ין דַּרְכָּ֑הּ וְ֝ה֗וּא יָדַ֥ע אֶת־מְקוֹמָֽהּ
verses.translation: God understands the way to it—Knowing its source,
verses.rashi: God understood its way, and He knew its place For He knows where it is situated, and they will also praise it and say of it that God understood its way; He looked at it and created the world with its letters in their order and according to their weight. He created all creatures as it is written in the secret of Sefer Yetzirah.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·standing place

verses.hebrew_full: כִּי־ה֭וּא לִקְצוֹת־הָאָ֣רֶץ יַבִּ֑יט תַּ֖חַת כׇּל־הַשָּׁמַ֣יִם יִרְאֶֽה
verses.translation: Seeing to the ends of the earth,Observing all that is beneath the heavens.
verses.rashi: For He looks to the ends of the earth how He should build them and create them, and under all the heavens He saw in it the needs of their creation.

verses.hebrew_full: לַעֲשׂ֣וֹת לָר֣וּחַ מִשְׁקָ֑ל וּ֝מַ֗יִם תִּכֵּ֥ן בְּמִדָּֽה
verses.translation: When [God] fixed the weight of the winds,Set the measure of the waters,
verses.rashi: To make with it a weight for the wind, for each land according to its strength, and so the water according to its measure, according to what each land required to water it. There is an arid land which requires much rain, and there is a land that does not require so much.

verses.hebrew_full: בַּעֲשֹׂת֣וֹ לַמָּטָ֣ר חֹ֑ק וְ֝דֶ֗רֶךְ לַחֲזִ֥יז קֹלֽוֹת
verses.translation: And made a rule for the rainAnd a course for the thunderstorms,
verses.rashi: a decree A set decree according to all the lands, and a spring and rivulet to water each land by His making a way for all the clouds, so that two thunders do not emerge through one hole, lest it destroy the world.

verses.hebrew_full: אָ֣ז רָ֭אָהּ וַֽיְסַפְּרָ֑הּ הֱ֝כִינָ֗הּ וְגַם־חֲקָרָֽהּ
verses.translation: Then [God] saw it and gauged it,Measured it and probed it.
verses.rashi: Then He saw He saw it, looked at it, and with its counsel made everything. and counted it He counted its letters, the double ones [such as בּב] and the simple ones, the first one (א), middle one (מ), and the final one (ת). אֶמֶת, is the seal of the Holy One, blessed be He. And so with the remaining orders He created everything with these letters. Everything is explained in the secret of Sefer Yetzirah. prepared it He prepared it [wisdom] for the Creation.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר לָאָדָ֗ם הֵ֤ן יִרְאַ֣ת אֲ֭דֹנָי הִ֣יא חׇכְמָ֑ה וְס֖וּר מֵרָ֣ע בִּינָֽה
verses.translation: [God] said to humankind,“See! Fear of the Sovereign is wisdom;To shun evil is understanding.”
verses.rashi: Behold, the fear of the Lord is wisdom One requires the other, and wisdom is unseemly without [the] fear [of the Lord].

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֣סֶף אִ֭יּוֹב שְׂאֵ֥ת מְשָׁל֗וֹ וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Job again took up his theme and said:

verses.hebrew_full: מִֽי־יִתְּנֵ֥נִי כְיַרְחֵי־קֶ֑דֶם כִּ֝ימֵ֗י אֱל֣וֹהַּ יִשְׁמְרֵֽנִי
verses.translation: O that I were as in months gone by,In the days when God watched over me,
verses.rashi: Would that I were as [in] my early months Like the early days, when I was in my greatness.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּהִלּ֣וֹ נֵ֭רוֹ עֲלֵ֣י רֹאשִׁ֑י לְ֝אוֹר֗וֹ אֵ֣לֶךְ חֹֽשֶׁךְ
verses.translation: When God’s lamp shone over my head,When I walked in the dark by its light,
verses.rashi: when He lit His candle [This is] an expression of light and joy. by His light I would go. In every occasion of darkness and trouble that came to the world, I had light.

verses.hebrew_full: כַּאֲשֶׁ֣ר הָ֭יִיתִי בִּימֵ֣י חׇרְפִּ֑י בְּס֥וֹד אֱ֝ל֗וֹהַּ עֲלֵ֣י אׇהֳלִֽי
verses.translation: When I was in my prime,When God’s company graced my tent,
verses.rashi: in my early days Heb. חרפי, in my early days. This is an Aramaism, early crops and late crops (חרפי ואפלי). when God’s counsel was upon my tent When the righteous of the generation would come to my tent, to take counsel in the words of the Holy One, blessed be He, in the Torah and in enacting temporary safeguards.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּע֣וֹד שַׁ֭דַּי עִמָּדִ֑י סְבִ֖יבוֹתַ֣י נְעָרָֽי
verses.translation: When Shaddai was still with me,When my attendants surrounded me,
verses.rashi: my young men My servants.

verses.hebrew_full: בִּרְחֹ֣ץ הֲלִיכַ֣י בְּחֵמָ֑ה וְצ֥וּר יָצ֥וּק עִ֝מָּדִ֗י פַּלְגֵי־שָֽׁמֶן
verses.translation: When my feet were bathed in cream,And rocks poured out streams of oil for me.
verses.rashi: my feet Heb. הליכי, my feet. with cream Heb. בְּחֵמָה, like בחמאה. and the rock that was with me would pour forth rivulets of oil i.e., all the desires of my heart, which are as pleasing as rivulets of oil. would pour as in (Lev. 2:6), “and you shall pour.”

verses.hebrew_full: בְּצֵ֣אתִי שַׁ֣עַר עֲלֵי־קָ֑רֶת בָּ֝רְח֗וֹב אָכִ֥ין מוֹשָׁבִֽי
verses.translation: When I passed through the city gatesTo take my seat in the square,
verses.rashi: the ceiling a high ceiling, prepared for a chair for the elders of the city to sit on. and when I was in the square, I would prepare my seat.

words.eng: broad or open place or plaza

verses.hebrew_full: רָא֣וּנִי נְעָרִ֣ים וְנֶחְבָּ֑אוּ וִ֝ישִׁישִׁ֗ים קָ֣מוּ עָמָֽדוּ
verses.translation: Young men saw me and hid,Elders rose and stood;
verses.rashi: Youths saw me They would see me and hide, and old men stood in my presence.

verses.hebrew_full: שָׂ֭רִים עָצְר֣וּ בְמִלִּ֑ים וְ֝כַ֗ף יָשִׂ֥ימוּ לְפִיהֶֽם
verses.translation: Nobles held back their words;They clapped their hands to their mouths.
verses.rashi: Princes refrained from talking and would wait until I started the conversation.

verses.hebrew_full: קוֹל־נְגִידִ֥ים נֶחְבָּ֑אוּ וּ֝לְשׁוֹנָ֗ם לְחִכָּ֥ם דָּבֵֽקָה
verses.translation: The voices of princes were hushed;Their tongues stuck to their palates.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י אֹ֣זֶן שָׁ֭מְעָה וַֽתְּאַשְּׁרֵ֑נִי וְעַ֥יִן רָ֝אֲתָ֗ה וַתְּעִידֵֽנִי
verses.translation: The ear that heard me acclaimed me;The eye that saw, commended me.
verses.rashi: For the ear that heard my word praised me. and the eye [that] saw my greatness and the propriety of my traits in all places.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־אֲ֭מַלֵּט עָנִ֣י מְשַׁוֵּ֑עַ וְ֝יָת֗וֹם וְֽלֹא־עֹזֵ֥ר לֽוֹ
verses.translation: For I saved the pauper who cried out,The orphan who had no helper.

verses.hebrew_full: בִּרְכַּ֣ת אֹ֭בֵד עָלַ֣י תָּבֹ֑א וְלֵ֖ב אַלְמָנָ֣ה אַרְנִֽן
verses.translation: I received the blessing of the lost;I gladdened the heart of the widow.
verses.rashi: The blessing of the lost who died because of his iniquity and was banished. would come upon me because I would sustain his children and his wife. Our Sages said, however, that he [Job] would rob the orphans of their field, improve it and [then] return it to them (Baba Bathra 16a). But the simple meaning of the verse does not appear to be so, for no one is called אובד, but a thing that is itself lost, like (Ps. 31:13), “like a lost vessel”; and so (ibid. 119:176), “I have gone astray like a lost sheep”, (Jer. 50:6), “lost sheep.” and I would make the widow’s heart sing for joy for he would attach his name to widows, saying, “She is my kinswoman,” or “I will marry her,” in order that men should be anxious to marry her.

verses.hebrew_full: צֶ֣דֶק לָ֭בַשְׁתִּי וַיִּלְבָּשֵׁ֑נִי כִּֽמְעִ֥יל וְ֝צָנִ֗יף מִשְׁפָּטִֽי
verses.translation: I clothed myself in righteousness and it robed me;Justice was my cloak and turban.
verses.rashi: I put on righteousness and it clothed me I pursued righteousness and it is present with me as a coat and a turban, which are beautiful ornaments; so was my judgment beautiful and pure.

verses.hebrew_full: עֵינַ֣יִם הָ֭יִיתִי לַעִוֵּ֑ר וְרַגְלַ֖יִם לַפִּסֵּ֣חַ אָֽנִי
verses.translation: I was eyes to the blindAnd feet to the lame.

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: אָ֣ב אָ֭נֹכִֽי לָאֶבְיוֹנִ֑ים וְרִ֖ב לֹא־יָדַ֣עְתִּי אֶחְקְרֵֽהוּ
verses.translation: I was a father to the needy,And I looked into the case of the stranger.

verses.hebrew_full: וָ֭אֲשַׁבְּרָה מְתַלְּע֣וֹת עַוָּ֑ל וּ֝מִשִּׁנָּ֗יו אַשְׁלִ֥יךְ טָֽרֶף
verses.translation: I broke the jaws of the wrongdoer,And I wrested prey from his teeth.

verses.hebrew_full: וָ֭אֹמַר עִם־קִנִּ֣י אֶגְוָ֑ע וְ֝כַח֗וֹל אַרְבֶּ֥ה יָמִֽים
verses.translation: I thought I would end my days with my family, And be as long-lived as the phoenix,
verses.rashi: And I said, I will perish with my nest Because of the propriety of my traits, I would think to myself that when I die, I would have my nest and my house established with me. and I will multiply days as the phoenix Heb. וכחול. This is a bird named חול, phoenix, upon which the punishment of death was not decreed because it did not taste of the Tree of Knowledge, and at the end of one thousand years, it renews itself and returns to its youth.

verses.hebrew_full: שׇׁרְשִׁ֣י פָת֣וּחַ אֱלֵי־מָ֑יִם וְ֝טַ֗ל יָלִ֥ין בִּקְצִירִֽי
verses.translation: My roots reaching water,And dew lying on my branches;
verses.rashi: My root will be open etc. All this I was saying to myself, that my root would not dry up, and my branches would not die, like (above 14:9), “and it will produce a branch (קציר).” They are יונקות, tender branches of a tree.

verses.hebrew_full: כְּ֭בוֹדִי חָדָ֣שׁ עִמָּדִ֑י וְ֝קַשְׁתִּ֗י בְּיָדִ֥י תַחֲלִֽיף
verses.translation: My vigor refreshed,My bow ever new in my hand.
verses.rashi: And I thought that my honor would be renewed. which is with meconstantly. and my bow And my strength will constantly change for the better.

verses.hebrew_full: לִֽי־שָׁמְע֥וּ וְיִחֵ֑לּוּ וְ֝יִדְּמ֗וּ לְמ֣וֹ עֲצָתִֽי
verses.translation: Men would listen to me expectantly,And wait for my counsel.
verses.rashi: They listened to me and waited Those who listened to my words would wait until they would be realized, and they would be realized because the tradition of wisdom was in my hand.

verses.hebrew_full: אַחֲרֵ֣י דְ֭בָרִי לֹ֣א יִשְׁנ֑וּ וְ֝עָלֵ֗ימוֹ תִּטֹּ֥ף מִלָּתִֽי
verses.translation: After I spoke they had nothing to say;My words were as drops [of dew] upon them.
verses.rashi: my speech would drip Heb. תטף. This is an expression of prophecy. Similarly (Amos 7:16), “and do not prophesy (תטיף) concerning the house of Isaac.” That is to say that my word was like prophecy.

verses.hebrew_full: וְיִחֲל֣וּ כַמָּטָ֣ר לִ֑י וּ֝פִיהֶ֗ם פָּעֲר֥וּ לְמַלְקֽוֹשׁ
verses.translation: They waited for me as for rain,For the late rain, their mouths open wide.
verses.rashi: after a long time After a long time they would speak, because most of their time was spent in silence, for they were anxiously waiting to hear my words.

verses.hebrew_full: אֶשְׂחַ֣ק אֲ֭לֵהֶם לֹ֣א יַאֲמִ֑ינוּ וְא֥וֹר פָּ֝נַ֗י לֹ֣א יַפִּילֽוּן
verses.translation: When I smiled at them, they would not believe it;They never expected as from pll; cf. Gen. 48.11. a sign of my favor.
verses.rashi: they would not believe it that I was jesting with them, because of my esteem[ed position]. and they would not cast down the light of my countenance They were afraid to get near me and to become familiar with me.

verses.hebrew_full: אֶ֥בְחַ֣ר דַּרְכָּם֮ וְאֵשֵׁ֢ב רֹ֥֫אשׁ וְ֭אֶשְׁכּוֹן כְּמֶ֣לֶךְ בַּגְּד֑וּד כַּאֲשֶׁ֖ר אֲבֵלִ֣ים יְנַחֵֽם
verses.translation: I decided their course and presided over them;I lived like a king among his troops,Like one who consoles mourners.
verses.rashi: I would choose their way All their ways and their counsel I would choose for them, for they would ask me, “Which way shall we go and what shall we do?” and sit at the head if they were reclining at a banquet, I would recline at the head of the diners.

verses.hebrew_full: וְעַתָּ֤ה שָׂ֥חֲק֣וּ עָלַי֮ צְעִירִ֥ים מִמֶּ֗נִּי לְיָ֫מִ֥ים אֲשֶׁר־מָאַ֥סְתִּי אֲבוֹתָ֑ם לָ֝שִׁ֗ית עִם־כַּלְבֵ֥י צֹאנִֽי
verses.translation: But now those younger than I deride me,Those whose fathers I would have disdained to put among my sheep dogs.

verses.hebrew_full: גַּם־כֹּ֣חַ יְ֭דֵיהֶם לָ֣מָּה לִּ֑י עָ֝לֵ֗ימוֹ אָ֣בַד כָּֽלַח
verses.translation: Of what use to me is the strength of their hands?All their vigor is gone.
verses.rashi: Moreover, why did I need the strength of their hands in those days they were wicked, and they were useless, for trouble came because of them. old age Heb. כָּלַח, old age.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּחֶ֥סֶר וּבְכָפָ֗ן גַּ֫לְמ֥וּד הַעֹרְקִ֥ים צִיָּ֑ה אֶ֝֗מֶשׁ שׁוֹאָ֥ה וּמְשֹׁאָֽה
verses.translation: Wasted from want and starvation,They flee to a parched land,To the gloom of desolate wasteland.
verses.rashi: Because of want and because of hunger they were in solitude. they would flee to desolation For in his days, they would drive them from civilization to go in darkness. darkness of waste and desolation broine in Old French, obscurity.

verses.hebrew_full: הַקֹּטְפִ֣ים מַלּ֣וּחַ עֲלֵי־שִׂ֑יחַ וְשֹׁ֖רֶשׁ רְתָמִ֣ים לַחְמָֽם
verses.translation: They pluck saltwort and wormwood;The roots of broom are their food.
verses.rashi: They pluck salt-wort on shrubs When they were in the deserts, they would pluck for themselves salt-wort [that grew] on the trees of the forests and eat. salt-wort Heb. מלוח. It is the name of an herb. In Aramaic (Pes. 114a), it is called קַקוּלִין and in the language of the Mishnah מַלוּחִים (malves in French), mallows, as we learned in (Kiddushin 66a): “They brought up mallows on golden tables.” shrubs a tree, like (Gen. 21:15), “under one of the shrubs (השיחים).”

verses.hebrew_full: מִן־גֵּ֥ו יְגֹרָ֑שׁוּ יָרִ֥יעוּ עָ֝לֵ֗ימוֹ כַּגַּנָּֽב
verses.translation: Driven out from society, They are cried at like a thief.
verses.rashi: from the midst From the midst of the city they were driven.

verses.hebrew_full: בַּעֲר֣וּץ נְחָלִ֣ים לִשְׁכֹּ֑ן חֹרֵ֖י עָפָ֣ר וְכֵפִֽים
verses.translation: They live in the gullies of wadis,In holes in the ground, and in rocks,
verses.rashi: in the crevices of the valleys There is a salty land that splits, where they would lie in the holes in the earth and the holes in the crevices of the rocks. and rocks Heb. וכפים, the Aramaic translation of סלעים, rocks. (Another explanation:) in the crevice of the streams That is the crevice that the stream makes around itself.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּין־שִׂיחִ֥ים יִנְהָ֑קוּ תַּ֖חַת חָר֣וּל יְסֻפָּֽחוּ
verses.translation: Braying among the bushes,Huddling among the nettles,
verses.rashi: under the nettles they gather (Orties in French), nettles. They would gather, as in (Isa. 14:1), “and join (ונספחו) the house of Jacob.”

verses.hebrew_full: בְּֽנֵי־נָ֭בָל גַּם־בְּנֵ֣י בְלִי־שֵׁ֑ם נִ֝כְּא֗וּ מִן־הָאָֽרֶץ
verses.translation: Scoundrels, nobodies,Stricken from the earth.
verses.rashi: were broken (in my days) from the land for I would humble them. [נִכְאוּ is] an expression of (Isa. 66:2), “of crushed spirit (נכה רוח).”

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֭עַתָּה נְגִינָתָ֣ם הָיִ֑יתִי וָאֱהִ֖י לָהֶ֣ם לְמִלָּֽה
verses.translation: Now I am the butt of their gibes;I have become a byword to them.

verses.hebrew_full: תִּ֭עֲבוּנִי רָ֣חֲקוּ מֶ֑נִּי וּ֝מִפָּנַ֗י לֹא־חָ֥שְׂכוּ רֹֽק
verses.translation: They abhor me; they keep their distance from me;They do not withhold spittle from my face.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־יִתְרִ֣י (יתרו) פִ֭תַּח וַיְעַנֵּ֑נִי וְ֝רֶ֗סֶן מִפָּנַ֥י שִׁלֵּֽחוּ
verses.translation: Because [God] has disarmed and humbled me,They have thrown off restraint in my presence.
verses.rashi: For He untied my bowstring He untied the string of my bow (that I said above that my bow would become stronger); i.e., He weakened my strength—He Who has the power to do so. and they have cast off the bridle My bridle, with which they were bridled to me—they cast it off before me because they no longer fear me.

verses.hebrew_full: עַל־יָמִין֮ פִּרְחַ֢ח יָ֫ק֥וּמוּ רַגְלַ֥י שִׁלֵּ֑חוּ וַיָּסֹ֥לּוּ עָ֝לַ֗י אׇרְח֥וֹת אֵידָֽם
verses.translation: Mere striplings assail me at my right hand:They put me to flight;They build their roads for my ruin.
verses.rashi: On my right on my right, stand “little sprouts” as [with] any other simple person, because I am no longer so esteemed in their sight [that they] subordinate themselves before Me. they push away my feet If the place is crowded, they push me away. and they cast up against me their ways of destruction Any shameful way [i.e., act] that they have to do, they make their way before me or beside me [to perform it] and they have no fear.

verses.hebrew_full: נָֽתְס֗וּ נְֽתִיבָ֫תִ֥י לְהַוָּתִ֥י יֹעִ֑ילוּ לֹ֖א עֹזֵ֣ר לָֽמוֹ
verses.translation: They tear up my path;They promote my fall,Although it does them no good.
verses.rashi: They break up my path Heb. נתסוּ. [My] good [path] and do not care for it. נתסוּ is like נתצוּ the “sammech” replacing the “zaddi.” they serve to increase my calamity they continue to provoke me.

verses.hebrew_full: כְּפֶ֣רֶץ רָחָ֣ב יֶאֱתָ֑יוּ תַּ֥חַת שֹׁ֝אָ֗ה הִתְגַּלְגָּֽלוּ
verses.translation: They come as through a wide breach;They roll in like raging billows.
verses.rashi: As [through] a wide breach in a fence, through which everyone comes and passes through, so they come together against me to embarrass me. under darkness they roll i.e., they come rolling along in secret until they reach me, to provoke me, so that I should not be able to escape from them.

verses.hebrew_full: הׇהְפַּ֥ךְ עָלַ֗י בַּלָּ֫ה֥וֹת תִּרְדֹּ֣ף כָּ֭רוּחַ נְדִבָתִ֑י וּ֝כְעָ֗ב עָבְרָ֥ה יְשֻׁעָתִֽי
verses.translation: Terror tumbles upon me;It sweeps away my honor like the wind;My dignity taken as related to shoaʻ, “noble.” vanishes like a cloud.
verses.rashi: turned upon me Heb. הָהְפַךְ, [equivalent to] נֶהְפַּךְ. Terrors demons. as a spirit An evil [spirit]. my nobility The noble spirit that rested upon me from the beginning.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: וְעַתָּ֗ה עָ֭לַי תִּשְׁתַּפֵּ֣ךְ נַפְשִׁ֑י יֹ֖אחֲז֣וּנִי יְמֵי־עֹֽנִי
verses.translation: So now my life runs out;Days of misery have taken hold of me.
verses.rashi: And now to this trouble my soul pours out like a man whose spirit is troubled, for... days of affliction in which I am [now] seize me and coerce me to pour out my soul.

verses.hebrew_full: לַ֗יְלָה עֲ֭צָמַי נִקַּ֣ר מֵעָלָ֑י וְ֝עֹרְקַ֗י לֹ֣א יִשְׁכָּבֽוּן
verses.translation: By night my bones feel gnawed;My sinews never rest.
verses.rashi: are picked off Heb. נקר, like (Num. 16:14), “will you put out (תנקר) the eyes of those people?” (forer in French, to bore). The worms pick my flesh off my bones. my sinews Heb. עֹרְקַי. My sinews have no rest. Sinew in Arabic is “orek.” So did Dunash explain it (Teshuvoth Dunash p.85). Another explanation: עֹרְקַי, my pursuers who cause me to flee. (Machbereth Menachem p. 139)

verses.hebrew_full: בְּרׇב־כֹּ֭חַ יִתְחַפֵּ֣שׂ לְבוּשִׁ֑י כְּפִ֖י כֻתׇּנְתִּ֣י יַאַזְרֵֽנִי
verses.translation: With great effort I change clothing;The neck of my tunic fits my waist.
verses.rashi: is my garment changed My “garment” changes, layer after layer. When a person removes his garments and puts on other garments different from the first ones, he calls these בִּגְדֵי חֹפֶשׁ, like (I Kings 22:30), “I will disguise myself (התחפש) and go into battle.” This is translated into Aramaic by the word אשתני, I will change. like the opening of my shirt it girds me Like the opening of my shirt, which girds and encompasses my neck, so do my garments gird me all around.

verses.hebrew_full: הֹרָ֥נִי לַחֹ֑מֶר וָ֝אֶתְמַשֵּׁ֗ל כֶּעָפָ֥ר וָאֵֽפֶר
verses.translation: [God] regarded me as clay,I have become like dust and ashes.
verses.rashi: It directed me to the mud These boils directed me and taught me to sit in the mud, as we said above (2:8). “and he sat down in the ashes.” Another explanation: הוֹרַנִי means: it cast me, an expression of (Exod. 15:4), “He cast (ירה) into the sea.” and I was compared Was likened. And in the Midrash of Rabbi Tanchuma (Buber, Vayishlach 8): Said Rabbi Berechiah, “Transpose the verse.” I was compared to Abraham in my righteousness, who called himself dust and ashes (Gen.18:27), and He judges me like the wicked of the Generation of the Dispersion (who rebelled against Him by building the tower), concerning whom it is written (Gen. 11:3): “and the mortar served them as clay.”

verses.hebrew_full: אֲשַׁוַּ֣ע אֵ֭לֶיךָ וְלֹ֣א תַעֲנֵ֑נִי עָ֝מַ֗דְתִּי וַתִּתְבֹּ֥נֶן בִּֽי
verses.translation: I cry out to You, but You do not answer me;I wait, but You do [not] consider me.
verses.rashi: I stood to see, to remain silent, and to understand, as in (Neh. 8:5), “And when he opened [it], all the people stood,” and no longer replied. and You ponder me You think about me to change my plagues.

verses.hebrew_full: תֵּהָפֵ֣ךְ לְאַכְזָ֣ר לִ֑י בְּעֹ֖צֶם יָדְךָ֣ תִשְׂטְמֵֽנִי
verses.translation: You have become cruel to me;With Your powerful hand You harass me.

verses.hebrew_full: תִּשָּׂאֵ֣נִי אֶל־ר֭וּחַ תַּרְכִּיבֵ֑נִי וּ֝תְמֹגְגֵ֗נִי (תשוה) תּוּשִׁיָּֽה
verses.translation: You lift me up and mount me on the wind;You make my courage melt.
verses.rashi: You lift me up to the spirit of the demons. You cause me to ride [on it], and weakness melts me away Weakness and feebleness of strength melted me away. and weakness melts me away Heb. ותמגגני, a feminine form, the subject of which is תֻּשִיָה, and this “tav” is not like the “tav” of “You lift me up (תשאני).”

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־יָ֭דַעְתִּי מָ֣וֶת תְּשִׁיבֵ֑נִי וּבֵ֖ית מוֹעֵ֣ד לְכׇל־חָֽי
verses.translation: I know You will bring me to death,The house assigned for all the living.
verses.rashi: For I knew For I knew that You would return me to death, a place which is a meeting place for all living, namely the grave.

verses.hebrew_full: אַ֣ךְ לֹֽא־בְ֭עִי יִשְׁלַח־יָ֑ד אִם־בְּ֝פִיד֗וֹ לָהֶ֥ן שֽׁוּעַ
verses.translation: Surely [God] would not strike at a ruinIf, in calamity, one cried out.
verses.rashi: But not with destruction was the Judge accustomed to stretch forth His hand; with destruction, like (Micah 1:6), “into a heap (לעי) in the field.” if with His misfortune If He would send a misfortune, which is a breach upon His creatures. He would dandle them He would dandle them with partial consolations. And so did our Sages (Pesikta d’Rav Kahana p. 1 27a) explain it: The Holy One, blessed be He, does not smite a nation and leave it desolate, but He brings misfortune to one and dandles it with its fellow that suffered already, and it is consoled thereby. He brought misfortune upon Assyria and dandled it with Egypt, as it is said (Nahum 3:8): “Are you better than the great No- Amon?” He brought misfortune upon Egypt and dandled them with Assyria, as it is said (Ezek. 31: 3): “Behold Assyria was [a cedar] in the Lebanon.” The words להן שוע may also be interpreted as: Prayer and supplication avail them.

words.eng: not·Ai or Aija or Aiath or Hai = "heap of ruins"

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־לֹ֣א בָ֭כִיתִי לִקְשֵׁה־י֑וֹם עָֽגְמָ֥ה נַ֝פְשִׁ֗י לָאֶבְיֽוֹן
verses.translation: Did I not weep for the unfortunate?Did I not grieve for the needy?
verses.rashi: ...whether I did not weep for one who had a difficult time [You] know and recognize whether I was not compassionate and [did not] weep for the poor, who had difficult times, and [whether] my soul [did not] grieve for the needy, that this befell me because of all these.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י ט֣וֹב קִ֭וִּיתִי וַיָּ֣בֹא רָ֑ע וַאֲיַחֲלָ֥ה לְ֝א֗וֹר וַיָּ֥בֹא אֹֽפֶל
verses.translation: I looked forward to good fortune, but evil came;I hoped for light, but darkness came.
verses.rashi: For I hoped for good etc. That is to say that this reward was fit for me.

verses.hebrew_full: מֵעַ֖י רֻתְּח֥וּ וְלֹא־דָ֗מּוּ קִדְּמֻ֥נִי יְמֵי־עֹֽנִי
verses.translation: My bowels are in turmoil without respite;Days of misery confront me.
verses.rashi: and did not become silent They cannot be silent.

verses.hebrew_full: קֹדֵ֣ר הִ֭לַּכְתִּי בְּלֹ֣א חַמָּ֑ה קַ֖מְתִּי בַקָּהָ֣ל אֲשַׁוֵּֽעַ
verses.translation: I walk about in sunless gloom;I rise in the assembly and cry out.
verses.rashi: without the sun The sun did not gaze upon me, yet I am blackened.

verses.hebrew_full: אָ֭ח הָיִ֣יתִי לְתַנִּ֑ים וְ֝רֵ֗עַ לִבְנ֥וֹת יַעֲנָֽה
verses.translation: I have become a brother to jackals,A companion to ostriches.
verses.rashi: I was a brother to jackals etc. Jackals and ostriches spend all their days wailing.

verses.hebrew_full: ע֭וֹרִי שָׁחַ֣ר מֵעָלָ֑י וְעַצְמִי־חָ֝֗רָה מִנִּי־חֹֽרֶב
verses.translation: My skin, blackened, is peeling off me;My bones are charred by the heat.
verses.rashi: dried out Heb. חרה, an expression of a thing that is burned and dried through the heat of the fire, like (Jer. 6:29), “The bellows is heated (נחר)”; (Ezek. 15:4), “and the middle of it is burned (נחר).”

verses.hebrew_full: וַיְהִ֣י לְ֭אֵבֶל כִּנֹּרִ֑י וְ֝עֻגָבִ֗י לְק֣וֹל בֹּכִֽים
verses.translation: So my lyre is given over to mourning,My pipe, to accompany weepers.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּ֭רִית כָּרַ֣תִּי לְעֵינָ֑י וּמָ֥ה אֶ֝תְבּוֹנֵ֗ן עַל־בְּתוּלָֽה
verses.translation: I have covenanted with my eyesNot to gaze on a maiden.
verses.rashi: I made a covenant with my eyes not to gaze upon a married woman. and why should I gaze upon a virgin? Why should I gaze upon her? Such was Job’s piety, not to lay an eye even on an unmarried woman. [He would say,] “Perhaps, after a time, she will be married, and I will find myself attracted to her.” So is [this verse] explained in Avoth D’Rabbi Nathan (2:5).

verses.hebrew_full: וּמֶ֤ה חֵ֣לֶק אֱל֣וֹהַּ מִמָּ֑עַל וְֽנַחֲלַ֥ת שַׁ֝דַּ֗י מִמְּרֹמִֽים
verses.translation: What fate is decreed by God above?What lot, by Shaddai in the heights?
verses.rashi: Now what is the portion etc. that He recompensed me so?

verses.hebrew_full: הֲלֹא־אֵ֥יד לְעַוָּ֑ל וְ֝נֵ֗כֶר לְפֹ֣עֲלֵי אָֽוֶן
verses.translation: Calamity is surely for the iniquitous;Misfortune, for the worker of mischief.
verses.rashi: Is not misfortune, for the unjust He deserves it, not I. and deliverance Heb. ונכר, after the pattern of (Obadiah 1:12) “[And] you should not have looked on the day of your brother on the day of his being delivered (נכרו).”

verses.hebrew_full: הֲלֹא־ה֭וּא יִרְאֶ֣ה דְרָכָ֑י וְֽכׇל־צְעָדַ֥י יִסְפּֽוֹר
verses.translation: Surely [God] observes my ways,Takes account of my every step.
verses.rashi: and count all my steps and see whether I went with falsehood.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־הָלַ֥כְתִּי עִם־שָׁ֑וְא וַתַּ֖חַשׁ עַל־מִרְמָ֣ה רַגְלִֽי
verses.translation: Have I walked with the worthless,Or my feet hurried to deceit?
verses.rashi: hastened Heb. ותחש, and hurried, like (Isa. 60:22), “I will hasten it (אחישנה)”; (Ps. 90:10) “for it is soon (חיש) cut off.”

verses.hebrew_full: יִשְׁקְלֵ֥נִי בְמֹֽאזְנֵי־צֶ֑דֶק וְיֵדַ֥ע אֱ֝ל֗וֹהַּ תֻּמָּתִֽי
verses.translation: Let me be weighed on the scale of righteousness;Let God ascertain my integrity.

verses.hebrew_full: אִ֥ם־תִּטֶּ֣ה אַשֻּׁרִי֮ מִנִּ֢י הַ֫דָּ֥רֶךְ וְאַחַ֣ר עֵ֭ינַי הָלַ֣ךְ לִבִּ֑י וּ֝בְכַפַּ֗י דָּ֣בַק מֽאוּם
verses.translation: If my feet have strayed from their course,My heart followed after my eyes,And a stain sullied my hands,
verses.rashi: wrong Heb. מאום, that is a blemish, a shameful thing. This “alef” is written but not read, in the Great Masorah, and it is of the form of (Exod. 5:7), “You shall not continue (תאספון).” If I did this, I would deserve that this curse come upon me.

verses.hebrew_full: אֶ֭זְרְעָה וְאַחֵ֣ר יֹאכֵ֑ל וְֽצֶאֱצָאַ֥י יְשֹׁרָֽשׁוּ
verses.translation: May I sow, but another reap,May the growth of my field be uprooted!
verses.rashi: may I sow and another eat, and my produce be uprooted their roots should be torn up.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־נִפְתָּ֣ה לִ֭בִּי עַל־אִשָּׁ֑ה וְעַל־פֶּ֖תַח רֵעִ֣י אָרָֽבְתִּי
verses.translation: If my heart was ravished by the wife of my neighbor,And I lay in wait at his door,
verses.rashi: and if I lurked at my neighbor’s doorway for his wife, He should requite me...

verses.hebrew_full: תִּטְחַ֣ן לְאַחֵ֣ר אִשְׁתִּ֑י וְ֝עָלֶ֗יהָ יִכְרְע֥וּן אֲחֵרִֽין
verses.translation: May my wife grind for another,May others kneel over her!
verses.rashi: may my wife grind for someone else Our Sages explained this as an expression of sexual intercourse, as in (Jud. 16:21), “and he did grind in the prison house.”

verses.hebrew_full: כִּי־הִ֥יא (הוא) זִמָּ֑ה (והיא) וְ֝ה֗וּא עָוֺ֥ן פְּלִילִֽים
verses.translation: For that would have been debauchery,A criminal offense,
verses.rashi: For that is lewdness and I feared that if I committed it, that it is iniquity deserving punishment iniquity for which judgment of suffering is fit to come.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י אֵ֣שׁ הִ֭יא עַד־אֲבַדּ֣וֹן תֹּאכֵ֑ל וּֽבְכׇל־תְּב֖וּאָתִ֣י תְשָׁרֵֽשׁ
verses.translation: A fire burning down to Abaddon,Consuming the roots of all my increase.
verses.rashi: and it uproots all my grain i.e., this fire. Therefore, I refrained from committing [this sin]. When he speaks with an expression of uprooting, he says תְּשָׁרֵשׁ, and to express roots, he also uses תַּשְׁרֵשׁ. Similarly, יְשֹׁרָשׁוּ is an expression of uprooting and יַשְׁרִשׁוּ is an expression of a root.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־אֶמְאַ֗ס מִשְׁפַּ֣ט עַ֭בְדִּי וַאֲמָתִ֑י בְּ֝רִבָ֗ם עִמָּדִֽי
verses.translation: Did I ever brush aside the case of my servants, man or maid,When they made a complaint against me?

verses.hebrew_full: וּמָ֣ה אֶ֭עֱשֶׂה כִּֽי־יָק֣וּם אֵ֑ל וְכִי־יִ֝פְקֹ֗ד מָ֣ה אֲשִׁיבֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: What then should I do when God arises;When I am called to account, what should I answer?
verses.rashi: then what will I do etc. I took this to heart, and when He visits, what will I answer Him?

verses.hebrew_full: הֲֽלֹא־בַ֭בֶּטֶן עֹשֵׂ֣נִי עָשָׂ֑הוּ וַ֝יְכֻנֶ֗נּוּ בָּרֶ֥חֶם אֶחָֽד
verses.translation: Did not the One who made me in my mother’s belly make him?Did not One form us both in the womb?
verses.rashi: and form us both in the womb? I in my mother’s womb and he in his mother’s womb—did not One creator form us?

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־אֶ֭מְנַע מֵחֵ֣פֶץ דַּלִּ֑ים וְעֵינֵ֖י אַלְמָנָ֣ה אֲכַלֶּֽה
verses.translation: Did I deny the poor their needs,Or let a widow pine away,
verses.rashi: I cause...to fail in that I did not fulfill her desire, which is “failure of the eyes.” One who desires and does not achieve is called “failure of the eyes.”

verses.hebrew_full: וְאֹכַ֣ל פִּתִּ֣י לְבַדִּ֑י וְלֹֽא־אָכַ֖ל יָת֣וֹם מִמֶּֽנָּה
verses.translation: By eating my food alone,The fatherless not eating of it also?

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֣י מִ֭נְּעוּרַי גְּדֵלַ֣נִי כְאָ֑ב וּמִבֶּ֖טֶן אִמִּ֣י אַנְחֶֽנָּה
verses.translation: Why, from my youth he grew up with me as though I were his father;Since I left my mother’s womb I was the widow’s guide.
verses.rashi: For, from my youth it raised me This virtue [raised me] as a father who raises me and teaches me. So did the uprightness of my heart teach me from my youth. and from my mother’s womb I led it This trait.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־אֶרְאֶ֣ה א֭וֹבֵד מִבְּלִ֣י לְב֑וּשׁ וְאֵ֥ין כְּ֝ס֗וּת לָאֶבְיֽוֹן
verses.translation: I never saw an unclad wretch,Someone needy without clothing,

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־לֹ֣א בֵרְכ֣וּנִי חֲלָצָ֑ו וּמִגֵּ֥ז כְּ֝בָשַׂ֗י יִתְחַמָּֽם
verses.translation: Whose loins did not bless meAs they warmed themselves with the shearings of my sheep.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־הֲנִיפ֣וֹתִי עַל־יָת֣וֹם יָדִ֑י כִּֽי־אֶרְאֶ֥ה בַ֝שַּׁ֗עַר עֶזְרָתִֽי
verses.translation: If I raised my hand against the fatherless,Looking to my supporters in the gate,

verses.hebrew_full: כְּ֭תֵפִי מִשִּׁכְמָ֣הֿ תִפּ֑וֹל וְ֝אֶזְרֹעִ֗י מִקָּנָ֥הֿ תִשָּׁבֵֽר
verses.translation: May my arm drop off my shoulder;My forearm break off at the elbow.
verses.rashi: from its shoulder blade That is the width of the shoulder. from its bone That is the upper bone, which is round as a reed (קנה), and because it is round as a reed, it is called so.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י פַ֣חַד אֵ֭לַי אֵ֣יד אֵ֑ל וּ֝מִשְּׂאֵת֗וֹ לֹ֣א אוּכָֽל
verses.translation: For I am in dread of God-sent calamity;I cannot bear such a threat.
verses.rashi: fear to me His misfortune, which He brought upon the wicked, was fear in my eyes; therefore, I refrained from doing it.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־שַׂ֣מְתִּי זָהָ֣ב כִּסְלִ֑י וְ֝לַכֶּ֗תֶם אָמַ֥רְתִּי מִבְטַחִֽי
verses.translation: Did I put my reliance on gold,Or regard fine gold as my bulwark?
verses.rashi: my hope Heb. כסלי, like (8:14), “whose confidence (כסלו) shall be cut off.” My hope and my thought, and because the kidneys are on the flanks (כסלים), and the kidneys advise, the thought is called כֶּסֶל. and to jewelry A gathering of gold and treasures. I said, ‘My confidence.’ therewith to strengthen myself over the poor.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־אֶ֭שְׂמַח כִּֽי־רַ֣ב חֵילִ֑י וְכִֽי־כַ֝בִּ֗יר מָצְאָ֥ה יָדִֽי
verses.translation: Did I rejoice in my great wealth,In having attained plenty?
verses.rashi: If I rejoiced because my wealth I did not behave with joy in the presence of the poor so as not to make them envious of me.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־אֶרְאֶ֣ה א֭וֹר כִּ֣י יָהֵ֑ל וְ֝יָרֵ֗חַ יָקָ֥ר הֹלֵֽךְ
verses.translation: If ever I saw the light shining,The moon on its course in full glory,
verses.rashi: If I saw light shining The sun in the orbit of its might, and its brightness. and the moon becoming brighter Heb. יקר. Progressively brighter, like (Zech. 14:6), “bright (יקרות) light”; (Ps. 37:20), “like the glory (כיקר) off the meadows.”

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּ֣פְתְּ בַּסֵּ֣תֶר לִבִּ֑י וַתִּשַּׁ֖ק יָדִ֣י לְפִֽי
verses.translation: And I secretly succumbed,And my hand touched my mouth in a kiss,
verses.rashi: And my heart was secretly enticed to the sun and the moon, saying that they are gods, like some heathens, who have gone astray (and worship) all the host of heaven.

verses.hebrew_full: גַּם־ה֭וּא עָוֺ֣ן פְּלִילִ֑י כִּֽי־כִחַ֖שְׁתִּי לָאֵ֣ל מִמָּֽעַל
verses.translation: That, too, would have been a criminal offense,For I would have denied God above.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־אֶ֭שְׂמַח בְּפִ֣יד מְשַׂנְאִ֑י וְ֝הִתְעֹרַ֗רְתִּי כִּֽי־מְצָ֥אוֹ רָֽע
verses.translation: Did I rejoice over my enemies’ misfortune?Did I thrill because evil befell them?

verses.hebrew_full: וְלֹא־נָתַ֣תִּי לַחֲטֹ֣א חִכִּ֑י לִשְׁאֹ֖ל בְּאָלָ֣ה נַפְשֽׁוֹ
verses.translation: I never let my mouth sinBy wishing their death in a curse.
verses.rashi: but I did not allow my palate to sin to provoke my enemy and to sue him for money that he does not owe me, in order to adjure him and to ask for the soul of my enemy with an oath.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־לֹ֣א אָ֭מְרוּ מְתֵ֣י אׇהֳלִ֑י מִי־יִתֵּ֥ן מִ֝בְּשָׂר֗וֹ לֹ֣א נִשְׂבָּֽע
verses.translation: (Indeed, those of my clan said,“We would consume his flesh insatiably!”)
verses.rashi: We will not be sated Because of the hatred that they bore against me for burdening them with hospitality toward guests.

verses.hebrew_full: בַּ֭חוּץ לֹא־יָלִ֣ין גֵּ֑ר דְּ֝לָתַ֗י לָאֹ֥רַח אֶפְתָּֽח
verses.translation: No sojourner spent the night in the open;I opened my doors to the road.
verses.rashi: to the road The doors of my house were not inside and enclosed by a gatehouse outside, or surrounded by corners to stop the wayfarers from coming, but open to the crossroads so that whoever wishes to come may do so.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־כִּסִּ֣יתִי כְאָדָ֣ם פְּשָׁעָ֑י לִטְמ֖וֹן בְּחֻבִּ֣י עֲוֺנִֽי
verses.translation: Did I hide my transgressions like Adam,Bury my wrongdoing in my bosom,
verses.rashi: in my hiding place Heb. בחבי, like בְּמַחַבוֹאִי.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י אֶעֱר֨וֹץ הָ֘מ֤וֹן רַבָּ֗ה וּבוּז־מִשְׁפָּח֥וֹת יְחִתֵּ֑נִי וָ֝אֶדֹּ֗ם לֹא־אֵ֥צֵא פָֽתַח
verses.translation: That I should [now] fear the great multitude,And am shattered by the contempt of families,So that I keep silent and do not step outdoors?
verses.rashi: For I would subdue Originally I would subdue a great multitude of wicked men, but now, even among the Chaldeans—“This people has never been” (Isa. 23:13).

verses.hebrew_full: מִ֤י יִתֶּן־לִ֨י שֹׁ֘מֵ֤עַֽ לִ֗י הֶן־תָּ֭וִי שַׁדַּ֣י יַעֲנֵ֑נִי וְסֵ֥פֶר כָּ֝תַ֗ב אִ֣ישׁ רִיבִֽי
verses.translation: O that I had someone to give me a hearing;O that Shaddai would reply to my writ,Or my accuser draw up a true bill!
verses.rashi: behold my inscription; may the Almighty bear witness for me Would that the Omnipresent bear witness for me in judgment, as He wrote concerning me (1:8): “For there is none like him on earth, a sincere and upright man, God-fearing.” Also Moses, who wrote his Book and my Book, will bear witness concerning me. (Another explanation:) This is what Job says, “Give it to inscribe me and my virtue.” may the Almighty answer me for this request. and may my opponent write a book May he too write a book to contend with me.

words.eng: to give·inflected pers. pron. meaning ‘to me’.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־לֹ֣א עַל־שִׁ֭כְמִי אֶשָּׂאֶ֑נּוּ אֶעֶנְדֶ֖נּוּ עֲטָר֣וֹת לִֽי
verses.translation: I would carry it on my shoulder;Tie it around me for a wreath.
verses.rashi: Would I not carry him The writer of the book. bind it I will bind the book to me as crowns.

verses.hebrew_full: מִסְפַּ֣ר צְ֭עָדַי אַגִּידֶ֑נּוּ כְּמוֹ־נָ֝גִ֗יד אֲקָֽרְבֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: I would give an account of my steps,Offer it as to a commander.
verses.rashi: I will tell him the number of my steps to that man.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־עָ֭לַי אַדְמָתִ֣י תִזְעָ֑ק וְ֝יַ֗חַד תְּלָמֶ֥יהָ יִבְכָּיֽוּן
verses.translation: If my land cries out against me,Its furrows weep together;
verses.rashi: If my soil complains about me concerning the gleanings, the forgotten sheaves, and the end [of the field], as well as tithes, [claiming] that I did not extract tithes properly. and its furrows weep together that I plowed with an ox and with a donkey together.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־כֹּ֭חָהּ אָכַ֣לְתִּי בְלִי־כָ֑סֶף וְנֶ֖פֶשׁ בְּעָלֶ֣יהָ הִפָּֽחְתִּי
verses.translation: If I have eaten its produce without payment,And made its [rightful] owners despair,
verses.rashi: without money to hire workers and to oppress them [by not paying them]. Another explanation. This refers to the second tithe, concerning which it is written (Deut. 14:25): “And you shall tie the money in your hand.” and I caused pain to its owner The sharecropper who tills it.

verses.hebrew_full: תַּ֤חַת חִטָּ֨ה יֵ֥צֵא ח֗וֹחַ וְתַֽחַת־שְׂעֹרָ֥ה בׇאְשָׁ֑ה תַּ֝֗מּוּ דִּבְרֵ֥י אִיּֽוֹב
verses.translation: May nettles grow there instead of wheat;Instead of barley, stinkweed!The words of Job are at an end.
verses.rashi: noisome weeds Heb. באשה, (a malodorous plant) like בְּאֻשִׁים (Isa. 5:2). And if I did not do so, may thistles emerge instead of wheat. (Rabbi Oshia taught: The Torah teaches you practical advice, that in a field beset with thistles it is advisable to sow wheat, and in a field beset with weeds, it is advisable to sow barley. This is the reading of Yalkut.) Now what is the reason? “Instead of wheat, thistles shall emerge.” Until this point, he repeatedly prophesies many prophecies, and you say: Job’s words are ended? Rather, Job says as follows, “If I have not done so, may Job’s words be ended, and may he have no pretext for saying before You (Deut. 26:13), ‘I have cleared away what is holy.’” I saw this in the Pesikta of the portion עשר תעשר (ibid. 14), explained in this manner from these verses.

verses.hebrew_full: וַֽיִּשְׁבְּת֡וּ שְׁלֹ֤שֶׁת הָאֲנָשִׁ֣ים הָ֭אֵלֶּה מֵעֲנ֣וֹת אֶת־אִיּ֑וֹב כִּ֤י ה֖וּא צַדִּ֣יק בְּעֵינָֽיו
verses.translation: These three men ceased replying to Job, for he considered himself right.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Job = "hated"

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּ֤חַר אַ֨ף אֱלִיה֣וּא בֶן־בַּרַכְאֵ֣ל הַבּוּזִי֮ מִמִּשְׁפַּ֢חַ֫ת רָ֥ם בְּ֭אִיּוֹב חָרָ֣ה אַפּ֑וֹ עַֽל־צַדְּק֥וֹ נַ֝פְשׁ֗וֹ מֵאֱלֹהִֽים
verses.translation: Then Elihu son of Barachel the Buzite, of the family of Ram, was angry—angry at Job because he thought himself right against God.
verses.rashi: Elihu’s Addressof the family of Ram Abraham, as it is stated (Josh. 14:15): “the greatest man among the Anakim.” This is Abraham. than God More than the Omnipresent.

verses.hebrew_full: וּבִשְׁלֹ֣שֶׁת רֵעָיו֮ חָרָ֢ה אַ֫פּ֥וֹ עַ֤ל אֲשֶׁ֣ר לֹא־מָצְא֣וּ מַעֲנֶ֑ה וַ֝יַּרְשִׁ֗יעוּ אֶת־אִיּֽוֹב
verses.translation: He was angry as well at his three friends, because they found no reply, but merely condemned Job.
verses.rashi: and condemned Job This is one of the verses wherein the Scribes rectified the language of the Scripture. “And they condemned,” as directed against the Omnipresent, by remaining silent, should have been written, but Scripture euphemized. Similarly (Num. 11:15), “and do not let me see my misfortune.” It should have been written: “and do not let me see their misfortune,” but Scripture euphemized, and so many [other] instances, in Sifrei (ad loc.) and in the Great Masorah.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Job = "hated"

verses.hebrew_full: וֶאֱלִיה֗וּ חִכָּ֣ה אֶת־אִ֭יּוֹב בִּדְבָרִ֑ים כִּ֤י זְֽקֵנִים־הֵ֖מָּה מִמֶּ֣נּוּ לְיָמִֽים
verses.translation: Elihu waited out Job’s speech, for they were all older than he.
verses.rashi: because they were older than he in days [i.e.,] the others [were older than he]. Therefore, he refrained until they were still.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Job = "hated"

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֤רְא אֱלִיה֗וּא כִּ֘י־אֵ֤ין מַעֲנֶ֗ה בְּ֭פִי שְׁלֹ֥שֶׁת הָאֲנָשִׁ֗ים וַיִּ֥חַר אַפּֽוֹ
verses.translation: But when Elihu saw that the three men had nothing to reply, he was angry.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֤עַן אֱלִיה֖וּא בֶן־בַּרַכְאֵ֥ל הַבּוּזִ֗י וַיֹּ֫אמַ֥ר צָ֘עִ֤יר אֲנִ֣י לְ֭יָמִים וְאַתֶּ֣ם יְשִׁישִׁ֑ים עַל־כֵּ֖ן זָחַ֥לְתִּי וָאִירָ֓א מֵחַוֺּ֖ת דֵּעִ֣י אֶתְכֶֽם
verses.translation: Then Elihu son of Barachel the Buzite said in reply:I have but few years, while you are old;Therefore I was too awestruck and fearfulTo hold forth among you.

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)
sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: אָ֭מַרְתִּי יָמִ֣ים יְדַבֵּ֑רוּ וְרֹ֥ב שָׁ֝נִ֗ים יֹדִ֥יעוּ חׇכְמָֽה
verses.translation: I thought, “Let age speak;Let advanced years declare wise things.”

verses.hebrew_full: אָ֭כֵן רוּחַ־הִ֣יא בֶאֱנ֑וֹשׁ וְנִשְׁמַ֖ת שַׁדַּ֣י תְּבִינֵֽם
verses.translation: But truly it is the spirit in mortals,The breath of Shaddai, that gives them understanding.
verses.rashi: Indeed, it is a spirit in man Wisdom [is a spirit in man] and [comes] not through days or old age, but it is the spirit of the Omnipresent.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־רַבִּ֥ים יֶחְכָּ֑מוּ וּ֝זְקֵנִ֗ים יָבִ֥ינוּ מִשְׁפָּֽט
verses.translation: It is not the aged who are wise,The elders, who understand how to judge.

verses.hebrew_full: לָכֵ֣ן אָ֭מַרְתִּי שִׁמְעָה־לִּ֑י אֲחַוֶּ֖ה דֵעִ֣י אַף־אָֽנִי
verses.translation: Therefore I say, “Listen to me;I too would hold forth.”

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֤ן הוֹחַ֨לְתִּי לְֽדִבְרֵיכֶ֗ם אָ֭זִין עַד־תְּב֥וּנֹֽתֵיכֶ֑ם עַֽד־תַּחְקְר֥וּן מִלִּֽין
verses.translation: Here I have waited out your speeches,I have given ear to your insights,While you probed the issues;

verses.hebrew_full: וְעָ֥דֵיכֶ֗ם אֶתְבּ֫וֹנָ֥ן וְהִנֵּ֤ה אֵ֣ין לְאִיּ֣וֹב מוֹכִ֑יחַ עוֹנֶ֖ה אֲמָרָ֣יו מִכֶּֽם
verses.translation: But as I attended to you,I saw that none of you could argue with Job,Or offer replies to his statements.
verses.rashi: Yea, I attended to you I attended to you, and I saw that there is no one to convince Job.
