verses.hebrew_full: פֶּן־תֹּ֣֭אמְרוּ מָצָ֣אנוּ חׇכְמָ֑ה אֵ֖ל יִדְּפֶ֣נּוּ לֹא־אִֽישׁ
verses.translation: I fear you will say, “We have found the wise course;God will defeat him, not other people.”
verses.rashi: Lest you say You shall not say We have found wisdom by remaining silent in order not to provoke him anymore. for God will crush him and not a man You should not have belittled the honor of the Omnipresent thereby.

verses.hebrew_full: וְלֹא־עָרַ֣ךְ אֵלַ֣י מִלִּ֑ין וּ֝בְאִמְרֵיכֶ֗ם לֹ֣א אֲשִׁיבֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: He did not set out his case against me,Nor shall I use your reasons to reply to him.
verses.rashi: He did not set up words against me This is a plaint: Woe that he did not set up words against me. I would not reply to him with your sayings I would not reply to him with those sayings with which you replied to him.

verses.hebrew_full: חַ֭תּוּ לֹא־עָ֣נוּ ע֑וֹד הֶעְתִּ֖יקוּ מֵהֶ֣ם מִלִּֽים
verses.translation: They have been broken and can no longer reply;Words fail them.
verses.rashi: have removed themselves Words have turned away from them.

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֭הוֹחַלְתִּי כִּי־לֹ֣א יְדַבֵּ֑רוּ כִּ֥י עָ֝מְד֗וּ לֹא־עָ֥נוּ עֽוֹד
verses.translation: I have waited till they stopped speaking,Till they ended and no longer replied.

verses.hebrew_full: אַעֲנֶ֣ה אַף־אֲנִ֣י חֶלְקִ֑י אֲחַוֶּ֖ה דֵעִ֣י אַף־אָֽנִי
verses.translation: Now I also would have my say;I too would like to hold forth,

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)
I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֭י מָלֵ֣תִי מִלִּ֑ים הֱ֝צִיקַ֗תְנִי ר֣וּחַ בִּטְנִֽי
verses.translation: For I am full of words;The wind in my belly presses me.

verses.hebrew_full: הִנֵּֽה־בִטְנִ֗י כְּיַ֥יִן לֹֽא־יִפָּתֵ֑חַ כְּאֹב֥וֹת חֲ֝דָשִׁ֗ים יִבָּקֵֽעַ
verses.translation: My belly is like wine not yet opened,Like jugs of new wine ready to burst.
verses.rashi: Behold my belly is full of wind, words to answer eagerly. like wine that is not opened Like new wine that is not opened, and which blows up, and even [if it is] within new wineskins, it bursts them. like...wineskins Wineskins made as tall as a man’s height.

verses.hebrew_full: אֲדַבְּרָ֥ה וְיִֽרְוַֽח־לִ֑י אֶפְתַּ֖ח שְׂפָתַ֣י וְאֶעֱנֶֽה
verses.translation: Let me speak, then, and get relief;Let me open my lips and reply.

words.eng: to be wide·inflected pers. pron. meaning ‘to me’.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־נָ֭א אֶשָּׂ֣א פְנֵי־אִ֑ישׁ וְאֶל־אָ֝דָ֗ם לֹ֣א אֲכַנֶּֽה
verses.translation: I would not show regard for anybody,Or temper my speech for the sake of any mortal;
verses.rashi: neither will I euphemize I will not change my words to replace them [with milder expressions] in deference to his [Job’s] honor, as I said above, “And they condemned Job.” This is a euphemism, which changes the expression because of His honor. I will not even do this, but I will reprove him explicitly.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י לֹ֣א יָדַ֣עְתִּי אֲכַנֶּ֑ה כִּ֝מְעַ֗ט יִשָּׂאֵ֥נִי עֹשֵֽׂנִי
verses.translation: For I do not know how to temper my speech—My Maker would soon carry me off!
verses.rashi: or else...would carry me away He would take me away from the world. Others interpret יִשָׂאֵנִי like (Nahum 1:5), “and the land raised up (ותשא) from before me [a pillar of smoke].”

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽאוּלָ֗ם שְׁמַֽע־נָ֣א אִיּ֣וֹב מִלָּ֑י וְֽכׇל־דְּבָרַ֥י הַאֲזִֽינָה
verses.translation: But now, Job, listen to my words,Give ear to all that I say.

verses.hebrew_full: הִנֵּה־נָ֭א פָּתַ֣חְתִּי פִ֑י דִּבְּרָ֖ה לְשׁוֹנִ֣י בְחִכִּֽי
verses.translation: Now I open my lips;My tongue forms words in my mouth.

verses.hebrew_full: יֹשֶׁר־לִבִּ֥י אֲמָרָ֑י וְדַ֥עַת שְׂ֝פָתַ֗י בָּר֥וּר מִלֵּֽלוּ
verses.translation: My words bespeak the uprightness of my heart;My lips utter insight honestly.

verses.hebrew_full: רֽוּחַ־אֵ֥ל עָשָׂ֑תְנִי וְנִשְׁמַ֖ת שַׁדַּ֣י תְּחַיֵּֽנִי
verses.translation: The spirit of God formed me;The breath of Shaddai sustains me.
verses.rashi: The spirit of God made me and I am no stronger than you.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־תּוּכַ֥ל הֲשִׁיבֵ֑נִי עֶרְכָ֥הֿ לְ֝פָנַ֗י הִתְיַצָּֽבָה
verses.translation: If you can, answer me;Argue against me, take your stand.

verses.hebrew_full: הֵן־אֲנִ֣י כְפִ֣יךָ לָאֵ֑ל מֵ֝חֹ֗מֶר קֹרַ֥צְתִּי גַם־אָֽנִי
verses.translation: You and I are the same before God;I too was nipped from clay.
verses.rashi: Behold I am like your mouth to God As your mouth asked to debate with one who would not terrify you, behold I am instead of the Holy One, blessed be He, and on His behalf, to speak His words.

words.eng: they·I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)
also·I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: הִנֵּ֣ה אֵ֭מָתִי לֹ֣א תְבַעֲתֶ֑ךָּ וְ֝אַכְפִּ֗י עָלֶ֥יךָ לֹֽא־יִכְבָּֽד
verses.translation: You are not overwhelmed by fear of me;My pressure does not weigh heavily on you.
verses.rashi: and my coercion Heb. אכפי, my coercion, like (Prov. 16:26), “his mouth forces (אכף).”

verses.hebrew_full: אַ֭ךְ אָמַ֣רְתָּ בְאׇזְנָ֑י וְק֖וֹל מִלִּ֣ין אֶשְׁמָֽע
verses.translation: Indeed, you have stated in my hearing,I heard the words spoken,

verses.hebrew_full: זַ֥ךְ אֲנִ֗י בְּֽלִ֫י־פָ֥שַׁע חַ֥ף אָנֹכִ֑י וְלֹ֖א עָוֺ֣ן לִֽי
verses.translation: “I am guiltless, free from transgression;I am innocent, without iniquity.
verses.rashi: I am clean Heb. חף, pure and cleansed, like (Bezah 35b), “A nazirite may not cleanse (יחף) his head...” and like (Shabbath 50a),” we may cleanse (חפין) vessels with anything.”

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֣ן תְּ֭נוּאוֹת עָלַ֣י יִמְצָ֑א יַחְשְׁבֵ֖נִי לְאוֹיֵ֣ב לֽוֹ
verses.translation: But [God] finds reasons to oppose me,And considers me an enemy.
verses.rashi: pretenses Heb. תאונות, pretenses to turn away from the Omnipresent, an expression of (Jud. 14: 4), “that he sought a pretense (תאנה).”

verses.hebrew_full: יָשֵׂ֣ם בַּסַּ֣ד רַגְלָ֑י יִ֝שְׁמֹ֗ר כׇּל־אׇרְחֹתָֽי
verses.translation: My feet are placed in stocks,And all my ways are watched.”

verses.hebrew_full: הֶן־זֹ֣את לֹא־צָדַ֣קְתָּ אֶעֱנֶ֑ךָּ כִּי־יִרְבֶּ֥ה אֱ֝ל֗וֹהַּ מֵאֱנֽוֹשׁ
verses.translation: In this you are not right;I will answer you: God is greater than any mortal.
verses.rashi: for God exceeds man God does not have to be so exact with man because the Holy One, blessed be He, has more virtues than man.

verses.hebrew_full: מַ֭דּוּעַ אֵלָ֣יו רִיב֑וֹתָ כִּ֥י כׇל־דְּ֝בָרָ֗יו לֹ֣א יַעֲנֶֽה
verses.translation: Why do you level the complaintThat there is no reply to anyone’s charges?

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־בְאַחַ֥ת יְדַבֶּר־אֵ֑ל וּ֝בִשְׁתַּ֗יִם לֹ֣א יְשׁוּרֶֽנָּה
verses.translation: For God speaks time and again—Though no one perceives it—

verses.hebrew_full: בַּחֲל֤וֹם חֶזְי֬וֹן לַ֗יְלָה בִּנְפֹ֣ל תַּ֭רְדֵּמָה עַל־אֲנָשִׁ֑ים בִּ֝תְנוּמ֗וֹת עֲלֵ֣י מִשְׁכָּֽב
verses.translation: In a dream, a night vision,When deep sleep falls on everyone,While they slumber on their beds.

verses.hebrew_full: אָ֣ז יִ֭גְלֶה אֹ֣זֶן אֲנָשִׁ֑ים וּבְמֹ֖סָרָ֣ם יַחְתֹּֽם
verses.translation: Then everyone’s understanding is opened,And God’s signature is left by disciplining them—
verses.rashi: Then He opens the ear of men as He did to Abimelech in the dream of the night. and with their chastisement He seals [them] He seals them and binds them with suffering because of their iniquity.

verses.hebrew_full: לְ֭הָסִיר אָדָ֣ם מַעֲשֶׂ֑ה וְגֵוָ֖ה מִגֶּ֣בֶר יְכַסֶּֽה
verses.translation: To turn a mortal away from an action,To suppress pride in a man.
verses.rashi: To restrain man in his illness. a deed that he had intended to do. and He covers haughtiness from man with his suffering, in order to vanquish him and humble him to repent, so that he withhold his soul from utter death. haughtiness Heb. וְגֵוָה, like גַאֲוָה.

verses.hebrew_full: יַחְשֹׂ֣ךְ נַ֭פְשׁוֹ מִנִּי־שָׁ֑חַת וְ֝חַיָּת֗וֹ מֵעֲבֹ֥ר בַּשָּֽׁלַח
verses.translation: [God] spares him from the Pit,His person, from perishing by the sword.
verses.rashi: and his life from perishing by the sword by the weapon and the sword of the Angel of Death; that is to say, He brought suffering upon you for your good. by the sword Heb. בשלח, a weapon, and so in Ezra (Neh. 4:11): “and one was holding the weapon (השלח).”

verses.hebrew_full: וְהוּכַ֣ח בְּ֭מַכְאוֹב עַל־מִשְׁכָּב֑וֹ (וריב) וְר֖וֹב עֲצָמָ֣יו אֵתָֽן
verses.translation: He is reproved by pains on his bed,And the trembling in his bones is constant.
verses.rashi: And he is chastised an expression of reproof that he repent of his iniquity, and most of his bones, which are strong, He chastises with pain.

verses.hebrew_full: וְזִהֲמַ֣תּוּ חַיָּת֣וֹ לָ֑חֶם וְ֝נַפְשׁ֗וֹ מַאֲכַ֥ל תַּאֲוָֽה
verses.translation: He detests food;Fine food [is repulsive] to him.
verses.rashi: And his living spirit causes him to abhor food The soul and the life of the sick person cause him to abhor all food. and his soul abhors all tasty food.

verses.hebrew_full: יִ֣כֶל בְּשָׂר֣וֹ מֵרֹ֑אִי (ושפי) וְשֻׁפּ֥וּ עַ֝צְמֹתָ֗יו לֹ֣א רֻאּֽוּ
verses.translation: His flesh wastes away till it cannot be seen,And his bones are rubbed away till they are invisible.
verses.rashi: His flesh is consumed from sight Heb. יכל בשרו מראי, His flesh is withdrawn from sight, like (Lam 2:11): “My eyes are spent (כלו) with tears”; (Lev. 26:16), “that cause the eyes to pine way (מכלות). and his bones are dislocated Heb. ושפו. They are dislocated from their place. [The word] שֻׁפּוּ is like (Hullin 42b), “that sprung (שף) from its place.” to unsightliness They have neither appearance nor form.

verses.hebrew_full: וַתִּקְרַ֣ב לַשַּׁ֣חַת נַפְשׁ֑וֹ וְ֝חַיָּת֗וֹ לַֽמְמִתִֽים
verses.translation: He comes close to the Pit,His life [verges] on death.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־יֵ֤שׁ עָלָ֨יו מַלְאָ֗ךְ מֵלִ֗יץ אֶחָ֥ד מִנִּי־אָ֑לֶף לְהַגִּ֖יד לְאָדָ֣ם יׇשְׁרֽוֹ
verses.translation: If he has a representative,One advocate against a thousandTo declare the person upright,
verses.rashi: one out of a thousand of those who testify to his guilt, and he intercedes with his uprightness.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיְחֻנֶּ֗נּוּ וַיֹּ֗אמֶר פְּ֭דָעֵהוּ מֵרֶ֥דֶת שָׁ֗חַת מָצָ֥אתִי כֹֽפֶר
verses.translation: Then [God] has mercy on him and decrees,“Redeem him from descending to the Pit,For I have obtained his ransom;
verses.rashi: He is gracious to him i.e., the Omnipresent. Redeem him Heb. פדעהו, like פְדָאֵהוּ. The “alef” is replaced by an “ayin.” The context proves it. “I found ransom,” which is redemption.

verses.hebrew_full: רֻֽטְפַ֣שׁ בְּשָׂר֣וֹ מִנֹּ֑עַר יָ֝שׁ֗וּב לִימֵ֥י עֲלוּמָֽיו
verses.translation: Let his flesh be healthier than in his youth;Let him return to his younger days.”
verses.rashi: His flesh is agitated from shaking Heb. רֻטֲפַש means escos in French, shaken. from shaking His flesh was agitated from the shaking, that it was shaken [by the suffering of] his illness. This word [רֻטֲפַש] has no similarity in Scripture, and according to the context, we found it necessary to explain according to the meaning of the topic. But the one who interprets it as an expression of mire (רפש) and claims that the “teth” is added to it, as in (Gen. 44:16), “How can we clear ourselves (נצטדק)?” is in great error, because the “teth” is added to a word only in the reflexive voice, and to a word in which there is a radical “zaddi,” but nor in other words. for example, צדק and נצטדק, and like (Lev. 13:44) צרוּע, stricken with “zaraath,” and נצטרע, was stricken with “zaraath.” (Prov. 25: 13), “an emissary (ציר) faithful,” and (Josh. 9:4), “and they went and disguised as ambassadors (ויצטירו)”; (Gen. 45:21) “צֵדָה, provision,” and (Josh. 9:12) “הִצְטַיַדְנוּ, we took... for our provision.” he will be restored with his strength and with healing to the days of his youth.

verses.hebrew_full: יֶעְתַּ֤ר אֶל־אֱל֨וֹהַּ וַיִּרְצֵ֗הוּ וַיַּ֣רְא פָּ֭נָיו בִּתְרוּעָ֑ה וַיָּ֥שֶׁב לֶ֝אֱנ֗וֹשׁ צִדְקָתֽוֹ
verses.translation: He prays to God, who accepts him;He enters the divine presence with shouts of joy,For [God] requites humans for their righteousness.

verses.hebrew_full: יָשֹׁ֤ר עַל־אֲנָשִׁ֗ים וַיֹּ֗אמֶר חָ֭טָאתִי וְיָשָׁ֥ר הֶעֱוֵ֗יתִי וְלֹא־שָׁ֥וָה לִֽי
verses.translation: The contrite person declares to all,“I have sinned; I have perverted what was right;But I was not paid back for it.”
verses.rashi: He makes a row of men He makes a row of men when he is saved from his illness and confesses [before them] to his Creator. but I did not profit I had no profit in its matters.

verses.hebrew_full: פָּדָ֣ה (נפשי) נַ֭פְשׁוֹ מֵעֲבֹ֣ר בַּשָּׁ֑חַת (וחיתי) וְ֝חַיָּת֗וֹ בָּא֥וֹר תִּרְאֶֽה
verses.translation: He is redeemed from passing into the Pit;He, “me…I.” will enjoy the light.

verses.hebrew_full: הֶן־כׇּל־אֵ֭לֶּה יִפְעַל־אֵ֑ל פַּעֲמַ֖יִם שָׁל֣וֹשׁ עִם־גָּֽבֶר
verses.translation: Truly, God does all these thingsTwo or three times to a man,
verses.rashi: twice or thrice He chastises him for his iniquities with his illness in order not to destroy him, but if he provokes Him further, he should be concerned with Gehinnom or death, as it is written, “Yea for four, I will not return them” (Amos 1:3).

verses.hebrew_full: לְהָשִׁ֣יב נַ֭פְשׁוֹ מִנִּי־שָׁ֑חַת לֵ֝א֗וֹר בְּא֣וֹר הַחַיִּֽים
verses.translation: To bring him back from the Pit,That he may bask in the light of life.

verses.hebrew_full: הַקְשֵׁ֖ב אִיּ֥וֹב שְֽׁמַֽע־לִ֑י הַ֝חֲרֵ֗שׁ וְאָנֹכִ֥י אֲדַבֵּֽר
verses.translation: Pay heed, Job, and hear me;Be still, and I will speak;

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־יֵשׁ־מִלִּ֥ין הֲשִׁיבֵ֑נִי דַּ֝בֵּ֗ר כִּֽי־חָפַ֥צְתִּי צַדְּקֶֽךָּ
verses.translation: If you have what to say, answer me;Speak, for I am eager to vindicate you.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־אַ֭יִן אַתָּ֥ה שְֽׁמַֽע־לִ֑י הַ֝חֲרֵ֗שׁ וַאֲאַלֶּפְךָ֥ חׇכְמָֽה
verses.translation: But if not, you listen to me;Be still, and I will teach you wisdom.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֥עַן אֱלִיה֗וּא וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Elihu said in reply:

verses.hebrew_full: שִׁמְע֣וּ חֲכָמִ֣ים מִלָּ֑י וְ֝יֹדְעִ֗ים הַאֲזִ֥ינוּ לִֽי
verses.translation: Listen, O wise men, to my words;You who have knowledge, give ear to me.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּי־אֹ֭זֶן מִלִּ֣ין תִּבְחָ֑ן וְ֝חֵ֗ךְ יִטְעַ֥ם לֶאֱכֹֽל
verses.translation: For the ear tests argumentsAs the palate tastes food.
verses.rashi: For the ear tests words as the palate tastes food.

verses.hebrew_full: מִשְׁפָּ֥ט נִבְחֲרָה־לָּ֑נוּ נֵדְעָ֖ה בֵינֵ֣ינוּ מַה־טּֽוֹב
verses.translation: Let us decide for ourselves what is just;Let us know among ourselves what is good.

words.eng: to choose·inflected pers. pron. meaning ‘to us’.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־אָמַ֣ר אִיּ֣וֹב צָדַ֑קְתִּי וְ֝אֵ֗ל הֵסִ֥יר מִשְׁפָּטִֽי
verses.translation: For Job has said, “I am right;God has deprived me of justice.
verses.rashi: my right [my] To prove my words.

verses.hebrew_full: עַל־מִשְׁפָּטִ֥י אֲכַזֵּ֑ב אָנ֖וּשׁ חִצִּ֣י בְלִי־פָֽשַׁע
verses.translation: I declare the judgment against me false;My arrow-wound is deadly, though I am free from transgression.”
verses.rashi: I call a liar I accuse my Judge of being a liar, because He did not judge me fairly. incurable Heb. אנוש, My wound is sore, like (II Sam. 12:15), “and it became mortally ill (ויאנש).” my wound Heb. חצי [lit. my arrow].

verses.hebrew_full: מִי־גֶ֥בֶר כְּאִיּ֑וֹב יִֽשְׁתֶּה־לַּ֥עַג כַּמָּֽיִם
verses.translation: What man is like Job,Who drinks mockery like water;

verses.hebrew_full: וְאָרַ֣ח לְ֭חֶבְרָה עִם־פֹּ֣עֲלֵי אָ֑וֶן וְ֝לָלֶ֗כֶת עִם־אַנְשֵׁי־רֶֽשַׁע
verses.translation: Who makes common cause with evildoers,And goes with those who are wicked?

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־אָ֭מַר לֹ֣א יִסְכׇּן־גָּ֑בֶר בִּ֝רְצֹת֗וֹ עִם־אֱלֹהִֽים
verses.translation: For he says, “A man gains nothingWhen he is in God’s favor.”
verses.rashi: A man derives no benefit A man derives no benefit if he perfects his ways.

words.eng: to be of use or service or profit or benefit·adj

verses.hebrew_full: לָכֵ֤ן אַ֥נְשֵׁ֥י לֵבָ֗ב שִׁמְע֫וּ־לִ֥י חָלִ֖לָה לָאֵ֥ל מֵרֶ֗שַׁע וְשַׁדַּ֥י מֵעָֽוֶל
verses.translation: Therefore, men of understanding, listen to me;Wickedness be far from God,Wrongdoing, from Shaddai!

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י פֹ֣עַל אָ֭דָם יְשַׁלֶּם־ל֑וֹ וּֽכְאֹ֥רַח אִ֝֗ישׁ יַמְצִאֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: For [God] pays mortals according to their actions,And provides for people according to their conduct;

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace·unto him; v. ל־.

verses.hebrew_full: אַף־אׇמְנָ֗ם אֵ֥ל לֹֽא־יַרְשִׁ֑יעַ וְ֝שַׁדַּ֗י לֹא־יְעַוֵּ֥ת מִשְׁפָּֽט
verses.translation: For God surely does not act wickedly;Shaddai does not pervert justice.

verses.hebrew_full: מִֽי־פָקַ֣ד עָלָ֣יו אָ֑רְצָה וּמִ֥י שָׂ֝֗ם תֵּבֵ֥ל כֻּלָּֽהּ
verses.translation: Who placed the earth in God’s charge?Who ordered the entire world?
verses.rashi: Who gave Him a charge over the earth Who rules over Him, saying, ‘Why did You do so?’ or ‘I did not command You so’? For who gave Him a charge over His dominion, and why should He find pretexts for His creatures? He need not answer one who rules over Him by saying ‘Because of this I harmed him.’

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־יָשִׂ֣ים אֵלָ֣יו לִבּ֑וֹ רוּח֥וֹ וְ֝נִשְׁמָת֗וֹ אֵלָ֥יו יֶאֱסֹֽף
verses.translation: If [God] but intends it,Spirit and breath can be recalled;
verses.rashi: If He puts His heart on man to destroy him, why should He come upon him with a pretext? He can immediately gather in to Himself... His spirit and His soul His spirit and His soul that He blew into him, and no one will stop Him. Thus, all flesh will perish together.

verses.hebrew_full: יִגְוַ֣ע כׇּל־בָּשָׂ֣ר יָ֑חַד וְ֝אָדָ֗ם עַל־עָפָ֥ר יָשֽׁוּב
verses.translation: All flesh would at once expire,And humankind return to dust.

verses.hebrew_full: וְאִם־בִּ֥ינָה שִׁמְעָה־זֹּ֑את הַ֝אֲזִ֗ינָה לְק֣וֹל מִלָּֽי
verses.translation: If you would understand, listen to this;Give ear to what I say.
verses.rashi: And if you wish, understand And if you wish, understand. hearken to this This בּינה is not a noun but an imperative expression, like (Ps. 5:2), “consider (בּינה) my meditation.” Therefore, these two are accented on the first syllable, on the “beth,” whereas all the others are accented on the final syllable, on the “nun.”

verses.hebrew_full: הַאַ֬ף שׂוֹנֵ֣א מִשְׁפָּ֣ט יַחֲב֑וֹשׁ וְאִם־צַדִּ֖יק כַּבִּ֣יר תַּרְשִֽׁיעַ
verses.translation: Would one who hates justice govern?Would you condemn the Just Mighty One?
verses.rashi: Will...even one who hates judgment Will the Omnipresent heal [even the one who hates the judgment] of the just Judge? If you say that [to do so] is not judgment, for He acted not in accordance with the law, is it possible that you should condemn One Who is very righteous? very Heb. כביר, an expression denoting “exceedingly.”

verses.hebrew_full: הַאֲמֹ֣ר לְמֶ֣לֶךְ בְּלִיָּ֑עַל רָ֝שָׁ֗ע אֶל־נְדִיבִֽים
verses.translation: Would you call a king a scoundrel,Nobles, wicked?
verses.rashi: Is it proper to say of a king, ‘scoundrel’ Is it fit and proper to say of a king, ‘scoundrel,’ or ‘wicked’ of nobles?

verses.hebrew_full: אֲשֶׁ֤ר לֹֽא־נָשָׂ֨א פְּנֵ֥י שָׂרִ֗ים וְלֹ֣א נִכַּר־שׁ֭וֹעַ לִפְנֵי־דָ֑ל כִּֽי־מַעֲשֵׂ֖ה יָדָ֣יו כֻּלָּֽם
verses.translation: [God] is not partial to princes;The honorable are not preferred to the wretched;For all of them are the work of God’s hands.
verses.rashi: Who did not respect princes That is to say, the King of the world, before Whom there is no favoritism. a prince Heb. שוע, a prince, as in (Isa. 32:5), “nor shall a deceitful person be said to be noble (שוע).”

verses.hebrew_full: רֶ֤גַע יָמֻתוּ֮ וַחֲצ֢וֹת לָ֥֫יְלָה יְגֹעֲשׁ֣וּ עָ֣ם וְיַעֲבֹ֑רוּ וְיָסִ֥ירוּ אַ֝בִּ֗יר לֹ֣א בְיָֽד
verses.translation: Some die suddenly in the middle of the night;People are in turmoil and pass on;Even heroes are removed—not by human hands.
verses.rashi: In a moment, they die when He wishes. and at midnight as He did to the Egyptians, an [entire] people quakes in a moment and passes away. it is not in their hands it is not in their hands, and they have no strength.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־עֵ֭ינָיו עַל־דַּרְכֵי־אִ֑ישׁ וְֽכׇל־צְעָדָ֥יו יִרְאֶֽה
verses.translation: For God’s eyes are upon everyone’s ways,Observing their every step.

verses.hebrew_full: אֵֽין־חֹ֭שֶׁךְ וְאֵ֣ין צַלְמָ֑וֶת לְהִסָּ֥תֶר שָׁ֝֗ם פֹּ֣עֲלֵי אָֽוֶן
verses.translation: Neither darkness nor gloom offersA hiding-place for evildoers.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י לֹ֣א עַל־אִ֭ישׁ יָשִׂ֣ים ע֑וֹד לַהֲלֹ֥ךְ אֶל־אֵ֝֗ל בַּמִּשְׁפָּֽט
verses.translation: There is no set time for anyoneTo appear before God in judgment.
verses.rashi: For He will not place additional [guilt] on a man That is to say: You said (23:6), ‘He will not place upon me anything but my sins,’ only my sins alone, and I reply to you that the Holy One, blessed be He, will not place on His creatures additional accusations over their transgression. [The word] עוד is an expression of addition. to go to God in judgment When He comes upon His creatures to debate with them.

verses.hebrew_full: יָרֹ֣עַ כַּבִּירִ֣ים לֹא־חֵ֑קֶר וַיַּעֲמֵ֖ד אֲחֵרִ֣ים תַּחְתָּֽם
verses.translation: The mighty are shattered without numberAnd others set in their place.
verses.rashi: He shatters Heb. ירע, [like] ירוֹצֵץ. He shatters, and like (Isa. 24:19), “has broken (התרעעה).” and He stands others in their stead.

verses.hebrew_full: לָכֵ֗ן יַ֭כִּיר מַעְבָּדֵיהֶ֑ם וְהָ֥פַךְ לַ֝֗יְלָה וְיִדַּכָּֽאוּ
verses.translation: Truly, [God] knows their deeds;Night is over, and they are crushed.
verses.rashi: Because He recognizes their deeds Because He recognizes their deeds, whether good or evil, He does not need to debate. He turns night A time of darkness and trouble for them, and they are crushed.

verses.hebrew_full: תַּֽחַת־רְשָׁעִ֥ים סְפָקָ֗ם בִּמְק֥וֹם רֹאִֽים
verses.translation: The wicked are struck downWhere people can see,
verses.rashi: In the place of the wicked Heb. תחת, in their location. He smote them The people of Sodom. in the place of viewers Before everyone’s eyes.

verses.hebrew_full: אֲשֶׁ֣ר עַל־כֵּ֭ן סָ֣רוּ מֵאַחֲרָ֑יו וְכׇל־דְּ֝רָכָ֗יו לֹ֣א הִשְׂכִּֽילוּ
verses.translation: Because they acted disloyallyAnd have not understood any of God’s ways;
verses.rashi: Because they turned away from following Him Heb. אשר על-כן as in (Num. 10:31), “because (על- כן) you knew our camping in the desert.” [This is] like: because (מפני אשר) they turned away from following Him, and they brought before Him and upon Him the cry of the poor.

verses.hebrew_full: לְהָבִ֣יא עָ֭לָיו צַעֲקַת־דָּ֑ל וְצַעֲקַ֖ת עֲנִיִּ֣ים יִשְׁמָֽע
verses.translation: Thus the cry of the poor is admitted;The cry of the needy is heard.

verses.hebrew_full: וְה֤וּא יַשְׁקִ֨ט וּמִ֥י יַרְשִׁ֗עַ וְיַסְתֵּ֣ר פָּ֭נִים וּמִ֣י יְשׁוּרֶ֑נּוּ וְעַל־גּ֖וֹי וְעַל־אָדָ֣ם יָֽחַד
verses.translation: When [God] is silent, who will condemn?If the divine face becomes hidden, who will see it,Be it nation or person?
verses.rashi: When He quiets He gives the poor [some peace and] quiet from those who oppress [them]. Now who will deal wickedly anymore? And as long as He hid His face, who will see Him? whether [it be done] to a nation or to a man, alike As for individuals, [or] as for a multitude it is the same to Him to quiet [them] and to hide His face.

verses.hebrew_full: מִ֭מְּלֹךְ אָדָ֥ם חָנֵ֗ף מִמֹּ֥קְשֵׁי עָֽם
verses.translation: No one impious rules anymore,Nor do those who ensnare the people.
verses.rashi: And it shall be quiet, that a flattering man shall not reign because of the snares of the people Over the poor, who reigned. because of the snares of the people [who were guilty] of iniquity, and concerning such a God, you should not have said, ‘scoundrel,’ and ‘wicked.

words.eng: to be or become king or queen

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־אֶל־אֵ֭ל הֶאָמַ֥ר נָשָׂ֗אתִי לֹ֣א אֶחְבֹּֽל
verses.translation: Has anyone said to God,“I will bear [my punishment] and offend no more.
verses.rashi: For to God should be said To be said to Him. It is fitting and proper for all those who suffer pains to say, ‘I bear, accept, and tolerate Your judgments, and I will not destroy myself.’ Further, the one who is judged must say...

verses.hebrew_full: בִּלְעֲדֵ֣י אֶ֭חֱזֶה אַתָּ֣ה הֹרֵ֑נִי אִֽם־עָ֥וֶל פָּ֝עַ֗לְתִּי לֹ֣א אֹסִֽיף
verses.translation: What I cannot see You teach me.If I have done iniquity, I shall not do so again”?
verses.rashi: Besides what I see Besides what I know to perceive in Your words, You instruct me, and if I have committed injustice, I will not continue.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲֽמֵעִמְּךָ֬ יְשַׁלְּמֶ֨נָּה כִּֽי־מָאַ֗סְתָּ כִּֽי־אַתָּ֣ה תִבְחַ֣ר וְלֹא־אָ֑נִי וּֽמַה־יָּדַ֥עְתָּ דַבֵּֽר
verses.translation: Should [God] requite as you see fit?But you have acted with disdain!You must decide, not I;Speak what you know.
verses.rashi: From you should He pay it, for you despised etc. so did Elihu say to Job, “Should the Holy One, blessed be He, have taken counsel from you about repaying you?” for you despised saying (above 7: 16), “I despised [my life]; I will not live forever”; (above 6:9) “enlarge His hand and finish me off.” Do you think that He would collect from you according to your thoughts and your desire, and you would choose the payment—not I? Elihu spoke instead of the Holy One, blessed be He, with a question.

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace
not·I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: אַנְשֵׁ֣י לֵ֭בָב יֹ֣אמְרוּ לִ֑י וְגֶ֥בֶר חָ֝כָ֗ם שֹׁמֵ֥עַֽ לִֽי
verses.translation: Men of understanding say to me,Wise men who hear me,
verses.rashi: Men of understanding will say to me that Job does not speak with knowledge.

verses.hebrew_full: אִ֭יּוֹב לֹא־בְדַ֣עַת יְדַבֵּ֑ר וּ֝דְבָרָ֗יו לֹ֣א בְהַשְׂכֵּֽיל
verses.translation: “Job does not speak with knowledge;His words lack understanding.”

verses.hebrew_full: אָבִ֗י יִבָּחֵ֣ן אִיּ֣וֹב עַד־נֶ֑צַח עַל־תְּ֝שֻׁבֹ֗ת בְּאַנְשֵׁי־אָֽוֶן
verses.translation: Would that Job were tried to the limitFor answers that befit those who are evil.
verses.rashi: My Father! May [Job] be tried You are my Father. The Holy One, blessed be He, is the Father of all. If only Job were tried to eternity, in order that there be repentance among men of iniquity. And also to men Scripture speaks in this manner (II Kings 5:13): “‘Master (אבי), if the prophet spoke to you to do a difficult thing etc.’” Another explanation: אבי is like (Ecc. 12:5), “and the sexual desire (האביונה) will fail,” an expression of desire. Menachem (Machbereth p. 11) associated it in this manner, and this is its interpretation: אָבִי יִבָּחֵן אִיוֹב עַד נֶצַח, my wish and my desire are that Job should be tried to eternity (omitted in some editions).

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֥י־יֹ֘סִ֤יף עַֽל־חַטָּאת֣וֹ פֶ֭שַׁע בֵּינֵ֣ינוּ יִשְׂפּ֑וֹק וְיֶ֖רֶב אֲמָרָ֣יו לָאֵֽל
verses.translation: He adds to his sin;He increases his transgression among us;He multiplies his statements against God.
verses.rashi: he speaks loquaciously Heb. יספוק is an expression of many words and raising the voice. among us he speaks loquaciously He asks, Why should we be concerned with his words? (above 19:4), ‘And even if I have indeed erred, let my error stay with me,’ and he does not know that we are punished, and he increases iniquities for us because we are listening. So it is explained in Leviticus Rabbah (4:6).

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֥עַן אֱלִיה֗וּ וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Elihu said in reply:

verses.hebrew_full: הֲ֭זֹאת חָשַׁ֣בְתָּ לְמִשְׁפָּ֑ט אָ֝מַ֗רְתָּ צִדְקִ֥י מֵאֵֽל
verses.translation: Do you think it justTo say, “I am right against God”?
verses.rashi: Elihu’s AddressDo you consider this to be customary that this is the custom of the creatures toward their Creator, what you said—‘My righteousness is greater than that of the Creator’?

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־תֹ֭אמַר מַה־יִּסְכׇּן־לָ֑ךְ מָה־אֹ֝עִ֗יל מֵחַטָּאתִֽי
verses.translation: If you ask how it benefits you,“What have I gained from not sinning?”
verses.rashi: That you say, ‘What will it benefit you ways of uprightness, and what profit will I have more than if I had sinned?’

words.eng: interr pron·to be of use or service or profit or benefit·unto thee

verses.hebrew_full: אֲ֭נִי אֲשִֽׁיבְךָ֣ מִלִּ֑ין וְֽאֶת־רֵעֶ֥יךָ עִמָּֽךְ
verses.translation: I shall give you a reply,You, along with your friends.
verses.rashi: and your companions with you who remained silent at your words.

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)
sign of the definite direct object·to pasture

verses.hebrew_full: הַבֵּ֣ט שָׁמַ֣יִם וּרְאֵ֑ה וְשׁ֥וּר שְׁ֝חָקִ֗ים גָּבְה֥וּ מִמֶּֽךָּ
verses.translation: Behold the heavens and see;Look at the skies high above you.
verses.rashi: Gaze at the heavens And since He is high and you are low, and He has no benefit from your wickedness and righteousness, why do you boast to Him about your righteousness?

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־חָ֭טָאתָ מַה־תִּפְעׇל־בּ֑וֹ וְרַבּ֥וּ פְ֝שָׁעֶ֗יךָ מַה־תַּֽעֲשֶׂה־לּֽוֹ
verses.translation: If you sin, what do you do to [God]?If your transgressions are many,How do you affect [God]?

words.eng: interr pron·to do·inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ
interr pron·to do·unto him; v. ל־.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־צָ֭דַקְתָּ מַה־תִּתֶּן־ל֑וֹ א֥וֹ מַה־מִּיָּדְךָ֥ יִקָּֽח
verses.translation: If you are righteous,What do you offer;What does [God] receive from your hand?

words.eng: interr pron·to give·unto him; v. ל־.

verses.hebrew_full: לְאִישׁ־כָּמ֥וֹךָ רִשְׁעֶ֑ךָ וּלְבֶן־אָ֝דָ֗ם צִדְקָתֶֽךָ
verses.translation: Your wickedness affects parties like yourself;Your righteousness, other people.
verses.rashi: a man like yourself your wickedness or your righteousness can and will benefit. Observe [that there are] many wicked men. who,...

verses.hebrew_full: מֵ֭רֹב עֲשׁוּקִ֣ים יַזְעִ֑יקוּ יְשַׁוְּע֖וּ מִזְּר֣וֹעַ רַבִּֽים
verses.translation: Because of contention the oppressed cry out;They shout because of the power of the great.
verses.rashi: Because of the many oppressed ones whom they oppress, they [the wicked] cause the creatures to cry out before Him, and the poor cry for help from the arm of those who oppress them.

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽלֹא־אָמַ֗ר אַ֭יֵּה אֱל֣וֹהַּ עֹשָׂ֑י נֹתֵ֖ן זְמִר֣וֹת בַּלָּֽיְלָה
verses.translation: But none says, “Where is my God, my Maker,Who gives strength in the night;
verses.rashi: And he did not say The wicked man [did not say] ‘Where is God, my Maker?’ to fear Him. Who deals destruction Heb. זמירות like (Lev. 25:4), “nor prune (תזמר) your vineyard,” for He cuts off the wicked at night, e.g., Amraphel and his allies, Egypt, and Sennacherib.

verses.hebrew_full: מַ֭לְּפֵנוּ מִבַּהֲמ֣וֹת אָ֑רֶץ וּמֵע֖וֹף הַשָּׁמַ֣יִם יְחַכְּמֵֽנוּ
verses.translation: Who gives us more knowledge than the beasts of the earth,Makes us wiser than the birds of the sky?”
verses.rashi: Who teaches us He teaches us more wisdom than [He does] the beasts; i.e., He esteemed us and made us greater than the beasts and the birds.

verses.hebrew_full: שָׁ֣ם יִ֭צְעֲקוּ וְלֹ֣א יַעֲנֶ֑ה מִ֝פְּנֵ֗י גְּא֣וֹן רָעִֽים
verses.translation: Then they cry out, but they are not answeredBecause of the arrogance of evildoers.
verses.rashi: There they cry out And see that there the poor cry out because of the pride of those who oppress them, and He does not answer, for...

verses.hebrew_full: אַךְ־שָׁ֭וְא לֹא־יִשְׁמַ֥ע אֵ֑ל וְ֝שַׁדַּ֗י לֹ֣א יְשׁוּרֶֽנָּה
verses.translation: Surely it is false that God does not listen,That Shaddai does not take note of it.
verses.rashi: Indeed, God will not hear vanity immediately. neither will the Almighty see it to avenge it immediately, but He is longsuffering.

verses.hebrew_full: אַ֣ף כִּֽי־תֹ֭אמַר לֹ֣א תְשׁוּרֶ֑נּוּ דִּ֥ין לְ֝פָנָ֗יו וּתְח֥וֹלֵֽל לֽוֹ
verses.translation: Though you say, “You do not take note of it,”The case is before [God];So you must wait.
verses.rashi: Surely you who say Surely you, whose cry comes only because of Him, and you say and cry that you do not see Him to debate with Him. That is to say that those who complain and cry, He does not hasten to answer, yet you cry that you do not see Him? present your case before Him (Your arguments—absent in some editions) wherever He is. and wait And hope for consolations.

verses.hebrew_full: וְעַתָּ֗ה כִּי־אַ֭יִן פָּקַ֣ד אַפּ֑וֹ וְלֹֽא־יָדַ֖ע בַּפַּ֣שׁ מְאֹֽד
verses.translation: But since now it does not seem so,He vents his anger;He does not realize that it may be long drawn out.
verses.rashi: And now you should know that this visitation [of His Wrath upon you is nothing according [compared] to your many iniquities. and He did not know because of the great multiplicity Because of your many sins, the Creator acted as though He did not know them. of the...multiplicity Heb. בפש, an expression of multiplicity, like (Malachi 3:20), “and be fat (ופשתם),” and like (Jer. 50:11), “as you become fat (תפושו)”; (Lev. 13:51), “that the mark has spread (פשה).”

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֭אִיּוֹב הֶ֣בֶל יִפְצֶה־פִּ֑יהוּ בִּבְלִי־דַ֝֗עַת מִלִּ֥ין יַכְבִּֽר
verses.translation: Hence Job mouths empty words,And piles up words without knowledge.
verses.rashi: he increases Heb. כביר, an expression meaning, exceedingly.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֥סֶף אֱלִיה֗וּא וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Then Elihu spoke once more.
verses.rashi: Then Elihu continued I saw in the reasons of Rabbi Moshe Hadarshan (the preacher), that he delivered three addresses corresponding to Job’s three companions, and this is the fourth one. Therefore it is called an addition.

verses.hebrew_full: כַּתַּר־לִ֣י זְ֭עֵיר וַאֲחַוֶּ֑ךָּ כִּ֤י ע֖וֹד לֶאֱל֣וֹהַּ מִלִּֽים
verses.translation: Wait a little and let me hold forth;There is still more to say for God.
verses.rashi: Wait awhile for me and I will tell you This is completely Aramaic: Wait for me a little and I will tell you. [The word] כַּתַּר is an expression of waiting, as Jonathan rendered (Isa. 42:4): “and for his instruction, islands shall long (יכתרון).” God has Those representing Him have.

words.eng: to surround·inflected pers. pron. meaning ‘to me’.

verses.hebrew_full: אֶשָּׂ֣א דֵ֭עִי לְמֵרָח֑וֹק וּ֝לְפֹעֲלִ֗י אֶֽתֵּֽן־צֶֽדֶק
verses.translation: I will make my opinions widely known;I will justify my Maker.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־אׇ֭מְנָם לֹא־שֶׁ֣קֶר מִלָּ֑י תְּמִ֖ים דֵּע֣וֹת עִמָּֽךְ
verses.translation: In truth, my words are not false;Someone of sound opinions is before you.
verses.rashi: you have perfect knowledge Behold, I will now begin to speak with you since you think that your knowledge is perfect.

verses.hebrew_full: הֶן־אֵ֣ל כַּ֭בִּיר וְלֹ֣א יִמְאָ֑ס כַּ֝בִּ֗יר כֹּ֣חַֽ לֵֽב
verses.translation: See, God is mighty, but not contemptuous—Mighty in strength and mind—
verses.rashi: Behold God is great in wisdom and in mercy. and will not despise the poor man. He is great in strength of heart to take revenge. Therefore, He will not allow a wicked man to live.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־יְחַיֶּ֥ה רָשָׁ֑ע וּמִשְׁפַּ֖ט עֲנִיִּ֣ים יִתֵּֽן
verses.translation: Not letting the wicked live,granting justice to the lowly.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹֽא־יִגְרַ֥ע מִצַּדִּ֗יק עֵ֫ינָ֥יו וְאֶת־מְלָכִ֥ים לַכִּסֵּ֑א וַיֹּשִׁיבֵ֥ם לָ֝נֶ֗צַח וַיִּגְבָּֽהוּ
verses.translation: God’s eyes are trained upon the righteous,And upon kings on thrones;For they are seated forever, and exalted.
verses.rashi: He does not withdraw His eyes from the righteous until He seats him with kings.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·king

verses.hebrew_full: וְאִם־אֲסוּרִ֥ים בַּזִּקִּ֑ים יִ֝לָּכְד֗וּן בְּחַבְלֵי־עֹֽנִי
verses.translation: If they are bound in shacklesAnd caught in trammels of affliction,
verses.rashi: And if you see righteous men who are bound with chains of suffering and ailments, or caught with ropes of poverty. In an expression of lots or ropes, they are called חבלים, in an expression of worry, they are called חֶבְלֵי יוֹלֵדָה, pangs of a childbearing woman. with ropes of poverty Heb. בחבלי cordes in French. It is only because they sinned before Him, and He comes to requite them for their own good, to cleanse them and to admonish them to return to Him.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּגֵּ֣ד לָהֶ֣ם פׇּעֳלָ֑ם וּ֝פִשְׁעֵיהֶ֗ם כִּ֣י יִתְגַּבָּֽרוּ
verses.translation: [God] declares to them what they have done,And that their transgressions are excessive—
verses.rashi: He tells them their deed With these sufferings, He lets them know that they sinned before Him. All the words of Elihu were complete consolations and not chidings; i.e., do not worry about the sufferings if you are righteous, because they are for your good,

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּ֣גֶל אׇ֭זְנָם לַמּוּסָ֑ר וַ֝יֹּ֗אמֶר כִּֽי־יְשׁוּב֥וּן מֵאָֽוֶן
verses.translation: Opening their understanding by discipline,And ordering them back from mischief.
verses.rashi: and He says that with sufferings, they will repent of iniquity.

verses.hebrew_full: אִֽם־יִשְׁמְע֗וּ וְֽיַ֫עֲבֹ֥דוּ יְכַלּ֣וּ יְמֵיהֶ֣ם בַּטּ֑וֹב וּ֝שְׁנֵיהֶ֗ם בַּנְּעִימִֽים
verses.translation: If they will serve obediently,They shall spend their days in happiness,Their years in delight.
verses.rashi: If they understand and worship Him.

verses.hebrew_full: וְאִם־לֹ֣א יִ֭שְׁמְעוּ בְּשֶׁ֣לַח יַעֲבֹ֑רוּ וְ֝יִגְוְע֗וּ בִּבְלִי־דָֽעַת
verses.translation: But if they are not obedient,They shall perish by the sword,Die for lack of understanding.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽחַנְפֵי־לֵ֭ב יָשִׂ֣ימוּ אָ֑ף לֹ֥א יְ֝שַׁוְּע֗וּ כִּ֣י אֲסָרָֽם
verses.translation: But the impious in heart become enraged;They do not cry for help when afflicted.
verses.rashi: But the wicked of heart who are wicked—they bring about wrath; when suffering comes upon them, they curse and blaspheme, and they do not beg mercy with supplication when He binds them with sufferings; therefore He does not heal them.

verses.hebrew_full: תָּמֹ֣ת בַּנֹּ֣עַר נַפְשָׁ֑ם וְ֝חַיָּתָ֗ם בַּקְּדֵשִֽׁים
verses.translation: They die in their youth;[Expire] among the depraved.
verses.rashi: shall die by strangulation Their soul [shall die] by the strangulation that is upon them,

verses.hebrew_full: יְחַלֵּ֣ץ עָנִ֣י בְעׇנְי֑וֹ וְיִ֖גֶל בַּלַּ֣חַץ אׇזְנָֽם
verses.translation: Rescuing the lowly from their affliction,[God] opens their understanding through distress.
verses.rashi: but the poor man, who makes himself poor in the matter of supplications...He extricates from Gehinnom because of his poverty, which He brought upon him. and opens their ear through oppression Through the oppression that He brought upon him, He opens his ear to say, “Return to Me.”

verses.hebrew_full: וְאַ֤ף הֲסִֽיתְךָ֨ מִפִּי־צָ֗ר רַ֭חַב לֹא־מוּצָ֣ק תַּחְתֶּ֑יהָ וְנַ֥חַת שֻׁ֝לְחָנְךָ֗ מָ֣לֵא דָֽשֶׁן
verses.translation: Indeed, you are drawn away from the brink of distressTo a broad place where there is no constraint;Your table is laid out with rich food.
verses.rashi: Moreover This is yet another great benefit that He did for you with these sufferings: He allured you and enticed you out of Gehinnom, whose mouth is narrow and whose base is wide. not cramped so that its smoke should be gathered into its midst, and furthermore, that you should merit the world to come; and the bedecking of your table shall be full of fatness.

verses.hebrew_full: וְדִין־רָשָׁ֥ע מָלֵ֑אתָ דִּ֖ין וּמִשְׁפָּ֣ט יִתְמֹֽכוּ
verses.translation: You are obsessed with the case of someone wicked,But the justice of the case will be upheld.
verses.rashi: And if you have had your fill of the sentence of the wicked And if you have had your fill of the sufferings of the wicked, the judgment and the sentence—which are [represented by] the sufferings—will support you in the future from Gehinnom, and you must accept them lovingly.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־חֵ֭מָה פֶּן־יְסִֽיתְךָ֣ בְסָ֑פֶק וְרׇב־כֹּ֝֗פֶר אַל־יַטֶּֽךָּ
verses.translation: Let anger at his affluence not mislead you;Let much bribery not turn you aside.
verses.rashi: For wrath [There] shall be great [wrath]. lest it entice you [Lest] your evil inclination [entice you to speak] words. to multiply Heb. בשפק, lit. with multiplicity; [i.e., with] which you multiply and increase your words toward God; and after you have raised His wrath, much ransom, [i.e.,] much money will not turn you away [rescue you] from it.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲיַעֲרֹ֣ךְ שׁ֭וּעֲךָ לֹ֣א בְצָ֑ר וְ֝כֹ֗ל מַאֲמַצֵּי־כֹֽחַ
verses.translation: Will your limitless wealth avail you, All your powerful efforts?
verses.rashi: Will you set up your prayer so that no trouble Have you come to set up a cry that no trouble befall you, or any forces of strength to rob you? If so, with what will He collect His debt?

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תִּשְׁאַ֥ף הַלָּ֑יְלָה לַעֲל֖וֹת עַמִּ֣ים תַּחְתָּֽם
verses.translation: Do not long for the nightWhen peoples vanish where they are.
verses.rashi: Do not yearn for the night That is to say that you should not choose other sufferings. Do not yearn for, or desire the night that came to Amraphel, to Egypt, and to Sennacherib, to take away peoples from the world when they are in their place in tranquility, for they perished both from this world and from the world to come. are taken away Heb. לעלות, like (Ps. 102:25), “do not take me away (תעלני) in the midst of my days.”

verses.hebrew_full: הִ֭שָּׁמֶר אַל־תֵּ֣פֶן אֶל־אָ֑וֶן כִּֽי־עַל־זֶ֝֗ה בָּחַ֥רְתָּ מֵעֹֽנִי
verses.translation: Beware! Do not turn to mischief;Because of that you have been tried by affliction.
verses.rashi: Beware, do not turn to iniquity to contend and to say, “He should have judged me with the sufferings of poverty.” for...this These sufferings are to be chosen above poverty. A man to whom people behaved with respect and who has been accustomed to wealth all his life, will now stand and bargain, and having no money [to pay with], will be embarrassed and return home in disappointment. Now if you say that Job was a poor man in addition to his sufferings, we do not find that he lost anything except his livestock, but not silver or gold. And even if he lost his cattle with silver and gold, it is better for him to be a poor sick man than to be healthy and go in the marketplaces empty-handed [and be embarrassed as well]. In this manner, it is explained in the midrash of Rabbi Tanchuma (Mishpatim 11) from this verse, that Job’s sufferings are preferable to the sufferings of poverty, and so in the Aggadoth of the Talmud (Baba Bathra 116a).

verses.hebrew_full: הֶן־אֵ֭ל יַשְׂגִּ֣יב בְּכֹח֑וֹ מִ֖י כָמֹ֣הוּ מוֹרֶֽה
verses.translation: See, God is beyond reach in divine power;Who governs like this?
verses.rashi: Behold God deals loftily in His power Because His power is great, and one cannot be saved from before Him. Therefore...who is a teacher like Him? When He teaches a sinner to return to Him, He warns Him before the plague because He knows that he cannot beware of His plague, as He would warn Pharaoh concerning each plague (Ex. 8:17), “I shall let loose the beasts of the wilderness upon you etc.”; (ibid. 10:4), “I will bring locusts...tomorrow,” and so all of them. [In contrast,] when a mortal king wishes to wreak vengeance upon his enemies, he comes upon him suddenly, for, if he warns him, he will repent and be saved from him.

verses.hebrew_full: מִֽי־פָקַ֣ד עָלָ֣יו דַּרְכּ֑וֹ וּמִי־אָ֝מַ֗ר פָּעַ֥לְתָּ עַוְלָֽה
verses.translation: Who ever reproached [God] on account of conduct?Who ever said, “You have done wrong”?
verses.rashi: Who charged Him with His way [Who] commanded Him from time immemorial, “You shall follow this way”?

verses.hebrew_full: זְ֭כֹר כִּֽי־תַשְׂגִּ֣יא פׇעֳל֑וֹ אֲשֶׁ֖ר שֹׁרְר֣וּ אֲנָשִֽׁים
verses.translation: Remember, then, to magnify God’s work,Of which humankind has sung,
verses.rashi: Mention His ways, and when you mention [them], you will automatically magnify the praise of the works of His attributes. which men have seen Heb. שררו, which men have seen, like (Num. 24:17), “I behold it (אשורנו), but it is not near.”

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־אָדָ֥ם חָזוּ־ב֑וֹ אֱ֝נ֗וֹשׁ יַבִּ֥יט מֵרָחֽוֹק
verses.translation: Which all have beheld,Mortals have seen, from a distance.
verses.rashi: have seen it His work. from afar from the Creation or the world until now.

words.eng: Hazo = "vision"·inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: הֶן־אֵ֣ל שַׂ֭גִּיא וְלֹ֣א נֵדָ֑ע מִסְפַּ֖ר שָׁנָ֣יו וְלֹא־חֵֽקֶר
verses.translation: See, God is greater than we can know—Whose age in years cannot be counted—
verses.rashi: the number of His years is unfathomable Therefore man must look from afar to ask what happened to the first people, and he will learn from them.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֭י יְגָרַ֣ע נִטְפֵי־מָ֑יִם יָזֹ֖קּוּ מָטָ֣ר לְאֵדֽוֹ
verses.translation: Who forms the droplets of water,Which cluster into rain, from celestial mist.
verses.rashi: For He increases Heb. יגרע, for He increases, like (above 15:4), “and increase (ותגרע) speech.” Others interpret: He withdraws them from the heavens to bring them down to earth. they pour The firmaments [pour] the rain. into His cloud Heb. לְאֵדוֹ, into His cloud.

verses.hebrew_full: אֲשֶֽׁר־יִזְּל֥וּ שְׁחָקִ֑ים יִ֝רְעֲפ֗וּ עֲלֵ֤י אָדָ֬ם רָֽב
verses.translation: The skies rain;They pour down on all humankind.

verses.hebrew_full: אַ֣ף אִם־יָ֭בִין מִפְרְשֵׂי־עָ֑ב תְּ֝שֻׁא֗וֹת סֻכָּתֽוֹ
verses.translation: Can one, indeed, contemplate the expanse of clouds,The thunderings from God’s pavilion?
verses.rashi: Or will one understand Or will man understand what is in the spreadings of the clouds? This is a question. the darkness Heb. תשאות, an expression of the thick darkness of His pavilion.

verses.hebrew_full: הֵן־פָּרַ֣שׂ עָלָ֣יו אוֹר֑וֹ וְשׇׁרְשֵׁ֖י הַיָּ֣ם כִּסָּֽה
verses.translation: See, with the lightning spread over it,It fills the bed of the sea.
verses.rashi: Behold He spread (on the cloud His rain) and covered the roots of the sea therewith.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־בָ֭ם יָדִ֣ין עַמִּ֑ים יִֽתֶּן־אֹ֥כֶל לְמַכְבִּֽיר
verses.translation: By these things peoples are controlled;Food is given in abundance.
verses.rashi: For...therewith With the clouds He judges peoples and chastises them by withholding rain, causing famine, as well as all sorts of plagues. When the Generation of the Flood sinned (Gen. 7:11), “and the floodgates of the heavens were opened.” As for the people of Sodom (Deut. 29:22), “Sulfur and salt, all the soil is burned up.” As for Sisera (Jud. 5:20), “From heaven they fought...against Sisera.” He gives food to one who has many From there comes sustenance (Ex. 16:4): “See, I am about to have bread rain down from heaven for you.” This is to be compared to a baker who is standing before the oven. If an enemy comes in, he rakes coals and casts them at him; if his friend comes in, he takes out hot bread and gives it to him. to the one who has many to the one whose children are many and numerous; to one who increases children and requires food.

verses.hebrew_full: עַל־כַּפַּ֥יִם כִּסָּה־א֑וֹר וַיְצַ֖ו עָלֶ֣יהָ בְמַפְגִּֽיעַ
verses.translation: Lightning fills God’s hands,Who orders it to hit the mark.
verses.rashi: Over the clouds Heb. כפים, over the clouds. Similarly (Lam. 3:41), “Let us lift our heart to the clouds (כפים),” based on (I Kings 18:44), “...a cloud as small as a man’s palm (ככף).” But our Rabbis said (Taanith 7b): Because of the sin of (the robbery of) the hands He covers the rain The rain is withheld. and He commands it to come through one who entreats with prayer. (The beloved one of the generation, and the great man and sage thereof will relate to Him—before the Holy One, blessed be He—the straits of the generation and their necessities].

words.eng: to cover·to be or become light

verses.hebrew_full: יַגִּ֣יד עָלָ֣יו רֵע֑וֹ מִ֝קְנֶ֗ה אַ֣ף עַל־עוֹלֶֽה
verses.translation: Its noise tells of its Source.The kindling of anger against iniquity.
verses.rashi: Their beloved one preaches to them He preaches to them to search their deeds for themselves and to return to Him. the acquisition This is the acquisition whereby the rain is acquired. it also ascends on high Heb. על. It also [ascends] higher and higher [in this manner], like (Hosea 7: 16), “They will return, but not to the Most High (על)” or (II Sam. 23:1), “raised on high (על).” [See Comm. Dig. to Hosea 7:16.] Our Sages, however, interpret it (Taanith 8a) as the acquisition of anger and wrath upon one who becomes haughty [after his prayers have been answered], but the vowelization, viz. that מִקְנֶה is vowelized with a “pattah” [meaning a “seggol”], does not substantiate [the theory] that it is connected to אַף, for were it connected to אַף, it would be vowelized with a “kamatz” [meaning a “tzeireh”], and it would not be punctuated by a cantillation sign that denotes a pause, viz. a revi’i.

verses.hebrew_full: אַף־לְ֭זֹאת יֶחֱרַ֣ד לִבִּ֑י וְ֝יִתַּ֗ר מִמְּקוֹמֽוֹ
verses.translation: Because of this, too, my heart quakes,And leaps from its place.
verses.rashi: and spring Heb. ויתר, and spring, like (Lev. 11:21) “to hop (לנתר) with them upon the earth.” And this means that his heart is astonished.

verses.hebrew_full: שִׁמְע֤וּ שָׁמ֣וֹעַ בְּרֹ֣גֶז קֹל֑וֹ וְ֝הֶ֗גֶה מִפִּ֥יו יֵצֵֽא
verses.translation: Just listen to the noise of the rumbling,To the sound that comes out of God’s mouth.
verses.rashi: Hear attentively the noise of His voice The thunders that He thunders in the sky are like a sound that emanates from His mouth.

verses.hebrew_full: תַּֽחַת־כׇּל־הַשָּׁמַ֥יִם יִשְׁרֵ֑הוּ וְ֝אוֹר֗וֹ עַל־כַּנְפ֥וֹת הָאָֽרֶץ
verses.translation: It is let loose beneath the entire heavens—God’s lightning, to the ends of the earth.
verses.rashi: Beneath the entire heaven I have seen it Heb. ישרהו, I have seen it, like (Num. 24:17), “I behold it (אשורנו).” They are the lights illuminated by the lightnings.

verses.hebrew_full: אַחֲרָ֤יו יִשְׁאַג־ק֗וֹל יַ֭רְעֵם בְּק֣וֹל גְּאוֹנ֑וֹ וְלֹ֥א יְ֝עַקְּבֵ֗ם כִּֽי־יִשָּׁמַ֥ע קוֹלֽוֹ
verses.translation: After it, a roar is released—Thundered in God’s majestic voice.No one can find a trace of it by the time God’s voice is heard.
verses.rashi: In its wake [i.e., in the wake] of that light, the thunder roars, and it thunders with the voice of its pride. Those are the lightnings and the thunders, which bring the rain. and He will not cut them off The Omnipresent [will not cut off the thunder and the lightning]. for...will be heard before Him the voice of the one who prays. He will...cut them off Heb. יעקבם, He will cut them off, [and in the language of the Mishnah we learned (Sotah 16a): in three instances, the halachah supplants (עוקבת) the text].

verses.hebrew_full: יַרְעֵ֤ם אֵ֣ל בְּ֭קוֹלוֹ נִפְלָא֑וֹת עֹשֶׂ֥ה גְ֝דֹל֗וֹת וְלֹ֣א נֵדָֽע
verses.translation: Thundering marvelously with that voice,[God] works wonders that we cannot understand.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י לַשֶּׁ֨לֶג יֹאמַ֗ר הֱוֵ֫א־אָ֥רֶץ וְגֶ֥שֶׁם מָטָ֑ר וְ֝גֶ֗שֶׁם מִטְר֥וֹת עֻזּֽוֹ
verses.translation: The snow is commanded, “Fall to the ground!”And the downpour of rain, God’s mighty downpour of rain,
verses.rashi: For He says to the snow through these voices. ‘Be upon the earth’; likewise to the shower of rain and to the showers of His mighty rain That is to say that it comes from the four directions.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּיַד־כׇּל־אָדָ֥ם יַחְתּ֑וֹם לָ֝דַ֗עַת כׇּל־אַנְשֵׁ֥י מַעֲשֵֽׂהוּ
verses.translation: Is as a sign on everyone’s hand,That all may know God’s doings.
verses.rashi: By the hand of every man he seals When a man sins before Him, the man himself signs his signature on the day of his death to the sins that he committed, which are written before him. so that every man should know his deed to let him know for what he is being judged.

verses.hebrew_full: וַתָּב֣וֹא חַיָּ֣ה בְמוֹ־אָ֑רֶב וּבִמְע֖וֹנֹתֶ֣יהָ תִשְׁכֹּֽן
verses.translation: Then the beast enters its lair,And remains in its den.
verses.rashi: The beasts go into ambush That is to say that the Omnipresent has many more agents through which to mete out His punishments: the inciting of wild beasts, the tempest, and the cold.

verses.hebrew_full: מִן־הַ֭חֶדֶר תָּב֣וֹא סוּפָ֑ה וּֽמִמְּזָרִ֥ים קָרָֽה
verses.translation: The storm wind comes from its chamber,And the cold from the constellations.
verses.rashi: and from Mezarim There is a treasury, named Mezarim.

verses.hebrew_full: מִנִּשְׁמַת־אֵ֥ל יִתֶּן־קָ֑רַח וְרֹ֖חַב מַ֣יִם בְּמוּצָֽק
verses.translation: By the breath of God ice is formed,And the expanse of water becomes solid.
verses.rashi: From the breath of By His wind. and the breadth of the waters in a good When they are poured out in retribution.

verses.hebrew_full: אַף־בְּ֭רִי יַטְרִ֣יחַ עָ֑ב יָ֝פִ֗יץ עֲנַ֣ן אוֹרֽוֹ
verses.translation: [God] also loads the clouds with moistureAnd scatters the lightning-clouds.
verses.rashi: Aph Beri The name of the angel who is appointed over the clouds, and he scatters the Omnipresent’s rain cloud.

verses.hebrew_full: וְה֤וּא מְסִבּ֨וֹת מִתְהַפֵּ֣ךְ בְּתַחְבּוּלֹתָ֣ו לְפׇעֳלָ֑ם כֹּ֖ל אֲשֶׁ֥ר יְצַוֵּ֓ם עַל־פְּנֵ֖י תֵבֵ֣ל אָֽרְצָה
verses.translation: Turning and overturning events via stratagems, That they might accomplish all God’s commandsThroughout the inhabited earth,
verses.rashi: And he who is thus appointed. turns around in circuits by His guidance [i.e., by the guidance] of the Omnipresent. for their work namely, of the rains; for the work which is incumbent upon them to do, either good or evil, he is turned about with many circuits, as [the passage] that we learned (Rosh Hashanah 17b): if Israel were perfectly righteous on Rosh Hashanah so that [much] rain was decreed for them, and they should later become sinful—it would be impossible to decrease it [the amount of rain] because the decree would already have gone forth; but the Holy One, blessed be He, would let it come down out of its season and upon a land that did not require it. “And sometimes to do good”; how is this? etc. whatever He commands them Those rains, to do on earth. [Another explanation: And he turns around in circuits with His guidance—He causes the clouds and the winds to encompass the mountains so that they should be watered from all sides. In the Aggadah of “Vehayah ekev” (Sifrei Ekev 39).]

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־לְשֵׁ֥בֶט אִם־לְאַרְצ֑וֹ אִם־לְ֝חֶ֗סֶד יַמְצִאֵֽהוּ
verses.translation: [God] causes each of them to happen to the land,Whether as a scourge or as a blessing.
verses.rashi: Whether for the rod Whether for the punishment of men, He lets them fall on mountains and hills, which are not places for sowing. whether for His land To let them fall as originally decreed, neither for the rod nor within the boundary of justice [i.e., for more benefit than strict justice warrants], but midway between [these two extremes]. or for kindness If the generation became more righteous than they were on Rosh Hashanah, then the rain which was decreed to fall in an average manner, shall be turned about by the guidance of their circuits to fall, with kindness, in their season and upon the land that requires them, so that not a drop of them should go to waste. that He cause it to come The rain, in one of these ways.

verses.hebrew_full: הַאֲזִ֣ינָה זֹּ֣את אִיּ֑וֹב עֲ֝מֹ֗ד וְהִתְבּוֹנֵ֤ן נִפְלְא֬וֹת אֵֽל
verses.translation: Give ear to this, Job;Stop to consider the marvels of God.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲ֭תֵדַע בְּשׂוּם־אֱל֣וֹהַּ עֲלֵיהֶ֑ם וְ֝הֹפִ֗יעַ א֣וֹר עֲנָנֽוֹ
verses.translation: Do you know what charge God lays upon themBy making the lightning-clouds shine?
verses.rashi: how God places the law of the covenant upon them, how they are suspended by His decree.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲ֭תֵדַע עַל־מִפְלְשֵׂי־עָ֑ב מִ֝פְלְא֗וֹת תְּמִ֣ים דֵּעִֽים
verses.translation: Do you know the marvels worked upon the expanse of cloudsBy the One whose understanding is perfect,
verses.rashi: the revelations of the clouds Heb. מפלשי, the revelation of the clouds, an expression of an open (מפלש) alley, i.e., to the length and the width of the clouds. Do you know concerning them, the wonders of Him Who is perfect in knowledge?

verses.hebrew_full: אֲשֶׁר־בְּגָדֶ֥יךָ חַמִּ֑ים בְּהַשְׁקִ֥ט אֶ֝֗רֶץ מִדָּרֽוֹם
verses.translation: Why your clothes become hot When the land is becalmed by the south wind?
verses.rashi: How your garments are warm How your garments are warm when He silences the earth from the south wind, so that it should not kill the world with cold, because the east wind warms it. As it is stated (Jonah 4:8): “a stilling east wind,” which quiets all other winds before it.

verses.hebrew_full: תַּרְקִ֣יעַ עִ֭מּוֹ לִשְׁחָקִ֑ים חֲ֝זָקִ֗ים כִּרְאִ֥י מוּצָֽק
verses.translation: Can you help to stretch out the heavens,Firm as a mirror of cast metal?
verses.rashi: strong the skies. as a molten mirror Like a mirror into which women look. [molten tristre, which is very strong.]

verses.hebrew_full: ה֭וֹדִיעֵנוּ מַה־נֹּ֣אמַר ל֑וֹ לֹֽא־נַ֝עֲרֹ֗ךְ מִפְּנֵי־חֹֽשֶׁךְ
verses.translation: Inform us, then, what we may say to [God];We cannot argue because [we are in] darkness.
verses.rashi: Let us know what we shall say to Him To the Holy One, blessed be He, when we contend with Him. We will not be able to set up a case with Him because of the darkness which conceals and surrounds Him.

verses.hebrew_full: הַֽיְסֻפַּר־ל֭וֹ כִּ֣י אֲדַבֵּ֑ר אִֽם־אָ֥מַר אִ֝֗ישׁ כִּ֣י יְבֻלָּֽע
verses.translation: Is anything conveyed when I speak?Can a person say anything while confused?
verses.rashi: Need it be told to Him when I speak Is He like mortals, that He must be told what we are saying? or need a man tell utter with his mouth. what is kept a secret so that it should be revealed to Him immediately, like (II Sam. 17:16), “lest the king be told secretly (יבלע)” [lest] it be revealed. But our Sages (Meg. 18a) explained: Can all His praise be told Him, that I should speak That we should speak of Him and His might? Has any man said to tell them all, he would surely be destroyed from his place.

verses.hebrew_full: וְעַתָּ֤ה לֹ֘א־רָ֤אוּ א֗וֹר בָּהִ֣יר ה֭וּא בַּשְּׁחָקִ֑ים וְר֥וּחַ עָ֝בְרָ֗ה וַֽתְּטַהֲרֵֽם
verses.translation: Now, then, one cannot see the sun,Though it be bright in the heavens,Until the wind comes and clears them [of clouds].
verses.rashi: And now, they did not see the rain At the conclusion of his words, Elihu returns to chide Job’s three companions for not finding an answer. He therefore says, “And now, these three companions of yours, who should have answered you all these things that I said to you... did not see the rain” They are like stillborn babies, who did not see wisdom. it is [like] bright [clouds] in the skies It is like many bright clouds that appear in the skies to bring down rain, but the wind comes and clears them away, makes them pass away, and no rain falls. That is their analogy; they began to answer you, but at the end, they remained silent and did not answer.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: מִ֭צָּפוֹן זָהָ֣ב יֶאֱתֶ֑ה עַל־אֱ֝ל֗וֹהַּ נ֣וֹרָא הֽוֹד
verses.translation: By the north wind the golden rays emerge;The splendor about God is awesome.
verses.rashi: Gold[en splendor] comes from the north It is as though he is returning to his previous statements. [“And now, they did not see the rain.”] Another explanation: The homily of the Sages (Taanith 7b) is that when the sky is full of spots, [i.e., full] of many clouds, to cause rain to fall, a wind passes and cleanses them away, and no rain falls. This is a homily of the Sages, but it does not fit the sequence of the verses. Gold comes from the north The north wind blows and drives away the rain. Then the sun shines, like (Zech. 4:12), “that empty out the gold[en oil] from themselves.” on God From before Him. (Gold comes from the north The good gold is hidden for those who bring themselves to the commandments of the Holy One, blessed be He, and believe in the very awesome God.)

verses.hebrew_full: שַׁדַּ֣י לֹא־מְ֭צָאנֻהוּ שַׂגִּיא־כֹ֑חַ וּמִשְׁפָּ֥ט וְרֹב־צְ֝דָקָ֗ה לֹ֣א יְעַנֶּֽה
verses.translation: Shaddai—to whom we cannot attain—Is great in power and justiceAnd abundant in righteousness, and does not torment.
verses.rashi: We have not found the Almighty great in power to arouse His judgments upon the creatures with the greatness of His power, but with clemency. He makes atonements for the creatures with small things, according to their ability: a handful of [fine flour from] a meal offering, a half shekel, turtle doves and young pigeons, cattle and flocks; and He did not burden them to seek a wild ox or an antelope. and with judgment Sufferings. He does not afflict the creatures excessively. Likewise, He does nor afflict them to [force them into] becoming overly righteous to an impossible extent, but each man according to his ability.

verses.hebrew_full: לָ֭כֵן יְרֵא֣וּהוּ אֲנָשִׁ֑ים לֹֽא־יִ֝רְאֶ֗ה כׇּל־חַכְמֵי־לֵֽב
verses.translation: Therefore, humankind is in awe of the OneWhom none of the wise can perceive.
verses.rashi: Therefore, men fear Him to debate with Him. (because) He does not look upon any wise of heart who want to deal wisely with Him, because their wisdom is as nothing in His eyes. Our Sages (Rosh Hashanah 17a) explained this as referring to Job. Elihu says to him, “Because you would impose excessive fear upon your generation, you did not merit having wise-hearted sons remain alive to you.”

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּעַן־יְהֹוָ֣ה אֶת־אִ֭יּוֹב (מנהסערה) מִ֥ן הַסְּעָרָ֗ה וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Then GOD replied to Job out of the tempest and said:
verses.rashi: from the tempest Heb. מן הסערה. Count your hairs and I will give you as many answers. From the tempest with which you reproached Me (above 9:17), “He Who would crush me with a tempest,” I will answer you, as you will hear at the end of the chapter (verse 25), “Who has separated a place for the hair?” a shaft for every hair. Another explanation: With a tempest He afflicted him, and with a tempest He healed him, as is explained in Sifrei(?).

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Job = "hated"

verses.hebrew_full: מִ֤י זֶ֨ה מַחְשִׁ֖יךְ עֵצָ֥ה בְמִלִּ֗ין בְּֽלִי־דָֽעַת
verses.translation: Who is this who darkens counsel,Speaking without knowledge?
verses.rashi: who gives dark counsel Dark and foolish counsel. Another explanation: Who is this who gives dark counsel...without knowledge? Said the Holy One, blessed be He: Who is this who darkens counsel, frustrates words, i.e., with a multiplicity of words; for I wrote: “and that man was sincere and upright,” at the beginning of the Book, in order that My name should rest on Him, but he came and darkened and frustrated what I counseled with his many words. Whereupon Job replies to Him (below 42:3): “Who is this Who hides counsel?” Had I known Your counsel, I would not have spoken so much. Then the Holy One blessed be He, answers him, “Neither did Abraham know; yet he passed ten” [tests]. So I found.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱזׇר־נָ֣א כְגֶ֣בֶר חֲלָצֶ֑יךָ וְ֝אֶשְׁאָלְךָ֗ וְהוֹדִיעֵֽנִי
verses.translation: Gird your loins like a man;—prepare to defend yourself (verbally), for I am about to challenge you. I will ask and you will inform Me.
verses.rashi: Now gird your loins like a man He intimated to him that he should arouse himself from his illness and his sufferings.

verses.hebrew_full: אֵיפֹ֣ה הָ֭יִיתָ בְּיׇסְדִי־אָ֑רֶץ הַ֝גֵּ֗ד אִם־יָדַ֥עְתָּ בִינָֽה
verses.translation: Where were you when I laid the earth’s foundations?Speak if you have understanding.
verses.rashi: Where Heb. אֵי פֹה, lit. where here.

verses.hebrew_full: מִי־שָׂ֣ם מְ֭מַדֶּיהָ כִּ֣י תֵדָ֑ע א֤וֹ מִֽי־נָטָ֖ה עָלֶ֣יהָ קָּֽו
verses.translation: Do you know who fixed its dimensionsOr who measured it with a line?
verses.rashi: its measures Heb. מְמַדֶיהָ [like] מִדוֹתֶיהָ.

verses.hebrew_full: עַל־מָ֭ה אֲדָנֶ֣יהָ הׇטְבָּ֑עוּ א֥וֹ מִי־יָ֝רָ֗ה אֶ֣בֶן פִּנָּתָֽהּ
verses.translation: Onto what were its bases sunk?Who set its cornerstone
verses.rashi: laid its cornerstone in the middle of the sea, whence the entire world was founded. Where were you?

verses.hebrew_full: בְּרׇן־יַ֭חַד כּ֣וֹכְבֵי בֹ֑קֶר וַ֝יָּרִ֗יעוּ כׇּל־בְּנֵ֥י אֱלֹהִֽים
verses.translation: When the morning stars sang togetherAnd all the divine beings shouted for joy?
verses.rashi: When...sing together from the beginning the stars of light.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיָּ֣סֶךְ בִּדְלָתַ֣יִם יָ֑ם בְּ֝גִיח֗וֹ מֵרֶ֥חֶם יֵצֵֽא
verses.translation: Who closed the sea behind doorsWhen it gushed forth out of the womb,
verses.rashi: the sea with doors The sand, which is its boundary. when it broke forth Heb. בגיחו, when it broke forth from the deep, like (Ps. 22:10), “that You drew me (גחי) forth from the womb”; drew me forth.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּשׂוּמִ֣י עָנָ֣ן לְבֻשׁ֑וֹ וַ֝עֲרָפֶ֗ל חֲתֻלָּתֽוֹ
verses.translation: When I clothed it in clouds,Swaddled it in dense clouds,
verses.rashi: When I made the cloud around the ocean, which encompasses the world, and clouds encompass it like raiment. its wrapping Heb. חתלתו, its wrapping, like (Ezek. 16:4), “nor were you swaddled (והחתל לא חתלת).”

verses.hebrew_full: וָאֶשְׁבֹּ֣ר עָלָ֣יו חֻקִּ֑י וָ֝אָשִׂ֗ים בְּרִ֣יחַ וּדְלָתָֽיִם
verses.translation: When I made breakers My limit for it,And set up its bar and doors,
verses.rashi: and broke I made breaks in it [the sea] all around to keep it back within them, and they are its boundary, which it shall not pass, as in (Joshua 7:5), “and they pursued them until the quarries (השברים)” which are the ditches around the city.

verses.hebrew_full: וָאֹמַ֗ר עַד־פֹּ֣ה תָ֭בוֹא וְלֹ֣א תֹסִ֑יף וּפֹא־יָ֝שִׁ֗ית בִּגְא֥וֹן גַּלֶּֽיךָ
verses.translation: And said, “You may come so far and no farther;Here your surging waves will stop”?

verses.hebrew_full: הֲֽ֭מִיָּמֶיךָ צִוִּ֣יתָ בֹּ֑קֶר (ידעתה שחר) יִדַּ֖עְתָּ הַשַּׁ֣חַר מְקֹמֽוֹ
verses.translation: Have you ever commanded the day to break,Assigned the dawn its place,
verses.rashi: In [all] your days From the day you were born.

verses.hebrew_full: לֶ֭אֱחֹז בְּכַנְפ֣וֹת הָאָ֑רֶץ וְיִנָּעֲר֖וּ רְשָׁעִ֣ים מִמֶּֽנָּה
verses.translation: So that it seizes the corners of the earthAnd shakes the wicked out of it?
verses.rashi: To grasp the corners of the earth Moreover, in all your days, did you command [anyone] to grasp the corners of the earth like a man who grasps the corners of a garment and shakes it? So am I destined to grasp it [the earth] by its corners and shake the wicked [out of it]. Likewise, Scripture states (Isa. 31:3): “and the Lord shall turn His hand, and the helper shall stumble,” like a person who, holding something in his hand, drops it by inclining his hand.

verses.hebrew_full: תִּ֭תְהַפֵּךְ כְּחֹ֣מֶר חוֹתָ֑ם וְ֝יִֽתְיַצְּב֗וּ כְּמ֣וֹ לְבֽוּשׁ
verses.translation: It changes like clay under the sealTill [its hues] are fixed like those of a garment.
verses.rashi: changes in formation. The seal...like clay The cast of man’s countenance [changes] when he dies, and at the resurrection of the dead they will live again, and they will stand up in their clothing.

verses.hebrew_full: וְיִמָּנַ֣ע מֵרְשָׁעִ֣ים אוֹרָ֑ם וּזְר֥וֹעַ רָ֝מָ֗ה תִּשָּׁבֵֽר
verses.translation: Their light is withheld from the wicked,And the upraised arm is broken.
verses.rashi: And...shall be withheld then from the wicked their light, and the high arm shall be broken.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲ֭בָאתָ עַד־נִבְכֵי־יָ֑ם וּבְחֵ֥קֶר תְּ֝ה֗וֹם הִתְהַלָּֽכְתָּ
verses.translation: Have you penetrated to the sources of the sea,Or walked in the recesses of the deep?
verses.rashi: the locks of the sea Heb. נבכי, like (Ex. 14:3), “They are trapped (נבכים) in the land.”

verses.hebrew_full: הֲנִגְל֣וּ לְ֭ךָ שַׁעֲרֵי־מָ֑וֶת וְשַׁעֲרֵ֖י צַלְמָ֣וֶת תִּרְאֶֽה
verses.translation: Have the gates of death been disclosed to you?Have you seen the gates of deep darkness?
verses.rashi: the gates of death This is Gehinnom.

verses.hebrew_full: הִ֭תְבֹּנַנְתָּ עַד־רַחֲבֵי־אָ֑רֶץ הַ֝גֵּ֗ד אִם־יָדַ֥עְתָּ כֻלָּֽהּ
verses.translation: Have you surveyed the expanses of the earth?If you know of these—tell Me.

verses.hebrew_full: אֵי־זֶ֣ה הַ֭דֶּרֶךְ יִשְׁכׇּן־א֑וֹר וְ֝חֹ֗שֶׁךְ אֵי־זֶ֥ה מְקֹמֽוֹ
verses.translation: Which path leads to where light dwells,And where is the place of darkness,

words.eng: to settle down·to be or become light

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֣י תִ֭קָּחֶנּוּ אֶל־גְּבוּל֑וֹ וְכִי־תָ֝בִ֗ין נְתִיב֥וֹת בֵּיתֽוֹ
verses.translation: That you may take it to its domainAnd know the way to its home?

verses.hebrew_full: יָ֭דַעְתָּ כִּי־אָ֣ז תִּוָּלֵ֑ד וּמִסְפַּ֖ר יָמֶ֣יךָ רַבִּֽים
verses.translation: Surely you know, for you were born then,And the number of your years is many!
verses.rashi: You knew all this. for then you were born when I created (them).

verses.hebrew_full: הֲ֭בָאתָ אֶל־אֹצְר֣וֹת שָׁ֑לֶג וְאוֹצְר֖וֹת בָּרָ֣ד תִּרְאֶֽה
verses.translation: Have you penetrated the vaults of snow,Seen the vaults of hail,

verses.hebrew_full: אֲשֶׁר־חָשַׂ֥כְתִּי לְעֶת־צָ֑ר לְי֥וֹם קְ֝רָ֗ב וּמִלְחָמָֽה
verses.translation: Which I have put aside for a time of adversity,For a day of war and battle?
verses.rashi: That I saved I withheld. for a time of trouble For the five kings in Gibeon. for a day of battle The war of Gog and Magog.

verses.hebrew_full: אֵי־זֶ֣ה הַ֭דֶּרֶךְ יֵחָ֣לֶק א֑וֹר יָפֵ֖ץ קָדִ֣ים עֲלֵי־אָֽרֶץ
verses.translation: By what path is the west wind. dispersed,The east wind scattered over the earth?
verses.rashi: is the light parted The irradiation of the sun, which diverges this way and that way like the horns of a hind. the eastern one The sun of the east.

verses.hebrew_full: מִֽי־פִלַּ֣ג לַשֶּׁ֣טֶף תְּעָלָ֑ה וְ֝דֶ֗רֶךְ לַחֲזִ֥יז קֹלֽוֹת
verses.translation: Who cut a channel for the torrentsAnd a path for the thunderstorms,
verses.rashi: Who has separated a place for the hair Heb. לטשף. In Arabia, a hair is called שִׁיטְפָא. To every hair on the head I have given תְעָלָה, a separate shaft from which to draw sap. If two would draw from the same shaft, a person’s eyesight would fail. These have not been changed by Me, how then should I change אִיוֹב, Job, for אוֹיֵב, enemy? And this is the meaning of מן הסערה, from the hair. Another explanation: לַשֶטֶף a streaming raindrop. I set apart for each drop a hole in the clouds, for if they were to come down [together] from one opening, they would soften the earth and turn it into a mire. and a way for the cloud of the thunderclaps who parted? Every thunderclap has its own path, and if two of them would go along one path, the creatures would not be able to bear the sound.

verses.hebrew_full: לְ֭הַמְטִיר עַל־אֶ֣רֶץ לֹא־אִ֑ישׁ מִ֝דְבָּ֗ר לֹא־אָדָ֥ם בּֽוֹ
verses.translation: To rain down on uninhabited land,On the wilderness where no one lives,

words.eng: inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: לְהַשְׂבִּ֣יעַ שֹׁ֭אָה וּמְשֹׁאָ֑ה וּ֝לְהַצְמִ֗יחַ מֹ֣צָא דֶֽשֶׁא
verses.translation: To saturate the desolate wasteland,And make the crop of grass sprout forth?
verses.rashi: darkness A land that is dark because of hunger.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲיֵשׁ־לַמָּטָ֥ר אָ֑ב א֥וֹ מִי־ה֝וֹלִ֗יד אֶגְלֵי־טָֽל
verses.translation: Does the rain have a progenitor?Who begot the dewdrops?
verses.rashi: waves of dew Heb. אגלי, waves of גלי, dew, and the “aleph” is superfluous, as in (above 13: 17), “and to my speech (ואחותי) in your ears.” The “aleph” is superfluous at the beginning of the word because it is a noun, and similarly (Ezek. 21:20), “the scream of (אבחת) the sword,” like נבוח, barking, the scream of the sword. Another explanation: is like אַגְנֵי, basins of dew. A type of basins, divisions in which the dew is deposited. The “lammed” is interchangeable with a “nun,” and so in the Book of Ezra (Neh. 13:7), chamber (נִשְׁכָּה) like לִשְׁכָּה.

verses.hebrew_full: מִבֶּ֣טֶן מִ֭י יָצָ֣א הַקָּ֑רַח וּכְפֹ֥ר שָׁ֝מַ֗יִם מִ֣י יְלָדֽוֹ
verses.translation: From whose belly came forth the ice?Who gave birth to the frost of heaven?
verses.rashi: and the frost glass or glassa in Old French.

verses.hebrew_full: כָּ֭אֶבֶן מַ֣יִם יִתְחַבָּ֑אוּ וּפְנֵ֥י תְ֝ה֗וֹם יִתְלַכָּֽדוּ
verses.translation: Water congeals like stone,And the surface of the deep compacts.
verses.rashi: Like a stone...hides because of the ice that congeals in drops upon their faces. and the face of the deep is caught and held together by it.

verses.hebrew_full: הַֽ֭תְקַשֵּׁר מַעֲדַנּ֣וֹת כִּימָ֑ה אֽוֹ־מֹשְׁכ֖וֹת כְּסִ֣יל תְּפַתֵּֽחַ
verses.translation: Can you tie cords to PleiadesOr undo the reins of Orion?
verses.rashi: the chains of the Pleiades [Tie] the chains of the Pleiades so that all its cold should not go forth and destroy the world with cold. or loose the straps of Orion to bring out its heat, to mitigate the cold of the Pleiades.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲתֹצִ֣יא מַזָּר֣וֹת בְּעִתּ֑וֹ וְ֝עַ֗יִשׁ עַל־בָּנֶ֥יהָ תַנְחֵֽם
verses.translation: Can you lead out Mazzaroth in its season,Conduct the Bear with her children?
verses.rashi: constellations All the other constellations. and...Ayish The largest star in the Pleiades, to which many stars are attached. He took two stars from it to open the windows of the Deluge, and they were placed in the constellation of Aries and the Holy One, blessed be He, is destined to restore them to her. Tanchuma.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲ֭יָדַעְתָּ חֻקּ֣וֹת שָׁמָ֑יִם אִם־תָּשִׂ֖ים מִשְׁטָר֣וֹ בָאָֽרֶץ
verses.translation: Do you know the laws of heavenOr impose its authority on earth?
verses.rashi: his dominion The dominion of the constellations appointed to bring upon the earth cold and heat, summer and winter.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲתָרִ֣ים לָעָ֣ב קוֹלֶ֑ךָ וְֽשִׁפְעַת־מַ֥יִם תְּכַסֶּֽךָּ
verses.translation: Can you send up an order to the cloudsFor an abundance of water to cover you?
verses.rashi: Can you lift up your voice to the cloud to command it to tie up with the darkness of water so that an abundance of water cover you? That is the darkening of clouds, like (above 22:11), “or darkness so that you do not see.” [or] will an abundance of water from Heaven cover you by your commands.

verses.hebrew_full: הַֽתְשַׁלַּ֣ח בְּרָקִ֣ים וְיֵלֵ֑כוּ וְיֹאמְר֖וּ לְךָ֣ הִנֵּֽנוּ
verses.translation: Can you dispatch the lightning on a missionAnd have it answer you, “I am ready”?
verses.rashi: and say to you in the place where they were sent, ‘Here we are; we have performed our errand,’ because they need not return to their place to reply to their Sender, for the Shechinah is everywhere.

verses.hebrew_full: מִי־שָׁ֭ת בַּטֻּח֣וֹת חׇכְמָ֑ה א֤וֹ מִֽי־נָתַ֖ן לַשֶּׂ֣כְוִי בִינָֽה
verses.translation: Who put wisdom in the hidden parts?Who gave understanding to the mind?
verses.rashi: in the inward parts These are the reins. the rooster Heb. לשכוי. This is the rooster in the language of the Sages (Rosh Hashanah 26a). And some say: This is the heart, which looks (שוכה), watches, and considers the coming events.

verses.hebrew_full: מִֽי־יְסַפֵּ֣ר שְׁחָקִ֣ים בְּחׇכְמָ֑ה וְנִבְלֵ֥י שָׁ֝מַ֗יִם מִ֣י יַשְׁכִּֽיב
verses.translation: Who is wise enough to give an account of the heavens?Who can tilt the bottles of the sky,
verses.rashi: charges Heb. יספר, will speak and charge with their mission. the bottles Made like bottles in which the rains are collected, as in (I Sam. 1:24), “and an earthenware jug (ונבל) of wine.”

verses.hebrew_full: בְּצֶ֣קֶת עָ֭פָר לַמּוּצָ֑ק וּרְגָבִ֥ים יְדֻבָּֽקוּ
verses.translation: Whereupon the earth melts into a mass,And its clods stick together.
verses.rashi: When the dust was poured On the day that I poured the dust. into a mass for the foundation of the world, into its midst. and the clods cleaved together around it on all sides, until its length and width were filled.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲתָצ֣וּד לְלָבִ֣יא טָ֑רֶף וְחַיַּ֖ת כְּפִירִ֣ים תְּמַלֵּֽא
verses.translation: Can you hunt prey for the lion,And satisfy the appetite of the king of beasts?

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־יָשֹׁ֥חוּ בַמְּעוֹנ֑וֹת יֵשְׁב֖וּ בַסֻּכָּ֣ה לְמוֹ־אָֽרֶב
verses.translation: They crouch in their dens,Lie in ambush in their lairs.
verses.rashi: they crouch They stand crouching to lie in wait. to lie in wait so is the habit of those who lie in wait, to crouch and to stoop themselves so that they will not be recognized.

verses.hebrew_full: מִ֤י יָכִ֥ין לָעֹרֵ֗ב צֵ֫יד֥וֹ כִּֽי־יְ֭לָדָו אֶל־אֵ֣ל יְשַׁוֵּ֑עוּ יִ֝תְע֗וּ לִבְלִי־אֹֽכֶל
verses.translation: Who provides food for the ravenWhen its young cry out to GodAnd wander about without food?
verses.rashi: they wander for lack of food for their own fathers hate them, and the Holy One, blessed be He, prepares gnats for them, which are created from their dung, and they enter their mouths.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲיָדַ֗עְתָּ עֵ֭ת לֶ֣דֶת יַעֲלֵי־סָ֑לַע חֹלֵ֖ל אַיָּל֣וֹת תִּשְׁמֹֽר
verses.translation: Do you know the season when the mountain goats give birth?Can you mark the time when the hinds calve?
verses.rashi: mountain goats Estainboc in Old French. It hates its young, and, when preparing to give birth, goes to the top of a high rock, so that its young should fall to the earth and die. But the Holy One, blessed be He, prepares an eagle for it [the young], which receives it on its wings. Do you wait for the hinds to calve? Heb. חלל, an expression of (Jer. 6:24), “pain (חיל) as of a woman in travail.” The hind’s womb is narrow, and the young cannot come forth; and, at the time of birth, I prepare a snake for her that bites her womb so that it opens; should it be a moment too early or too late, she would die immediately. I know to distinguish these moments, so should the distinction between Job (אִיוֹב) and enemy (אוֹיֵב) escape Me?

verses.hebrew_full: תִּסְפֹּ֣ר יְרָחִ֣ים תְּמַלֶּ֑אנָה וְ֝יָדַעְתָּ֗ עֵ֣ת לִדְתָּֽנָה
verses.translation: Can you count the months they must complete?Do you know the season they give birth,

verses.hebrew_full: תִּ֭כְרַעְנָה יַלְדֵיהֶ֣ן תְּפַלַּ֑חְנָה חֶבְלֵיהֶ֥ם תְּשַׁלַּֽחְנָה
verses.translation: When they couch to bring forth their offspring,To deliver their young?
verses.rashi: they bring forth Their womb splits open and gives forth the young, and their birth pangs... they send forth from themselves in their birth at the moment that I prepare for them.

verses.hebrew_full: יַחְלְמ֣וּ בְ֭נֵיהֶם יִרְבּ֣וּ בַבָּ֑ר יָ֝צְא֗וּ וְלֹא־שָׁ֥בוּ לָֽמוֹ
verses.translation: Their young are healthy; they grow up in the open;They leave and return no more.
verses.rashi: and do not return to them When they have grown a little, they are raised on the grain, on seeds and grasses, and they do not require the raising of their father or mother.

verses.hebrew_full: מִי־שִׁלַּ֣ח פֶּ֣רֶא חׇפְשִׁ֑י וּמֹסְר֥וֹת עָ֝ר֗וֹד מִ֣י פִתֵּֽחַ
verses.translation: Who sets the wild ass free?Who loosens the bonds of the onager,
verses.rashi: free For no man can raise a wild donkey to teach it the work of cattle. who loosed his bands so that the yoke of man is not upon him.

verses.hebrew_full: אֲשֶׁר־שַׂ֣מְתִּי עֲרָבָ֣ה בֵית֑וֹ וּֽמִשְׁכְּנוֹתָ֥יו מְלֵחָֽה
verses.translation: Whose home I have made the wilderness,The salt land its dwelling-place?

verses.hebrew_full: יִ֭שְׂחַק לַהֲמ֣וֹן קִרְיָ֑ה תְּשֻׁא֥וֹת נֹ֝גֵ֗שׂ לֹ֣א יִשְׁמָֽע
verses.translation: It scoffs at the tumult of the city,Does not hear the shouts of the driver.

verses.hebrew_full: יְת֣וּר הָרִ֣ים מִרְעֵ֑הוּ וְאַחַ֖ר כׇּל־יָר֣וֹק יִדְרֽוֹשׁ
verses.translation: It roams the hills for its pasture;It searches for any green thing.
verses.rashi: He spies out the mountains for his pasture He personally spies out for himself a place of pasture.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲיֹ֣אבֶה רֵּ֣ים עׇבְדֶ֑ךָ אִם־יָ֝לִ֗ין עַל־אֲבוּסֶֽךָ
verses.translation: Would the wild ox agree to serve you?Would it spend the night at your crib?
verses.rashi: wild ox Heb. רֵים, like רְאֵם. serve you To serve you.

words.eng: probably the great aurochs or wild bulls which are now  extinct. The exact meaning is not known.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲֽתִקְשׇׁר־רֵ֭ים בְּתֶ֣לֶם עֲבֹת֑וֹ אִם־יְשַׂדֵּ֖ד עֲמָקִ֣ים אַחֲרֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: Can you hold the wild ox by ropes to the furrow?Would it plow up the valleys behind you?
verses.rashi: to a furrow with his rope (Or did you tie a wild ox because of the furrow of your ox?) rope Heb. עבתו, a rope with which they tie [oxen] to the furrows of the plow. will he harrow, prepare the field.

words.eng: to bind·probably the great aurochs or wild bulls which are now  extinct. The exact meaning is not known.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲֽתִבְטַח־בּ֭וֹ כִּֽי־רַ֣ב כֹּח֑וֹ וְתַעֲזֹ֖ב אֵלָ֣יו יְגִיעֶֽךָ
verses.translation: Would you rely on its great strengthAnd leave your toil to it?
