verses.rashi: Will you rely on him to gather your grain because his strength is great and he can bear great burdens? or will you leave your toil for him to gather into the house?

words.eng: to trust·inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: הֲתַאֲמִ֣ין בּ֭וֹ כִּֽי־יָשִׁ֣יב (ישוב) זַרְעֶ֑ךָ וְֽגׇרְנְךָ֥ יֶאֱסֹֽף
verses.translation: Would you trust the ox to bring in the seedAnd gather it in from your threshing floor?

words.eng: inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: כְּנַף־רְנָנִ֥ים נֶעֱלָ֑סָה אִם־אֶ֝בְרָ֗ה חֲסִידָ֥ה וְנֹצָֽה
verses.translation: The wing of the ostrich beats joyously;Are its pinions and plumage like the stork’s?
verses.rashi: The wing of the renanim rejoices This is the name of a huge bird, called in the language of the Mishnah, Bar Yochni (Bechoroth 57b). the winged Heb. אֶבְרָה. Every bird is called אֶבְרָה because it flies; i.e., either the bird named “stork,” or the bird called nozah. Note her behavior.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּי־תַעֲזֹ֣ב לָאָ֣רֶץ בֵּצֶ֑יהָ וְֽעַל־עָפָ֥ר תְּחַמֵּֽם
verses.translation: It leaves its eggs on the ground,Letting them warm in the dirt,
verses.rashi: For she leaves her eggs on the ground and she goes and warms herself on the dust at a distance.

verses.hebrew_full: וַ֭תִּשְׁכַּח כִּי־רֶ֣גֶל תְּזוּרֶ֑הָ וְחַיַּ֖ת הַשָּׂדֶ֣ה תְדוּשֶֽׁהָ
verses.translation: Forgetting they may be crushed underfoot,Or trampled by a wild beast.
verses.rashi: She forgets that a foot will crack it Heb. תְּזוּרֶהָ [i.e., it will crack] her egg. [תְּזוּרֶהָ means:] will crack it.

verses.hebrew_full: הִקְשִׁ֣יחַ בָּנֶ֣יהָ לְּלֹא־לָ֑הּ לְרִ֖יק יְגִיעָ֣הּ בְּלִי־פָֽחַד
verses.translation: Its young are cruelly abandoned as if they were not its own;Its labor is in vain for lack of concern.
verses.rashi: She is hardened She deals harshly with her young ones, [distancing them] from her heart as though they were not hers. her toil is in vain and she is not afraid to lose them.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּי־הִשָּׁ֣הּ אֱל֣וֹהַּ חׇכְמָ֑ה וְלֹא־חָ֥לַק לָ֝֗הּ בַּבִּינָֽה
verses.translation: For God deprived it of wisdom,Gave it no share of understanding,

verses.hebrew_full: כָּ֭עֵת בַּמָּר֣וֹם תַּמְרִ֑יא תִּֽשְׂחַ֥ק לַ֝סּ֗וּס וּלְרֹכְבֽוֹ
verses.translation: Else it would soar on high,Scoffing at the horse and its rider.
verses.rashi: Like the time that she goes off on high [When she flies up, she scorns the horse. She is not afraid that he will tread on the eggs and crack them.] Every expression of הַמְרָאָה applies to a man whose heart prompts him to stray from his lifestyle, his upbringing, and his country, to roam in other countries and to try other lifestyles. Similarly (Deut. 21:18), “a stubborn and rebellious (וּמרֶה) son”; and in the language of the Talmud it is אִמְרָאִי: “Rav Zevid’s daughter-in-law rebelled (אִמְרָאִי) and went away” (Kethuboth 63b, Venice edition) (cf. Rashi to Hullin 58b, Baba Mezia 77a).

verses.hebrew_full: הֲתִתֵּ֣ן לַסּ֣וּס גְּבוּרָ֑ה הֲתַלְבִּ֖ישׁ צַוָּאר֣וֹ רַעְמָֽה
verses.translation: Do you give the horse its strength?Do you clothe its neck with a mane?
verses.rashi: fierceness Heb. רעמה, an expression of thunder and fright, like (Ezek. 27:35), “Their faces are as though thunderstruck (רעמו).”

verses.hebrew_full: הֲֽ֭תַרְעִישֶׁנּוּ כָּאַרְבֶּ֑ה ה֖וֹד נַחְר֣וֹ אֵימָֽה
verses.translation: Do you make it quiver like locusts,Its majestic snorting [spreading] terror?
verses.rashi: like a locust He skips and leaps like a locust and shakes himself all over. his snorting When he blows with his nostrils, it is with an awesome sound.

verses.hebrew_full: יַחְפְּר֣וּ בָ֭עֵמֶק וְיָשִׂ֣ישׂ בְּכֹ֑חַ יֵ֝צֵ֗א לִקְרַאת־נָֽשֶׁק
verses.translation: It paws with force, it runs with vigor,Charging into battle.
verses.rashi: They spy in the valley Heb. יחפרו, like (Deut. 1:22), “that they may spy out (ויחפרו) the land for us,” for it is the habit of the horsemen to lie in wait in the valleys and in the ravines. and rejoices with strength The horse rejoices and goes forth toward the weapons.

verses.hebrew_full: יִשְׂחַ֣ק לְ֭פַחַד וְלֹ֣א יֵחָ֑ת וְלֹא־יָ֝שׁ֗וּב מִפְּנֵי־חָֽרֶב
verses.translation: It scoffs at fear; it cannot be frightened;It does not recoil from the sword.
verses.rashi: and is not dismayed He will not be frightened.

verses.hebrew_full: עָ֭לָיו תִּרְנֶ֣ה אַשְׁפָּ֑ה לַ֖הַב חֲנִ֣ית וְכִידֽוֹן
verses.translation: A quiverful of arrows whizzes by it,And the flashing spear and the javelin.
verses.rashi: The quiver...rattles [i.e., the quiver that is] full of arrows, and they knock against each other so that their sound is heard. the blade of the spear An iron utensil (and an iron spear) is called לַהַב, as in (Jud. 3:22), “and the haft also went in after the blade (הלהב).”

verses.hebrew_full: בְּרַ֣עַשׁ וְ֭רֹגֶז יְגַמֶּא־אָ֑רֶץ וְלֹא־יַ֝אֲמִ֗ין כִּי־ק֥וֹל שׁוֹפָֽר
verses.translation: Trembling with excitement, it swallows the land;It does not turn aside at the blast of the trumpet.
verses.rashi: he hollows out the ground Heb. יגמא. He makes holes (גומות) with his feet. Another explanation: יְגַמֶא is like (Gen. 24:17), “Let me please swallow (הגמיאיני).” and he does not believe out of the great joy that he longs for battle. that it is the sound of a shofar of battle.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּדֵ֤י שֹׁפָ֨ר יֹ֘אמַ֤ר הֶאָ֗ח וּֽ֭מֵרָחוֹק יָרִ֣יחַ מִלְחָמָ֑ה רַ֥עַם שָׂ֝רִ֗ים וּתְרוּעָֽה
verses.translation: As the trumpet sounds, it says, “Aha!”From afar it smells the battle,The roaring and shouting of the officers.
verses.rashi: To many shofaroth Heb. בדי, an expression of (Lev. 25:28), “sufficient (די) to get it back,” to many shofaroth. he says, ‘Hurrah!’ An expression that (the passersby) (the slaves—Berechiah) say when they are happy, much as they say, ‘Woe!’ out of pain.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲֽ֭מִבִּינָ֣תְךָ יַאֲבֶר־נֵ֑ץ יִפְרֹ֖שׂ כְּנָפָ֣ו לְתֵימָֽן
verses.translation: Is it by your wisdom that the hawk grows pinions,Spreads its wings to the south?
verses.rashi: the hawk grows a wing Heb. יאבר, the hawk grows a wing. That is an angel resembling a hawk, who stays the severity of the south wind by spreading out his wings, lest it destroy the world.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־עַל־פִּ֭יךָ יַגְבִּ֣יהַּ נָ֑שֶׁר וְ֝כִ֗י יָרִ֥ים קִנּֽוֹ
verses.translation: Does the eagle soar at your command,Building its nest high,
verses.rashi: Or is it by your order that the eagle flies high [This refers to] the Shechinah, as it is said (Deut. 32:11): “As an eagle first stirs up its nest.” He would remove His court so that they should not injure Aaron when he entered the sanctuary on Yom Kippur.

verses.hebrew_full: סֶ֣לַע יִ֭שְׁכֹּן וְיִתְלֹנָ֑ן עַ֥ל שֶׁן־סֶ֝֗לַע וּמְצוּדָֽה
verses.translation: Dwelling in the rock,Lodging upon the fastness of a jutting rock?
verses.rashi: He dwells on the rock and lodges Aaron was confident when he placed the censer with the incense on the foundation stone [in the Holy of Holies].

verses.hebrew_full: מִשָּׁ֥ם חָֽפַר־אֹ֑כֶל לְ֝מֵרָח֗וֹק עֵינָ֥יו יַבִּֽיטוּ
verses.translation: From there it spies out its food;From afar its eyes see it.
verses.rashi: From there he searches for food He would pray for the needs of the entire year, and after all this honor he saw the death of his two eaglets, Nadab and Abihu, who died so that My name might be sanctified through them, for My fear fell upon those who remained.

verses.hebrew_full: וְאֶפְרֹחָ֥ו יְעַלְעוּ־דָ֑ם וּבַאֲשֶׁ֥ר חֲ֝לָלִ֗ים שָׁ֣ם הֽוּא
verses.translation: Its young gulp blood;Where the slain are, there it is.
verses.rashi: gulp down Heb. יעלעו. Only according to the measure that one is able to swallow of blood or water, but it appears as if one swallows much, in imitation of the sound “al al.” Similarly, in the language of the Sages, concerning the suspected adulteress [who must] drink [the cursing water] (Sotah 20a), “they make her swallow (מערערין) and drink against her will.” [Here too, the sound “ar ar” is alluded to.] Similarly (Isa. 15:5), “a cry of destruction they shall raise (יעוערו),” the prophet using the onomatopoetic word in imitation of the sound produced by the throat. and where there are slain, there he is to eat. Our Sages explain this entire passage as an allegory applying to Aaron, in the Pesikta to Acharei Moth (Pesikta Rabbathi, ch. 48, Meir Ayin edition, ch. 47).

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֖עַן יְהֹוָ֥ה אֶת־אִיּ֗וֹב וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: GOD said in reply to Job.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Job = "hated"

verses.hebrew_full: הֲ֭רֹב עִם־שַׁדַּ֣י יִסּ֑וֹר מוֹכִ֖יחַ אֱל֣וֹהַּ יַעֲנֶֽנָּה
verses.translation: Shall one who should be disciplined complain against Shaddai? He who arraigns God must respond.
verses.rashi: Will one who contends with the Almighty make himself master? May a man make himself master to contend with the Almighty? He who argues with God, let him answer it He who comes to argue, let him answer his words.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֖עַן אִיּ֥וֹב אֶת־יְהֹוָ֗ה וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Job said in reply to GOD:

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Commonly transcribed YHWH

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֣ן קַ֭לֹּתִי מָ֣ה אֲשִׁיבֶ֑ךָּ יָ֝דִ֗י שַׂ֣מְתִּי לְמוֹ־פִֽי
verses.translation: See, I am of small worth; what can I answer You?I clap my hand to my mouth.

verses.hebrew_full: אַחַ֣ת דִּ֭בַּרְתִּי וְלֹ֣א אֶעֱנֶ֑ה וּ֝שְׁתַּ֗יִם וְלֹ֣א אוֹסִֽיף
verses.translation: I have spoken once, and will not reply;Twice, and will do so no more.
verses.rashi: I said one [thing i.e., I have spoken little and I will add no more. Others explain: I said one What I said (above 9:22), “[It is all one; therefore I said,] ‘He destroys both the innocent and the wicked,’” I will no longer answer. and two What I said (above 13:20), “Do not do two things to me,” I will not add.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּעַן־יְהֹוָ֣ה אֶת־אִ֭יּוֹב (מנסערה) מִ֥ן סְעָרָ֗ה וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Then GOD replied to Job out of the tempest and said:

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Job = "hated"

verses.hebrew_full: אֱזׇר־נָ֣א כְגֶ֣בֶר חֲלָצֶ֑יךָ אֶ֝שְׁאָלְךָ֗ וְהוֹדִיעֵֽנִי
verses.translation: Gird your loins like a man; I will ask, and you will inform Me.

verses.hebrew_full: הַ֭אַף תָּפֵ֣ר מִשְׁפָּטִ֑י תַּ֝רְשִׁיעֵ֗נִי לְמַ֣עַן תִּצְדָּֽק
verses.translation: Would you impugn My justice?Would you condemn Me that you may be right?
verses.rashi: (God’s answer from the tempest)Will you even make void My judgment Will you even prove with your words to make My judgment void and to show that it is perverted?

verses.hebrew_full: וְאִם־זְר֖וֹעַ כָּאֵ֥ל לָ֑ךְ וּ֝בְק֗וֹל כָּמֹ֥הוּ תַרְעֵֽם
verses.translation: Have you an arm like God’s?Can you thunder with a voice like this?

verses.hebrew_full: עֲדֵ֥ה נָ֣א גָא֣וֹן וָגֹ֑בַהּ וְה֖וֹד וְהָדָ֣ר תִּלְבָּֽשׁ
verses.translation: Deck yourself now with grandeur and eminence;Clothe yourself in glory and majesty.
verses.rashi: Bedeck yourself Heb. עדה, an expression of an ornament (עדי) and decoration.

verses.hebrew_full: הָ֭פֵץ עֶבְר֣וֹת אַפֶּ֑ךָ וּרְאֵ֥ה כׇל־גֵּ֝אֶ֗ה וְהַשְׁפִּילֵֽהוּ
verses.translation: Scatter wide your raging anger;See all who are proud, and bring them low.

verses.hebrew_full: רְאֵ֣ה כׇל־גֵּ֭אֶה הַכְנִיעֵ֑הוּ וַהֲדֹ֖ךְ רְשָׁעִ֣ים תַּחְתָּֽם
verses.translation: See all who are proud, and humble them,And bring the wicked down where they stand.
verses.rashi: and crush Heb. והדך, an expression of (Num. 11:8), “or crushed (דכו) it in mortars.”

verses.hebrew_full: טׇמְנֵ֣ם בֶּעָפָ֣ר יָ֑חַד פְּ֝נֵיהֶ֗ם חֲבֹ֣שׁ בַּטָּמֽוּן
verses.translation: Bury them all in the earth;Hide their faces in obscurity.

verses.hebrew_full: וְגַם־אֲנִ֥י אוֹדֶ֑ךָּ כִּֽי־תוֹשִׁ֖עַ לְךָ֣ יְמִינֶֽךָ
verses.translation: Then even I would praise youFor the triumph your right hand won you.

words.eng: also·I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: הִנֵּה־נָ֣א בְ֭הֵמוֹת אֲשֶׁר־עָשִׂ֣יתִי עִמָּ֑ךְ חָ֝צִ֗יר כַּבָּקָ֥ר יֹאכֵֽל
verses.translation: Take now behemoth, whom I made as I did you;It eats grass, like the cattle.
verses.rashi: behemoth Prepared for the future.

verses.hebrew_full: הִנֵּה־נָ֣א כֹח֣וֹ בְמׇתְנָ֑יו וְ֝אוֹנ֗וֹ בִּשְׁרִירֵ֥י בִטְנֽוֹ
verses.translation: Its strength is in its loins,Its might in the muscles of its belly.
verses.rashi: his strength is in his loins His testicles are squashed and concealed, and have not been completely torn off. in the navel Heb. בשרירי, like (Song 7:3), “Your navel (שררך).”

verses.hebrew_full: יַחְפֹּ֣ץ זְנָב֣וֹ כְמוֹ־אָ֑רֶז גִּידֵ֖י פַחֲדָ֣ו יְשֹׂרָֽגוּ
verses.translation: It makes its tail stand up like a cedar;The sinews of its thighs are knit together.
verses.rashi: hardens Heb. יחפץ like יַחְפּוֹז, hardens. Similarly, in the language of the Sages: דַחַפִּיזָא כִּי אוּפְתָּא (Hullin 47b), hard as wood. his testicles Heb. פַחֲדָיו. are knit together Heb. ישרגו, twisted cords, like (Lam. 1:14), “they are knit together (ישתרגו),” or the webbing (שריגי) of a bed in the language of the Mishnah, (Moed Katan 1:8), and it is an expression of twisted cords.

verses.hebrew_full: עֲ֭צָמָיו אֲפִיקֵ֣י נְחֻשָׁ֑ה גְּ֝רָמָ֗יו כִּמְטִ֥יל בַּרְזֶֽל
verses.translation: Its bones are like tubes of bronze,Its limbs like iron rods.
verses.rashi: as a load Heb. כִּמְטִיל, as a burden of iron.

verses.hebrew_full: ה֭וּא רֵאשִׁ֣ית דַּרְכֵי־אֵ֑ל הָ֝עֹשׂ֗וֹ יַגֵּ֥שׁ חַרְבּֽוֹ
verses.translation: It is the first of God’s works;Only its Maker can draw the sword against it.
verses.rashi: He is the first of God’s ways He was created at the beginning to be the chief of all animals. his Maker The Holy One, blessed be He, Himself will slaughter him.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּי־ב֭וּל הָרִ֣ים יִשְׂאוּ־ל֑וֹ וְֽכׇל־חַיַּ֥ת הַ֝שָּׂדֶ֗ה יְשַׂחֲקוּ־שָֽׁם
verses.translation: The mountains yield it produce,Where all the beasts of the field play.
verses.rashi: For the mountains bear food for him And it is written in the Book of Psalms (50:10): “Behemoth upon a thousand mountains.” A thousand mountains bear food for him. He feeds on all of them every day, [and they are grown again the next day].

verses.hebrew_full: תַּֽחַת־צֶאֱלִ֥ים יִשְׁכָּ֑ב בְּסֵ֖תֶר קָנֶ֣ה וּבִצָּֽה
verses.translation: It lies down beneath the lotuses,In the cover of the swamp reeds.
verses.rashi: the shadows the shades of trees.

words.eng: the under part·a kind of lotus

verses.hebrew_full: יְסֻכֻּ֣הוּ צֶאֱלִ֣ים צִֽלְל֑וֹ יְ֝סֻבּ֗וּהוּ עַרְבֵי־נָֽחַל
verses.translation: The lotuses embower it with shade;The willows of the brook surround it.
verses.rashi: Do the shadows cover him as his shadow [Does] the shadow of the covert of the reeds and the swamp, which is entangled [or surrounded—Berechiah] and hedged around with [many] trees.

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֤ן יַעֲשֹׁ֣ק נָ֭הָר לֹ֣א יַחְפּ֑וֹז יִבְטַ֓ח כִּֽי־יָגִ֖יחַ יַרְדֵּ֣ן אֶל־פִּֽיהוּ
verses.translation: It can restrain the river from rushing away;It is confident the stream will gush at its command.
verses.rashi: Behold, he plunders the river, and [he] does not harden The Euphrates, which is a large river whose waters increase, he oppresses and plunders from the creatures by his drinking. and [he] does not harden [His belly does not harden from his drinking, like (verse 17), “His tail hardens.” he trusts that he will draw the Jordan He is confident [of his ability] to draw the whole Jordan into his mouth were he to drink there: like (Ps. 22: 10), “Who drew me (גחי) from the womb.”

verses.hebrew_full: בְּעֵינָ֥יו יִקָּחֶ֑נּוּ בְּ֝מוֹקְשִׁ֗ים יִנְקׇב־אָֽף
verses.translation: Can it be taken by its eyes?Can its nose be pierced by hooks?
verses.rashi: With His eyes He will take him The Omnipresent will take him by looking at him, and he quakes from fear of Him. with snares Fishermen’s tools, with which they catch fish.

verses.hebrew_full: תִּמְשֹׁ֣ךְ לִוְיָתָ֣ן בְּחַכָּ֑ה וּ֝בְחֶ֗בֶל תַּשְׁקִ֥יעַ לְשֹׁנֽוֹ
verses.translation: Can you draw out Leviathan by a fishhook?Can you press down its tongue by a rope?
verses.rashi: Can you pull the leviathan etc. When you pull him with a harpoon and insert a fishhook into his nose, will he offer much supplication to you? It means that you are unable to approach him with fishing implements. or press down his tongue Let the rope down (on his tongue) to draw him out of his place.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲתָשִׂ֣ים אַגְמֹ֣ן בְּאַפּ֑וֹ וּ֝בְח֗וֹחַ תִּקֹּ֥ב לֶחֱיֽוֹ
verses.translation: Can you put a ring through its nose,Or pierce its jaw with a barb?
verses.rashi: a fishhook Heb. התשים. It is bent, like (Isa. 9:13), “a large arch and a small arch (אגמן), and it is made of iron to catch fish with it. Can you insert Will you insert a fishhook into his nose to take him out of the sea? or...with a barb A fishermen’s implement. or pierce Heb. תִּקּב, like תִּנְקֹב.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲיַרְבֶּ֣ה אֵ֭לֶיךָ תַּחֲנוּנִ֑ים אִם־יְדַבֵּ֖ר אֵלֶ֣יךָ רַכּֽוֹת
verses.translation: Will it plead with you at length?Will it speak soft words to you?
verses.rashi: Will he offer much supplication to you Will he offer much supplication to you and beseech you to spare his life? or will he speak This is a repetitious word. soft words Words of supplication.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲיִכְרֹ֣ת בְּרִ֣ית עִמָּ֑ךְ תִּ֝קָּחֶ֗נּוּ לְעֶ֣בֶד עוֹלָֽם
verses.translation: Will it make an agreement with youTo be taken as your lifelong slave?
verses.rashi: Will he make a covenant with you Will he make a covenant to serve you, that you will take him as a lifelong slave?

verses.hebrew_full: הַֽתְשַׂחֶק־בּ֭וֹ כַּצִּפּ֑וֹר וְ֝תִקְשְׁרֶ֗נּוּ לְנַעֲרוֹתֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: Will you play with it like a bird,And tie it down for your maids?
verses.rashi: Will you play Will you play and stroll with him as though he were a small bird, and will you tie him to the necks of (other editions: for the sake of) your young lads, that they should stroll with him as with small birds that they give children to stroll and to placate him?

words.eng: to laugh·inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: יִכְר֣וּ עָ֭לָיו חַבָּרִ֑ים יֶ֝חֱצ֗וּהוּ בֵּ֣ין כְּֽנַעֲנִֽים
verses.translation: Shall traders traffic in it? Will it be divided up among merchants?
verses.rashi: Will charmers dig pits for him Will charmers and sorcerers dig pits to hunt him? To take him out of the sea by means of sorcery [so] that they can divide him and share him among the merchants, as the sea fishermen do? That is, they catch large fish with their cunning, and the merchants come and purchase from them. dig pits Heb. יכרו, an expression of (Jer. 18:22), digging a pit (כורה שוחה). charmers Heb. חַבָרִים, an expression of (Deut. 18:11), “a charmer (וְחבֵר חָבֶר).”

verses.hebrew_full: הַֽתְמַלֵּ֣א בְשֻׂכּ֣וֹת עוֹר֑וֹ וּבְצִלְצַ֖ל דָּגִ֣ים רֹאשֽׁוֹ
verses.translation: Can you fill its skin with dartsOr its head with fish-spears?
verses.rashi: Will you fulfill Will you fulfill your desire and make tents of his skin? or a shade of fishes of his head Will you make from his head a shade of fish? This is a repetitious expression. a shade Heb. צִלְצַל, a tent of fish. A tent made from his head is called a shade of fish because it is made from him. צִלְצַל is ombre in French. It is an expression of shadow, and that is one of the double words, like: green (ירקרק), red (אדמדם) [both in Lev. 13:49], twisted (פתלתל) [Deut. 32:5], tattoo (קעקע) [Lev. 19:28].

verses.hebrew_full: שִׂים־עָלָ֥יו כַּפֶּ֑ךָ זְכֹ֥ר מִ֝לְחָמָ֗ה אַל־תּוֹסַֽף
verses.translation: Lay a hand on it,And you will never think of battle again.
verses.rashi: Put your hand over him Strengthen yourself to wage battle against him, that you will put your hand over him to strike him. remember the battle, do not stop And do not stop. Remember your might to wage war against him. and do not [rest] תּוֹסַף is derived from יסף, like (Gen.38:26), “and he did not cease (יָסַף) being intimate with her” (regrettera in French), will regret. [In the expression] אַל-תּוֹסַף, the “sammech” is vowelized with a “pattah” because of the pause accent, as in (Gen. 49:4), “You shall not gain superiority (תּוֹתַר) “; (Ruth 2:14), “and she left over (וַתּתַר),” and many like it.

verses.hebrew_full: הֵן־תֹּחַלְתּ֥וֹ נִכְזָ֑בָה הֲגַ֖ם אֶל־מַרְאָ֣יו יֻטָֽל
verses.translation: See, any hope [of capturing] it must be disappointed;One is prostrated by the very sight of it.
verses.rashi: Behold his hope is in vain Behold if his hope is in vain, for he will have no strength to wage war and to move from his place. shall not one be cast down even at the sight of him if even at the sight of his face a man will be cast down from standing before him to look at him, because of the fear of him.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹֽא־אַ֭כְזָר כִּ֣י יְעוּרֶ֑נּוּ וּמִ֥י ה֝֗וּא לְפָנַ֥י יִתְיַצָּֽב
verses.translation: There is no one so fierce as to rouse it;Who then can stand up to Me?
verses.rashi: There is no one so fierce that he can stir him up There is no one so fierce, mighty, and strong who can arouse him from his sleep and stir him up from his place to raise him up. and who is he who will stand up before Me, Who makes all these?

verses.hebrew_full: מִ֣י הִ֭קְדִּימַנִי וַאֲשַׁלֵּ֑ם תַּ֖חַת כׇּל־הַשָּׁמַ֣יִם לִי־הֽוּא
verses.translation: Whoever confronts Me I will requite,For everything under the heavens is Mine.
verses.rashi: Who came to meet Me Who is it who came to meet Me by accepting to worship Me and keep My commandments, and I will pay him his reward. Everything under the heavens is Mine Everything that is under the heavens is Mine, and I have the power to pay him his reward, his recompense, and his remuneration.

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace

verses.hebrew_full: (לא) לוֹ־אַחֲרִ֥ישׁ בַּדָּ֑יו וּדְבַר־גְּ֝בוּר֗וֹת וְחִ֣ין עֶרְכּֽוֹ
verses.translation: I will not be silent concerning itOr the praise of its martial exploits.
verses.rashi: I will not be silent concerning his sons I will not be silent concerning the sons of a righteous man who went before Me sincerely, and his sons will be paid the good reward of their father, and they will find good in his merit. either for his acts of heroism and the arrangement of his supplication Either for his heroic deeds, that he strengthened himself to do what is good and upright, and the arrangement of the righteous man’s supplication [is that] I will pay his reward also to his sons after him. his sons Heb. בדיו, his branches, and they are his sons, like (Ezek. 17:6), “and brought forth branches (בדים) and sent out sprigs.”

words.eng: unto him; v. ל־.·to cut in

verses.hebrew_full: מִֽי־גִ֭לָּה פְּנֵ֣י לְבוּשׁ֑וֹ בְּכֶ֥פֶל רִ֝סְנ֗וֹ מִ֣י יָבֽוֹא
verses.translation: Who can uncover its outer garment?Who can penetrate the folds of its jowls?
verses.rashi: Who exposed the face of his raiment He returns to the previous topic to speak of the leviathan: who can expose the scales, which are... within his double bridle Into the fold of his lips, when they are open, who is it who will enter there? [The expression] בְּכֶפֶל, double, denotes the lips, which are double, one above and one below, into which no creature may enter because of fear of him.

verses.hebrew_full: דַּלְתֵ֣י פָ֭נָיו מִ֣י פִתֵּ֑חַ סְבִיב֖וֹת שִׁנָּ֣יו אֵימָֽה
verses.translation: Who can pry open the doors of its face?Its bared teeth strike terror.
verses.rashi: the doors of his face They are the lips when he closes his mouth; who can open them? Around his teeth is terror That because of his fright and his terror, no creature can approach his teeth.

verses.hebrew_full: גַּ֭אֲוָה אֲפִיקֵ֣י מָגִנִּ֑ים סָ֝ג֗וּר חוֹתָ֥ם צָֽר
verses.translation: Its protective scales are its pride,Locked with a binding seal.
verses.rashi: There is pride in his strong shields He takes great pride in the strength of his shields; they are the scales that are on him, which protect him like a shield. closed That is the seal of his coat of mail, which is narrow, closed, and strong, for each scale is attached to him and attached to the next one, so that no creature can strike him between the scales. This is what is stated: They are so close to each other, that all of them are brought together and are close to each other.

verses.hebrew_full: אֶחָ֣ד בְּאֶחָ֣ד יִגַּ֑שׁוּ וְ֝ר֗וּחַ לֹא־יָבֹ֥א בֵינֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: One scale touches the other;Not even a breath can enter between them.

verses.hebrew_full: אִישׁ־בְּאָחִ֥יהוּ יְדֻבָּ֑קוּ יִ֝תְלַכְּד֗וּ וְלֹ֣א יִתְפָּרָֽדוּ
verses.translation: Each clings to each;They are interlocked so they cannot be parted.
verses.rashi: they stick together and cannot be separated He is still repeating his language, that they [the scales] are stuck together and each one is joined to the next one so that they should not separate from each other.

verses.hebrew_full: עֲֽ֭טִישֹׁתָיו תָּ֣הֶל א֑וֹר וְ֝עֵינָ֗יו כְּעַפְעַפֵּי־שָֽׁחַר
verses.translation: Its sneezings flash lightning,And its eyes are like the glimmerings of dawn.
verses.rashi: His sneezes flash forth light With every sneeze that he sneezes, light flashes forth and shines. flash forth as in (above 29:3), “when He lit (בהלו) His candle.” and his eyes are like the rays of dawn His eyes shine and illuminate like the redness of the sun, like the time of morning when the sun becomes red.

verses.hebrew_full: מִ֭פִּיו לַפִּידִ֣ים יַהֲלֹ֑כוּ כִּיד֥וֹדֵי אֵ֝֗שׁ יִתְמַלָּֽטוּ
verses.translation: Firebrands stream from its mouth;Fiery sparks escape.
verses.rashi: From his mouth From the flame that goes forth from his mouth. From his mouth go firebrands Firebrands come forth from his mouth. sparks of fire Like firebrands. go forth like foam Heb. יתמלטו . Its interpretation is according to the context, escumicer in Old French [going forth like foam].

verses.hebrew_full: מִ֭נְּחִירָיו יֵצֵ֣א עָשָׁ֑ן כְּד֖וּד נָפ֣וּחַ וְאַגְמֹֽן
verses.translation: Out of its nostrils comes smokeAs from a steaming, boiling cauldron.
verses.rashi: From his nostrils smoke goes forth like a bubbling pot on the fire, which raises smoke. and an earthenware vessel ואגמן. Its interpretation is according to its context: an expression of an earthenware vessel, like a bubbling pot; according to the matter that is stated (Jer. 1:13): “I see a bubbling pot.”

verses.hebrew_full: נַ֭פְשׁוֹ גֶּחָלִ֣ים תְּלַהֵ֑ט וְ֝לַ֗הַב מִפִּ֥יו יֵצֵֽא
verses.translation: Its breath ignites coals;Flames blaze from its mouth.
verses.rashi: His breath kindles coals Burning and blazing coals. and flame And burning flames emerge from his mouth.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּֽ֭צַוָּארוֹ יָלִ֣ין עֹ֑ז וּ֝לְפָנָ֗יו תָּד֥וּץ דְּאָבָֽה
verses.translation: Strength resides in its neck;Power leaps before it.
verses.rashi: Strength lodges in his neck Mightiness. and sadness rejoices before him For there is no sadness or grief before him, since he does not fear any creature. [The word] תָּדוּץ is an expression of joy and happiness.

verses.hebrew_full: מַפְּלֵ֣י בְשָׂר֣וֹ דָבֵ֑קוּ יָצ֥וּק עָ֝לָ֗יו בַּל־יִמּֽוֹט
verses.translation: The layers of its flesh stick together;It is as though cast hard; it does not totter.
verses.rashi: The flakes of his flesh are joined together Heb. דבקו. They adhere to each other closely, for the fish are composed of many pieces in its flesh, and they adhere to one another. In a large fish, which is cooked, the pieces are noticeable when they are lying one upon the other and one beside the other, and so is the sequence of the world: First he speaks of the scales of the leviathan and then of the flakes of his flesh and the strength of his heart. flakes Heb. מפלי, an expression of folds of pieces. It is also possible to explain it as פְלָאֵי פלוּיֵי, [if it is split] in the language of the Talmud, an expression of the splits of his flakes. are joined together the “beth” is vowelized by a small “kamatz” (tzeireh), like (Job 29:1O), “and their tongues stuck (דבקה) to their palates,” and many other cantillated by “ethrahta” or “sof pasuk.” [These are the pause accents.] poured out upon it Heb. יצוק. Every flake poured out one upon the other, so that it should not move or separate one from the other. [The word] יָצוּק is an expression of being poured, and it is the passive participle, like יָרוּד, descended, from ירד ; יָצוּק, poured, from יצק ; יָדוּעַ, known, from ידע.

verses.hebrew_full: לִ֭בּוֹ יָצ֣וּק כְּמוֹ־אָ֑בֶן וְ֝יָצ֗וּק כְּפֶ֣לַח תַּחְתִּֽית
verses.translation: Its heart is cast hard as a stone,Hard as the nether millstone.
verses.rashi: His heart is as strong as stone His heart within him is as strong and basic as a permanent stone that is placed securely in the foundation of a building. and as strong as a lower millstone This clause is repetitious, meaning that his heart is as basic and permanent as the millstone that is placed under the upper millstone, for the upper millstone revolves and rotates and grinds, whereas the lower one does not move from its place because it is fastened to its place, so that it should not move. That is called פֶּלַח [רֶכֶב] as it is stated (Jud. 9:53): “And a certain woman cast an upper millstone: פֶּלַח רֶכֶב, upon Abimelech’s head.” The lower one is called פֶלַח תַּחתִּית the lower piece.

verses.hebrew_full: מִ֭שֵּׂתוֹ יָג֣וּרוּ אֵלִ֑ים מִ֝שְּׁבָרִ֗ים יִתְחַטָּֽאוּ
verses.translation: Divine beings are in dread as it rears up;As it crashes down, they cringe.
verses.rashi: From his fear the mighty are frightened From his fear, the mighty and strong, both fishes and other creatures, are frightened. the breakers fail The waves of the sea, which are called breakers, fail before him, because he breaks them with his swimming, as he swims and floats in the waves of the sea. [The word] יִתְחַטָּאוּ is an expression of a lack, like (I Kings 1:21), “and I and my son Solomon will be lacking (חַטָּאִים).”

verses.hebrew_full: מַשִּׂיגֵ֣הוּ חֶ֭רֶב בְּלִ֣י תָק֑וּם חֲנִ֖ית מַסָּ֣ע וְשִׁרְיָֽה
verses.translation: No sword that overtakes it can prevail,Nor spear, nor missile, nor lance.
verses.rashi: He who overtakes him with a sword—it will not remain A mighty man, who overtakes him to wage battle with a sword—his sword will not remain, because he will break it. [neither will] a spear, a slingshot, or a coat of mail Likewise, none of these weapons that are in the hands of the overtaker will either remain or stand up before the leviathan. The word מַסָע may be said to be one type of weapon, like the sword, spear, and coat of mail(?). מַסָע may also be interpreted after the sample of (I Kings 6:7), “whole stones as they were transported (מַסָע) etc.,” and this is its interpretation: a transported spear, which is large and not so easily moved, because it is large and very strong; as the matter is stated concerning Goliath the Philistine (I Sam. 17:7), “And the shaft of his spear was like a weaver’s beam, and the spear’s head was six hundred shekels, etc.” a coat of mail Heb. ושריה, a coat of mail (שריון).

verses.hebrew_full: יַחְשֹׁ֣ב לְתֶ֣בֶן בַּרְזֶ֑ל לְעֵ֖ץ רִקָּב֣וֹן נְחוּשָֽׁה
verses.translation: It regards iron as straw,Bronze, as rotted wood.
verses.rashi: He regards iron as straw Iron weapons are regarded by him as straw, and copper weapons are to him as rotten wood.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־יַבְרִיחֶ֥נּוּ בֶן־קָ֑שֶׁת לְ֝קַ֗שׁ נֶהְפְּכוּ־ל֥וֹ אַבְנֵי־קָֽלַע
verses.translation: No arrow can put it to flight;Slingstones turn into stubble for it.
verses.rashi: No arrow can put him to flight He will not flee before an archer, and arrows and slingstones are as stubble to him.

verses.hebrew_full: כְּ֭קַשׁ נֶחְשְׁב֣וּ תוֹתָ֑ח וְ֝יִשְׂחַ֗ק לְרַ֣עַשׁ כִּידֽוֹן
verses.translation: Clubs are regarded as stubble;It scoffs at the quivering javelin.
verses.rashi: Catapult [stones] a weapon, to be interpreted according to the context. and he plays at the noise of the harpoon At the noise of the tumult of warriors who carry harpoons, he plays and strolls, for he does not fear them.

verses.hebrew_full: תַּ֭חְתָּיו חַדּ֣וּדֵי חָ֑רֶשׂ יִרְפַּ֖ד חָר֣וּץ עֲלֵי־טִֽיט
verses.translation: Its underpart is jagged shards;It spreads a threshing-sledge on the mud.
verses.rashi: Under him are rays of the sun in the place he rests, there are rays of the sun, because his fins, which are under him on his belly, illuminate and shine like the sun. rays Heb. חדודי, like a sharp sword, which is sharpened, bright, and whitened. Because of this, he calls rays חַדוּדֵי. sun Heb. חרשׂ, the sun, like (above 9:7), “He who spoke to the sun (לחרס) and it did not shine”; (Jud. 14:18); “before the sun (החרסה) set. “ where he lies is gold upon the mire This is a repeated clause. “Where he lies is gold,” refers to “rays of the sun”; “upon the mire” refers to “under him,” in the place where he is on the sand; this is the mire where he lies. It shines like precious gold, as it is stated (Song 3:10): “its couch of gold.” חָרוּץ is a type of gold, as it is stated (Prov. 8:19): “My fruit is better than gold (מחרוץ), yea than fine gold.” Where he lies—an expression of a couch for lying down, like (Song 3:10), “its couch (רפידתו) of gold.”

verses.hebrew_full: יַרְתִּ֣יחַ כַּסִּ֣יר מְצוּלָ֑ה יָ֝֗ם יָשִׂ֥ים כַּמֶּרְקָחָֽה
verses.translation: It makes the depths seethe like a cauldron;It makes the sea [boil] like an ointment-pot.
verses.rashi: He makes the deep seethe like a pot Like a small bubbling pot, that quickly raises bubbles one after another, so does he cause the deep, which is large and deep, to seethe. he makes the sea like a seething mixture Like a mixture of a concoction of spices, which is compounded and mixed, so does he make and mix the sea when he moves from his place.

verses.hebrew_full: אַ֭חֲרָיו יָאִ֣יר נָתִ֑יב יַחְשֹׁ֖ב תְּה֣וֹם לְשֵׂיבָֽה
verses.translation: Its wake is a luminous path;It makes the deep seem white-haired.
verses.rashi: He makes a path shine after him When he swims and floats in the sea, he makes the path of the deep shine, for at the time he swims, there is no water because of the speed of his swimming. he considers the deep to be hoary To him, the deep is considered to be weak and feeble. to be hoary The weakness of old age and hoariness.

verses.hebrew_full: אֵֽין־עַל־עָפָ֥ר מׇשְׁל֑וֹ הֶ֝עָשׂ֗וּ לִבְלִי־חָֽת
verses.translation: There is no one on land who can dominate it,Made as it is without fear.
verses.rashi: On the dust there is none that rules over him The “mem” [is vowelized] with a “hataf kamatz,” and it means his rule, that no creature rules over him, and it is a noun denoting a ruler. Therefore, the “mem” is vowelized with a “hataf kamatz.” which is made Heb. העשו, like העשוי [the passive participle]. However, I know of no similar case [in Scripture]. It means one that was made without fear or dismay, that is not afraid of the creatures, as it is stated concerning the horse (above 39:22): “He scorns fear and is not dismayed.”

verses.hebrew_full: אֵֽת־כׇּל־גָּבֹ֥הַּ יִרְאֶ֑ה ה֝֗וּא מֶ֣לֶךְ עַל־כׇּל־בְּנֵי־שָֽׁחַץ
verses.translation: It sees all that is haughty;It reigns over all proud beasts.
verses.rashi: He looks at all high things All the high things in the world he sees beneath himself, for so is the custom of the world, that whoever stands above sees the one below more than the one below sees [him]. Also, the one below has no power or victory against the one above. Now this leviathan sees all mighty men; that is to say, they have no power against him. he is king over all proud beasts This is a repeated expression. He looks over all proud beasts and he is king over all the proud beasts, over all haughty creatures that I created in My world, who have no rule or dominion against him. Now how did you dare to lift your head before Me, since I perform all these wonders?

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·all·adj

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֖עַן אִיּ֥וֹב אֶת־יְהֹוָ֗ה וַיֹּאמַֽר
verses.translation: Job said in reply to GOD:

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Commonly transcribed YHWH

verses.hebrew_full: (ידעת) יָ֭דַעְתִּי כִּי־כֹ֣ל תּוּכָ֑ל וְלֹֽא־יִבָּצֵ֖ר מִמְּךָ֣ מְזִמָּֽה
verses.translation: I know that You can do everything,That nothing You propose is impossible for You.
verses.rashi: Job’s ReplyI knew that You can do everything That You can do anything that pleases You. and no design is restrained from You He repeats his words, that no design or plan is restrained or withheld from You, that You can complete and fulfill Your plans, for strength and might are in Your hand, and You have the power to do all that pleases You. This verse is similar to (Gen. 11:6), “Now will nothing that they have planned to do be restrained (יבצר) from them?”

verses.hebrew_full: מִ֤י זֶ֨ה מַעְלִ֥ים עֵצָ֗ה בְּֽלִ֫י־דָ֥עַת לָכֵ֣ן הִ֭גַּדְתִּי וְלֹ֣א אָבִ֑ין נִפְלָא֥וֹת מִ֝מֶּ֗נִּי וְלֹ֣א אֵדָֽע
verses.translation: Who is this who obscures counsel without knowledge?Indeed, I spoke without understandingOf things beyond me, which I did not know.
verses.rashi: Who is this who concealed and covered the counsel and the wonders of the Holy One, blessed be He, without knowledge? I knew that everything is in Your hand to do, and so I related some of Your mighty deeds as were in my heart; as I knew [them]. but I did not understand But I did not understand so much, as You informed me, for many wonders are hidden and concealed from me, which I do not know.

verses.hebrew_full: שְֽׁמַֽע־נָ֭א וְאָנֹכִ֣י אֲדַבֵּ֑ר אֶ֝שְׁאָלְךָ֗ וְהוֹדִיעֵֽנִי
verses.translation: Hear now, and I will speak;I will ask, and You will inform me.

verses.hebrew_full: לְשֵֽׁמַע־אֹ֥זֶן שְׁמַעְתִּ֑יךָ וְ֝עַתָּ֗ה עֵינִ֥י רָאָֽתְךָ
verses.translation: I had heard You with my ears,But now I see You with my eyes;
verses.rashi: I have heard of You by the hearing of the ear Many many times I have heard of You. and now, my eye has seen You Your Shechinah.

verses.hebrew_full: עַל־כֵּ֭ן אֶמְאַ֣ס וְנִחַ֑מְתִּי עַל־עָפָ֥ר וָאֵֽפֶר
verses.translation: Therefore, I recant and relent,Being but dust and ashes.
verses.rashi: Therefore Since I merited to see Your Shechinah. I despise I despise my life. on dust and ashes if I were resting in the grave, to return to dust and ashes whence I was taken.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיְהִ֗י אַחַ֨ר דִּבֶּ֧ר יְהֹוָ֛ה אֶת־הַדְּבָרִ֥ים הָאֵ֖לֶּה אֶל־אִיּ֑וֹב וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יְהֹוָ֜ה אֶל־אֱלִיפַ֣ז הַתֵּימָנִ֗י חָרָ֨ה אַפִּ֤י בְךָ֙ וּבִשְׁנֵ֣י רֵעֶ֔יךָ כִּ֠י לֹ֣א דִבַּרְתֶּ֥ם אֵלַ֛י נְכוֹנָ֖ה כְּעַבְדִּ֥י אִיּֽוֹב
verses.translation: After GOD had spoken these words to Job, GOD said to Eliphaz the Temanite, “I am incensed at you and your two friends, for you have not spoken the truth about Me as did My servant Job.
verses.rashi: and your two companions Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite. because you did not speak Because you did not speak to Me with a correct argument as did My servant Job, for he did not rebel against Me except for what he said (above 9:22), “He destroys both the innocent and the wicked,” and through the Adversary who denounces the world, as it is stated (ibid. verse 23): “If the scourge kills suddenly etc.” And if he continued to speak, he spoke because of the severity of the pains that burdened him and overwhelmed him; but you rebelled by condemning him, saying (4: 6), “Behold, your fear was your foolishness,” and you held him to be a wicked man, and at the end when you were silenced and defeated before him, you should have consoled him as Elihu did. Was it not enough for Job with his trouble and his sufferings, that you added rebellion to your sins to provoke him?

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·speech

verses.hebrew_full: וְעַתָּ֡ה קְחוּ־לָכֶ֣ם שִׁבְעָֽה־פָרִים֩ וְשִׁבְעָ֨ה אֵילִ֜ים וּלְכ֣וּ אֶל־עַבְדִּ֣י אִיּ֗וֹב וְהַעֲלִיתֶ֤ם עוֹלָה֙ בַּעַדְכֶ֔ם וְאִיּ֣וֹב עַבְדִּ֔י יִתְפַּלֵּ֖ל עֲלֵיכֶ֑ם כִּ֧י אִם־פָּנָ֣יו אֶשָּׂ֗א לְבִלְתִּ֞י עֲשׂ֤וֹת עִמָּכֶם֙ נְבָלָ֔ה כִּ֠י לֹ֣א דִבַּרְתֶּ֥ם אֵלַ֛י נְכוֹנָ֖ה כְּעַבְדִּ֥י אִיּֽוֹב
verses.translation: Now take seven bulls and seven rams and go to My servant Job and sacrifice a burnt offering for yourselves. And let Job, My servant, pray for you; for to him I will show favor and not treat you vilely, since you have not spoken the truth about Me as did My servant Job.”
verses.rashi: and offer up a burnt offering for yourselves So that you placate and appease Me, provided that My servant Job pray for you that I forgive your sins, and no longer remember your transgressions. for I will favor him Heb. כי אם, because I will favor him to accept his prayer (thus the word אִם is used for “that”), and likewise (Gen. 24:19), “until (עד אם) they have finished drinking”; (Ex. 21:30), “in which case (אם) an atonement is placed on him,” and many others. for...not Because...not.

words.eng: to take·inflected m. pers. pron. meaning ‘to you’.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֵּלְכוּ֩ אֱלִיפַ֨ז הַתֵּימָנִ֜י וּבִלְדַּ֣ד הַשּׁוּחִ֗י צֹפַר֙ הַנַּ֣עֲמָתִ֔י וַיַּעֲשׂ֔וּ כַּאֲשֶׁ֛ר דִּבֶּ֥ר אֲלֵיהֶ֖ם יְהֹוָ֑ה וַיִּשָּׂ֥א יְהֹוָ֖ה אֶת־פְּנֵ֥י אִיּֽוֹב
verses.translation: Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went and did as GOD had told them, and GOD showed favor to Job.
verses.rashi: and the Lord favored Job Heb. וישא, [lit. and the Lord lifted His countenance] as in (Gen. 19: 21), “I have favored you (נשאתי) also in this respect,” that He accepted his prayer and showed him a bright countenance.

words.eng: to turn to or from; to go. Targ. Y. Gen. XXIV

verses.hebrew_full: וַיהֹוָ֗ה שָׁ֚ב אֶת־שְׁב֣וּת (שבית) אִיּ֔וֹב בְּהִֽתְפַּֽלְל֖וֹ בְּעַ֣ד רֵעֵ֑הוּ וַיֹּ֧סֶף יְהֹוָ֛ה אֶת־כׇּל־אֲשֶׁ֥ר לְאִיּ֖וֹב לְמִשְׁנֶֽה
verses.translation: GOD restored Job’s fortunes when he prayed on behalf of his friends, and GOD gave Job twice what he had before.
verses.rashi: Now the Lord returned Job’s captivity when he prayed [The word] שָׁב is inappropriate in this matter, and it is as though it were written: “And He returned (נשאתי) Job’s captivity when he prayed.” for his friend In the merit that he prayed for each friend of his. twice as much Heb. למשנה, for one, two; and Scripture was not exact in its wording.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·captivity
sign of the definite direct object·all·(relative part.)

verses.hebrew_full: וַיָּבֹ֣אוּ אֵ֠לָ֠יו כׇּל־אֶחָ֨יו וְכׇל־אַחְיֹתָ֜יו וְכׇל־יֹדְעָ֣יו לְפָנִ֗ים וַיֹּאכְל֨וּ עִמּ֣וֹ לֶ֘חֶם֮ בְּבֵיתוֹ֒ וַיָּנֻ֤דוּ לוֹ֙ וַיְנַחֲמ֣וּ אֹת֔וֹ עַ֚ל כׇּל־הָ֣רָעָ֔ה אֲשֶׁר־הֵבִ֥יא יְהֹוָ֖ה עָלָ֑יו וַיִּתְּנוּ־ל֗וֹ אִ֚ישׁ קְשִׂיטָ֣ה אֶחָ֔ת וְאִ֕ישׁ נֶ֥זֶם זָהָ֖ב אֶחָֽד
verses.translation: All his brothers and sisters and all his former friends came to him and had a meal with him in his house. They consoled and comforted him for all the misfortune that GOD had brought upon him. Each gave him one kesitah A unit of unknown value. and each one gold ring.
verses.rashi: previous All who were his friends and acquaintances before the sufferings befell him. and they bemoaned him Complaindre in Old French, to complain. one piece of money a ma’ah. and each one and every one of them.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: וַיהֹוָ֗ה בֵּרַ֛ךְ אֶת־אַחֲרִ֥ית אִיּ֖וֹב מֵרֵֽאשִׁת֑וֹ וַֽיְהִי־ל֡וֹ אַרְבָּעָה֩ עָשָׂ֨ר אֶ֜לֶף צֹ֗אן וְשֵׁ֤שֶׁת אֲלָפִים֙ גְּמַלִּ֔ים וְאֶלֶף־צֶ֥מֶד בָּקָ֖ר וְאֶ֥לֶף אֲתוֹנֽוֹת
verses.translation: Thus GOD blessed the latter years of Job’s life more than the former. He had fourteen thousand sheep, six thousand camels, one thousand yoke of oxen, and one thousand jennies.
verses.rashi: more than his beginning More than his former wealth.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·after part

verses.hebrew_full: וַֽיְהִי־ל֛וֹ שִׁבְעָ֥נָה בָנִ֖ים וְשָׁל֥וֹשׁ בָּנֽוֹת
verses.translation: He also had seven sons and three daughters.
verses.rashi: fourteen Heb. שבענה. They were twice seven, two sevens, like (Lev. 12:5), שְׁבֻעַיִם which means two weeks.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּקְרָ֤א שֵׁם־הָאַחַת֙ יְמִימָ֔ה וְשֵׁ֥ם הַשֵּׁנִ֖ית קְצִיעָ֑ה וְשֵׁ֥ם הַשְּׁלִישִׁ֖ית קֶ֥רֶן הַפּֽוּךְ
verses.translation: The first he named Jemimah, the second Keziah, and the third Keren-happuch.
verses.rashi: And he named the first Jemimah They were named according to their beauty. Jemimah Bright and white as the sun (יום). Keziah She had a fragrant and perfumed scent like the spice, cassia. Keren-happuch Because of the horn in which they put stibium and lixivium, as it is stated (Jer. 4: 30): “that you enlarge your eye with paint (בפוך).”

verses.hebrew_full: וְלֹ֨א נִמְצָ֜א נָשִׁ֥ים יָפ֛וֹת כִּבְנ֥וֹת אִיּ֖וֹב בְּכׇל־הָאָ֑רֶץ וַיִּתֵּ֨ן לָהֶ֧ם אֲבִיהֶ֛ם נַחֲלָ֖ה בְּת֥וֹךְ אֲחֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: Nowhere in the land were women as beautiful as Job’s daughters to be found. Their father gave them estates together with their brothers.
verses.rashi: Nowhere...were women as beautiful as Job’s daughters to be found An instance of beautiful women like the phenomenon of the beauty of Job’s daughters was not found. This verse is on the pattern of (Num. 9:6), “But there was [an incident of] men who were unclean.” and their father gave them an inheritance among their brothers Because of (their esteem and) their beauty, he gave them an inheritance with their brothers.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיְחִ֤י אִיּוֹב֙ אַחֲרֵי־זֹ֔את מֵאָ֥ה וְאַרְבָּעִ֖ים שָׁנָ֑ה (וירא) וַיִּרְאֶ֗ה אֶת־בָּנָיו֙ וְאֶת־בְּנֵ֣י בָנָ֔יו אַרְבָּעָ֖ה דֹּרֽוֹת
verses.translation: Afterward, Job lived one hundred and forty years to see four generations of children and grandchildren.

sign of the definite direct object·son

verses.hebrew_full: וַיָּ֣מׇת אִיּ֔וֹב זָקֵ֖ן וּשְׂבַ֥ע יָמִֽים
verses.translation: So Job died old and contented.
verses.rashi: and sated with days Heb. וּשְׂבַע.From וְשָׂבֵעַ he says וּשְׂבַע, in the construct state.
